<a id="xeserver-overview"></a>

## XEServer Overview

> 📄 Source: [XEServer_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Overview.htm)

### XEServer Overview


Edifecs XEngine Server is an integrated interoperability solution that allows you to design, test, and execute business orchestrations for healthcare and general industry transactions. Edifecs XEngine Server reduces the complexity within your infrastructure and technology landscape by offering a unified channel management solution bringing the best capabilities of an EDI Gateway, HL7 messaging, an in-built Healthcare Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), integration engines and performance management.


The Edifecs XEngine delivers the widest range of formats, translation, and control functions of those available in the market to connect, comply, and cleanse data coming into your organization and ensures accurate and complete transmission of data out of your organization. With Edifecs XEngine, you can achieve industry-leading clean claim metrics, receive monthly updates to data code sets and formats, process high volume transactions, and detect not just errors but patterns and trends before system performance is impacted or trading partners report issues.


XEngine Server provides more than 20 connectors to integrate any protocol or application, and its powerful orchestration can quickly address the most common integration challenges. Healthcare organizations and their trading partners benefit from a robust information exchange, a broad range of clinical and administrative data to support business processes, improved first-pass rates, enhanced operational efficiency, and cost savings.


With XEngine Server, you can:

- Process a large number of message types spanning across proprietary formats to emerging standards such as JSON.
- Reduce the effort for compliance with 5010, WEDI SNIP, Operating Rules and ICD-10.
- Detect data errors at the edge of the enterprise to increase first-pass rates. The unified platform reduces costs and complexity.
- Leverage clinical and administrative data for multiple purposes in real time and batch.
- Integrate smoothly with core business processing systems including message queues and data lakes.
- Schedule, manage and monitor outgoing files using metering and balancing to prevent overflows.


XEngine Server is distributed in the following ways:

- As a full on-premise application,
- [As a Docker image](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/XESModuleDocker/9.2.2/Content/Home.htm). This option helps eliminate the need to configure environments for XEServer as the entire run-time environment - software (XEServer), all its dependencies, libraries, and configuration files required to run it are bundled into one self-sufficient image.

---

<a id="whats-new-in-xeserver-93"></a>

## What's New in XEServer 9.3?

> 📄 Source: [Whats_New_in_XEServer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Whats_New_in_XEServer.htm)

### What's New in this Release?


The following features are new in XEServer 9.4 and EAM 9.4released on 6/30/2025.


| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Enhancements | Note: To keep the XEngine Server's technical stack modern and secure, several third-party libraries included in the product have been upgraded or replaced. If your implementation includes custom Java, Groovy, or JavaScript code that relies on undocumented features or interfaces from these updated libraries, it may no longer function as expected. Therefore, it is strongly recommended that you thoroughly test your implementation in a lower environment before deploying it to production. Update to Config Tool: A new [-se] parameter is introduced to skip the existing configurations that are already present in the database when deploying the image. For more details, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ConfigTool_CLI_Usage.htm). Configure FHIR Validator definitions: EAM now allows you to view and configure the settings in XEngine Service to validate FHIR data against FHIR specification rules. XEngine Service supports multiple values for each [FHIR configuration property](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Data_Validation_Overview.htm#FHIR_Data_Validation). Configure buffer size in FS Outbound components: You can now set the buffer size using the **fs.outbound.write.buffer.size** environment variable in the [FS Outbound](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FS_Outbound.htm#Advance) component to improve performance when writing large files. For optimal performance with Network File System (NFS), a buffer size of 1M is recommended. *Update to Jetty based-services*: In Jetty based-services, the authentication type has been enhanced with the ability to configure it as a Macro. For more information, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Restful_Web_Service.htm#Authentication). *Macros for Authentication Mode*: The [XEC Authorization Service](https://help.stage.kollab.link/svc/product/xeconnect/9.4/Content/Authorization_Service.htm) has been improved with the ability to configure and set a stable and consistent [Authentication Method](https://help.stage.kollab.link/svc/product/xeconnect/9.4/Content/User_Authentication_Methods_Overview.htm) with the help of macros. You can now define an environment property and use it as macro for specifying the Authorization Method. Additionally, the settings for each Authorization Method can also be externalized and added as environment properties, enabling the user to manage the settings for each Authorization Method in one place – the profile's [Environment](https://help.stage.kollab.link/svc/product/xesmodulefhir/9.4/Content/FHIR_Authorization_Profile_Setup.htm) tab. Updates to Macros: The [XEServer Route Macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#XEConnec) now includes MD_XEC_HttpHeaderNames and MD_XEC_HttpHeader[]. These additions allow access to HTTP Headers for CORE Requests. SNI host check update: The host verification in Server Name Indication (SNI) introduced in Jetty-based services has been enhanced to comply with product security and provide flexibility to support backward compatibility with the previous releases and vice-versa. For more information, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Jetty_Server_Upgrade.htm#Host_check). **Note** This update requires XEServer 9.4 installation. Arbitrary attributes in Amazon SQS messages: To support communication with clients using the [Amazon SQS Extended Client Library](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-s3-messages.html), the ability to add arbitrary non-serialized attributes to SQS messages has been [introduced](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_SQS_Service.htm#Attributes). Mask Exchange/Message Log Properties: XEServer now allows you to mask Exchange/Message properties in your logs by configuring the [XESLoggerMaskedProperties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Logger_Tab.htm#LoggerMaskedProperties) environment property in your profile. Ignore Subroute failures: To simplify customization of error handling, the [Subroute component](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Subroute.htm#Settings) now allows to prevent propagation of subroute failures to parent routes in case of custom error handling implementation on subroutes. Updates to Balance Counters: Implemented a [new parameter](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ConfigTool_CLI_Usage.htm#Deploy_Bcounter_ZIP) in the tool to auto-detect and deploy only the counter groups that are not yet deployed. If a group is already present, the tool will issue a warning in the log indicating that the group was skipped. For example, if the Balance counter config image includes groups A, B, C, and D, and the environment’s database already has groups A, B, and C deployed, the tool will detect this and deploy only group D. It will also log that groups A, B, and C were skipped. XEServer allows you to set up [custom secret decoders](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Support_External_Secret_Providers.htm) that integrate with external secret storage systems or vaults (for example, CyberArk, HashiCorp Vault). Starting with version 9.4, XEServer no longer includes the embedded ActiveMQ Web Console. As an alternative, the product now provides a [CLI-based console](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ActiveMQ_Server_Service.htm#CLIConsole) that delivers equivalent functionality in a command-line format. XEServer now allows you to [externalize properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_in_Containerized_Environments.htm#ContainerizedEnvConf) and secrets by setting the *EDFX_PROPERTIES_CONTAINER_MODE_PATH* environment variable. Update AWS Clients: Amazon SQS component is migrated to AWS SDK for Java v2.x, since AWS SDK for Java v1.x is reaching its end-of-support. For more details, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_SQS_Service_Overview.htm). Edifecs Applications no longer support JMS Oracle OpenMQ Service and JMS HornetQ Service as certified messaging servers. RabbitMQ Server: The RabbitMQ server is not included in the package, but you can download it from the RabbitMQ website. For more details, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Edifecs_RabbitMQ_Server_Overview.htm). Updated Eclipse: Upgraded to the 2024-09 version to address the following vulnerabilities.[CVE-2020-27225](https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-27225) (High)[CVE-2023-4218](https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2023-4218) (Medium)[CVE-2010-4647](https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2010-4647) (Low) Fortify security fixes: Several patches that eliminate software vulnerabilities have been applied on the HP Fortify testing environment. Whitesource license compliance enhancements: Several patches that strengthen the product's security and monitor license compliance have been applied. |



#### Bug fixes


The following bugs have been fixed in XEServer 9.4 and EAM 9.4:


| Bug | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 227143 | For [Encryption](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Encryption.htm) and [Decryption](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Decryption.htm) components using Open PGP Algorithm, the COM_EDIFECS_XESERVER_PGP_IGNORE_FILTER_KEYS_BY_TYPE environment property was not working properly. |
| 233807 | In [DB Reader](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Reader.htm) component, the "order" parameter for statement groups was being altered by using Move Up and Move Down buttons on EAM, causing the skipping of execution of statement groups. |
| 229688 | Missing the ability to configure the timeout settings in [XEConnect CORE C4 Server](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xeconnect/Content/Configure_CORE_C4_Server.htm#Transport_tab) service, as the current 20-second timeout was hardcoded to comply with CAQH rules. To fix the issue, the timeout has been increased to 30 seconds. |
| 237609 | The Encryption component throws an exception indicating an invalid value when custom values are passed as property-based values. |
| 229476 | XEServer was unable to save profiles after adding the 'Sequencer' component, throwing an error:"Sequencer server is not specified”. |
| 229516 | The EAM user interface encountered challenges accessing Batcher data when the SQL database employed Kerberos authentication. |
| 229963 | Log Analyzer was unable to create reports or access logs, showing an error due to an invalid file path. |
| 228551 | The ECL Update component fails to handle certain scenarios correctly: The Unregister option does not report an error when the specified Code List is missing.A new route macro "MD_ECL_OperationStatus" is introduced to indicate ECL operation status. The values of this macro could be: SUCCESS, FAILED or ECL_NOT_FOUND. The Register option fails to parse XES macros correctly. Macro support has been added for Description and Comment field. |
| 238606 | BCounter throws an SQL exception "DB resource busy" error when running a profile on two servers simultaneously. |
| 229272 | The ECL Operation encounters an error while adding to the code list for Criteria, Description and Commentary fields not working with macros. Macro support has been added for the fields. |
| 233374 | The 837I claims generation process fails to display diagnosis codes after 17 (total number of diagnosis codes may vary from 20 to 25). |
| 238278 | Due to a path hashing issue, XEServer only removes records from the TDATA table, causing the EXPIRY table to accumulate more records. This issue has been resolved in the latest codebase. |
| 229532 | The Alternate ID is populated incorrectly in 33B and 24J on claim forms. The additional IDs have been added to the CMS-1500 Addendum page view. |
| 230729 | XES now supports integration with external secrets providers, such as CyberArk. |
| 231417 | Backup log files accumulate excessively in the rolling file appender, consuming significant disk space. |
| 229273 | The ECL Update component updates the incorrect Date type when inserting a code into a code list. |
| 229941 | The Encryption component fails to sign data when the signature option is enabled at runtime. This occurs due to issues in the Groovy Script component, which causes improper file routing. |
| 226820 | XEServer intermittently fails to upload files using the SFTP component. |
| 227264 | The FTP Command component experiences connectivity and file listing issues on the SFTP server. |
| 224476 | The SFTP component was unable to push files larger than 15KB. |
| 229354 | AWS connection throws an error when accessing the Amazon S3 service. |
| 229439 | SpecBuilder deployment fails. The issue was resolved by implementing new ECL fixes that run the H2 migration tool as a system process. |
| 236708 | The Data Scrubber component throws an error when processing data exactly 6,291,456 characters long (a power of 128×1024). The component has been updated to handle such input sizes correctly, preventing buffer overflows and ensuring stable processing. |



For the information on known issues in XEServer, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XES_Known_Issues.htm).


For the information on hardware and software required for XEServer 9.4, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_System_Requirements.htm).

<a id="xeserver-system-requirements"></a>

### XEServer System Requirements

> 📄 Source: [XEServer_System_Requirements.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_System_Requirements.htm)

#### XEServer System Requirements


XEngine Server 9.4 has the following requirements.


| Requirement | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Operating System (64-bit) | Microsoft Windows 11 Microsoft Windows Server 2019, 2022, 2025 Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7.x, 8.x, 9 SUSE Linux 12, 15 Amazon Linux 2 Kernel 5.10 AIX 7.2, 7.3 |
| Hardware | CPU: 4 - 32 cores 3.0 GHz (16 cores are recommended) RAM: 8 - 64 GB (16 GB are recommended) Hard disk space: 10 GB of free disk space Note: The actual CPU and RAM requirements may vary and should be based on the expected workload. |
| Certified Databases | Microsoft SQL Server 2017, 2019, 2022, 2025 Oracle Database 19c, 21c R21.3, 23c, 26ai([external installation required](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JDBC_Connectivity_Overview.htm)) IBM DB2 9.7 (Only for DB selector) PostgreSQL 13.3 MongoDB Community Server 7.0.4 |
| Certified Messaging Servers | Apache Active MQ v 6.0 IBM MQ v 9.2, 9.3.4 Note: Edifecs recommends using updated Jakarta version compatible with client jar and dependent json.jar. Oracle WebLogic Server 12cR2 (12.2.1.3), 14.1.1 Note: Edifecs recommends using the updated Jakarta version compatible with wlthint3client.jar. RabbitMQ 3.12.8 Apache Kafka 3.6.0 Amazon MQ 5.15.15 |
| Stream-processing software | Apache Kafka 1.0.1, 2.1.0 |
| Java | Java 17 is mandatory for the product. You must download and install JDK 17 prior to using the product. Adoptium OpenJDK 17.0.8+7 and 17.0.15+6 are certified Edifecs compatible for all supported operating systems. Edifecs supports the use of JDKs from Oracle, Adoptium, and other vendors if they are binary compatible. However, Edifecs does not endorse any of these options, and the use of these options is at your organization's own risk. Please seek legal counsel in selecting the JDK option that best suits your organization's needs and license requirements. |
| Other | Microsoft .NET Framework, version 4.0 or later. This is a requirement for using Microsoft .NET Framework wrappers. |

<a id="xeserver-installation"></a>

### XEServer Installation

> 📄 Source: [Book_XES_Installation.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_XES_Installation.htm)

#### XEServer Installation


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="set-java_home"></a>

#### Set JAVA_HOME

> 📄 Source: [Installation_Instructions/Set_JAVA_HOME.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Installation_Instructions/Set_JAVA_HOME.htm)

##### Set JAVA_HOME


Make sure that the JAVA_HOME variable is defined in your system before you run XEServer or EAM. If JAVA_HOME is not defined as an environment variable, add it to your permanent settings and point to the location of Java in your system.


Example


setx JAVA_HOME C:\jdk-11.0.11


Note JAVA_HOME must point to the location of the latest version of the Java Development Kit (JDK). Automatic Java updates may install the JDK in a directory different from the default one, so verify this setting if you have a problem connecting to the XEServer.

---

<a id="install-xeserver"></a>

#### Install XEServer

> 📄 Source: [Installation_Instructions/Install_XEServer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Installation_Instructions/Install_XEServer.htm)

##### Install XEServer


This section describes the default on-premise XEServer installation. To learn how to run XEServer in a Docker container, view [XESModule for Docker Help Center](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/XESModuleDocker/9.3/Content/Home.htm).


Before you start the installation process, be sure that you have set the necessary environment variables.


You also need to install Edifecs Application Manager. Edifecs Application Manager provides a simple interface for configuring and managing XEServer. For more information, see a [help documentation](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/eam/9.4/Content/Home_EAM.htm) for Edifecs Application Manager.


Note If in earlier versions of XEServer, you have customized the configuration of log4j 1.x, you should migrate your configuration to log4j 2.x since log4j 1.x has been upgraded to log4j 2.17.


To install XEServer, complete the following steps:

 *Prerequisites for installation on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.x You should install the libnsl.so.1 package first, before you install Edifecs Application Manager on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.x. Since the Edifecs Application Manager installer for UNIX systems requires the libnsl.so.1 library that is not installed in Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.x by default. Without this library, the Edifecs Application Manager installer will not be able to start. To install the library manually, set up a YUM repository, and then run the following command: yum install libnsl The library is installed at the following location: %ECRootPath%/usr/lib64/libnsl.so.1. #### Installation Steps To Do this Download XEServer To get the download instructions, contact our support team. Copy XEServer_(release number).(build number)_(archive date).exe or XEServer_(release number).(build number)_(archive date).msi to your workstation. Install XEServer To install XEServer using XEServer_(release number).(build number)_(archive date).msi: As an administrator, run XEServer_(release number).(build number)_(archive date).msi. The Windows Installer loads. Click Next. Review and accept the license agreement, and then click Next. Click Browse and provide a path to the license file (.lic), click the file (.lic) to use, and then click Next. Click Change to specify the folder where XEServer is installed, or click Next to accept the default location. By default, XEServer is installed in the directory indicated by the %ECRootPath% environment variable. Click Backup all files retained from previous installation of XEngine Server, to save any previous configuration changes. An XEServer.bak folder is created in the %ECRootPath% folder or the location that you specified for the installation. New configuration options may require updates to previous configuration files. Contact Edifecs support for help with updating configuration files. Click Install. Click Finish. XEServer is installed in the specified folder. To install XEServer using XEServer_(release number).(build number)_(archive date).exe: Double-click XEServer_(release number).(build number)_(archive date).exe to extract the installation files. Note The following executable files for the listed operating systems are available: Windows: XEServerInstaller.exe Linux: XEngineServerInstaller-linux-ix86 Double-click XEServerInstaller.exe to initiate the installation. Install XEServer Windows service Go to the ${XESRoot}\platform\agent\win-service directory. Run install_service.bat. Install XEServer with Edifecs Installation Manager Edifecs Installation Manager is a framework that allows you to remotely install and configure Edifecs products (XEngine, XEServer, and so on) on multiple hosts. Using Edifecs Installation Manager, you can: Install several products on different hosts. Configure installation parameters for these products. Manage products installed on remote hosts. To install XEServer using Edifecs Installation Manager, you should first install Edifecs Installation Manager. To get the Edifecs Installation Manager installer (eim-portal-dist-(release number).zip), contact Edifecs support*.

###### Installation


To install XEServer, complete the steps below.


###### Prerequisites


Before you install XEServer using Edifecs Installation Manager, make sure you have the following:

- Edifecs Installation Manager is [installed and configured](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/eim/1.0/Content/Set_up_Installation_Manager.htm) on the control host.
- Remote hosts for the Edifecs products are [prepared](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/eim/1.0/Content/Remote_Hosts_and_Third-Party_Software.htm). Edifecs Installation Manager can install XEServer on Windows and Linux. Before you install XEServer using Edifecs Installation Manager, make sure that a remote host has the required hardware and software installed.
- A remote host with installed and configured SSH Server is [added](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/eim/1.0/Content/EIM_Add_Server.htm) to Edifecs Installation Manager.


To install XEServer using Edifecs Installation Manager:

1. To get the download instructions, contact [*Edifecs support*](https://support.edifecs.com/).
2. On the control host, copy the distribution file named eim-xes-(release number).(build number).im.zip to the im_data [folder](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/eim/1.0/Content/Set_up_Installation_Manager.htm). The system environment variable $IM_DATA must point to this folder.
3. [Start](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/eim/1.0/Content/Set_up_Installation_Manager.htm) Edifecs Installation Manager if it has not yet been started.
4. On the Edifecs Installation Manager page, at the top right, in the PRODUCTS list, make sure that a corresponding XEServer item has appeared.
5. Under SERVERS, select a server (a remote host) you need.
6. Next to your server name, click , and then, from the drop-down menu, select Properties.
7. In the Edifecs Installation Manager page, under SERVERS, next to your server name, click Add Products/Components.
8. In the <YourServerName>/Add Products/Components pane, select a product (XEServer) you need. To specify installation parameters (for example, an XEServer license or EAM client credentials), right-click the corresponding element under XEServer, and then click Properties to enter the parameter value.
9. Click Add Selected. XEServer appears in your server list with products to install.
10. In the Edifecs Installation Manager page, under SERVERS, in your server, select XEServer, and then, at the top right, click Next.
11. On the DEPLOYMENT pane, click Start Deployment. XEServer is installed on your remote host.









After you have installed XEServer 9.4, you can find the RabbitMQ Server installers for Windows and Linux [here](https://github.com/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-server/releases/tag/v3.8.0). You can find the installers' source files here: for [Windows](https://github.com/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-server/archive/refs/tags/v3.8.0.zip) and [Linux](https://github.com/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-server/archive/refs/tags/v3.8.0.tar.gz). For more information on how to install RabbitMQ Server, see [Install RabbitMQ Server](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xeserver/9.4/Content/Install_Edifecs_RabbitMQ_Server.htm).

---

<a id="test-xeserver-installation"></a>

#### Test XEServer Installation

> 📄 Source: [Installation_Instructions/Test_XEServer_Installation.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Installation_Instructions/Test_XEServer_Installation.htm)

##### Test XEServer Installation


XEServer includes tools that can be used for testing JMS queues after the installation of XEServer is complete. A set of tools is available for certain queue types, such as IBM WebSphere MQ or Apache ActiveMQ. Each set of tools can be used to send JMS messages to a queue to verify that XEServer was able to receive the data that was sent through that queue for processing. They consist of a set of .bat and .properties files that define the queues and other properties. The files can be modified to match your configuration.


XEServer supports several queue types. After you have finished installing XEServer, you should verify that the queue type(s) being used in your environment are working properly. XEServer includes a set of tools that can be found at the following location: ${XESRoot}\test\jms\. XEServer provides the following queue testing tools:


| Routing Type | Tools |
| --- | --- |
| ActiveMQ | The following tools are available for the ActiveMQ queue type: receiver.bat sender.bat |
| IBM MQ | The following tools are available for the IBM MQ queue type: createCCDT.bat createManager.bat createQCFactory_BindingMode.bat createQCFactory_CCDT.bat createQCFactory_ClientMode.bat createQueues.bat deleteManager.bat receiver.bat removeQueues.bat sender.bat startManager.bat stopManager.bat |
| IBM MQ (SSL) | The following tools are available for the IBM MQ (SSL) queue type: createCCDT.bat createCertificates.bat createManager.bat createQCFactory_BindingMode.bat createQCFactory_CCDT.bat createQCFactory_ClientMode.bat createQueues.bat deleteManager.bat receiver.bat removeQueues.bat sender.bat startManager.bat stopManager.bat |
| WebLogic JMS | The following tools are available for the WebLogic JMS queue type: createQueues.bat receiver.bat removeQueues.bat sender.bat |

---

<a id="install-xeserver-using-docker-image"></a>

#### Install XEServer Using Docker Image

> 📄 Source: [Install_XEServer_Using_Docker_Image.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Install_XEServer_Using_Docker_Image.htm)

##### Install XEServer Using Docker Image


Beside the default on-premise installation, XEServer is distributed as a self-sufficient Docker image with all its dependencies, libraries, and configuration files. You can use this image as a module that can be bound together with other Edifecs products running in Docker containers and make them operate as a single mechanism using an orchestration tool.


For the information on how to get the Docker image for XEServer and run XEServer in a Docker container on Kubrnetes/Openshift cluster, view [XESModule for Docker Help Center](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/XESModuleDocker/9.3/Content/Home.htm).

---

<a id="uninstall-xeserver"></a>

#### Uninstall XEServer

> 📄 Source: [Installation_Instructions/Uninstall_XEServer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Installation_Instructions/Uninstall_XEServer.htm)

##### Uninstall XEServer


There are different methods for uninstalling XEServer, depending on how it was installed.


| To | Do this |
| --- | --- |
| Uninstall through Control Panel (assumes InstallShield or Wix installer was used) | Open the Control Panel and select Programs and Features. Select XEServer from the list, and then click Uninstall. |
| Uninstall Manually | If the installation was performed by using Perl script or by unpacking the .tar file, delete the XEServer directory. Note Do not use this method if you installed the product by using the UI Install Wizard. |

> 📄 Source: [Book_Upgrade_XEServer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Upgrade_XEServer.htm)

##### Upgrade XEServer
[Redirect TO: {DSA_Certificates_Support}](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DSA_Certificates_Support.htm)

This section has the following information:

---

<a id="dsa-certificates-support"></a>

##### DSA Certificates Support

> 📄 Source: [DSA_Certificates_Support.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DSA_Certificates_Support.htm)

###### DSA Certificates Support


This section has instructions on how to migrate from pre-9.1 XEServer versions to XEServer 9.2 and later. As a requisite to migrating to Java 17, there are a few key points that have to be taken into consideration.


###### DSA certificates are not supported for SSL connections


The migration of XEServer to Java 17 has affected the XEServer SSL connectivity. Since Java 17 introduced the [TLS 1.3](https://openjdk.java.net/projects/jdk/17/) to encrypt data for IP-based protocols (considering the TLS 1.1 to be insecure, and the TLS 1.2 to be vulnerable to certain threats), the latter no longer supports DSA certificates. A typical exception you may observe when using DSA certificates with XEServer is javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException. This means that if your REST, SOAP, FTPS, or SMTP components are configured to use DSA certificates for SSL connections to third-party systems, for example:

- An external HTTP client connects to the XEServer RESTful service,
- the REST Client component connects to an external web service,
- Tracking and Duplication Server service connects to an external Elasticsearch,
- Edifecs Rabbit MQ Server connects to an external JMS server,
- An FTPS service connects to an external FTP server,
- An Email client connects to an external SMTP/POP3 server,


then you have to replace them with RSA certificates.


Additionally, if you have replaced the default self-signed certificates used for connections to XESManager, Visualizer Server, or XProcess Management with DSA certificates, you may not be able to connect to these services and should use RSA certificates instead.


To replace the DSA certificates with RSA certificates:

1. Shutdown the XEServer profile that you want to update.
2. Update the XEServer keystore and the keystore of the external web service. For XEServer, you have three options: - Remove the existing DSA key and create a new RSA key with the same password. In this case, you do not have to update the XEServer configuration. - Generate a new RSA key in the existing XEServer keystore. In this case, you have to update the XEServer configuration and specify a password for the new key. - Create a new keystore and add a new RSA key to the keystore. In this case, you have to update the XEServer configuration and specify the path to the new keystore and the password for the new key.
3. Update XEServer configuration (if either the keystore password or the path to the keystore has changed). The following table shows you how to update the SSL configuration for certain XEServer components: | XEServer component / service | SSL settings location | Required action | | --- | --- | --- | | Email Inbound, Email Outbound components | Settings tab / SSL tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). Note For the Email Inbound component, ensure that the Connection Security option (located on the Common tab of the component's settings) is set to SSL/TLS or Start TLS. For the Email Outbound component, ensure that the Connection Security option (located on the Server Options tab of the component's settings) is set to SSL/TLS or Start TLS. | | FTP, SFTP, FTPS services | Service Security tab | In the User certificate store section, select the JKS type, enter a new Password (if the password has changed), and specify Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Job Scheduler Client service | Service Callback tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Job Scheduler Server service | Service Callback Server tab / SSL section | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Kafka 2 Service | Service SSL tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | RabbitMQ Service | Service SSL Settings tab | In the User certificate store section, select the JKS type, enter a new Password (if the password has changed), and specify Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | REST Client component | Settings tab / SSL tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Restful Web Service | Service Jetty tab / SSL section | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Sequence Manager Service | Service Jetty tab [Jetty Server = Dedicated] / SSL section | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | SOAP Call component | Settings tab / SSL tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Tracking and Duplication Client service | Service Configuration tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). Note To enable SSL settings, the RESTful WebService URL field must contain the "https" scheme. | | Tracking and Duplication Server service | Service Jetty tab [Jetty Server = Dedicated] / SSL section | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Visualizer Reporter Service | Service SSL Settings tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Web Service | Service Jetty tab [Jetty Server = Dedicated] / SSL section | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | Web Service Call component | Settings tab / SSL tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | XEConnect MLLP Channel service | Service SSL tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | XEConnect MLLP Client component | Settings tab / SSL tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). | | XPM Service | Service SSL tab | Enter a new Key password (if the password has changed) and the Path to the new keystore (if you created a new keystore). |
4. Start the profile.


If you still want to continue to use DSA certificates with XEServer 9.2 and later, you have a couple of ways to do that:

- You can specify the following properties on the Profile Startup tab to force XEServer to use a lower TLS protocol version: - -Djdk.tls.client.protocols=TLSv1.2Set this property if XEServer represents the client side. For example, the XEServer REST Client component connects to an external RESTful web service. - -Djdk.tls.server.protocols=TLSv1.2Set this property if XEServer represents the server side. For example, XEServer RESTful Web Service service listens for requests from external applications.
- You can start an XEServer process using an older Java version, for example, 1.8. Though this method forces XEServer to use TLS 1.2, Edifecs does not recommend that you use this method as a permanent solution.

<a id="crosswalk-updates-in-xeserver-92"></a>

##### Crosswalk Updates in XEServer 9.2

> 📄 Source: [Crosswalk_Updates_in_92.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Updates_in_92.htm)

###### Crosswalk Updates in 9.2


In 9.2, the Edifecs Crosswalk mechanism has undergone significant changes to simplify Crosswalk distribution and maintenance. The crosswalk file structure has been enriched with crosswalk configuration properties previously contained in a crosswalk registry. You do not have to register and unregister crosswalks manually, so you spend less time on crosswalk configuration. All the user crosswalk files are now saved to a common Crosswalks directory under {ECRootPath}\repository\ which is universal for all the Edifecs products that use crosswalks. System read-only crosswalks are stored under the related standard's installation directory, for example ${ECRootPath}\STANDARDS\HIPAA\Crosswalks.


###### Effortless migration


When you install XEngine 9.2, XEngine automatically detects the registered crosswalks (those defined in CrosswalkRegistry.xml), converts them to the new 9.2 format, and stores them in {ECRootPath}\repository\User\Crosswalks. If you want to migrate a pre-9.2 crosswalk that was not registered in CrosswalkRegistry.xml, you can bring such crosswalks to the new format by passing them through the Crosswalk Update component.


###### Migration notes


XEngine 9.2 applies all the changes to your crosswalks silently and requires no interaction. After you install XEngine 9.2, your migrated crosswalks are ready for work. Below you can find important features and limitations you should be aware of before you start working with 9.2 crosswalks.


###### Explicit crosswalk references unsupported


The ability to reference crosswalks explicitly (using the direct .crsw file notation) in map functions is no longer supported and works for backward compatibility only. We recommend that you revise your previously created map documents that use explicit crosswalk references and link crosswalks by their names instead.


###### Crosswalk match policy


For each crosswalk, you can specify a behavior in case of conflicts. If a repository contains crosswalks with the same Name, Trading Partners, and Criteria parameters, such crosswalks extend each other at runtime by default and XEngine processes them one by one in the alphabetic order of .crsw files. If you want a specific crosswalk override other crosswalks with identical parameters, you can set the Override behavior type for this crosswalk.


###### Crosswalk API changes


The crosswalk updates have brought about changes to the existing Java, JavaScript, and .NET functions/actions code on the documents that used Crosswalk API. For the information on how to accommodate your code to the recent crosswalk enhancements, see our Crosswalk API Changes in 9.2 guide.

---

> 📄 Source: [Book_Post_Installation_Steps_XES.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Post_Installation_Steps_XES.htm)
