<a id="log-extraction"></a>

#### Log Extraction

> 📄 Source: [Book_Log_Extraction.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Log_Extraction.htm)

##### Log Extraction


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="log-extraction-overview"></a>

##### Log Extraction Overview

> 📄 Source: [Log_Extraction_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Log_Extraction_Overview.htm)

###### Log Extraction Overview


Log Analyzer provides users with an opportunity to compile an extract from xes.events.log file. Log Extractor makes the information about the errors easier to analyze and understand since only those events that are logged within a specific time frame or are related to a particular route or task are displayed. This option is helpful in case it is necessary to find out what caused an error during processing to resolve this issue.


It is also possible to create an extract by using the XEServer command line tool. For more information about how to use the Log Extractor command line tool, see [Log Extractor Command Line Tool](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Log_Extractor_Command_Line_Tool.htm).

---

<a id="create-a-new-log-extraction"></a>

##### Create a New Log Extraction

> 📄 Source: [Create_a_New_Log_Extraction.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Log_Extraction.htm)

###### Create a New Log Extraction


Creating log extraction enables working with a specific part of a log file instead of a full log report.


To create a new log extraction:

1. Open Edifecs Application Manager.
2. On the System tab, expand XEServer.
3. Double-click Log Analyzer.
4. On the Outline tab, click Log extractions.
5. On the right side of the LogAnalyzer tab, click New log extraction.
6. In the File list field, specify the path to the xes.events.log file.
7. Specify one of the following:

- Time interval: The time frame with specified starting and ending points.


-or-

- Last time interval: The time interval in minutes from the last event that was logged.

1. Specify the extraction method:

- Route name list: The names of the routes, whose route event logs are included in the report.
- IDs: The ID of the tasks included in the report.

1. Click OK.
2. On the Outline tab, click the log extract to see the details.


*[Image: Extraction]*


To create a new log extraction for the same log that was used to generate a log report:

1. On the LogAnalyzer tab, double-click the report of which log file you want to use.
2. On the right side of the Files tab, click New log extraction.


*[Image: Log Analyzer]*

1. Specify one of the following:

- Time interval: The time frame with specified starting and ending points.


-or-

- Last time interval: The time interval in minutes from the last event that was logged.

1. Specify the extraction method:

- Route name list: The names of the routes, whose route event logs are included in the report.
- IDs: The ID of the tasks included into the report.


*[Image: Create a log extraction]*


Note If you want to get an extract from a specific log file, delete the paths to the other log files in the File list field.

1. Click OK.


Note An EAM Notification message confirms that the generation is completed successfully. The message is displayed in the top right-hand corner of the screen.

1. On the Outline tab, double-click the log extraction to see the details.


The generated extracts are stored at ${XESRoot}\tools\log-analyzer\log-extracts.

---

<a id="work-with-log-extraction"></a>

##### Work with Log Extraction

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Log_Extraction.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Work_with_Log_Extraction.htm)

###### Work with Log Extraction


As the log extract is generated, it is stored at ${XESRoot}\tools\log-analyzer\log-extracts. For more information about how to create a new extract, see [Create a New Log Extraction](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Log_Extraction.htm). You can view the generated log extract by using Edifecs Application Manager.


To open a log extract by using Edifecs Application Manager:

1. Open Edifecs Application Manager.
2. On the System tab, expand XEServer.
3. Double-click Log Analyzer.
4. On the Outline tab, expand Log extractions, and then click the extract you need.
5. The LogAnalyzer tab displays the Overview section and the log extract itself.
6. Expand Overview to see the details:


*[Image: Log Extraction overview]*


The Overview section contains the following information:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Report name | The name of the report from which the extract was generated. Go to option is provided to switch to the report from which the extract is generated. |
| File name | The name of the generated extract. |
| File size | The size of the extract. |
| Last modified | The last time the extract was updated. |



###### Log Extraction Tab


The log extract is displayed on the Log Extraction tab.


###### Properties Tab


Properties tab displays the information about the extraction properties.


###### Log Extraction Properties


Log Extraction Properties section displays the list of all properties.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | The name of the property. |
| Value | The value of the property. |



###### Errors


Errors section displays the list of all properties with errors.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | The name of the property. |
| Value | The value of the property. |



###### Error Description


This section contains the description of an error.

---

<a id="command-line-tools-for-log-analyzing"></a>

#### Command Line Tools for Log Analyzing

> 📄 Source: [Book_Command_Line_Tools_for_Log_Analyzing.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Command_Line_Tools_for_Log_Analyzing.htm)

##### Command Line Tools for Log Analyzing


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="log-analyzer-command-line-tool"></a>

##### Log Analyzer Command Line Tool

> 📄 Source: [Log_Analyzer_Command_Line_Tool.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Log_Analyzer_Command_Line_Tool.htm)

###### Log Analyzer Command Line Tool


Log Analyzer command line tool is another way to create an event log report that is compiled based on the log files.


The LogAnalyzer.bat file that initiates report generation is located at ${XESRoot}/tools/log-analyzer/bin. The resulting report is stored at ${XESRoot}/tools/log-analyzer/log-reports. By default, the report contains the event statistics of all the existing profiles.


Use the following command line parameters to customize the report:


| Parameter | Description | Default Value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| -h | Displays the command line parameters description. |   |
| -log-files <SetOfLogFiles> | Path to the log file. The paths to several profiles should be separated by commas. | ${XESRoot}/profiles |
| -encoding <Encoding> | The encoding of the log file. |   |
| -result-file <FilePath> | The path to the resulting report. | ${XESRoot}/tools/log-analyzer/log-reports |
| -task-duration-limit <Number> | Task duration limit in milliseconds. | 1000 milliseconds |
| -exchange-properties-limit<Number> | The maximum number of message exchange properties to track per task. | 0 |
| -report-format <xml\|html> | The format (XML or HTML) in which the report is stored. | html |
| -report-title <String> | The title of the resulting report. | "Log Report" |
| -problem-tasks-limit <Number> | The maximum number of the detected problem tasks. | -1 (not limited) |
| -errors-limit <Number> | The maximum number of the detected errors. | -1 (not limited) |
| -treat-task-with-warning-as-problem-task <true\|false> | Controls whether to include tasks with warnings in the problem tasks list or not. | False |
| -treat-task-with-error-as-problem-task <true\|false> | Controls whether to include tasks with errors in the problem tasks list or not. | True |
| -treat-not-started-or-not-completed-task-as-problem-task <true\|false> | Controls whether to include tasks that are not started or completed in the problem tasks list or not. | True |
| -treat-task-with-this-text-as-problem-task <string1, string2> | If the task contains at least one of the specified strings, it is included in the problem tasks list. |   |
| -treat-long-lasting-task-as-problem-task <true\|false> | Controls whether to include tasks with duration that exceeds the specified value in the problem tasks list or not. | False |
| -ignore-warning-ids <Event_ID_1; Event_ID_2...> | Specify warning IDs that should be ignored. |   |



You can also use Edifecs Application Manager to generate log reports by using User Interface (UI). For more information about how to generate reports by using Edifecs Application Manager, see [Create a New Report](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_New_Report.htm).

---

<a id="log-extractor-command-line-tool"></a>

##### Log Extractor Command Line Tool

> 📄 Source: [Log_Extractor_Command_Line_Tool.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Log_Extractor_Command_Line_Tool.htm)

###### Log Extractor Command Line Tool


Log Extractor is a command line tool used to generate extracts from the event log files.


The LogExtractor.bat file that initiates log extract generation is located at ${XESRoot}/tools/log-analyzer/bin. The resulting extracts in text format with a property file are stored at ${XESRoot}/tools/log-analyzer/log-extracts. By default, Log Extractor generates an xes.event.extr.log file containing all the log events for each existing profile.


Use the following command line parameters to customize the resulting extract:


| Parameter | Description | Default Value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| -h | Displays the command line parameters description. |   |
| -log-files <SetOfLogFiles> | Path to the log file. The paths to several profiles should be separated by commas. | ${XESRoot}/profiles |
| -result-dir <FilePath> | The path to the resulting report in text format. | ${XESRoot}/tools/log-analyzer/log-extracts |
| -start-date "YYYY-MM-dd hh:mm:ss.SSS" | The starting point of the time interval. |   |
| -end-date "YYYY-MM-dd hh:mm:ss.SSS" | The ending point of the time interval. |   |
| -ids <Ids> | The IDs of the task that are included in the log extract. The IDs should be separated by commas. |   |
| -skip-result-properties | Disables saving the properties. |   |
| -encoding <CharsetName> | The name of the character set of the log file and the resulting extract. | Platform Encoding |
| -route-names <SetOfRouteNames> | The names of the routes that are included in the extract. |   |
| -end-minutes <EndMinutes> | The time interval in minutes ending with the last logged event. |   |
| -trace-id-prefix <TraceIdPrefix> | Include all TraceIDs that start with the specified prefix. | TRACE- |

---

<a id="log-analyzer-use-cases"></a>

#### Log Analyzer Use Cases

> 📄 Source: [Log_Analyzer_Use_Cases.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Log_Analyzer_Use_Cases.htm)

##### Log Analyzer Use Cases


This scenario demonstrates how Log Analyzer can be used to find out what caused processing failure.


There is an expectation that a certain task, initiated by the inbound file, should produce certain output, but the target folder is empty. With Log Analyzer, users are able to run the analytical report and see what happened.


Upon completion of the scenario, you are able to:

- Locate an error by analyzing the log report
- Resolve the issue that caused the error


Before you go through this scenario, you should do the following:

- Create a new profile. For more information, see [Create a New Profile](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Profile.htm).
- Create a new route. For more information, see [Create a New Route](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Route.htm).


To configure the route:

1. On the Palette, in the Inbound Endpoints section, click File System, and in the Route Viewer, click the place where you want to locate the selected component. An FS Inbound component is displayed in the Route Viewer.

1. On the Palette, in the Enterprise Integration Patterns section, click Aggregation, and in the Route Viewer, click the place where you want to locate the selected component. An Aggregation component is displayed in the Route Viewer.

1. Click Aggregation component, and then click the Settings tab.
2. On the Completion tab, make sure that the number of messages coincide with the following ones:


*[Image: Aggregation Completion tab]*

1. On the Aggregation tab, specify the aggregation strategy as Create multimessage exchange.
2. On the Palette, in the XEngine section, click Native Processing, and in the Route Viewer, click the place where you want to locate the selected component.
3. On the Palette, in the Outbound Endpoints section, click File System, and in the Route Viewer, click the place where you want to locate the selected component.
4. Connect the FS Inbound components to the Aggregation component.
5. Connect the Aggregation component aggregated output to the Native Processing component.
6. Connect the Native Processing component xdata, reports, and acks outputs to the FS Outbound component.
7. Click Save () or press CTRL+S to save changes.


*[Image: Log Analyzer Use Case Route]*


To process the test data files:

1. In your File Manager, go to ${XERoot}samples\standards\X12\data.
2. Copy the 101.dat file and rename it as 101_1.dat, so that you have two similar files in native format with different names.
3. Copy two data files to the source folder for the inbound endpoints. By default, it is located at ${XESRoot}\profiles\[ProfileName]\workspace\test-data\inbound.


###### Expected results:


By default, the results are located at ${XESRoot}\profiles\[ProfileName]\workspace\test-data\outbound. But the outbound folder is empty.


To find out on what caused an error do the following:

1. Using Log Analyzer, create a log report. For more information about how to create a log report, see [Create a New Report](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_New_Report.htm).

- Open the generated report.
- The Overview section displays the number of errors that occurred during processing.


*[Image: LogAnalyzer Problem Tasks]*

1. On the Route Report tab, the Error Component field contains the name of the component on which an error occurred.


*[Image: LogAnalyzer Error Component]*


As the File System Inbound component is configured properly, the only reason for an error to occur is that further processing fails because of the incoming messages. Due to the fact that the Aggregation component is configured to pick up data files with the same correlation ID, no resulting data is generated from the incoming files because these files do not have correlation ID properties.


To resolve the issue:

1. Navigate to location of the 101.dat and 101_1.dat files.
2. For the data files in native format, create the corresponding property files:

- For the 101.dat file, create an empty text file with the name 101.dat.properties so that you have a file in native format and a property file.
- Open the 101.dat.properties file and type CorrelationID=true.
- Create the property file 101_1.dat.properties for 101_1.dat following the preceding instructions.

1. Copy two data files with their properties to the source folder for the inbound endpoints. By default, it is located at ${XESRoot}\profiles\[ProfileName]\workspace\test-data\inbound.


###### Expected results:


The outbound folder contains a file in XData format, reports, and acknowledgments.

---

<a id="tools"></a>

### Tools

> 📄 Source: [Book_Tools.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Tools.htm)

#### Tools


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="encryption-tool"></a>

#### Encryption Tool

> 📄 Source: [Encryption_Tool.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Encryption_Tool.htm)

##### Encryption Tool


XEServer comes with a command-line Encryption tool that you can use to create and modify encrypted properties containing sensitive data like passwords, tokens, and so on.


The tool is located at ${XESRoot}\tools\xes-crypto-tool


The Encryption tool is designed as an alternative for creating categories of [encrypted environment properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Environment_Tab.htm#EncryptedProperties) in EAM:



When you add new encrypted properties categories, EAM creates files (one for each category) in the Edifecs Encrypted Properties file format (.eproperties) at the following locations:

- If set for [global environment](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Environment_Tab.htm): ${XESRoot}\environment\<PropertiesCategory>.eproperties
- If set for [profile environment](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Environment_Tab.htm): ${XESRoot}\profiles\<XESProfileName>\config\environment\<PropertiesCategory>.eproperties


###### Tool Usage


To use the tool, run the encrypt.bat script. You can pass the properties to encrypt either in the form of command-line parameters or/and within a Java properties file containing unencrypted properties.


The basic template for a command looks as follows:


encrypt.bat -i <path_to_unencrypted_input_properties_file> -p <property_name>=<property_value> -s '<encryption_scheme>' -o <path_to_encrypted_output_properties_file>


###### Tool CLI parameters


Below are the parameters that the encrypt.bat script accepts:


| Parameter | Description | Is Mandatory |
| --- | --- | --- |
| -i <path_to_unencrypted_input_properties_file> | Path to an input file containing a collection of properties to encrypt. You can specify this parameter more than one time, as well as combine it with `-p` parameter(s) within one command. The file must be of the Java properties file format defined in the Oracle documentation for the [Properties java class](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/util/Properties.html#load(java.io.Reader)). | You must specify at least one of these parameters. |
| -p <property_name>=<property_value> | A single property with the value to encrypt. You can specify this parameter more than one time, as well as combine it with `-i` parameter(s) within one command. | |
| -s '<encryption_scheme>' | The following encryption scheme options are supported: `'AES'`: The Advanced Encryption Standard (used by default). `'XE'`: The Data Encryption Standard (PBEWithMD5AndDES). This option is deprecated as considered to be vulnerable and should be used for backward compatibility only. | No. If this parameter is not specified, than the AES scheme is used. |
| -o <path_to_encrypted_output_properties_file> | Path to the output file with the properties encrypted. If the file already exists, the tool changes only those properties in this file that you specify in input parameters (`-i` and `-p`). | Yes |



###### Examples


Below are the common tool usage examples.

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)
 Example 1


```
::This command adds/changes the "MSSQL_PASSWORD" property value within encrypted "MyPasswords" category for the global environment
encrypt.bat -p MSSQL_PASSWORD=12345 -o "${XESRoot}/environment/MyPasswords.eproperties"
```






 

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)
 Example 2


```
:: This command adds/changes the "MSSQL_PASSWORD" property value within encrypted "MyPasswords" category for the "MyProfile" XEServer profile
encrypt.bat -p MSSQL_PASSWORD=12345 -o "${XESRoot}/profiles/MyProfile/config/environment/MyPasswords.eproperties"
```






 

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)
 Example 3


```
:: This command encrypts properties' values contained in the unencrypted "props_to_encrypt.properties" input file and adds/changes these values in the "MyPasswords.eproperties" output file
encrypt.bat -i props_to_encrypt.properties -o "${XESRoot}/environment/MyPasswords.eproperties"
```






 

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)
 Example 4


```
:: This command encrypts unencrypted properties' values contained in the "MyPasswords.eproperties" file keeping all existing encrypted properties in this file unchanged
encrypt.bat -i "${XESRoot}/environment/MyPasswords.eproperties" -o "${XESRoot}/environment/MyPasswords.eproperties"
```

---

<a id="sdk-directory"></a>

### SDK Directory

> 📄 Source: [SDK_Directory.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SDK_Directory.htm)

#### SDK Directory


XEServer SDK directory (${XESRoot}/sdk) contains packages with built-in XEServer features that you can extend or customize. A package includes integration tools, source code, API documentation, and compiled examples of XEServer components and services.


The following packages are available:

 kafka


The directory contains source code, API documentation, and the default implementation of Kafka Serialization/Deserialization API. This API defines how XEServer [Kafka](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Kafka_Service.htm) and [Kafka2](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Kafka_2_Service.htm) services map XEServer message exchanges to/from Kafka messages. The source code includes all the necessary Java classes and interfaces required to build your own Serializer/Deserializer.

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


kafka




```
kafka
├───apidocs
│       API documentation
├───src
│   └───com
│       └───edifecs
│           └───etools
│               └───xeserver
│                   └───kafka
│                       ├───serialization
│                       │     XEServer classes and interfaces
│                       │
│                       └───impl
│                           Default (De)Serializer implementation used
│                           by XEServer Kafka services.
├───v0
│   A complete Serialization API JAR intended for the Kafka service.
│   This is a copy of the JAR that is used by the XEServer Kafka service by default.
│
└───v2
    A complete Serialization API JAR intended for the Kafka2 service.
    This is a copy of the JAR that is used by the XEServer Kafka2 service by default.
```






Tip You can modify JARs and source code under the Kafka SDK directory and it will not affect the default Kafka services behavior.


If you want to create your own Kafka message Serializer/Deserializer, you should:

1. Create your custom Java classes that implement `ISerializer`/`IDeserializer` interfaces. The names of your classes must differ from those classes contained in the kafka package, for example com.example.MyKafkaKeySerializer/com.example.MyKafkaValueSerializer, and com.example.MyKafkaKeyDeserializer/com.example.MyKafkaValueDeserializer.
2. Put the resulting xes-kafka-serialization-api-9.4.0-SNAPSHOT.jar to ${XESRoot}\features\kafka\lib\v0(to work with the Kafka service) or to ${XESRoot}\features\kafka\lib\v2 (to work with the Kafka2 service).
3. Declare the following properties on the Consumer tab of the corresponding Kafka service: - key.deserializer = com.example.MyKafkaKeyDeserializer - value.deserializer = com.example.MyKafkaValueDeserializer
4. Declare the following properties on the Producer tab of the corresponding Kafka service: - value.serializer = com.example.MyKafkaKeySerializer - value.deserializer = com.example.MyKafkaValueSerializer
5. Restart the profile with the Kafka / Kafka2 services.

---

<a id="configure-xeserver"></a>

## Configure XEServer

> 📄 Source: [Book_Configure_XEServer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Configure_XEServer.htm)

### Configure XEServer


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="xeserver-configuration-overview"></a>

### XEServer Configuration Overview

> 📄 Source: [XEServer_Configuration_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Configuration_Overview.htm)

[# XEServer Configuration Overview Use Edifecs Application Manager](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Route.htm) to configure XEServer. In this Help Center, all configuration samples, screen shots, and instructions relate to the EAM user interface.
To open XEServer profile configuration:

1. Open EAM Client.
2. On the EAM System tab, expand XEServer, then expand Profiles, and then expand the XEServer profile you want to work with.
3. Double-click Configuration. The profile's configuration is displayed.


##### Profile Configuration


The profile configuration has the following view. Hover or click a section on the image below to learn more about the section:

---

<a id="profile-properties"></a>

### Profile Properties

> 📄 Source: [Book_Profile_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Profile_Properties.htm)

#### Profile Properties
[Redirect to: {Profile_Information_Tab}](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Information_Tab.htm)

---

<a id="profile-information-tab"></a>

#### Profile Information Tab

> 📄 Source: [Profile_Information_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Information_Tab.htm)

##### Profile Information Tab


On this tab, you can view the last-saved profile information. Also, you can create a note to include useful information about the profile, such as the profile purpose and workflow description, prerequisites, critical sections, and so on.


*[Image: Profile information tab ]*

---

<a id="profile-repository-tab"></a>

#### Profile Repository Tab

> 📄 Source: [Profile_Repository_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Repository_Tab.htm)

##### Profile Repository Tab


On this tab, you can select the type of repository to use. This can be either [Smart Trading Repository](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository_Overview.htm) or local directory:



For more information on account types and local repository folder, see [Connect to Smart Trading Cloud Repository](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Connect_To_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository.htm).

---

<a id="cloud-account-tab"></a>

#### Cloud Account Tab

> 📄 Source: [Profile_Cloud_Account_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Cloud_Account_Tab.htm)

##### Cloud Account Tab


On this tab, you can enter credentials to enable access to [Smart Trading Cloud services](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Home.htm) for the features that require authentication at Smart Trading Cloud (for example, [Cloud Archive components](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Components_Overview.htm), [Cloud selector](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Service.htm#Authentication), and so on).



You can provide credentials of two types:

- User credentials: a username-password pair used to access your Smart Trading Account.
- Application credentials: a special token used for programmatic access to your Smart Trading Account. In this case, you can either feed credentials.stcc file or enter the token string. For more information on how to get application credentials, see [Application Users](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Application_Users.htm).


Note When you update ST credentials on this tab, the credentials are also updated on the [Environment Cloud Account tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Account_Tab.htm).


###### Smart Trading Cloud domain


By default, XEServer connects to the Smart Trading Account service hosted on [https://edifecsfedcloud.com](https://edifecsfedcloud.com/). If you want to work with the Smart Trading Cloud services hosted on a different domain (for example, www.cоllablynk.com), uncomment the required domain name in ${ECRootPath}\Common\cloud.properties. After you update the file, EAM restart is required.

---

<a id="profile-environment-tab"></a>

#### Profile Environment Tab

> 📄 Source: [Profile_Environment_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Environment_Tab.htm)

##### Profile Environment Tab


On this tab, you can define profile-level environment properties that can be used only within a profile. The properties defined on this tab override the XEServer [global environment properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Environment_Tab.htm).



###### Environment Properties Declaration


XEServer stores all environment properties in groups - Environment properties categories. These are containers used to group logically related properties. To define a new environment property, first you have to create a new Environment properties category and then you can add properties to this category. XEServer stores each properties category as a separate .properties file on the file system.


###### Environment Properties Usage


To reference a property defined on this tab, use the notation ${propertyName}. For example, when you configure an XEServer entity (a service, a route, or a route component), you can reference the environment properties as shown in the image below:



###### Encrypted Properties


You can make your Environment properties categories encrypted. This means that all the properties in such a category are stored in an encrypted form. To create an encrypted category, select the option Encrypted properties file when you create a new Environment properties category as shown in the image:



Note Once your properties are encrypted, you cannot bring your environment properties file (.properties) back to the decrypted form.


When you configure a route component and have to specify a password (or other sensitive data) in the component's settings, a best practice is to store your password as an encrypted environment property and then reference this property in the component configuration (for example, ${myPassword}).


You can also create and change encrypted properties using command-line [Encryption tool](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Encryption_Tool.htm).


###### Business Environment tab


The Business Environment tab contains the profile environment properties that you want to have easily accessible. This tab displays the same properties as the Profile Environment tab but allows you to choose the ones you use most frequently and customize their names, categories, allowed values, and more. For example, suppose you would like to have the TracerMode property readily available to be able to change its name and/or the names of its supported values tracer.by.message, tracer.always, and tracer.never. To add the properties you want to have at hand to the Business Environment tab, do the following:

1. Create the schema.json file using the template below.
2. Specify the profile environment properties with their metadata.
3. Place the schema.json file in the directory ${XESProfile}/config/environment/ui. Each XEServer profile requires a separate schema for the Business Environment tab.

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)
 UI schema template


```
{
    “properties” : [
        {
            //property metadata
        },
        {
            //property metadata
        }
        …
    ]
}
```






EAM supports the following JSON fields for the UI schema:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | The environment property name. The value must be the same as the property name on the Profile Environment tab. |
| display.name | The environment property name that is displayed on the user interface. |
| description | The property description that is displayed when you select a property and click Edit. |
| type | The property value type. If not specified, the string type is used. The following types are supported: integer: A numeric value from -2147483648 to 2147483647 or a system macro. To specify the minimum and maximum values for a property, use the min and max fields. Empty value is not supported. boolean: A boolean value (true/false) or a system macro. Empty value is not supported. string: Any string value except an empty string. To define the list of possible values, use the values field. In this case, only the values from the list or a system macro will be allowed. |
| min | The minimum value for the integer attribute type; can be any numeric value between -2147483648 and 2147483647. |
| max | The maximum value for the integer attribute type; can be any numeric value between -2147483648 and 2147483647. |
| values | An array with the supported property value options of the string type. If you specify this field, only the values from the list or a system macro are allowed. A value in the array must contain the following fields: name: A value name to be stored as an environment property value. display.name: A property name that is displayed on the user interface. |
| allow.empty.value | Add this field if you want to allow (true) or restrict (false) adding an empty value for an environment property. |
| allow.custom.value | Add this field if you want to allow (true) or restrict (false) values not specified in the values list if a system macro is used. |
| group | Add this field if you want to group logically related properties. The group field must contain the following nested field: name: The name of a group. This name can be different from the name of the environment properties category on the Profile Environment tab. |



The example below illustrates how to create a UI schema for environment properties using the JSON fields described above.

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


Example




```
{
    "properties": [    
        {
            "name": "ReportAuditToVS",
            "display.name": "Report Audit to VS",
            "type": "boolean", 
            "group" : {
                "name" : "Lineage and Audit"
            },
            "description": "Report Audit events to Visualizer Server"
        },    
        {
            "name": "TracerMode",
            "display.name": "Tracer mode",
            "description": "Tracer Activation",
            "allow.empty.value": true,
            "allow.custom.value": true,
            "values": [
                {
                    "name": "tracer.always",
                    "display.name": "Always"
                },
                {
                    "name": "tracer.never",
                    "display.name": "Never"
                },
                {
                    "name": "tracer.by.message",
                    "display.name": "By message"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "name": "errors.count",
            "display.name": "Allowed errors count",
            "type": "integer",
            "description": "Provide the number of allowed errors",
            "min": -1,
            "max": 1000
        }
    ]
}
```






With this sample schema, the environment properties will be displayed on the Business Environment tab as follows:



###### Effective Environment Tab


This tab displays read-only information about your operating system and XEServer environment properties. Effective environment properties can be referenced from any profile.


*[Image: Effective Environment tab]*

---

<a id="profile-startup-tab"></a>

#### Profile Startup Tab

> 📄 Source: [Profile_Startup_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm)

##### Profile Startup Tab


On this tab, you can specify JVM parameters (-Xmx, -Xrs, Garbage Collection settings, and so on) to be used to start the profile:


*[Image: Startup Tab]*


Use the Effective Startup tab to view all the startup properties effective for this profile. All the properties provided on the Startup tab are also displayed here:


*[Image: Effective Startup tab]*


Example


-vmargs


-Xmx512M


-Xrs


-Dcom.edifecs.xeserver.startup.timeout.sec=60


-Djava.io.tmpdir=${XESWorkspace}/temp


-Djavax.net.ssl.trustStore=${XESRoot}/platform/security/KeyringClient.jks


-Djavax.net.ssl.trustStorePassword=password


-Djavax.net.ssl.keyStore=${XESRoot}/platform/security/KeyringClient.jks


-Djavax.net.ssl.keyStorePassword=password

---

<a id="profile-logger-tab"></a>

#### Profile Logger Tab

> 📄 Source: [Logger_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Logger_Tab.htm)

##### Profile Logger Tab


On this tab, you can control the generation of XEServer logs and define the severity of events that should be included in the log files. Depending on your business requirements, you can set Logger to generate detailed logs and log every step of data processing, or you can set Logger to only track events that are vital for troubleshooting in your business environment, such as errors.


*[Image: Logger tab]*


###### Logger Configuration


In this section, you can select a strategy for generating log files and for keeping them in a file system.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| File appender: Rolling file appender | |
| Maximum file size before rollover | Enter the maximum size of a log file. When the size is reached, a separate log file with the corresponding backup index is created. The range of available values is 1MB - 1GB. |
| Backup index | Enter the number of backup log files to be retained after a file reaches its size limit. If the number of generated files exceeds the entered value, the oldest log files are removed. |
| File appender: Daily rolling file appender | |
| Log files will be deleted in | Enter the number of days after which the log files are removed. Every log file is checked by the corresponding profile every hour and the files which are older than the specified value are removed. |
| Do not include Exchange/Message properties | Use this option to exclude message properties and message exchange properties from a log output. This is useful when you do not want to include critical information that may be stored in message properties or message exchange properties, for example, protected health information (PHI), to the logs. In the following cases, logs may still contain some property details even if this option is enabled: This option does not affect entries directed to the root (xes.root.log) and system (xes.system.log) logs by the XEServer components that use third-party libraries. For example, JMS, REST Client, Email, and, potentially, other components may put out properties to the logs. Edifecs recommends that you do not use the Trace and Debug log levels on a Production environment to prevent protected information from appearing in the logs. If you use a specific property in component settings explicitly, the value of this property may appear in logs. For example, if you enter {Prp[MethodName]} in the HTTP method field of the REST Client component, the value of the MethodName property may be written to a log. If you put out property values explicitly by using, for example, the println method in the Groovy component. |



###### Logging levels


In this section, you can define the levels of XEServer events that should be included in logs.


| Log Level | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Trace | Select to include all Trace events in the system. As this is the lowest log level, by default all higher levels are included in the log file. |
| Debug | Select to include Debug events and higher levels (for example, Information, Warning, Error and Fatal Error) in the log file. |
| Information | Select to include Information events and higher levels (for example, Warning, Error and Fatal Error) in the log file. |
| Warning | Select to include Warning events and higher levels (for example, Error and Fatal Error) in the log file. |
| Error | Select to include Error events and higher levels (for example, Fatal Error) in the log file. |
| Fatal Error | This is the highest log level. Select to include only Fatal Error events. |



###### Advanced logger configuration


In this pane, you can set logging configuration to log component-specific events.


To set the log configuration:

1. On the Logger configuration tab, select the log levels for the Route, System, Root, Command, Memory Tracer logs.


-or-

1. Expand the Advanced logger configuration pane, and then specify the logging levels for the required logs.
2. Save your profile.


The result log file is stored at ${XESProfile}\log.


For more information about the log files, see [Log Files Types](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Log_Files_Types.htm).


###### Mask Exchange/Message Properties


You can mask Exchange/Message properties in your logs by configuring theXESLoggerMaskedProperties environment property in your profile. This ensures that the values of the configured properties are excluded from the logs.


Note: This property is not present by default and must be configured manually.


Follow these steps to configure the XESLoggerMaskedProperties property in the Profile Environment tab:

1. Navigate to the [Profile Environment tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Environment_Tab.htm#Groups) of the required profile.
2. Select any environment property category or add a new one. Click on theNew option in the side navigation of the Profile Environment tab.
3. In the dialog box that opens, add the XESLoggerMaskedProperties property in the Name column.
4. Add the property name in the Value column. You can list multiple properties by separating them with a semicolon (;) without any spaces between them.


###### How Masking Works


When the logger logs an event and sends it to the Rewrite Appender, the appender identifies the properties that need to be masked. It replaces the values with a set of filters and passes the file to the File/Console Appender, which writes the data to the console to display logs. The configured properties specified in the XESLoggerMaskedProperties will be masked in all XEServer logs using a string of seven asterisks (*******).


Note: These masking properties do not apply to tracer files. The specified properties will be masked only in the log files. Masking applies only to events logged by XES.erver External logging will not be masked.

On this tab, you can set the Restart mode for a profile.



The following options are available:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restart mode | The following modes are available: Manual: Disables the automatic profile restart. If a profile goes down, you have to start the profile manually. Crash Recovery: Restarts the profile if the profile has crashed. Automatic: Restarts the profile if the profile has crashed or has been shut down. |
| Restart Timeout | The time (in minutes) after which the XEServer Agent restarts a profile (Automatic or Crash recovery mode). |
| Timeout Increase | The length of time (in minutes) to increase the restart timeout after each restart failure. |
| Restart attempts | The maximum number of restart attempts. To disable the limit, set the value to -1. |



###### Container mode


If you run XEServer profile configurations in a containerized environment, read [this topic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_in_Containerized_Environments.htm) to learn about the required changes in settings.

##### Profile Advanced Tab


On this tab, you can assign a profile to a domain. If a profile belongs to a specific domain, all the Visualizer, Visual Tracking, and audit events data generated by this profile becomes limited to this domain. When the profile submits such domain-specific data (for example, audit events) to XESManager, this data is available only for those XESManager users who have access to the domain.


For more information on how set up domain-based access to audit events, Visualizer data, and Visual Tracking data, see [Manage Domains](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xesmanager/9.4/Content/Manage_Domains.htm#Domain_based_access_example).

<a id="balance-counter"></a>

<a id="balance-counter-service"></a>

##### Balance Counter Service

> 📄 Source: [Balance_Counter_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Balance_Counter_Service.htm)

###### Balance Counter Service


Balance Counter Service is an XEServer Service that interacts with database to submit counter-related events to the route components.


Note The db_owner role is required for the database user to work with Balance Counters Service.


BCounters Service provides primary counters functionality to XEServer. The service gets configuration parameters from the database. Counter group metadata is read on service start, and then used during the profile run.


The service is available for monitoring in the [XEServer Manager](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XESManager_Overview.htm).


The functionality that is encapsulated in this service is as follows:

- Database access layer
- Counters cache. Cache for the counters that are read from database.
- Counter operations persistent queue. A queue that stores counter update operations for asynchronous processing.
- Counter update thread. The thread that applies updates to counters.
- Counter expiration thread. The thread that monitors counter expiration.


The following parameters are present on the BCounters Service Settings tab:


*[Image: BCounter Service General settings tab]*


###### General Settings


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service** | Enter a name for the service. This name is used to display the service in the EAM Outline tab. |
| **Type** | The type of the XEServer service. This cannot be changed. |
| **Status** | Select to enable or disable the service. If enabled you can use a condition (written in Expression language) that evaluates to TRUE/FALSE and defines whether the corresponding route or service must start together with the profile (TRUE) or not (FALSE). The expression can use only system macros. |
| **Path** | The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip: Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. |
| **Auto start** | Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. |



###### Configuration Tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| DB configuration | From the drop-down list, select the [Counter Manager](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Counter_Manager_Overview.htm) configuration to be used by the BCounters Service. Click Edit to change the [database connection properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Database_Connection_Properties.htm) for the selected configuration. To add a new database configuration, use the Counter Managers on the EAM System tab. TipYou can use an environment property (for example, ${pathToBCountersDB}) to specify a database configuration. In this case, the property value must hold the name of the corresponding Counter Manager configuration. |



###### QoS Tab


On this tab, you can specify the behavior of a service by setting an additional Action when the service encounters a critical error. The following additional actions are available:


| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restart | Restarts the current service after a critical error. Use the Restart delay field to set a time delay in milliseconds before restarting the service. |
| Restart services | Restarts certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be restarted. |
| Stop services | Stops certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be stopped. |
| None | No action is performed after a critical error. |



###### Dependences Tab


On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.


###### Service Information Tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **User name** | The last user to make changes to the service settings. |
| **License owner** | The name of the license owner. |
| **Modified** | The date and time of the last route modification. |
| **Note** | Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route. |



###### Container mode


If you run XEServer profile configurations in a containerized environment, read [this topic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_in_Containerized_Environments.htm) to learn about the required changes in settings.

| **Status** | Select to enable or disable the service. If enabled you can use a condition (written in Expression language) that evaluates to TRUE/FALSE and defines whether the corresponding route or service must start together with the profile (TRUE) or not (FALSE). The expression can use only system macros. |
| **Path** | The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip: Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. |
| **Auto start** | Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. |



###### Configuration Tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Disable master mode | Use to disable the Master mode.   If this mode is enabled, the Batcher service is responsible for processing of the data items and Batch Groups according to the channel configuration. |
| Batcher configuration | Select the Batcher configuration to be used. Tip Starting from EAM 9.2.2 hotfix 3 you can use an environment property (for example, ${pathToBatcherDB}) to specify a database configuration. In this case, the property value must hold the name of the corresponding Batcher configuration. |

| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restart | Restarts the current service after a critical error. Use the Restart delay field to set a time delay in milliseconds before restarting the service. |
| Restart services | Restarts certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be restarted. |
| Stop services | Stops certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be stopped. |
| None | No action is performed after a critical error. |



###### Dependencies Tab


On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.


###### Service Information Tab

<a id="mft-service"></a>

##### MFT Service

> 📄 Source: [Book_MFT_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_MFT_Service.htm)

###### MFT Service


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="mft-service-overview"></a>

##### MFT Service Overview

> 📄 Source: [MFT_Service_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MFT_Service_Overview.htm)

###### MFT Service Overview


[Edifecs Managed File Transfer](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Welcome_to_Managed_File_Transfer.htm) (MFT) Service is a SaaS solution that seamlessly connects you with your partners to allow you to exchange data securely, quickly, and efficiently. Edifecs MFT Service allows you to create a secure data transfer channel between you and your partner without intermediators or complicated communication systems. This helps to avoid manual laborious audits and thus, reduce costs on data transfers and promote protection level for your data without investing any significant time and resources. The Managed File Transfer service also allows you to track all incoming and outgoing data, get the information about the status of transfer, errors if any, filter the data by a partner or date that simplifies a search and troubleshooting and, therefore, reduces time and costs on support. Data transfer is performed through the secured two-end channels that connect you and your partner. During the transfer, your data is fully secured from the drop-off point until its final destination, is not mixed up, and its integrity is not compromised.


Note Before you start working with Managed File Transfer (Edifecs MFT Service), you have to create a [Smart Trading account](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Create_Account.htm) and enable the MFT service subscription. For more information, see [Quick Start](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Get_Started.htm) in the help documentation of Managed File Transfer (Edifecs MFT Service).


XEServer provides you with convenient and versatile inbound and outbound MFT route components to allow you to interact (send and receive files) with Edifecs MFT Service directly from XEServer routes.


Before you proceed with this section, review the following key Edifecs MFT Service concepts:

- [Connectivity Agreement](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Connectivity_Agreements_Overview.htm): A contract between two Smart Trading accounts that defines relationships between the trading partners (between these accounts). Agreements contain information about the organizations involved in the data exchange, users that have access to input/output endpoints, permissions, and so on. Before you can start data transfer, you should create such an agreement with your trading partner.
- [Inbox](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Create_Inboxes.htm): An abstract endpoint in the data transfer channel. Whereas the MFT stack (an MFT server and MFT client applications) can be considered as a bidirectional channel or a pipe between two users, Inboxes represent the end points of this channel - this is where users push data to the channel (Outgoing Inboxes) and receive data from the channel (Inbound Inboxes). Inboxes are associated with partners and can be shared with those users you want to exchange data with. On the user's file system, inboxes are represented as directories that consume data files and send them to the channel (Outgoing) or store newly received files from the channel (Inbound).

---

<a id="archival-service-overview"></a>

##### Archival Service Overview

> 📄 Source: [Archival_Service_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Archival_Service_Overview.htm)

###### Archival Service Overview


With the growing amounts of data being processed and generated, the question of maintaining a long-term data storage becomes especially important. Processed data as well as data generated during processing may require to be retrieved during a legal inquiry or audit. The sensitive nature of medical data imposes additional regulatory requirements to data storages and data transfer mechanisms to prevent data corruption, unauthorized access, and to provide absolute security.


On-site data centers usually entail support expenses, require proper administration, and manual data handling. Data corruption issues with aging servers further complicate database maintenance and require laborious manipulations. To provide you with a secure cloud data storage and to greatly simplify the procedure of data backup, Edifecs has introduced the [Archival Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Welcome_to_Archival_Service.htm) that consolidates the required data, transfers it to a single cloud database, and ensures long-term access to your data. The Archival service can store and transfer confidential information including private health information and ensures that your data is protected during the transfer to and from the storage and within the storage.


With XEServer Archival Service Outbound component, you can upload data directly from XEServer routes to Edifecs Archival Service. The component uses the XEServer [MFT](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MFT_Service.htm) service to establish a connection to the Archival Service server.

---

<a id="mft-service"></a>

##### MFT Service

> 📄 Source: [MFT_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MFT_Service.htm)

###### MFT Service


With this service, you can establish a connection to [Edifecs Managed File Transfer (MFT) Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Welcome_to_Managed_File_Transfer.htm) to allow route components to upload and retrieve data from MFT Service.


When you add the service to a profile, you can view and edit the following settings:



In addition to generic service settings, the service has the following settings:


###### Configuration Tab


On this tab, you can activate the XEServer service to connect to MFT Service. The MFT Service server communicates with client applications (MFT Agents, XEServer MFT Service) using special tokens - [MFT activation codes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Set_Up_MFT_Connections.htm). Activation codes are issued for one-time use, which means that once you activate the XEServer MFT Service, the activation code cannot be reused to activate another client application. To generate a new activation code, click [here](https://mft.edifecsfedcloud.com/#/agents).


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Key ID | Enter your MFT activation code. Click to open the activation code generation page. Activate: After you have pasted the activation code, click to activate your XEServer MFT Service. Test Connection: Click to check if the entered key is valid and can be used to connect to Edifecs MFT Service. Note One [MFT activation code](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Set_Up_MFT_Connections.htm) can be used by one XEServer MFT Service at a time, so if you want to read and send data using different [Smart Trading Accounts](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Create_Account.htm) in the same profile, ensure you have separate XEServer MFT Service instances each using a different activation code. |
| Advanced | |
| Connection timeout (sec) | Specify the timeout in seconds within which the connection to Edifecs MFT Service has to be established. |

---

<a id="cloud-archive-service"></a>

##### Cloud Archive Service

> 📄 Source: [Cloud_Archive_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Service.htm)

###### Cloud Archive Service


This service is used to establish a connection with the [Smart Trading Archival Service](https://archive.edifecsfedcloud.com/). Route components [Cloud Archive Download](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Download_Component.htm), [Cloud Archive Upload](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Upload_Component.htm), and [Cloud Archive Search](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Search_Component.htm) use this service to perform operations on the Smart Trading Archival Service.


Note To connect to the Smart Trading Archival Service, you have to provide your Smart Trading Account credentials on the [Profile Cloud Account tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Cloud_Account_Tab.htm).


###### Service Configuration


Apart from generic service settings, the service has the following settings:



###### Account Tab


On this tab, you can provide Smart Trading Account credentials for the service to connect to the Smart Trading Archival Service. Click Enter Credentials to go to the [Profile Cloud Account](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Cloud_Account_Tab.htm) tab and enter your credentials.


###### Connection Tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Timeout Settings | |
| Connection Timeout (ms) | The timeout within which the connection to the Smart Trading Archival Service has to be established. |
| Read Timeout (ms) | The maximum time allowed to get the request data from the Smart Trading Archival Service. |
| Write Timeout (ms) | The maximum time allowed to write (send) the request data to the Smart Trading Archival Service. |
| Connection Poll Settings | |
| Max Idle Connections | The maximum number of simultaneous connections. Those connections will be kept in the connection pool when idle. Once the pool is exhausted, any operation requiring a connection will get block and will have to wait for an available connection. The default value is 5. |
| Connection Max Idle Timeout (ms) | The maximum time a connection may remain idle in the connection pool. A pooled connection that has exceeded its idle time will be closed and replaced when necessary by a new connection. Setting the value to 0 removes the limit. |



###### Dependencies Tab


On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.


###### Service Information


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **User name** | The last user to make changes to the service settings. |
| **License owner** | The name of the license owner. |
| **Modified** | The date and time of the last route modification. |
| **Note** | Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route. |

<a id="amazon-sqs-service"></a>

##### Amazon SQS Service

> 📄 Source: [Book_Amazon_SQS_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Amazon_SQS_Service.htm)

###### Amazon SQS Service


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="amazon-sqs-service-overview"></a>

##### Amazon SQS Service Overview

> 📄 Source: [Amazon_SQS_Service_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_SQS_Service_Overview.htm)

###### Amazon SQS Service Overview


Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) is a cloud-based message queuing service used to store messages as they travel between applications or microservices. Amazon SQS provides users with the full set of features peculiar to traditional middleware. For more information about Amazon SQS features and benefits, see [Amazon SQS Documentation](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/Welcome.html).


XEServer Amazon SQS service and components allow you to:

- interact with existing Amazon SQS queues from your routes,
- create new queues,
- and track your message delivery.


**Note** Ensure to update your AWS Clients to version 2.x as version 1.x is reaching its end of support. For additional details, visit [here](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/developer/announcing-end-of-support-for-aws-sdk-for-java-v1-x-on-december-31-2025/).


Before you start working with Amazon SQS, you have to complete the following steps:

1. Create an [Amazon AWS account](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-setting-up.html).
2. Create an [Amazon IAM User](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-setting-up.html#sqs-creating-iam-user).
3. To authorize Java applications (XEServer) connecting to Amazon SQS:
4. Create a queue to send messages. Alternately, you can create a queue using XEServer.


In this section, when we refer to Producer and Consumer, we refer to Amazon SQS users who send and read messages from a queue respectively.


Amazon SQS allows you to deliver messages of up to 256 KB in size. An Amazon message contains a body with the actual message contents and a set of attributes. XEServer converts the message exchange properties and message properties to Amazon attributes when sending a message to an Amazon queue. The 256 KB limit is applied to the whole message, including body and attributes.


Amazon SQS supports two types of queues:

- **Standard** - Follows the At-least-once Delivery principle. This approach excludes potential message loss by resending the message, if required. For every message consumed, an acknowledgment is sent back to Amazon SQS. If the acknowledgment is not received after a certain period of time, the same message can be retrieved by other consumers. This queue type offers nearly unlimited throughput and the best message-ordering mechanism (messages may be delivered in an order different from which they were sent). Summarily, this queue type is the best when throughput is vital.
- **FIFO** - Follows the First-In-First-Out Delivery principle. This queue type is available in a limited number of regions: US East (N. Virginia), US East (Ohio), US West (Oregon), and EU (Ireland). With this approach, messages are delivered exactly once and strictly in the order they were submitted. Queues of this type offer a limited throughput at 300 transactions for every second.


Click these links to learn about Amazon queue [limitations](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-limits.html) and [recommendations](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-best-practices.html).

---

<a id="amazon-sqs-service"></a>

##### Amazon SQS Service

> 📄 Source: [Amazon_SQS_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_SQS_Service.htm)

###### Amazon SQS Service


With this service, you can establish a connection with an Amazon SQS server. The route components [Amazon SQS Inbound](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_SQS_Inbound.htm) and [Amazon SQS Outbound](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_SQS_Outbound.htm) use this service to read from and write data to Amazon SQS queues.


Note Use of this component requires an XEServer Module for AWS license. Contact [Edifecs Customer Support](https://support.edifecs.com/) to obtain the XEServer Module for AWS license.


###### IAM User Permissions


The service can invoke the following [AWS actions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/APIReference/API_Operations.html):

- GetQueueUrl
- CreateQueue
- ReceiveMessage
- SendMessage
- GetQueueAttributes
- DeleteMessageBatch
- ChangeMessageVisibilityBatch


To perform these actions, the corresponding IAM user must have sufficient permissions defined in the user policies. To view the mapping of Amazon SQS actions and permissions, see [Amazon SQS API permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-api-permissions-reference.html).
(missing or bad snippet)

###### Message Attributes
Due to certain [limitations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-message-metadata.html) on the Amazon SQS side, such as limit of 10 attribute per message, the XEServer Amazon SQS components do not support reading arbitrary attributes from the SQS by default.

Instead, the attributes are handled as follows:
1. The SQS Outbound component serializes all XES message attributes in JSON format and puts them in a single SQS Message attribute Properties.
2. The SQS Inbound component reads the SQS message attribute Properties and deserializes the JSON value into XES message attributes.

You can explicitly specify the attributes to be added without being serialized to the SQS message and to be read from the message. To do this, add the amazon.sqs.message.properties.attributes parameter with the attributes name(s) on the [Profile Environment](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Environment_Tab.htm) tab (for example, amazon.sqs.message.properties.attributes=Attribute1,Attribute2,Attribute3), or on the [Profile Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) tab (for example, -Damazon.sqs.message.properties.attributes=Attribute1,Attribute2,Attribute3).

###### Service Configuration
When you add the service to a profile, you can view and edit the following settings:

To see the list of macros supported by this service, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#AmazonSQS).

###### General Settings
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service** | Enter a name for the service. This name is used to display the service in the EAM Outline tab. |
| **Type** | The type of the XEServer service. This cannot be changed. |
| **Status** | Select to enable or disable the service. If enabled you can use a condition (written in Expression language) that evaluates to TRUE/FALSE and defines whether the corresponding route or service must start together with the profile (TRUE) or not (FALSE). The expression can use only system macros. |
| **Path** | The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip: Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. |
| **Auto start** | Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. |

###### Configuration Tab
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Endpoint Region | Select the geographic region closest to your physical XEServer installation to obtain optimal latency. |
| Access Key ID | (Optional) Enter your Amazon Access Key ID. You can [view your Access Key ID](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-setting-up.html#sqs-getting-access-key-id-secret-access-key) in the AWS IAM console.If this field is empty, the system looks for AWS credentials in the following [sources](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/AWS_Default_Credentials_Provider_Chain.htm). |
| Secret Access Key | (Optional) Enter your Secret Access Key. You can [view your Secret Access Key](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-setting-up.html#sqs-getting-access-key-id-secret-access-key) in the AWS IAM console.XEServer encrypted properties can be used in this field. If you have encrypted properties declared in the current profile, they are available in the drop-down list.If this field is empty, the system looks for AWS credentials in the following [sources](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/AWS_Default_Credentials_Provider_Chain.htm). |
| AdvancedThe settings in this section are used to fine-tune the connection between XEServer and Amazon SQS servers. The default values would probably satisfy most of your business needs. For more information about connection parameters, see [Amazon SQS API reference](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/APIReference/Welcome.html). | |
| Run with IAM role | The **Run with IAM role** option is used in combination with an Amazon EC2 instance. If the IAM role to assume is the same as the IAM role attached to the Amazon EC2 instance, then there is no need to select the **Run with IAM role** option. Because if selected, the IAM role would try to assume itself, which is not the correct expectation.If the IAM role to assume is different from the IAM role attached to the Amazon EC2 instance, select the **Run with IAM role** option and configure it as described below.When selected, XEServer assumes a [role](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles.html) to access the Amazon S3 service. Specify the following:ARN: [Amazon resource name](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html) for the IAM role to assume. The **ARN** (IAM role to assume) should be added to the trust policy of the IAM role attached to the Amazon EC2 instance by modifying the trust relationships or policies using the **Trust Relationships** tab in the [AWS Management Console:](http://console.aws.amazon.com/sqs)External ID: (Optional) An unique identifier for [assuming the role by a third-party account](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_create_for-user_externalid.html).Session duration (min): The maximum duration of the role session in minutes. This parameter cannot exceed the [maximum session duration in IAM console](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_use.html#id_roles_use_view-role-max-session). |
| Connection max number | The maximum number of simultaneous connections to an Amazon server. |
| Connection timeout | The timeout in milliseconds within which the connection should be established. |
| Connection max idle timeout | The maximum time period to keep connections in the connection pool. |
| Request retry attempt | The number of repetitive request attempts to an Amazon SQS server if the server does not respond. |
| Request retry delay | The delay between attempts. |

###### Dependencies Tab
On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.

###### Service Information Tab
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **User name** | The last user to make changes to the service settings. |
| **License owner** | The name of the license owner. |
| **Modified** | The date and time of the last route modification. |
| **Note** | Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route. |

###### Reported Metrics
XEServer Amazon SQS Service reports the following metrics to XESManager:

| Metric | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Total number of empty receives | The number of requests to Amazon SQS that returned 0 messages. |
| Total number of failed requests | The number of requests to Amazon SQS that failed due to connectivity issues. |
| Total number of received messages | The total number of messages received from the queue. |
| Total number of sent messages | The total number of messages sent to the queue. |
| Total number of sent requests | The total number of requests to Amazon SQS. |
| Total received size | The total number of bytes received. |
| Total sent size | The total number of bytes sent. |
