<a id="configure-an-ibm-websphere-mq-service"></a>

##### Configure an IBM WebSphere MQ Service

> 📄 Source: [IBM_WebSphere_MQ_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/IBM_WebSphere_MQ_Service.htm)

###### IBM WebSphere MQ Service


This service allows you to establish a connection with an IBM WebSphere MQ server (the supported WebSphere versions 9.2, 9.3.4). The route components [IBM WebSphere MQ Inbound](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/IBM_Websphere_MQ_Inbound.htm) and [IBM WebSphere MQ Outbound](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/IBM_WebSphere_MQ_Outbound.htm) use this service to read and write messages to an IBM Websphere MQ server.


Note: You cannot use this service with regular JMS Inbound and JMS Outbound route endpoints. Edifecs recommends using updated Jakarta version compatible with client jar and dependent json.jar.


The service supports IBM WebSphere MQ 9.2 and 9.3.4.


Queue managers' security has been significantly improved in IBM WebSphere MQ (version 7.1 and later), when compared to previous versions. However, there are still some known issues announced by the IBM MQ. For example, IBM MQ 7.1+ users have to define administrative access to their queue managers explicitly. If you observe the error "2035 MQRC_NOT_AUTHORIZED" when you connect to an IBM MQ server, you should modify the channel authentication records of your queue manager. For more information on this issue, see the detailed [description and solution](https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/mq-rc-2035-mqrcnotauthorized-or-amq4036-or-jmswmq2013-when-using-client-connection-mq-administrator-chlauth-channel-authentication-records) of the problem. For more information about the IBM WebSphere MQ security mechanism, see the [IBM WebSphere MQ documentation](http://www-01.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSFKSJ_8.0.0/com.ibm.mq.sec.doc/q010250_.htm).


For information about package installation, see [Obtaining the IBM MQ classes for JMS separately](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.2?topic=jms-obtaining-mq-classes-separately#taskq128170___steps__1) in official IBM documentation.


###### IBM WebSphere MQ Service Settings


When you add the service to a profile, you see the following settings:


*[Image: IBM WebSphere MQ Service General settings]*


###### General Settings


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service** | Enter a name for the service. This name is used to display the service in the EAM Outline tab. |
| **Type** | The type of the XEServer service. This cannot be changed. |
| **Status** | Select to enable or disable the service. If enabled you can use a condition (written in Expression language) that evaluates to TRUE/FALSE and defines whether the corresponding route or service must start together with the profile (TRUE) or not (FALSE). The expression can use only system macros. |
| **Path** | The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip: Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. |
| **Auto start** | Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. |



###### Connection Settings


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| IBM queue manager | Enter the name of the [queue manager](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.0?topic=types-mq-queue-managers). IBM MQ queue managers provide queuing services to applications, and manage the queues that belong to them. |
| CCSID | Enter [Coded Character Set Identifiers](https://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/ssw_ibm_i_72/nls/rbagsccsidcdepgscharsets.htm). By default, the service uses the code 1208 (which is UTF-8 with IBM PUA). |
| **Transacted** | Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with an IBM WebSphere MQ server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. The behavior of the components listed below help you better understand how the service handles transactions. Local IBM Websphere MQ Inbound (activation mode = Route Start): reads a message from a IBM Websphere MQ server, reports the message to the next component, closes the transaction. IBM Websphere MQ Inbound (activation mode = By Command): receives a trigger XEServer message, opens a new transaction, reads messages from a IBM Websphere MQ server based on the trigger command, closes the transaction. IBM Websphere MQ Outbound: receives a message exchange, opens a new transaction, sends all messages from the message exchange to a IBM Websphere MQ queue, closes the transaction. Shared IBM Websphere MQ Inbound (activation mode = Route Start): Reads a message from an IBM WebSphere MQ server by using a new transaction. The transactions are bound to the XEServer task. IBM Websphere MQ Inbound (activation mode = By Command): Uses a single transaction to read all the messages from an IBM WebSphere MQ server within the scope of handling a single command. IBM Websphere MQ Outbound: Uses the same transaction to send the results to the IBM Websphere MQ server. The transaction is closed when the XEServer task is complete. Note: You can have several transactions bound to a single XEServer task. For this to work, each transaction must have a unique name. Components that use shared transactions can pick up the correct transaction from the XEServer task using the transaction name. |
| **Transaction name** | This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out. |
| **Retry attempt** | The number of attempts to establish a connection with an IBM Websphere MQ server. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| Connect using client channel definition table. | Select this option if you want the service to connect to an IBM Websphere MQ server using [CCDT](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.0?topic=server-client-channel-definition-table) (client channel definition table). For this to work, you have to enter a URL to the CCDT file. The CCDT contains information on all client-connection channels defined in an IBM WebSphere MQ session. The table is a binary file (for example, AMQCLCHL.TAB) that contains information required to establish a connection to the IBM Websphere MQ server. |
| Connect through specified connection channel | If you do not want to use CCDT to connect, you can explicitly specify the following connection properties:**Host**: Enter the host name or the IP address of the connection channel.**Port**: Enter the port number to be used for the connection.IBM channel: Enter the channel name as defined by the IBM MQ server. |



###### Default options tab


On this tab, you can specify the default IBM MQ server options. These properties define how the service performs the operations with a target IBM Websphere MQ server (opening a queue, reading or writing a message, and so on). You can enter a single value or several values separated by a pipe (|).


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Inbound default options | |
| Queue open options | Enter IBM MQ Queue open options ([MQOO_*](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/8.0?topic=constants-mqoo-open-options)). Using these options, you can determine how the service opens a queue for reading. |
| **Get options** | Enter IBM MQ Message get options ([MQGMO_*](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/8.0?topic=options-overview-mqgmo)). Using these options, you can determine how the service removes messages from queues. |
| Outbound default options | |
| Queue open options | Specify IBM MQ Queue open options ([MQOO_*](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/8.0?topic=constants-mqoo-open-options)). Using these options, you can control how the service opens a queue for reading. |
| Put options | Specify IBM MQ Put message options ([MQPMO_*](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.3?topic=mqi-mqpmo-put-message-options)). Using these options, you can determine how messages are placed on queues, or published to topics. |



###### SSL options tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use SSL connection | Select yes if you want to use Secure Socket Layer for the connection between the service and an IBM MQ channel. Warning: DSA certificates are not supported because of the migration to Java 17. For SSL connections, you have to [use RSA certificates](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DSA_Certificates_Support.htm) instead. For more information on how to secure a connection to an IBM MQ channel with SSL, click [here](https://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/en/SSFKSJ_8.0.0/com.ibm.mq.explorer.doc/e_ssl.htm). |
| Cipher suite | Select the [CipherSuite](https://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/en/SSFKSJ_8.0.0/com.ibm.mq.dev.doc/q113220_.htm) that matches the CipherSuite set on the SVRCONN channel. If you set it to null, the service does not encrypt data with SSL. |
| Cert store | Enter a list of LDAP servers used for certificate revocation list (CRL) checking. The service tries each LDAP server in turn until the service finds one that can be successfully used to verify the queue manager's certificate. This string must consist of a sequence of space-delimited LDAP URIs in the format ldap://<host>:<:port>. If you do not specify a port , the service uses the default LDAP port 389. If you set it to null (the default value), the service does not verify the queue manager's certificate. |
| Fips required | Defines whether the channel should use [FIPS-certified CipherSpecs](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/7.5?topic=suwmmcs-specifying-that-only-fips-certified-cipherspecs-are-used-run-time-mqi-client). |
| Peer name | Enter a distinguished name (DN) pattern. If you set a value for cipherSuite, this pattern ensures that the correct queue manager is used. The connection attempt fails if the distinguished name provided by the queue manager does not match this pattern. |
| Socket factory | Enter a fully qualified name of the SSLSocketFactory class to be used with SSL encryption. If you select to use a custom SSLSocketFactory, the MQConnectionFactory cannot be bound to a JNDI namespace. If you attempt to do this, it will result in an exception. If you do not enter the class name, the service uses the JSSE default SSLSocketFactory when SSL encryption is requested. The service ignores this property when you do not enter a value for Cipher suite. |
| Reset count | Enter an integer between 0 (disabled) and 999,999,999 to set the SSL reset count. |



###### Pool tab


On this tab, you can specify the connection pooling settings. The following properties are available:


| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Exhausted action | Select an action to perform when the pool gets "exhausted" (the maximum number of active objects has been reached). The following numeric values are available: 0 ([WHEN_EXHAUSTED_FAIL](http://dev.cs.ovgu.de/java/commons-pool/docs/api/org/apache/commons/pool/impl/GenericObjectPool.html#WHEN_EXHAUSTED_FAIL)): throws an exception. 1 ([WHEN_EXHAUSTED_BLOCK](http://dev.cs.ovgu.de/java/commons-pool/docs/api/org/apache/commons/pool/impl/GenericObjectPool.html#WHEN_EXHAUSTED_BLOCK)): waits until a new connection object becomes available, or maximum wait time has been reached. 2 ([WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW](http://dev.cs.ovgu.de/java/commons-pool/docs/api/org/apache/commons/pool/impl/GenericObjectPool.html#WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW)): creates a new connection object anyway. |
| Min evictable idle time (ms) | The minimum amount of time an object may remain idle in the pool before it is eligible for eviction by the idle object evictor (if any). |
| Pool max number of active connections | The maximum number of active connections that can be allocated from this pool at the same time. The default value has no limit. |
| Pool max number of idle connections | The maximum number of connections that can be kept in the pool at all times. |
| Pool max wait (ms) | The maximum time in milliseconds the pool waits for a connection to be returned before throwing an exception. If set to 0, the pool waits indefinitely. |
| Pool min number of idle connections | The maximum number of connections that can be kept in the pool at all times. |
| Pool number tests per eviction run | The number of objects to analyze during each run of the idle object evictor thread (if any). |
| Pool soft min evictable idle time (ms) | The minimum amount of time an object may remain idle in the pool before it becomes eligible for eviction by the idle object evictor (if any). |
| Pool time between eviction runs (ms) | The time interval in milliseconds between the runs of the idle object evictor thread. When set to a non-positive value, no idle object evictor thread is run. |
| testOnBorrow | Indicates whether or not the connection objects are validated before being borrowed from the pool. If an object fails validation, the service removes the object from the pool, and attempts to borrow another object. |
| testOnReturn | Indicates whether or not objects are validated before being returned to the pool. |
| testWhileIdle | Indicates whether or not objects are validated by the idle object evictor (if any). If an object fails validation, the service removes the object from the pool. |



###### MQ Environments tab


On this tab, you can set additional properties supported by an IBM MQ server. Apart from custom properties, the following properties are predefined:


| Property Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **UserID** | The user id that acts as a login to an IBM MQ server. The service passes userID to a queue manager. The queue manager (on the IBM MQ server side) verifies the user credentials to authorize the connection. |
| **Password** | The user password. |



###### QoS Tab


On this tab, you can specify the behavior of a service by setting an additional Action when the service encounters a critical error. The following additional actions are available:


| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restart | Restarts the current service after a critical error. Use the Restart delay field to set a time delay in milliseconds before restarting the service. |
| Restart services | Restarts certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be restarted. |
| Stop services | Stops certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be stopped. |
| None | No action is performed after a critical error. |



###### Dependencies Tab


On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.


###### Service Information Tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **User name** | The last user to make changes to the service settings. |
| **License owner** | The name of the license owner. |
| **Modified** | The date and time of the last route modification. |
| **Note** | Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route. |

---

<a id="jms-apache-activemq-service"></a>

##### JMS Apache ActiveMQ Service

> 📄 Source: [JMS_Apache_ActiveMQ_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Apache_ActiveMQ_Service.htm)

###### JMS Apache ActiveMQ Service


With JMS ActiveMQ Service, you can establish a connection with a JMS server. The supported JMS provider is Apache ActiveMQ.


*[Image: JMS Apache ActiveMQ Service General settings]*


###### General Settings


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service** | Enter a name for the service. This name is used to display the service in the EAM Outline tab. |
| **Type** | The type of the XEServer service. This cannot be changed. |
| **Status** | Select the service status. The following options are available: Enabled: The service starts together with the profile.Using the [Expression Language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm), you can specify a startup condition that allows you to start a service based on a condition. If the startup condition evaluates to `true`, the service starts together with the profile. The startup condition can use only system macros (`${propName}`). For example, you can enable a service by checking for the environment property (`${testStartupProperty}='foo'`). Disabled: The service does not start with the profile. |
| **Path** | The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. |
| **Auto start** | Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. |



###### Service Configuration


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Provided URL** | A URL used to connect to the JMS Server. |
| **Initial context factory** | Location of the context factory which is the class storing the needed properties. |
| **Connection factory jndi name** | JNDI name of the queues and topics connection factory. It should be registered in JNDI. |
| **Retry attempt** | The number of attempts to restart the component when a failure occurs. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| **Number of connections** | The number of possible connection to the server. |
| **User name** | Enter the name used to log in to JMS Server. |
| **Password** | Enter the password used to log in to JMS Server. Click to show the password. |
| **Delivery mode** | The mode for the data delivery. Used for JMS Outbound. Persistent. Saves the message when there is an error in the work of the server. Non-persistent. Doesn't save the message when there is an error in the work of the server. |
| **Transacted** | Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. |
| **Transaction name** | This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out. |



###### JNDI Properties
On this tab, you can specify additional JNDI properties that should be passed to the JMS server.
The following properties can be added:

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| java.naming.security.authentication | Specifies the security level for JNDI lookup. Its value is one of the following strings: "none", "simple", "strong". If this property is unspecified, the behavior is determined by the service provider. |
| java.naming.security.credentials | Used for authenticating the caller to the service; depends on the authentication scheme. It could be a hashed password, clear-text password, key, certificate, or other. |
| java.naming.security.principal | Profile name. |
| java.naming.security.protocol | Constant that holds the name of the property for specifying the security protocol to use. |

###### QoS Tab
On this tab, you can specify the behavior of a service by setting an additional Action when the service encounters a critical error. The following additional actions are available:

| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restart | Restarts the current service after a critical error. Use the Restart delay field to set a time delay in milliseconds before restarting the service. |
| Restart services | Restarts certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be restarted. |
| Stop services | Stops certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be stopped. |
| None | No action is performed after a critical error. |

###### Dependencies Tab
On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.

###### Service Information Tab
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **User name** | The last user to make changes to the service settings. |
| **License owner** | The name of the license owner. |
| **Modified** | The date and time of the last route modification. |
| **Note** | Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route. |

---

<a id="jms-generic-service"></a>

##### JMS Generic Service

> 📄 Source: [JMS_Generic_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Generic_Service.htm)

###### JMS Generic Service


This service allows you to establish a connection with a JMS server.


Tip XEServer comes with vendor-specific JMS services such as: Apache ActiveMQ, IBM WebSphereMQ, Oracle BEA WebLogic, RabbitMQ. These services are specially tailored to specific JMS providers and provide more capabilities compared to the Generic JMS Service. You should use the JMS Generic Service only if you work with a JMS provider that is none of the above.


To configure a generic JMS server:

- On the JMS Inbound or Outbound Settings tab, next to the Service name, click View. If no service exists, click Create new and add it first.


*[Image: JMS Generic Service General settings]*


Note Properties defined on this tab override the properties defined in an external JNDI properties file.


###### General Settings


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service** | Enter a name for the service. This name is used to display the service in the EAM Outline tab. |
| **Type** | The type of the XEServer service. This cannot be changed. |
| **Status** | Select the service status. The following options are available: Enabled: The service starts together with the profile.Using the [Expression Language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm), you can specify a startup condition that allows you to start a service based on a condition. If the startup condition evaluates to `true`, the service starts together with the profile. The startup condition can use only system macros (`${propName}`). For example, you can enable a service by checking for the environment property (`${testStartupProperty}='foo'`). Disabled: The service does not start with the profile. |
| **Path** | The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. |
| **Auto start** | Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. |



###### Service Configuration


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Provided URL** | A URL used to connect to the JMS Server. |
| **Initial context factory** | Location of the context factory which is the class storing the needed properties. |
| **Connection factory jndi name** | JNDI name of the queues and topics connection factory. It should be registered in JNDI. |
| **Retry attempt** | The number of attempts to restart the component when a failure occurs. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| **Number of connections** | The number of possible connection to the server. |
| **User name** | Enter the name used to log in to JMS Server. |
| **Password** | Enter the password used to log in to JMS Server. Click to show the password. |
| **Delivery mode** | The mode for the data delivery. Used for JMS Outbound. Persistent. Saves the message when there is an error in the work of the server. Non-persistent. Doesn't save the message when there is an error in the work of the server. |
| **Transacted** | Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. |
| **Transaction name** | This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out. |



###### JNDI Properties
On this tab, you can specify additional JNDI properties that should be passed to the JMS server.
You can specify JNDI properties using the EAM Name-Value interface, or you can specify a path to the external file that contains JNDI property pairs.

The following properties can be added:

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| java.naming.factory.initial | Location of the context factory; use samples or your own. |
| java.naming.provider.url | URL for connection to the server. |

Caution You need to add custom libraries for your server to ${XESRoot}\platform\lib\jmsext.

###### QoS Tab
On this tab, you can specify the behavior of a service by setting an additional Action when the service encounters a critical error. The following additional actions are available:

| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restart | Restarts the current service after a critical error. Use the Restart delay field to set a time delay in milliseconds before restarting the service. |
| Restart services | Restarts certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be restarted. |
| Stop services | Stops certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be stopped. |
| None | No action is performed after a critical error. |

###### Dependencies Tab
On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.

###### Service Information Tab
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **User name** | The last user to make changes to the service settings. |
| **License owner** | The name of the license owner. |
| **Modified** | The date and time of the last route modification. |
| **Note** | Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route. |

---

<a id="jms-hornetq-service"></a>

##### JMS HornetQ Service

> 📄 Source: [Configure_JMS_HornetQ_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Configure_JMS_HornetQ_Service.htm)

###### Configure JMS HornetQ Service


With JMS HornetQ Service, you can establish a connection with a JMS server. The supported JMS provider is JBoss (HornetQ 3.6.x).


Note This functionality is only available in the preview mode.


To configure a JMS HornetQ server:


XEServer has been upgraded to use HornetQ JMS Client 2.4.8 Final. You should migrate your servers to HornetQ Server 2.4.0 Final (released on 16, December, 2013) or later if you use earlier versions.

- On the JMS Inbound or Outbound Settings tab, next to Service name, click View. If no service exists, you have to click Create new add it first.


*[Image: JMS HornetQ Service General settings tab]*


###### General Settings


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service** | Enter a name for the service. This name is used to display the service in the EAM Outline tab. |
| **Type** | The type of the XEServer service. This cannot be changed. |
| **Status** | Select the service status. The following options are available: Enabled: The service starts together with the profile.Using the [Expression Language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm), you can specify a startup condition that allows you to start a service based on a condition. If the startup condition evaluates to `true`, the service starts together with the profile. The startup condition can use only system macros (`${propName}`). For example, you can enable a service by checking for the environment property (`${testStartupProperty}='foo'`). Disabled: The service does not start with the profile. |
| **Path** | The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. |
| **Auto start** | Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. |



###### Service Configuration


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Provided URL** | A URL used to connect to the JMS Server. |
| **Initial context factory** | Location of the context factory which is the class storing the needed properties. |
| **Connection factory jndi name** | JNDI name of the queues and topics connection factory. It should be registered in JNDI. |
| **Retry attempt** | The number of attempts to restart the component when a failure occurs. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| **Number of connections** | The number of possible connection to the server. |
| **User name** | Enter the name used to log in to JMS Server. |
| **Password** | Enter the password used to log in to JMS Server. Click to show the password. |
| **Delivery mode** | The mode for the data delivery. Used for JMS Outbound. Persistent. Saves the message when there is an error in the work of the server. Non-persistent. Doesn't save the message when there is an error in the work of the server. |
| **Transacted** | Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. |
| **Transaction name** | This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out. |



###### JNDI Properties


| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| java.naming.factory.url.pkgs | Constant that holds the name of the environment property for specifying the list of package prefixes to use when loading in URL context factories. The value of the property should be a colon-separated list of package prefixes for the class name of the factory class that creates a URL context factory. This property may be specified in the environment, an applet parameter, a system property, or one or more resource files. The prefix com.sun.jndi.url is always appended to the possibly empty list of package prefixes. |



###### QoS Tab


On this tab, you can specify the behavior of a service by setting an additional Action when the service encounters a critical error. The following additional actions are available:


| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restart | Restarts the current service after a critical error. Use the Restart delay field to set a time delay in milliseconds before restarting the service. |
| Restart services | Restarts certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be restarted. |
| Stop services | Stops certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be stopped. |
| None | No action is performed after a critical error. |



###### Dependencies Tab


On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.


###### Service Information Tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **User name** | The last user to make changes to the service settings. |
| **License owner** | The name of the license owner. |
| **Modified** | The date and time of the last route modification. |
| **Note** | Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route. |

<a id="jms-ibm-websphere-mq-edifecs-contextfactory-service"></a>

##### JMS IBM WebSphere MQ (Edifecs ContextFactory) Service

> 📄 Source: [JMS_IBM_WebSphere_MQ_(Edifecs_ContextFactory)_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_IBM_WebSphere_MQ_(Edifecs_ContextFactory)_Service.htm)

###### JMS IBM WebSphere MQ (Edifecs ContextFactory) Service


With IBM WebSphere MQ (Edifecs ContextFactory) Service, you can establish a connection with a JMS server. This component uses the InitialContextFactory implemented by Edifecs. The route components [IBM WebSphere MQ Inbound](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/IBM_Websphere_MQ_Inbound.htm) and [IBM WebSphere MQ Outbound](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/IBM_WebSphere_MQ_Outbound.htm) use this service to read and write messages to an IBM Websphere MQ server.


###### IBM required .JAR files


Before proceeding with configuring the JMS IBM WebSphere MQ (Edifecs ContextFactory) Service, the following .JAR files have to be installed or copied to the *${XESRoot}\platform\lib.user\jms\IBMMQ* folder in order for the system to be able to run IBM MQ classes for JMS or IBM MQ classes for Jakarta Messaging:

 *Click here to see the files bcpkix-jdk15to18.jar bcpkix-jdk18on.jar bcprov-jdk15to18.jar bcprov-jdk18on.jar bcutil-jdk15to18.jar bcutil-jdk18on.jar com.ibm.mq.allclient.jar com.ibm.mq.jakarta.client.jar com.ibm.mq.traceControl.jar fscontext.jar jackson-annotations.jar jackson-core.jar jackson-databind.jar jakarta.jms-api.jar jms.jar org.json.jar providerutil.jar The .JAR files can also be automatically installed by executing the **9.4.2.1-IBM-MQ-Install-Java-All.jar** file, which can be found on the IBM website. **Note** For more information, see IBM website. The service supports IBM WebSphere MQ 9.2 and 9.3.4. Queue managers' security has been significantly improved in IBM WebSphere MQ (version 7.1 and later), when compared to previous versions. However, there are still some known issues announced by the IBM MQ. For example, IBM MQ 7.1+ users have to define administrative access to their queue managers explicitly. If you observe the error "2035 MQRC_NOT_AUTHORIZED" when you connect to an IBM MQ server, you should modify the channel authentication records of your queue manager. For more information on this issue, see the detailed description and solution of the problem. For more information about the IBM WebSphere MQ security mechanism, see the IBM WebSphere MQ documentation. Starting with the 9.3.3 release, XEServer supports IBM WebSphere MQ 9.2 and 9.3.4 where MQCSP is the default authentication mode. ## General Settings Field Description **Service** Enter a name for the service. This name is used to display the service in the EAM Outline tab. **Type** The type of the XEServer service. This cannot be changed. **Status** Select the service status. The following options are available: Enabled: The service starts together with the profile.Using the Expression Language, you can specify a startup condition that allows you to start a service based on a condition. If the startup condition evaluates to true, the service starts together with the profile. The startup condition can use only system macros (${propName}). For example, you can enable a service by checking for the environment property (${testStartupProperty}='foo'). Disabled: The service does not start with the profile. **Path** The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. **Auto start** Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. ## Service Configuration Field Description **Provided URL** A URL used to connect to the JMS Server. **Initial context factory** Location of the context factory which is the class storing the needed properties. **Connection factory jndi name** JNDI name of the queues and topics connection factory. It should be registered in JNDI. **Retry attempt** The number of attempts to restart the component when a failure occurs. **Retry delay** The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. **Number of connections** The number of possible connection to the server. **IBM queue manager** Queue manager name defined by the MQ server. **IBM channel** Channel name defined by the MQ server. Default value = SYSTEM.AUTO.SVRCONN. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. **IBM connection mode** Select the connection mode to connect to the queue manager. **User name** Enter the name used to log in to JMS Server. **Password** Enter the password used to log in to JMS Server. Click to show the password. **Delivery mode** The mode for the data delivery. Used for JMS Outbound. Persistent. Saves the message when there is an error in the work of the server. Non-persistent. Doesn't save the message when there is an error in the work of the server. **Transacted** Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. **Transaction name** This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out. ## JNDI Properties On this tab, you can specify additional JNDI properties that should be passed to the JMS server.The following properties can be added:PropertyDescriptionCCDTURLClient channel definition table URL. CCDT URL specifies the file location of CCDT. File and FTP protocols are supported. Example File:///C:/table/AMQCLCHL.TAB' or ftp://userName:password@myServer/path/AMQCLCHL.TAB. Only one property CCDTURL or Channel can be specified at a time. If CCDT URL is specified, the following configuration properties are ignored: hostname, port and all properties starting with SSL.CCSIDThe coded character set identifier.Cert storeSpecifies a list of LDAP servers used for certificate revocation list (CRL) checking. Each LDAP server is tried until it is successfully used to verify the queue manager's certificate. This string must consist of a sequence of space-delimited LDAP URIs of the form ldap://host[:port]. If no port is specified, the LDAP default of 389 is assumed. If set to null (the default), no checking of the queue manager's certificate is performed.Cipher suiteSets the CipherSuite used for SSL encryption. Set this to the CipherSuite matching the CipherSpec set on the SVRCONN channel. cipherSuite - the CipherSuite used for SSL encryption. If set to null (default), no SSL encryption is performed.Fips requiredSets whether sslFips (FIPS) is required (Federal Information Processing Standards).java.naming.security.authenticationSpecifies the security level for JNDI lookup. Its value is one of the following strings: "none", "simple", "strong". If this property is unspecified, the behavior is determined by the service provider.java.naming.security.credentialsUsed for authenticating the caller to the service; depends on the authentication scheme. It could be a hashed password, clear-text password, key, certificate, or other.java.naming.security.principalProfile name.java.naming.security.protocolConstant that holds the name of the property for specifying the security protocol to use.MessageFormatThe message format (IBM WebShpere specific).Peer nameSets a distinguished name (DN) pattern. If sslCipherSuite is set, this pattern can ensure that the correct queue manager is used. The connection attempt fails if the distinguished name provided by the queue manager does not match this pattern.Reset countSets the SSL reset count. It must be an integer, with a value between 0 (disabled) and 999,999,999.Socket factorySets the SSLSocketFactory to be used with SSL encryption. If a custom SSLSocketFactory is specified, the MQConnectionFactory cannot be bound into a JNDI namespace. Attempting to do so results in an exception. This parameter specifies the full class name of the SSLSocketFactory class. If not specified, the JSSE default SSLSocketFactory is used when SSL encryption is requested. This property is ignored if SSL.CipherSuite is not specified. ## QoS Tab On this tab, you can specify the behavior of a service by setting an additional Action when the service encounters a critical error. The following additional actions are available:ActionDescriptionRestartRestarts the current service after a critical error. Use the Restart delay field to set a time delay in milliseconds before restarting the service.Restart servicesRestarts certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be restarted.Stop servicesStops certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be stopped.NoneNo action is performed after a critical error. ## Dependencies Tab On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor. ## Service Information Tab FieldDescription**User name**The last user to make changes to the service settings.**License owner**The name of the license owner.**Modified**The date and time of the last route modification.**Note**Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route.Note JMS WMQInitialContextFactory is not supported by IBM any more. ## Enable SSL Connection To configure a JMS service to enforce an SSL connection, you have to enable SSL for IBMMQ Server (for more information, click here) and configure the connection in the JMS IBM WebSphere MQ service.To enable an SSL connection:On the Service Configuration tab, in Provider URL, enter xesjmsnaming.ibmmq.ssl://localhost:1414*.**Note** To enable SSL, you should use the *xesjmsnaming.ibmmq.ssl* schema.On the JNDI Properties tab, add the following properties:Cipher suite: Set the CipherSuite used for SSL encryption. Set this to the CipherSuite matching the CipherSpec set on the SVRCONN channel. If you set it to null (default), no SSL encryption is performed.And the following optional properties:*Fips required*: Set whether sslFips (FIPS) is required. (Federal Information Processing Standards).*Reset count*: Set the SSL reset count. This must be an integer, with a value between 0 (disabled) and 999,999,999.*Peer name*: Set a distinguished name (DN) pattern. When sslCipherSuite is set, this pattern ensures that the correct queue manager is used. The connection attempt fails when the distinguished name provided by the queue manager does not match this pattern.If you want to use a keystore and a truststore that is different from the JDK specific ones, set the following system properties:ibm.ssl.keystoreibm.ssl.keystore.passwordibm.ssl.key.passwordibm.ssl.truststoreibm.ssl.truststore.password ## WMQInitialContextFactory Migration Since the IBM WebSphere MQ (WMQInitialContextFactory) is no longer supported (since 8.6 release), we recommend that you use IBM WebSphere MQ (Edifecs ContextFactory) instead.If the value of your Initial context factory is com.ibm.mq.jms.context.WMQInitialContextFactory, then you can use the WMQInitialContextFactory from mqcontext.jar which is not supported officially by IBM.However, you can migrate the existing JMS IBM WebSphere MQ Service to work with a different initial context factory.To migrate your JMS IBM WebSphere MQ Service:Copy the following .JAR files to the *${XESRoot}\platform\lib.user\jms\IBMMQ* folder to be able to run IBM MQ classes for JMS or IBM MQ classes for Jakarta Messaging.For more information, see **IBM required .JAR files** above.Click here to see the files
- bcpkix-jdk15to18.jar
- bcpkix-jdk18on.jar
- bcprov-jdk15to18.jar
- bcprov-jdk18on.jar
- bcutil-jdk15to18.jar
- bcutil-jdk18on.jar
- com.ibm.mq.allclient.jar
- com.ibm.mq.jakarta.client.jar
- com.ibm.mq.traceControl.jar
- fscontext.jar
- jackson-annotations.jar
- jackson-core.jar
- jackson-databind.jar
- jakarta.jms-api.jar
- jms.jar
- org.json.jar
- providerutil.jar

Open the configuration of your old WMQInitialContextFactory Service that you want to migrate (Configuration A).Create a new JMS IBM WebSphere MQ (Edifecs ContextFactory) Service (Configuration B).In configuration A, verify the value of the Provider URL parameter. It should have the <host>:<port>/<channel> format. For example, localhost:1414/SYSTEM.AUTO.SVRCONN.In configuration B, change the value of the Provider URL to xesjmsnaming.ibmmq://<host>:<port>, where <host> and <port> are values, copied from the configuration A.Copy the User name value from the configuration A to the configuration B.Copy the Password value from the configuration A to the configuration B. Note, that you cannot copy the password value directly from the UI field, as it is stored in the encrypted form. Type the password manually.Select the Delivery mode to match the configuration A.Copy the Transacted and Transaction name values to the configuration B.Copy the Retry Attempt and Retry delay values to the configuration B.Save the configuration. If all parameters are correctly specified, your JMS IBM WebSphere MQ (Edifecs ContextFactory) Service is ready for work with the Edifecs ContextFactory.
Another option is to use fscontext or any other initial context factory. For more information, see [IBM website](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.3?topic=objects-adding-initial-context).

---

<a id="jms-oracle-bea-weblogic-service"></a>

##### JMS Oracle BEA WebLogic Service

> 📄 Source: [JMS_Oracle_BEA_WebLogic_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Oracle_BEA_WebLogic_Service.htm)

###### JMS Oracle BEA WebLogic Service


With this service, you can establish a connection with [Oracle WebLogic JMS server](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E13222_01/wls/docs81/jms/intro.html).


To allow XEServer to interact with a WebLogic JMS server, you have to:

1. Import WebLogic JMS Client to XEServer
2. Specify the XEServer connection parameters.


###### Import WebLogic JMS Client


WebLogic JMS Client is an open-source application developed by Oracle to allow third-party software to connect to WebLogic JMS servers. According to [Oracle documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E21764_01/web.1111/e13717/wlthint3client.htm#SACLT379), the optimal choice among different types of WebLogic clients is the light-weight WebLogic Thin T3 client, so this client works well with XEServer.


Note: Oracle WebLogic Full Client has been deprecated and Oracle WebLogic Thin Client may have problems with Java 9+ versions. Edifecs recommends using the updated Jakarta version compatible with wlthint3client.jar.


To import the WebLogic Thin T3 client:

1. Remove all .jar files from ${XESroot}/platform/lib.user/jms/WebLogic.
2. Install the latest version of [Oracle WebLogic Server](https://www.oracle.com/technetwork/middleware/weblogic/downloads/wls-main-097127.html).
3. Go to the WebLogic Server installation directory and copy the wlserver\server\lib\wlthint3client.jar client file to ${XESroot}/platform/lib.user/jms/WebLogic.
4. (Optional). To enable the XEServer [WebLogic JMS Monitoring Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Monitoring_Weblogic_Service.htm), go to ${WebLogicRoot}/wlserver/server/lib and copy the wljmxclient.jar file to ${XESRoot}/platform/lib.user/jms/WebLogic.
5. Shut down the profiles with JMS Oracle BEA WebLogic Service for the changes to take effect.


Note: Only one client executable file should reside at ${XESroot}/platform/lib.user/jms/WebLogic at a time.


###### Specify the connection parameters


When you add the service to the route, the following options are available:


*[Image: JMS Oracle BEA WebLogic Service General settings]*


###### Service Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Provided URL** | The URL of the WebLogic JMS Server. |
| **Initial context factory** | The fully qualified name of the [Initial Context Factory](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E11035_01/wls100/javadocs/weblogic/jndi/WLInitialContextFactory.html) class. This class file is located in the JMS client .jar archive and contains instructions on how to handle JMS objects. The default value weblogic.jndi.WLInitialContextFactory points to the corresponding class in WebLogic Thin T3 client. |
| **Connection factory jndi name** | The JNDI name of the [connection factory](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E13222_01/wls/docs103/jms/fund.html). A connection factory with the JNDI name used in this field must be registered on the JMS server. |
| **Retry attempt** | The number of retry attempts to establish a connection, in case a failure occurs. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts, in milliseconds. |
| **Number of connections** | The maximum number of parallel connections to the JMS Server. Different JMS Servers have their own algorithms on how to handle simultaneous connection attempts; some of them allow writing and reading messages in parallel, others block pending messages until the current active connection closes. If there are several JMS Outbound components in your profile that write to the same queue, and you notice that messages are pushed in a way that blocks the queue (one-by-one), you can increase this value to improve throughput. |
| **User name** | The user name to log in to the JMS Server. |
| **Password** | The password used to log in to the JMS Server. |
| **Delivery mode** | Select to decide if the data should be backed up on the JMS Server to provide recovery capabilities in the case of a JMS Server failure. Persistent. The data is backed up and can be recovered. Non-persistent. No backup is performed. If the JMS Server goes down, the messages in queues are lost. |
| **Transacted** | Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. |
| **Transaction name** | This transaction name is only applied when the messages are sent as a Shared transaction. The JMS Outbound identifies the transaction by its name to send the results out. |



To pass additional JNDI properties to the JMS Server, use the service JNDI Properties tab.

<a id="jms-oracle-openmq-service"></a>

##### JMS Oracle OpenMQ Service

> 📄 Source: [JMS_Oracle_OpenMQ_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Oracle_OpenMQ_Service.htm)

###### JMS Oracle OpenMQ Service


With Oracle OpenMQ Service, you can establish a connection with a JMS server. The supported JMS provider is Oracle BEA WebLogic.


*[Image: JMS Oracle OpenMQ Service General settings]*


###### General Settings


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service** | Enter a name for the service. This name is used to display the service in the EAM Outline tab. |
| **Type** | The type of the XEServer service. This cannot be changed. |
| **Status** | Select to enable or disable the service. If enabled you can use a condition (written in Expression language) that evaluates to TRUE/FALSE and defines whether the corresponding route or service must start together with the profile (TRUE) or not (FALSE). The expression can use only system macros. |
| **Path** | The read-only location of the XEServer service within a profile. Tip: Use the EAM Outline tab to arrange services within a profile. You can create new folders and drag-and-drop services into folders. |
| **Auto start** | Select Off if you do not want the service to start automatically on profile startup. If you want the service to start together with the profile, select On with startup level and specify the startup priority index which is a value between 1 and 100. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Services with a higher priority are started first. You cannot use the startup level "0" as it is a reserved value used for system services. |



###### Service Configuration


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Provided URL** | A URL used to connect to the JMS Server. |
| **Initial context factory** | Location of the context factory which is the class storing the needed properties. |
| **Connection factory jndi name** | JNDI name of the queues and topics connection factory. It should be registered in JNDI. |
| **Retry attempt** | The number of attempts to restart the component when a failure occurs. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| **Number of connections** | The number of possible connection to the server. |
| **User name** | Enter the name used to log in to JMS Server. |
| **Password** | Enter the password used to log in to JMS Server. Click to show the password. |
| **Delivery mode** | The mode for the data delivery. Used for JMS Outbound. Persistent. Saves the message when there is an error in the work of the server. Non-persistent. Doesn't save the message when there is an error in the work of the server. |
| **Transacted** | Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. |
| **Transaction name** | This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out. |



###### JNDI Properties
On this tab, you can specify additional JNDI properties that should be passed to the JMS server.

###### QoS Tab
On this tab, you can specify the behavior of a service by setting an additional Action when the service encounters a critical error. The following additional actions are available:

| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restart | Restarts the current service after a critical error. Use the Restart delay field to set a time delay in milliseconds before restarting the service. |
| Restart services | Restarts certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be restarted. |
| Stop services | Stops certain services after a critical error. Enter the path to the service(s) that has to be stopped. |
| None | No action is performed after a critical error. |

###### Dependencies Tab
On this tab, you can view the route components associated with the current service. Double-click a component to view the component in the Route Editor.

###### Service Information Tab
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **User name** | The last user to make changes to the service settings. |
| **License owner** | The name of the license owner. |
| **Modified** | The date and time of the last route modification. |
| **Note** | Notes about the service. You can edit this field any time while working on a route. |

<a id="transaction-processing-in-local-and-shared-modes"></a>

##### Transaction Processing in Local and Shared Modes

> 📄 Source: [JMS_Transaction_Processing_in_Local_Shared_Modes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Transaction_Processing_in_Local_Shared_Modes.htm)

###### Transaction Processing in Local and Shared Modes


XEServer JMS components support an entity called Transactions to prevent data loss and to roll back to the original file when route processing fails. This case study describes the various circumstances and operation modes where Transactions are used. There are two mode types – Local and Shared that can be set on the Inbound and Outbound JMS endpoints. The component where a mode is set, drives the logic of Transaction roll-back.


The following case study demonstrates the peculiarities of transaction processing in the routes that contain JMS components.


###### The JMS Outbound component in Local and Shared modes


The following is a sample route that demonstrates simple message processing in a route with a JMS Outbound component:



In this route, the inbound file is picked up, validated by XEngine and the resulting XData or multiple XData files are sent to the outbound JMS Queue. With the JMS Outbound component, Transactions come to the rescue when the XES message exchange (sent by the Native Processing component) contains multiple internal messages.


A Transaction ensures that all or no messages from the message exchange are delivered. If at least one message is lost because of an exception, then all other messages from this delivery must be cleaned up to prevent partial processing by the downstream system, reading from this queue. The Transaction modes affect the processing in the way described below.


If the Local mode is set in the JMS Outbound component settings, the processing is performed the following way:

1. A message exchange is processed in the Native Processing component. The resulting message exchange with one or multiple messages inside is then sent to a JMS Outbound component.
2. The message exchange is added to the JMS Outbound component the following way:
3. A new session is created, and every message in the message exchange is added to the JMS queue.
4. If there is an exception, then the transaction is rolled back (all the messages are deleted), and the session is closed.
5. If there are no errors, and all messages have been added to the queue successfully, the transaction is committed, and the session is closed.
6. A processing report file from the Native component is saved to the file system.


The following diagram illustrates the processing in the Local mode:



If the Shared mode is set in the JMS Outbound component settings, the processing is performed the following way:

1. A message exchange is processed in the Native Processing component.
2. The message exchange is added to the JMS Outbound component the following way:
3. A new session is created, and each message in the message exchange is added to the JMS queue.
4. If there is an exception, then the transaction is rolled back (all messages are deleted), and the session is closed.
5. If there is no exception, the process moves to the next component.

1. A processing report file is saved to the file system.
2. If there is an error occurs during the file saving, the transaction is rolled back (all messages from the queue are deleted), and the session is closed.
3. If a file is saved successfully, the transaction is committed (messages from the JMS queue are committed), and the session is closed.


The following diagram illustrates the processing in the Shared mode:



Note In case of the multimessage exchange, a message with an exception is always rejected.


The key difference between Local and Shared modes is that in the Shared mode, Transaction is rolled back even if there is an exception in any other component of the same route.


###### The JMS Inbound component in Local and Shared modes (the non-command driven mode)


The following is a sample route that demonstrates message processing in the route with the JMS Inbound component in the non-command driven mode:



If the Local mode is set in the JMS Inbound component settings, the processing is performed the following way:

1. Messages are read from the queue, one by one.

1. If an exception is thrown during the reading process (for example, a task cannot be created), the message remains in the queue.
2. If the reading is performed successfully, the message is removed from the queue.


The following diagram illustrates the processing in the local mode:



If the Shared mode is set in the JMS Inbound component settings, the processing is performed the following way:

1. A message is read from the queue.
2. A message exchange is processed in the Native Processing component. If an exception is thrown, a transaction is rolled back, the message remains in the queue and will be processed later again. The next message is read.

1. A result of processing is saved to the file system. If an exception is thrown, a transaction is rolled back, the message remains in the queue, and the next message is read.

1. A processing report file is saved to the file system. If an exception is thrown, a transaction is rolled back, the message remains in the queue, and the next message is read. If no errors occur, a session is closed.


The following diagram illustrates the processing in the shared mode:



###### The JMS Inbound component in Local and Shared modes (the command driven mode)


In the command-driven mode of the JMS Inbound endpoint the processing is performed differently. The following is a sample route that demonstrates message processing in the route with the JMS Inbound component in the command driven mode:



In the Local mode, the processing is performed the following way:

1. Once reading is triggered, all messages are read from the queue as one batch.
2. If an exception is thrown in any massage of the batch, a batch with all messages stays in the queue.
3. If reading is performed successfully, a batch with all messages is removed from the queue.


In the Shared mode, the processing is performed the following way:

1. A message is read from the queue.
2. A message exchange is processed in the Native Processing component. If an exception is thrown, a transaction is rolled back, the message remains in the queue, and will not be attempted to be processed again.
3. A result of processing is saved to the file system. If an exception is thrown, a transaction is rolled back, the message remains in the queue.
4. A processing report file is saved to the file system. If an exception is thrown, a transaction is rolled back, the message remains in the queue. If no errors occur, a session is closed.

---

<a id="use-jms-transaction"></a>

##### Use JMS Transaction

> 📄 Source: [Use_JMS_Transaction.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_JMS_Transaction.htm)

###### Use JMS Transaction


Using JMS Transaction to recover the lost data is not recommended if the recovery requires the long-term processing. The data is committed to the JMS Server for the further processing without any loss. The are several ways to roll back the data containing error.


To select the corresponding recovery type:

- In the JMS components Settings tab, in the Transacted list, select one of the following:
- None - Transactions are not used. All messages are passed to the components separately. If an error occurred in one message, this particular message gets an exception. All the other messages are still processed.
- Local - In case of an outbound component, each message in a message exchange is added to the queue within the one session. Once the last message is successfully added to the queue, a transaction is committed, and the session is close. If an error occurs, the transaction is rolled back, and changes are not applied. In this case, the current session is close and processing proceeds. In case of an inbound component, it reads a message from the queue and sends it to the route for further processing. When the JMS inbound component gets the control back, the local transaction is committed, and the JMS message is removed from the queue. For example, if a route is synchronous, the JMS inbound component gets the control back when the task is complete. If a route uses asynchronous connectors or components, for example, JMS inbound component is bound by the asynchronous connector, it gets the control back once the message is submitted to the bound component.
- Shared – If a shared transaction is used, inbound and outbound components on a route share one transaction. Reading and writing is performed within one single transaction. The transaction is committed when a task is complete without errors. If an error occurs in a task, the transaction is rolled back and changes are not applied.


On the following route, the task is started within JMS Inbound and processed by Native Processing component. JMS Outbound 1 receives the xdata, and JMS Outbound 2 receives reports. Whenever an errors is reported to JMS Outbound 2, the task is rolled back for both outbound components and is retried in the JMS Inbound.


*[Image: JMS Transaction route]*

---

<a id="mongodb-service"></a>

##### MongoDB Service

> 📄 Source: [MongoDB_Service.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Service.htm)

###### MongoDB Connection Service


The MongoDB Connection service provides connectivity with a MongoDB server and is used by [MongoDB Command components](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Command.htm) to run MongoDB commands. The service is responsible for establishing a connection with MongoDB instances, managing connection pooling, handling transactions, and so on.


The service uses [MongoDB Java Driver 4.5](https://mongodb.github.io/mongo-java-driver/) which supports MongoDB versions from 2.6 up to 5.2. For more information on which MongoDB version is supported by a specific driver, see [Compatibility](https://docs.mongodb.com/drivers/java/sync/current/compatibility/#compatibility) table.


###### Service Settings


The service has the following settings:


###### Configuration tab



| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Connection URL | The connection URL to connect to a MongoDB server. A connection string has the following format: ``` mongodb://[username:password@]host1[:port1][,host2[:port2],...[,hostN[:portN]]][/[database.collection][?options]] ``` |
| Database name | The MongoDB database to connect. |
| Journaled write | Select whether to write acknowledgements to the on-disk journal. By default, acknowledgements are not written to the journal (false). |
| Keystore path | The path to the keystore file used for X.509 authentication. |
| Keystore password | The password to access the keystore. |
|   |   |
| Read concern | The [Read Concern](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/read-concern/) option allows you to control the consistency and isolation properties of the data read from replica sets and replica set shards. A Read Concern can be set at a connection level, and at operation level (in the Transaction components settings). The following options are available: [LOCAL](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/read-concern-local/), [AVAILABLE](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/read-concern-available/): A query with this read concern type returns data from a MongoDB instance with no guarantee that the data has been written to a majority of the replica set members. [MAJORITY](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/read-concern-majority/): Guarantees that the data read has been acknowledged by a majority of the replica set members (the documents read are durable and guaranteed not to roll back). [LINEARIZABLE](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/read-concern-linearizable/): A query returns data that reflects all successful majority-acknowledged writes that completed prior to the start of the read operation. [SNAPSHOT](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/read-concern-snapshot/): Available for multi-document transactions, and starting in MongoDB 5.0, certain read operations outside of multi-document transactions |
| Read preference | The [Read Preference](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/core/read-preference/) option defines how MongoDB clients must route read operations to the members of a replica set. The following options are available: [PRIMARY](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/read-preference/#mongodb-readmode-primary): All read operations use only the primary of the current replica set. [PRIMARY_PREFERRED](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/read-preference/#mongodb-readmode-primaryPreferred): In most cases, operations read from the primary member of the set. However, if the primary is unavailable, as is the case during failover situations, operations read from secondary members too. [SECONDARY](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/read-preference/#mongodb-readmode-secondary): Operations read only from the secondary members of the set. If no secondaries are available, then this read operation produces an error or exception. [SECONDARY_PREFERRED](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/read-preference/#mongodb-readmode-secondaryPreferred): In most situations, operations read from secondary members, but in situations where the set consists of a single primary (and no other members), the read operation will use the replica set's primary. [NEAREST](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/read-preference/#mongodb-readmode-nearest): The driver reads from a member whose network latency falls within the acceptable latency window. Reads in the nearest mode do not consider whether a member is a primary or secondary when routing read operations: primaries and secondaries are treated equivalently. |
| Truststore path | The path to the truststore file. |
| Truststore password | The password to access the truststore. |
| Use SSL | Indicates whether to use SSL for connection. |
| Write concern | The [Write Concern](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/write-concern/) option describes the level of acknowledgment requested from MongoDB for write operations to a standalone mongod or to replica sets or to sharded clusters. In sharded clusters, mongos instances will pass the write concern on to the shards. |



###### Authentication tab


On this tab, you can configure the authentication to connect to a MongoDB server.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Authentication type: SCRAM_SHA_1, SCRAM_SHA_256 Salted Challenge Response Authentication Mechanism ([SCRAM](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/security-scram/)) is the default authentication mechanism for MongoDB. `SCRAM_SHA_1` uses the SHA-1 hash function and `SCRAM_SHA_256` uses SHA-256. | |
| Username | The user name to authenticate the connection to a MongoDB server. |
| Password | The user password. |
| Source | The name of the database where the user record is stored. |
| Authentication type: PLAIN Use this option to work with a MongoDB server that has preconfigured [integration with an LDAP server](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/tutorial/authenticate-nativeldap-activedirectory/). For this type, you have to provide MongoDB user credentials. The LDAP integration must be configured by a MongoDB adminsistrator. | |
| Username | The user name to authenticate the connection to a MongoDB server. |
| Password | The user password. |
| Source | The name of the database where the user record is stored. |
| Authentication type: GSSAPI Use this option for [Kerberos](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/kerberos/) authentication. | |
| Username | The Kerberos user name, including the realm, for example "user1@MYREALM.ME". |
| Service name | Provide a service name to override the default service name. |
| Canonicalize host name | To force canonicalization of the host name prior to authentication, set the property value to `true`. |
| Java subject | To override the default `javax.security.auth.Subject` class involved in the authentication flow, provide a custom `Subject` instance in the property value. |
| Authentication type: MONGODB_AWS Use this option for [AWS-based authentication](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/kafka-connector/current/security-and-authentication/mongodb-aws-auth/). | |
| Username | The AWS access key. |
| Password | The AWS secret key. |
| AWS Credential Provider | The AWS credential provider. |
| AWS Session Token | The AWS session token. |
| Authentication type: MONGODB_X509 Use this option for X.509 authentication. | |
| Distinguished name | The user-distinguished name, for example `CN=TEST-USERNAME,OU=REMOTE,O=EDIFECS,L=WA,ST=WA,C=US`. |



###### Pool tab


On this tab, you can configure MongoDB connection pooling parameters.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Max idle time | The maximum time a connection may remain idle in the connection pool. A pooled connection that has exceeded its idle time will be closed and replaced when necessary by a new connection. Setting the value to 0 removes the limit. |
| Max life time | The maximum time for a pooled connection to live. A pooled connection that has exceeded its life time will be closed and replaced when necessary by a new connection. If set to 0, then there is no limit to the life time |
| Max wait time | The maximum time to wait for a connection to become available. The default value is 2 minutes. If set to 0, the service does not wait. Using a negative value makes it wait indefinitely. |
| Pool max number of active connections | The maximum number of connections a pool may keep active simultaneously. |
| Pool max number of idle connections | The maximum number of simultaneous connections to a database. Those connections will be kept in the connection pool when idle. Once the pool is exhausted, any operation requiring a connection will block and wait for an available connection. The default value is 100. |
| Pool min number of idle connections | The minimum number of connections to be available in the pool at all times. Those connections will be kept in the pool when idle, and the pool will ensure that it contains at least this minimum number of connections. Default is 0. |



###### Cluster Mode


The service supports native MongoDB clustering. That is, you can use this service to work with multiple [clustered](https://www.mongodb.com/basics/clusters) MongoDB servers. For this, specify MongoDB servers in the connection string as shown below:



```
mongodb://{hostName1}:{port},{hostName2}:{port},{hostNameN}:{port}?{options}
```






Note MongoDB transactions are [known](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/transactions/#transactions-api) to be working in a cluster mode only.
