<a id="azure-queue-storage-inbound"></a>

#### Azure Queue Storage Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Azure_Queue_Storage_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Queue_Storage_Inbound.htm)

##### Azure Queue Storage Inbound


With this component, you can read (consume) messages from [Azure Queue Storage](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/queues/storage-queues-introduction). After consuming, the component removes messages from the queue.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



To see the list of macros supported by this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#AzureQueue).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration Tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Activation mode | Select a way to start the component: Route start. The component starts with the route and scans the specified queue for new messages at specified Polling intervals. This activation mode allows you to use only system macros. By command. The component starts when it receives an XEServer message. In this mode, the inbound component obtains an extra input called "data" for receiving messages. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Queue name | From the list of queues [created](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/queues/storage-quickstart-queues-portal#create-a-queue) in your Azure Storage account, specify the queue from which the component should retrieve messages. Click to fetch the list of queues available in your account (maximum 50 items are displayed). |
| Message format | Select the format in which the messages are written to Azure Queue Storage. This determines how the Azure Queue Storage Inbound component maps Azure Queue message to XEServer message. RAW: The component maps Azure Queue message body with XEServer message body as is. XEServer message does not contain any attributes since they are not supported in Azure Queue messages. EDIFECS: The component recognizes the body and [attributes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Queue_Storage_Outbound.htm#Attributes) written to Azure Queue message body and maps them to XEServer message body and XEServer message attributes correspondingly. The EDIFECS message format for Azure Queue messages can be represented as follows:[Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)EDIFECS message example ``` EDFXAAAAHw=={"color":"green","form":"oval"}success ``` where: `EDFX`: The format indicator occupying the first four bytes in a message. `AAAAHw==`: The Base64 encoded value of a four-byte integer that indicates the size of [message attributes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Queue_Storage_Outbound.htm#Attributes) in bytes. In the example above, the length of JSON serialized string {"color":"green","form":"oval"} is 31, which corresponds to the four-byte 0x0000001F integer. The result of the 0x0000001F integer encoding is AAAAHw==, which occupies eight bytes. `{"color":"green","form":"oval"}`: The JSON serialized message attributes in UTF-8 encoding. The length of these attributes is indicated by the Base64 encoded value AAAAHw==. `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><my-transaction>success</my-transaction>`: The XEServer message body. |
| Message encoding | Select BASE64 or NONE to specify whether the queue messages are Base64 encoded. |
| Polling interval | The component polls the queue at regular intervals and reads all the available messages, if any. To optimize the frequency of component's requests to Azure Queue Storage, set the time interval which the component should wait after reading the last available message. After this pause, the component reads the next queue messages. |
| Acknowledgment strategy | When the component successfully reads a message from the queue, it sends an acknowledgment to Azure Queue Storage and the consumed message is removed from the queue. Define when such acknowledgment should be sent: IMMEDIATE: The component sends an acknowledgment immediately after reading a message from Azure Queue Storage. If errors occur further down the route, the message is not returned to the queue. TRANSACTIONAL: The component sends an acknowledgment only after the entire XEServer task is complete. If errors occur further down the route, the message consuming is rolled back. The consumed message is not removed from the queue until the task is finished, which allows other consumers to read this message. |
| Lock renew interval | When the component is reading a message in the queue, this message becomes invisible for other readers of this queue during the time you specify in the Lock duration parameter. Specify the Lock renew interval at which the message status is checked. If the message is still in processing and unavailable, the invisibility time is prolonged (that is, the lock duration time is counted from the beginning). |
| Lock duration | Specify for how long a message becomes invisible for other readers of this queue when the component is reading the message. If XEServer task processing fails, the message invisibility timeout is changed to 0 to allow other consumers to read the message. |

---

<a id="bcounter-inbound"></a>

#### BCounter Inbound

> 📄 Source: [BCounter_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/BCounter_Inbound.htm)

##### BCounter Inbound


BCounter Inbound Endpoint is a route component that monitors the counters and generates an empty XEServer message with properties that consists of all information about the counter when certain events take place.


Note At the time when an inbound endpoint is configured some counters group should be already pre-configured, their metadata should be read from the database and it should be available at the service level. Hence, the Balance Counter service should be fully configured before any route components are configured.


The counter completed or expired state is reported through the BCounter Inbound component. For more information about the possible states of the counters, see [The Balance Counter State](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Balance_Counters_States.htm).


Note If several inbound components are used across environment, notification about counter completion or expiration is only reported through one of them.


You can set BCounter Inbound properties on the Route component properties tab.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Activation mode** | Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Counter group | Balance Counter Group to be used. |
| Data format | The date format. The default value is yyyyMMdd-HHmmss.SSS. |

---

<a id="batcher-inbound"></a>

#### Batcher Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Batcher_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Batcher_Inbound.htm)

##### Batcher Inbound


The Batcher Inbound component is designed to take out all Batch Groups that match the trigger criteria. The result is a persistent batch package (ZIP file). The Batch Groups are reported according to the specified filters.


Note Only one Batcher Inbound component can be used across multiple boxes to handle one trigger.

 Click to see the processing diagram


*[Image: Batcher Outbound Processing diagram]*






You can set Batcher Inbound properties on the Route component properties tab.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Displays the service name. Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Load package | Displays the packaging method. The component can report all Batch Groups or filter them by channel mask. |
| Channel name mask | Displays the channel name mask. The component reports Batch Groups from channels with a name that fits the specified mask. |

---

<a id="data-storage-inbound"></a>

#### Data Storage Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Data_Storage_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Data_Storage_Inbound.htm)

##### Data Storage Inbound


The component allows you to read messages from data storage.


*[Image: Data Storage Inbound Settings tab]*


###### READ Retry for NULL Results


When the component receives NULL from an underlying database, this can mean:

- no records were found in the database, -or-
- a failed database operation. This typically happens as a result of concurrent access issues.


By default, to ensure that NULL is the actual value and not the after effect of a concurrent access error, the component uses a special retry mechanism: the component retries reading the same record 5 times at 100-millisecond intervals. This behavior is controlled by the following properties:


-Dcom.edifecs.common.datastorage.lock.retries=5


-Dcom.edifecs.common.datastorage.lock.interval=100


However, in a low speed environment (especially, when the database connection has a high latency), this mechanism can lead to processing delays during READ operations. If the performance of READ operations is unacceptable, you can decrease the number of retries, decrease the retry interval, or disable the retry mechanism completely. When you change these parameters, make sure that the mentioned concurrency issues (concurrent reading from data storage returns NULL) do not take place in your particular environment and READ operations run reliably and consistently. To disable the retry mechanism, set the following property on the profile [Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) tab to 0:


-Dcom.edifecs.common.datastorage.lock.retries=0


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Activation mode | Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Default command | Available only for the By command activation mode. Select an action to perform when the component detects a data item in the data storage. Extract: Reads the data, then removes the data item, and sends the data further down the route. Get: Reads the data, and then sends the data further down the route without removing the data item from the data storage. Remove: Removes all detected data items without sending the data further down the route. |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Source path | Specify a path to the Data Storage directory to read data items. Data Storage stores data items in a hierarchical Linux-style file system manner, for example /dir0/dir1/dataItem. |
| ID | The ID of the data items to be extracted. This can be an exact ID, a mask, or an empty value (when empty, the component reads all the data items from the specified Source Path). This field is effective in the Route start mode and when the received command does not carry the parameter ID. Note Using an empty ID value leads to heavier database operations when compared to retrieving a specific item by a known ID. This can potentially increase the database response time, especially when the database is addressed concurrently. A good practice is to select items precisely by IDs as it minimizes the interaction with the underlying database. |
| Scan timeout (ms) | The timeout in milliseconds to rescan the source path in Data Storage. |
| Results batching | Indicates the batching behavior. The following options are available: Batch all results: Batches all the data items retrieved from Data Storage. The component sends the results further down the route in the form of a multi-message exchange where each message represents a data item. Batch results by folder: Batches data items that were detected in the same Data Storage directory under Source Path. The component creates a multi-message exchange for each directory with the retrieved data items. None: No batching is performed. Each data item is reported separately - one XEServer exchange with one message. |
| Scan mode | Select the mode to scan the source folder: Non-recursive: Scans only the directory defined as Source folder. Recursive. Recursively scans the file system starting from Source folder deep down the directory tree. |

---

<a id="email-inbound"></a>

#### Email Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Email_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Email_Inbound.htm)

##### Email Inbound


The Email Inbound component is used for scanning an email box for email messages and gather the required information from the email message body. By using this component, you can receive email messages through the POP3 and IMAP protocols, filter them, decrypt, and verify against a signature.


To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Email).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


*### Settings tab #### Common tab By using the Common tab, you can provide basic connection and activation settings for the Email Inbound component. Field Description Activation mode Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. Default command Select the action to perform with messages in the email box. The following actions are available: Read All: All messages in the email box are scanned. Read All and Delete: All messages in the email box are removed after scanning. Read Unseen: Only the unseen messages are scanned. Read Unseen and Delete: The unseen messages are removed after scanning. Note that deletion of messages can be prevented by server configuration. In this case, the Read All and Delete and the Read Unseen and Delete commands cannot override the email server's configuration, that is, the messages are not deleted after scanning. Protocol Select either the POP3 or the IMAP protocol to be used for receiving email messages. Note that the IMAP protocol allows you to read messages from the specified email box folder, such as Drafts, Trash, Spam, and so on. Mailbox folder Specify the email box folder to be scanned for email messages. **Note** This option is only available for the IMAP protocol. For the POP3 protocol, all messages are stored in a single **INBOX** folder. Connection security Select a security type for your connection. The following types are available: None: A plain connection type is used. SSL/TLS: A TLS or SSL connection type is used to provide secure connection to the email server. Start TLS: Enables the STARTTLS mechanism for your connection. This option allows upgrading a plain connection to a secure one. For more information about the STARTTLS connection type, see STARTTLS documentation. Scan interval(sec) Specify the time interval at which the email box is scanned for new messages. **Note** This option is only available for the Route start activation mode. Host Specify the host name of the mail server. Port Specify a port number to be used for connection. By default, the following ports are used: For the POP3 protocol with a plain connection type: 110. For the POP3 protocol with the SSL/TLS connection type: 995. For the IMAP protocol with a plain connection type: 143. For the IMAP protocol with the SSL/TLS connection type: 993. User name Specify a user name for the email box. Password Specify a password for the email box. Receiving limit Specify the number of messages that can be read from the email box with one command. If the value is set to '0', all messages in the email box are scanned. **Note** This option is only available for the By command activation mode. #### Filters tab By using the Filters tab, you can filter messages by their headers. The body of the message is scanned when the parameters of a message match the field values. Field Description Enable mail filtering Select this option to enable/disable message filtering. Subject Type a subject to scan messages with the specified subject. Sender Type a sender's name to scan the messages only from the specified sender. Receiver Type a receiver's name to scan messages sent only to the specified receiver. Include mails with size greater (KB) Specify the minimum size threshold in kilobytes for messages to be scanned. Include mails with size less (KB) Specify the maximum size threshold in kilobytes for messages to be scanned. **Note** You can specify more than one value for Subject, Sender, and Receiver fields by using the ';' character as a separator. For example, Subject: subject1;subject2;subject3*. When you set multiple values XEServer performs a serial verification of the defined values until one of them matches the header. Thus, the Email Inbound component receives messages that have subject1, or subject2, or subject3 subject header.


Leave the Include mails with size greater (KB) and the Include mails with size less (KB) fields empty to disable filtering email messages by their size.


###### SSL tab


On the SSL tab, you can specify paths and passwords to your trusted and/or key stores. To do this, ensure that the SSL/TLS connection security type is selected on the Common tab and select the Use custom Trusted and(or) Key stores option. If you disable the Use custom Trusted and(or) Key stores option, the certificates from the default Java trusted store are used.


| Section | Field | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Trusted store | Keystore | Specify the path to a key store with trusted certificates. |
| Password | Specify the password to a key store with trusted certificates. | |
| Key store | Keystore | Specify the path to a file that contains key store certificates. |
| Password | Specify the password to a file that contains key store certificates. | |
| Key password | Specify the password to a private key. | |



###### S/MIME tab


On the S/MIME tab, you can configure decryption and mail signature settings to set the maximum security level for your connection. An email signature is used to verify messages against its sender and receiver credibility to ensure that messages were not intercepted by a third party. The decryption allows authorized parties to decode messages, if they are exchanged in the encrypted form. The messages are verified for a signature, and if it is valid, the decryption is performed.


| Section | Field | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|   | Verify mail signature | Select this option to enable resolving email signatures. |
| Verify using a certificate | Type | Select a key store format to be used. The JKS and the PKCS12 formats are supported. |
| Keystore | Specify a path to a file that contains key store certificates. | |
| Password | Specify a password to a file that contains key store certificates. | |
| Common name | Specify a mask to filter certificates by their subject parameters. Commonly used parameters are common name (CN) and email address (emailAddress). Use the following syntax to filter certificates by these subject parameters: CN=www.example.com,emailAddress=emailbox@example.com Macros are available for this field, for example CN=${MD_EMAIL_SenderName},emailAddress=${MD_EMAIL_SenderAddress}. Use the ',' character as a delimiter to specify more than one value for a subject parameter. For more information about certificate subject, see the [X.509 documentation](https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.1.1/man1/x509.html). | |
| Use certificate from signature (if exists) | Select this option to use the certificate that was inserted into the signature by the sender. | |
|   | Decrypt mail | Select this option to decrypt incoming messages. |
| Decrypt using a private key | Type | Select a key store format to be used. The JKS and the PKCS12 formats are supported. |
| Keystore | Specify the path to the file that contains key store certificates. | |
| Password | Specify the password to the file that contains key store certificates. | |
| Key alias | Specify an alias that is used as an ID to select the required key. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. | |
| Key password | Specify the password to the private key. | |



###### Advanced tab


By using the Advanced tab, you can configure tracing parameters and specify connection and scanning timeouts.


| Section | Field | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Email tracer | Enabled | Select this option to enable data exchange tracing. |
| Tracer directory | Specify the path to the directory, where tracer files are located. | |
| Connection timeout (sec) | A timeout within which the connection to the specified email box is established. | |
|   | Read timeout (sec) | A timeout within which messages in the specified email box are scanned. |

---

<a id="error-channel-inbound"></a>

#### Error Channel Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Error_Channel_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Error_Channel_Inbound.htm)

##### Error Channel Inbound


Error channel is designed to collect errors from the route. Error channel accumulates the errors from all the spare error outputs of all the components on the route. If an error output is connected to some other component, then no errors are reported to the [error channel](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Route_Quality_of_Service_Settings.htm#ErrorChannelTab). Thus, if you manually direct errors to some other storage or to other process, error channel is not able to get them.


###### Component Settings


This component has only [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings.

---

<a id="ftp-inbound"></a>

#### FTP Inbound

> 📄 Source: [FTP_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Inbound.htm)

##### FTP Inbound


This component is used to download files from an FTP server. To establish a connection with a target FTP server, the component can use [FTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Service.htm)/[SFTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service.htm)/[FTPS](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTPS_Service.htm) service as a bridge or can connect directly using the component-level settings.


*[Image: FTP Inbound Settings tab]*


To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#FTP).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use connection settings on component | Use this option to allow the component to connect to the target FTP server directly, without using [FTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Service.htm)/[SFTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service.htm)/[FTPS](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTPS_Service.htm) services. If selected, you have to provide connectivity parameters on the component's Connection tab. |
| Use connection pool | Select this option to enable connection pooling. Opening and closing a physical connection with an FTP server is a costly operation. With connection pooling, the service opens several connections and then reuses them when needed keeping the connections in the pool. If disabled, the service opens a new physical connection each time an FTP operation is requested. To configure connection pooling parameters, use the Pool configuration tab on the [FTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Service.htm)/[SFTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service.htm)/[FTPS](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTPS_Service.htm) service. If you select Use connection settings on component, the connection pooling is still available. In this case, to configure connection pooling parameters, you have to define the following properties on the profile [Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) tab. Click to see per-component connection pooling properties `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.maxActive`: The maximum number of objects that can be allocated by the pool at a time. If value < 0 the number of objects is unlimited. The default value is 4. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.maxIdle`: The maximum number of idle objects in the pool at a time. If value < 0 the number of objects is unlimited. The default value is 4. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.minIdle`: The maximum amount of time to wait for getting an object from the pool. The default value is 0. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.maxWait`: The maximum amount of time to wait for getting an object from the pool. The default value is 1000. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.minEvictableIdleTimeMillis`: The minimum amount of time for an idle object in the pool before it is eligible for eviction due to idle state. The default value is 60000. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.numTestsPerEvictionRun`: The number of objects to be examined during each run of idle eviction thread. The default value is 3. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.softMinEvictableIdleTimeMillis`: The minimum amount of time an object may remain idle in the pool before it is eligible for eviction by the idle evictor, with the extra condition that at least "minIdle" object remains in the pool. The default value is 1. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.testOnBorrow`: Defines whether objects pass a validation before being borrowed from the pool. If the validation fails, the object is dropped from the pool. The default value is true. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.testOnReturn`: Defines whether objects pass a validation before being returned to the pool. The default value is false. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.testWhileIdle`: Defines whether objects pass a validation by the idle object evictor. If the validation fails, the object is dropped from the pool. The default value is true. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.timeBetweenEvictionRunsMillis`: Defines whether objects pass a validation before being returned to the pool. The default value is 10000. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.whenExhaustedAction`: Select the behavior of getting an object from the pool if the pool is exhausted (the list number matches the selected value): 0 - throws NoSuchElementException. 1 - blocks according to the pool.maxWait value and throws NoSuchElementException after timeout exceeds. 2 - creates a new object and returns it. The pool.maxActive parameters becomes meaningless for such behavior. The default value is 1. |
| **Activation mode** | Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Default command | Available only for the By command activation mode.Select an action to perform when the component detects a file in the inbound location:Extract: Reads data from the file, submits the data further down the route for processing, and deletes the original file once processing is complete.Get: Reads data from the file and submits the data further down the route for further processing without deleting the file.Remove: Removes the file without reading and sending the data further down the route.Note If the file processing fails during the Extract or Get action, the file is moved to the folder defined by the system property com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.fs.recovery.template or to ${XESWorkspace}/error/{R_Name}/OriginalData/{C_Name}_{T_ID}{CDT}_{MD_File_Name} if the system property is not set. In case of Remove operation failure, the file remains in the inbound location. |
| **Service name** | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| **Source folder** | The path to the source folder to scan. |
| **Include masks** | With these options, you can provide a file name mask to include or exclude specific files based on their name and extension. The following wildcards are supported: `**`: any folder with all its subfolders. `*` : any set of characters. `?`: any character. Note The Include masks field is case-sensitive. Example Mask: folder1/**/data?.dat Files matched: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/data1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/dataX.dat, and so on. Mask: 5010* Files matched: <SourceFolder>/5010_837P_x222_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_837D_x224_A2.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. To use multiple masks simultaneously, separate masks by using a comma or semicolon without the white space character. Example Masks: folder1/**/Test?.dat;5010* Files matched: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/Test1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/TestN.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_270_x279_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. By default, the value is set to `**/*` that picks up files with any name in all subfolders under the source directory. To use comma or semicolon in your file mask, double them: ,, or ;; in the Include masks field. For example, to pick up a single file with the name: Data;Test.txt or Data,Test.txt, the value for the Include masks field must be Data;;Test.txt or Data,,Test.txt. Examples Masks: Data1.dat,Test2.txt,Data3.dat,Test4.txt // the comma is used as the mask delimiter. Files matched: Data1.dat Test2.txt Data3.dat Test4.txt Masks: Data1,,Test2.txt,Data3,,Test4.txt // double commas is resolved as a regular character , in the file names. Files matched: Data1,Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt //commas for file names are escaped in the mask. Masks: Data1;;Test2.txt;Data3,,Test4.txt,Data5;;Test6.txt Accepted files: Data1;Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt Data5;Test6.txt // the delimiters' mix is escaped. To use characters as delimiters instead of a comma or semicolon, define the property -Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.file_mask_delimiters on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) and specify the characters you need. To use the delimiter characters as regular characters in file names, double them in the Include masks field. Consider the following sample configuration behaviors for the explicit Include masks field (when wildcard characters * and ? are not used): Include masks: *.txt: the FTP inbound endpoint performs the LIST command first and iteratively matches each file name returned from the FTP server with the constraints specified in the fields: Include masks, Exclude masks, Include files with size less, Include files with size greater, Inventory Size. Files that pass all the constraints are downloaded. Include masks: Data1.dat,Test1.txt: Since the mask has explicit file names (wildcard characters * and ? are not used), the FTP inbound endpoint skips the LIST operation and attempts to download Data1.dat first, then Test1.txt from a source folder. Other fields, such as: Exclude masks, Include files with size less, Include files with size greater, Inventory Size, do not have any effect since they are used only in the LIST operation. |
| **Exclude masks** | Set a filter to exclude only those files that match your naming condition. To set a condition, use the following masks: ** : Resolved as any folder with all its subfolders. * : Resolved as any set of characters. ? : Resolved as any character. Note The Exclude masks field is case-sensitive. Example Mask: folder1/**/data?.dat Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/data1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/dataX.dat, and so on. Mask: 5010* Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/5010_837P_x222_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_837D_x224_A2.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on.   To use multiple masks simultaneously, separate masks by using a comma or semicolon without the white space character. Example Masks: folder1/**/Test?.dat;5010* Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/Test1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/TestN.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_270_x279_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. To use a comma or semicolon as regular characters in file names, double them: ,, or ;; in the Exclude masks field. For example, you want to exclude a single file with the name: Data;Test.txt or Data,Test.txt, so a required value for the Exclude masks field is Data;;Test.txt or Data,,Test.txt. Examples Masks: Data1.dat,Test2.txt,Data3.dat,Test4.txt //the comma is used as the mask delimiter. Accepted files: Data1.dat Test2.txt Data3.dat Test4.txt Masks: Data1,,Test2.txt,Data3,,Test4.txt //double commas will be resolved as a regular character , in the file names. Accepted files: Data1,Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt //commas for file names are escaped in the mask. Masks: Data1;;Test2.txt;Data3,,Test4.txt,Data5;;Test6.txt Accepted files: Data1;Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt Data5;Test6.txt // the delimiters' mix is escaped. To use other mask delimiters instead of a comma or semicolon, define the property -Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.file_mask_delimiters on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) and specify the required characters as the value. To further use these characters as regular ones in file names, double them in the Exclude masks field. |
| **Include files with size less** | Specify the maximum file size. The component picks up only files less than the size specified. You can set the file size in the following format: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. |
| **Include files with size greater** | Specify the minimum file size. The component picks up only files greater than the size specified. You can set the file size in the following format: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. |
| **Polling interval (ms)** | Set a time interval in milliseconds to scan the source folder. |
| **Inventory size** | Specify the size of the data buffer. You can set the number of files to be taken to the buffer from the source folder. |
| **Scan mode** | Select the mode to scan the source folder: Non-recursive: Scans only the directory defined as Source folder. Recursive. Recursively scans the file system starting from Source folder deep down the directory tree. |
| **Remove empty subfolders** | Select this option to remove empty subfolders under Source folder. |
| Create new missed folder | Select this option to allow XEServer creating the specified directory (Source folder) if such directory does not exist on FTP server. |
| Report error if no permission | When you enable this option, the component throws an exception for files and folders with insufficient permissions on an FTP server. When you disable this option, the component ignores the files and folders that have insufficient permissions. |



###### Connection tab


On this tab, you can specify connection parameters effective only for the current component. Component-level settings have a higher priority than the service-level settings. This tab becomes active after you select the Use connection settings on component option.


Note If you select to use component-level settings and do not provide connection settings on this tab, the service-level settings are used.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Connection Protocol** | The box displays which service protocol is selected currently. To change from current FTP service type to another, select the corresponding protocol from the drop-down menu. |
| **Host** | Enter the host name or the IP address of the server. |
| **Port** | Enter the port number to be used for the connection. |
| **User name** | Enter the name used to log in to FTP Server. |
| **Password** | Enter the password used to log in to FTP Server. |
| **Retry attempt** | The number of attempts to restart the component when a failure occurs. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| **Transfer mode** | The mode for the data transfers. Mode selection is not available for SFTP Service. |
| Enable active mode | When the active mode is enabled, the FTP Service sends the connection details to the FTP Server and the FTP Server initiates a data connection that the FTP Service accepts. By default, the data connection is established in the passive mode and initiated by the FTP Service. |



###### Security tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Server certificate store** | |
| **Type** | The type of the keystore used in the Server keystore. |
| **Path** | The keystore location. |
| **Subject** | The address of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password used to work with the key from the keystore. |
| **User certificate store** | |
| **Type** | The type of the keystore used in the User keystore. |
| **Path** | The keystore location. |
| **Subject** | The address of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password used to work with the key from the keystore. |
| **Primary key password** | The password to the private key. |
| *Connection start mode | Select the FTPS method to provide the security. The following options are available: auto - If the remote port matches the standard plain text port, the explicit mode is used. In all other cases, the implicit mode is used. explicit - XEServer requests the SSL/TLS encryption type, and then SSL/TLS negotiation is started. implicit - A deprecated mode. XEServer connects to an FTP server, and SSL negotiation is started once the connection is established. none - The communication is performed in the plain text mode. |
| *Connection security mode | Select the SSL or TLS cryptographic protocol for communication with an FTP server. |
| *Authentication mode | Select the authentication mode for the SFTP protocol. The following options are available: certificate - The authentication is performed through a private key. mixed - An FTP server verifies the user private key, and then prompts the user password. password - The authentication is performed by entering the user name and the password. none - No authentication is performed. |



*Note The marked fields are available only for FTPS and SFTP protocols.


###### Locking tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lock type | When using FTP endpoints, you may encounter a situation where several instances of XEServer are trying to use the same file. To avoid using partially processed files, a lock functionality is provided. The following options are available: **None** If the file is being used by another process, the current process performs no action on this file. If a file is not used, it is locked for the current process. **Rename** If the file is not used by another process, it is renamed and the current process is started. |

---

<a id="fs-inbound"></a>

#### FS Inbound

> 📄 Source: [FS_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FS_Inbound.htm)

##### FS Inbound


With this component, you can read files from your file system.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab Field Description Common tab **Activation mode** Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. Default command Select an action to perform when the component detects a file in the inbound location: Extract: Reads data from the file, submits the data further down the route for processing, and deletes the original file once processing is complete. Get: Reads data from the file and submits the data further down the route for further processing without deleting the file. Remove: Removes the file without reading and sending the data further down the route. **Source folder** Enter the path to the directory that should be scanned by the component. **Include masks** With these options, you can provide a file name mask to include or exclude specific files based on their name and extension. The following wildcards are supported: **: any folder with all its subfolders. * : any set of characters. ?: any character. Note The Include masks field is case-sensitive. Example Mask: folder1/**/data?.dat Files matched: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/data1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/dataX.dat, and so on. Mask: 5010* Files matched: <SourceFolder>/5010_837P_x222_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_837D_x224_A2.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. To use multiple masks simultaneously, separate masks by using a comma or semicolon without the white space character. Example Masks: folder1/**/Test?.dat;5010* Files matched: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/Test1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/TestN.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_270_x279_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. By default, the value is set to **/* that picks up files with any name in all subfolders under the source directory. To use comma or semicolon in your file mask, double them: ,, or ;; in the Include masks field. For example, to pick up a single file with the name: Data;Test.txt or Data,Test.txt, the value for the Include masks field must be Data;;Test.txt or Data,,Test.txt. Examples Masks: Data1.dat,Test2.txt,Data3.dat,Test4.txt // the comma is used as the mask delimiter. Files matched: Data1.dat Test2.txt Data3.dat Test4.txt Masks: Data1,,Test2.txt,Data3,,Test4.txt // double commas is resolved as a regular character , in the file names. Files matched: Data1,Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt //commas for file names are escaped in the mask. Masks: Data1;;Test2.txt;Data3,,Test4.txt,Data5;;Test6.txt Accepted files: Data1;Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt Data5;Test6.txt // the delimiters' mix is escaped. To use characters as delimiters instead of a comma or semicolon, define the property -Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.file_mask_delimiters on the Profile Startup Tab and specify the characters you need. To use the delimiter characters as regular characters in file names, double them in the Include masks field. **Exclude masks** Set a filter to exclude only those files that match your naming condition. To set a condition, use the following masks: ** : Resolved as any folder with all its subfolders. * : Resolved as any set of characters. ? : Resolved as any character. Note The Exclude masks field is case-sensitive. Example Mask: folder1/**/data?.dat Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/data1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/dataX.dat, and so on. Mask: 5010* Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/5010_837P_x222_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_837D_x224_A2.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on.   To use multiple masks simultaneously, separate masks by using a comma or semicolon without the white space character. Example Masks: folder1/**/Test?.dat;5010* Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/Test1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/TestN.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_270_x279_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. To use a comma or semicolon as regular characters in file names, double them: ,, or ;; in the Exclude masks field. For example, you want to exclude a single file with the name: Data;Test.txt or Data,Test.txt, so a required value for the Exclude masks field is Data;;Test.txt or Data,,Test.txt. Examples Masks: Data1.dat,Test2.txt,Data3.dat,Test4.txt //the comma is used as the mask delimiter. Accepted files: Data1.dat Test2.txt Data3.dat Test4.txt Masks: Data1,,Test2.txt,Data3,,Test4.txt //double commas will be resolved as a regular character , in the file names. Accepted files: Data1,Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt //commas for file names are escaped in the mask. Masks: Data1;;Test2.txt;Data3,,Test4.txt,Data5;;Test6.txt Accepted files: Data1;Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt Data5;Test6.txt // the delimiters' mix is escaped. To use other mask delimiters instead of a comma or semicolon, define the property -Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.file_mask_delimiters on the Profile Startup Tab and specify the required characters as the value. To further use these characters as regular ones in file names, double them in the Exclude masks field. **Include files with size less** Specify the maximum file size. The component picks up only files less than the size specified. You can set the file size in the following format: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. **Include files with size greater** Specify the minimum file size. The component picks up only files greater than the size specified. You can set the file size in the following format: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. Advanced tab Sorting parameters Mode System. Size. Data is sorted by size. Name. Data is sorted by name. Creation Time. Data is sorted by the time of creation. Note Creation time may not be available on some systems. If the file system implementation does not support a time stamp to indicate the time when the file was created then method BasicFileAttributes.creationTime() can be used to return an implementation specific value. Last Update Time. Data is sorted by the time of the last update. Last Access Time. Data is sorted by the time it was accessed for the last time. Note Last access time is disabled by default in Windows 7. Dy default, the sorting mode is set to None as sorting large numbers of files stored in a network file system (NFS) can significantly decrease the system's performance. Direction Ascending. The sort starts from the smallest or lowest value and proceeds to the largest or highest value. Descending. The sort starts with the highest and proceeds to the lowest value. Order Folders first Folders last Unspecified Traversing parameters **Polling interval (ms)** Set a time interval in milliseconds to scan the source folder. **Inventory size** Specify the size of the data buffer. You can set the number of files to be taken to the buffer from the source folder. File Limit Limit the number of files simultaneously picked up for processing within one XEServer task in the By command component activation mode. As an indication of reaching the limit, the component adds a FileLimitReached property for the original output. Once the allowed number of files is processed, the component picks up the next portion. **Scan mode** Select the mode to scan the source folder: Non-recursive: Scans only the directory defined as Source folder. Recursive. Recursively scans the file system starting from Source folder deep down the directory tree. **Remove empty subfolders** Select this option to remove empty subfolders under Source folder. **Locking tab** **Lock type** When using FS, HDFS, and FTP endpoints, you may encounter a situation where multiple processes are trying to use the same file. To avoid using partially processed files, a lock functionality is provided. By default, all processed files are added to the process memory list to be locked on a profile level, however the Lock type setting has several additional options: **none**. The prefix .open_ is prepended to the file name to lock the file for external processes. **check for stable size**. Checks if the file is currently used by another process by tracking its size and modification date changes at regular intervals (Lock timeout). This mode can be useful when working with UNIX file systems that allow multiple processes to write to a file without explicitly locking it. **rename**. If the file is used by another process, it is renamed and the current process cannot be started. For this option, files are renamed by adding corresponding prefix to their name. **check for OS lock**. Checks if the file is used by another process, sets lock if it is possible, and then removes the lock. check locking service instance: File locks are controlled by the Locking service. In Custom properties, you can specify any attributes to be added to locks as meta information (for example, a trading partner name). These additional details are added to the record of a lock in the lock storage and reported by the Expired Lock Reporter component. You can also use macros for lock type configuration. To do this: In Lock type, add the macro ${LockingType}. On the Profile environment tab, add the environment property LockingType=<locking-type>. The following values are supported: none, rename, checksize, oslock, locking.service. Cluster mode Note You must turn on this mode if you use several XEServer instances that read the same file(s). If the cluster mode is enabled, the component applies a lock (creates an empty .lock file) on the currently processed file. You should use this mode to prevent multiple XEServer instances from processing the same file concurrently. With the cluster mode enabled, the first XEServer instance to access the file fileName.** automatically creates the file *fileName.lock* when the processing begins. If another XEServer instance attempts to process the same file *fileName.**, XEServer does not create another *.lock* file with the same file name.


For more information on file locking capabilities and troubleshooting recommendations, view [File System Locking Considerations](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/File_System_Locking_Considerations.htm).


When two or more FS Inbound components running in By commandmode scan the same directory, these components may block each other. For example, the FS Inbound 1 locks the file A and attempts to lock the file B which is already locked by the FS Inbound 2. To prevent this deadlock, use the **rename** lock type. In this case, FS Inbound components treat renamed files as not existing and skip them.









###### Files recovery


If the file processing is interrupted unexpectedly (for example, due to a JVM crash or power outage), the profiles that were not properly stopped have a recovery file in their corresponding ${XESProfile} folders. On the next profile start-up, XEServer detects this recovery file (it serves as an indicator where recovery is needed) and launches its build-in mechanism that recovers the files located in the inbound / outbound directories:

- For the FS Inbound endpoints, XEServer traverses source folders and removes the .inprogress_ prefixes in file names (for example, .inprogress_data.dat becomes data.dat). The traversal is recursive or non-recursive depending on the Scan modesetting.
- For the FS Outbound endpoints, XEServer recursively traverses destination folders and removes the .backup_ prefixes in file names (for example, .backup_data.dat becomes data.dat).


If you want to disable automatic data recovery for all FS Inbound and FS Outbound endpoints, set the system property `-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.fs.startup.recovery=false` on the profile [Startup tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) or `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.fs.startup.recovery=false` on the profile [Environment tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Environment_Tab.htm). In this case, if you want XEServer to reprocess files that were under processing at the moment of crash, you will have to manually remove prefixes in file names in all source and destination folders prior to restarting XEServer profiles.


###### Container mode


If you run XEServer profile configurations in a containerized environment, read [this topic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_in_Containerized_Environments.htm) to learn about the required changes in settings.

---

<a id="hdfs-inbound"></a>

#### HDFS Inbound

> 📄 Source: [HDFS_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HDFS_Inbound.htm)

##### HDFS Inbound


With HDFS Inbound endpoint you can read data from Hadoop Distributed file system clusters. Such clusters provide distributed data storage to ensure its reliability. You can configure HDFS inbound settings on the Route component properties tab.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Activation Mode | Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Default command | Select an action to perform when the component detects a file in the inbound location: Extract: Reads data from the file, submits the data further down the route for processing, and deletes the original file once processing is complete. Get: Reads data from the file and submits the data further down the route for further processing without deleting the file. Remove: Removes the file without reading and sending the data further down the route. Note If the file processing fails during the Extract or Get action, the file is moved to the folder defined by the system property com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.fs.recovery.template or to ${XESWorkspace}/error/{R_Name}/OriginalData/{C_Name}_{T_ID}{CDT}_{MD_File_Name} if the system property is not set. In case of Remove operation failure, the file remains in the inbound location. |
| Host | Enter the host name or IP address of the Hadoop Distributed file system name node you want to connect to. Macros are available for this field. |
| Port | Enter the port number to be used for the connection. |
| Connection timeout(sec) | The timeout in milliseconds for a socket to establish connection to the server. |
| Connection retry attempts | The number of retry attempts that is made during the connection timeout to establish connection to the server. |
| Source folder | Specify a path to the Hadoop Distributed source directory. |
| Include masks | With these options, you can provide a file name mask to include or exclude specific files based on their name and extension. The following wildcards are supported: `**`: any folder with all its subfolders. `*` : any set of characters. `?`: any character. Note The Include masks field is case-sensitive. Example Mask: folder1/**/data?.dat Files matched: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/data1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/dataX.dat, and so on. Mask: 5010* Files matched: <SourceFolder>/5010_837P_x222_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_837D_x224_A2.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. To use multiple masks simultaneously, separate masks by using a comma or semicolon without the white space character. Example Masks: folder1/**/Test?.dat;5010* Files matched: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/Test1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/TestN.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_270_x279_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. By default, the value is set to `**/*` that picks up files with any name in all subfolders under the source directory. To use comma or semicolon in your file mask, double them: ,, or ;; in the Include masks field. For example, to pick up a single file with the name: Data;Test.txt or Data,Test.txt, the value for the Include masks field must be Data;;Test.txt or Data,,Test.txt. Examples Masks: Data1.dat,Test2.txt,Data3.dat,Test4.txt // the comma is used as the mask delimiter. Files matched: Data1.dat Test2.txt Data3.dat Test4.txt Masks: Data1,,Test2.txt,Data3,,Test4.txt // double commas is resolved as a regular character , in the file names. Files matched: Data1,Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt //commas for file names are escaped in the mask. Masks: Data1;;Test2.txt;Data3,,Test4.txt,Data5;;Test6.txt Accepted files: Data1;Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt Data5;Test6.txt // the delimiters' mix is escaped. To use characters as delimiters instead of a comma or semicolon, define the property -Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.file_mask_delimiters on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) and specify the characters you need. To use the delimiter characters as regular characters in file names, double them in the Include masks field. |
| Exclude masks | Set a filter to exclude only those files that match your naming condition. To set a condition, use the following masks: ** : Resolved as any folder with all its subfolders. * : Resolved as any set of characters. ? : Resolved as any character. Note The Exclude masks field is case-sensitive. Example Mask: folder1/**/data?.dat Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/data1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/dataX.dat, and so on. Mask: 5010* Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/5010_837P_x222_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_837D_x224_A2.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on.   To use multiple masks simultaneously, separate masks by using a comma or semicolon without the white space character. Example Masks: folder1/**/Test?.dat;5010* Excluded files: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/Test1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/TestN.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_270_x279_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. To use a comma or semicolon as regular characters in file names, double them: ,, or ;; in the Exclude masks field. For example, you want to exclude a single file with the name: Data;Test.txt or Data,Test.txt, so a required value for the Exclude masks field is Data;;Test.txt or Data,,Test.txt. Examples Masks: Data1.dat,Test2.txt,Data3.dat,Test4.txt //the comma is used as the mask delimiter. Accepted files: Data1.dat Test2.txt Data3.dat Test4.txt Masks: Data1,,Test2.txt,Data3,,Test4.txt //double commas will be resolved as a regular character , in the file names. Accepted files: Data1,Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt //commas for file names are escaped in the mask. Masks: Data1;;Test2.txt;Data3,,Test4.txt,Data5;;Test6.txt Accepted files: Data1;Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt Data5;Test6.txt // the delimiters' mix is escaped. To use other mask delimiters instead of a comma or semicolon, define the property -Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.file_mask_delimiters on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) and specify the required characters as the value. To further use these characters as regular ones in file names, double them in the Exclude masks field. |
| Include files with size greater | Specify the minimum file size. The component picks up only files greater than the size specified. You can set the file size in the following format: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. |
| Include files with size less | Specify the maximum file size. The component picks up only files less than the size specified. You can set the file size in the following format: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. |
| Post processing action | This option is only available for the Extract default command. You can define an action to be performed after the incoming files are processed. Select Remove to delete incoming files after sending them for further processing, or select Rename to save files with corresponding names. If file names in the destination folder are duplicated, original files are overwritten and a warning is displayed. If a file cannot be overwritten, an error is raised, and the current file is left with the .open prefix. You can select between two templates, or provide your custom one depending on the processing results. Note Leaving the template field empty results in deletion of original files. |



###### Advanced tab


| Section | Field | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  Traversing parameters | Polling interval (ms) | Specify the time interval in milliseconds between scans of the source directory. This option is only available for the Route start activation mode. |
| Scan mode | Select the mode to scan the source folder: Non-recursive: Scans only the directory defined as Source folder. Recursive. Recursively scans the file system starting from Source folder deep down the directory tree. | |
| Remove empty subfolders | Select this option to remove empty subfolders under Source folder. | |




###### Security tab


For XEServer to connect to a [secured HDFS cluster](https://hadoop.apache.org/docs/r2.10.1/hadoop-project-dist/hadoop-common/SecureMode.html), use the Security settings tab to configure the HDFS components.


To configure a secure connection between XEServer and your name node, select Enable Kerberos Authentication and specify the following parameters:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Principal | Enter the Kerberos principal name of the name node (for example, hdfs/hdfs-client@EXAMPLE.COM). |
| Keytab File | Specify the path to the Kerberos keytab file for the name node (for example, ${ECRootPath}/hdfs.client.keytab). |
| RPC Protection | XEServer interacts with the name node using the Remote Procedure Call (RPC) protocol. Select an RPC protection option: authentication: Simple authentication using Kerberos (default option). integrity: Integrity check in addition to authentication. privacy: Data encryption with symmetric keys in addition to simple authentication and integrity check. |



If you want to use Kerberos authentication on XEServer side and the [Kerberos configuration properties file](https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-1.12/doc/admin/conf_files/krb5_conf.html) is not in the default location, set the JVM property java.security.krb5.conf on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) (for example,` -Djava.security.krb5.conf=C:\Edifecs\krb5.conf`). The default locations and file names for Kerberos configuration properties are the following:

- Windows: C:\windows\krb5.ini
- Linux: /etc/krb5.conf
- Other UNIX-based operating systems: /etc/krb5/krb5.conf


###### Locking tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lock type | When using FS, HDFS, and FTP endpoints, you may encounter a situation where multiple processes are trying to use the same file. To avoid using partially processed files, a lock functionality is provided. By default, all processed files are added to the process memory list to be locked on a profile level, however the Lock type setting has several additional options: **none**. The prefix .open_ is prepended to the file name to lock the file for external processes. **check for stable size**. Verifies whether the file is being used by another process by tracking its size and modification date changes. |
| Lock timeout (ms) | If the file size is not changed on expiration of the specified timeout, the access is granted to this file. |



###### QoS Tab


The [QoS tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_QoS_Settings.htm) is only available for the By command activation mode.
