<a id="ibm-websphere-mq-inbound"></a>

#### IBM WebSphere MQ Inbound

> 📄 Source: [IBM_Websphere_MQ_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/IBM_Websphere_MQ_Inbound.htm)

##### IBM Websphere MQ Inbound


IBM MQ Inbound components allows to receive messages from IBM WebSphere MQ by using native MQ interfaces. You can configure your JMS Inbound by using Settings tab on the Route component properties tab.


*## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab Field Description **Activation mode** Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. **Service name** Select the XEServer service associated with the current component. **Destination** Provide a path to the queue of messages to be scanned. Name of the queue or topic. **Queue open options** Supported IBM MQ server options. Single value or multiple values separated by a pipe (|). **Get options** Input message options separated by a pipe (|). **Transacted** Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. **Transaction name** This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out. **Encoding** Provide the encoding of the message that the IBM MQ component receives. Options can include: UTF-16UTF-8ASCII **Group ID** The identifier that provides logical ordering of messages and enables the grouping of related messages. **Correlation ID** (Optional) Specify a unique identifier to be used in matching responses with requests. Use the {GUID} macro for XEServer to generate a unique ID automatically. **Hexadecimal format:** If the value starts with ID:, the remainder is interpreted as a hexadecimal byte string (e.g., ID:414D51205445535420202020202020207F3C896401010101*). This format is typically used when copying correlation IDs from IBM MQ tools or logs.




**Plain text format:** If no ID: prefix is present, the value is used as-is and converted to bytes using the default character encoding.




For more information, refer to the JMSCorrelationID section in *Java Message Service Specification.*






 **Message ID**



 ID of the message. ID of messages that should be extracted. It can be the exact ID, mask, or empty value. Parameter is
							used if Activation mode parameter is Route start or the received command does not have this parameter.






 **Timeout (ms)**



 Time to wait for a single message. If XEServer does not receive any new messages during this timeout then command task ends.
							Time count is only started when no messages are present in the queue.







 **Number of messages in one batch**



 Specifies the number of messages in the batch. If value is **all messages**, there is no limit on the number of messages. For JMS, this parameter works with Timeout to determine when receiving is complete.
							Batch is considered to be complete if either this number is received or the timeout is exceeded.
							The default value is **all messages**.

---

<a id="inter-route-inbound"></a>

#### Inter Route Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Inter_Route_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Inter_Route_Inbound.htm)

##### Inter Route Inbound


The XEServer Inter Route components allow you to transfer data from one route to another. The inbound and outbound Inter Route components are used in pairs and allow you to set up a unidirectional data stream (channel) between a source route and one or more target routes and transfer XEServer message exchanges with all their properties through this stream. The transfer mechanism is based on a traditional FIFO (first in, first out) queue which preserves the order of message exchanges that travel between the routes.


Note During the transition phase of XEServer messages between the routes, the data is not persisted but remains in the queue in the RAM. So, if the XEServer process terminates at the moment when there are messages in the queue, these messages are lost and cannot be recovered.


Unlike the [Subroute](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Subroute.htm) functionality, where data transferred between the routes is bound to an XEServer task, the Inter Route components exchange data in the send-and-forget mode - once the data reaches the destination route, the data has no correlation with the source route anymore.


Note Only one Inter Route Inbound component is allowed for each XEServer route.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Queue Size | The maximum size of the queue through which data travels between the routes. |

---

<a id="jms-inbound"></a>

#### JMS Inbound

> 📄 Source: [JMS_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Inbound.htm)

##### JMS Inbound


The JMS Inbound endpoint component allows to receive messages from a JMS server. The component can use the following services to connect to JMS servers:

- [JMS Apache ActiveMQ](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Apache_ActiveMQ_Service.htm)
- [JMS Generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Generic_Service.htm)
- [JMS IBM WebSphere MQ (Edifecs Context Factory)](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_IBM_WebSphere_MQ_(Edifecs_ContextFactory)_Service.htm)
- [JMS Oracle BEA WebLogic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Oracle_BEA_WebLogic_Service.htm)


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: JMS Inbound Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Activation mode** | Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| **Service name** | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| **Type** | Select the type of destination to use. Options are: Queue: To use point-to-point messaging (one consuming receiver). Topic: To use publish and subscribe messaging (one or more consuming subscribers). XEServer JMS components support [persistent/non-persistent](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/7.5?topic=features-jms-persistent-messages) queues and [non-durable](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/8.0?topic=consumers-non-durable-subscribers) topics. Non-persistent queues and non-durable topics can be used to deliver data that does not affect the main processing flow (for example, statistics, events, real-time data, or any data that can be easily restored in case of its loss during processing). In XEServer, the session expiration timeout for JMS topics is set to 30 minutes to reduce the number of opened client sessions and avoid subscriber data and metadata overflow when a profile is stopped or failed. However, if data processing is not complete within this timeout, the messages not consumed will be lost. To avoid data loss due to non-durable subscription, you can increase the session timeout to keep JMS subscription active as long as the profile is working by setting the system property `-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.jms.session.expiration=120` (120 minutes in this example) on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Startup_Tab.htm). However, if the profile is stopped unexpectedly, the messages that are not consumed will be lost. Using persistent queues ensures reliable data delivery, but performance is lower than in case of using topics and non-persistent queues. |
| **Destination** | Provide a path to a queue or topic to be scanned. |
| **JMS Selector** | (Optional) Specify message selection criteria to limit the messages delivered to this component by the JMS provider. The value must adhere to the conditional expression syntax (for example, `JMSCorrelationID = 'my_jms_correlation_id'`). For more information on selector expressions, refer to the Message Selector Syntax section in [Java Message Service Specification](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E19957-01/816-5904-10/816-5904-10.pdf). |
| **Encoding** | Provide the encoding of the message that the JMS component receives. Options can include: UTF-16UTF-8ASCII |
| **Transacted** | Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions. None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected. Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred. Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back. |
| **Transaction name** | This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out. |
| **Timeout** | The maximum time frame the consumer (JMS Inbound component) waits for receiving a single message. The precise behavior of this timeout depends on the used JMS Provider. If the component's Activation mode is set to By Command, and the component does not receive new messages during this timeout, the current XEServer route task ends. If the component's Activation mode is set to Route Start, the XEServer route task starts only when the first message is received from the queue. |
| **Number of messages in one batch** | Specifies the number of messages in the batch. If value is **all messages**, there is no limit on the number of messages. For JMS, this parameter works with Timeout to determine when receiving is complete. Batch is considered to be complete if either this number is received or the timeout is exceeded. The default value is **all messages**. |

---

<a id="kafka-inbound"></a>

#### Kafka Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Kafka_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Kafka_Inbound.htm)

##### Kafka Inbound


The Kafka Inbound component is used to connect to a Kafka cluster and subscribe to published messages.


You can configure the Kafka Inbound component by using the Settings tab.



To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Kafka).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Activation mode | Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Service name | Select the service to be used. Click View to go to the service settings page. If there are no services available on the page, click Create new to add a new service. |
| Topic | Specify the name of the topic, from which messages are pulled by consumers. |
| Group ID | Specify a string that uniquely identifies the group of consumers. This ID defines the strategy of data distribution among consumers. You need to specify different Group IDs if you need to allow two or more consumers (XEServer Kafka Inbound components) to read the same message from the Kafka cluster. If two or more consumers have the same Group ID, the message can be read by only one consumer. |

---

<a id="mft-inbound"></a>

#### MFT Inbound

> 📄 Source: [mft_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/mft_Inbound.htm)

##### MFT Inbound


The MFT Inbound component is used to retrieve files from [Edifecs Managed File Transfer (MFT) Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Welcome_to_Managed_File_Transfer.htm). The component reads all files available for the current Smart Trading Account. Each file is wrapped into XEServer message exchange and directed further down the route for processing.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#MFT). In addition to generic component settings, the component has the following settings:


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Activation mode | Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component.Note One [MFT activation code](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Set_Up_MFT_Connections.htm) can be used by one XEServer MFT Service at a time, so if you want to read and send data using different [Smart Trading Accounts](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Create_Account.htm) in the same profile, ensure you have separate XEServer MFT Service instances each using different Smart Trading Account activation codes. |
| Polling Interval (sec) | The component polls Edifecs MFT Service at regular intervals and retrieves available files, if any. Set the time interval for the component to sleep after the component retrieves the last available data. With this option, you can optimize the number of requests made to Edifecs MFT Service. |

---

<a id="parent-route-inbound"></a>

#### Parent Route Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Parent_Route_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Parent_Route_Inbound.htm)

##### Parent Route Inbound


The Parent Route Input endpoint serves as an entry point for passing the processing from a parent route to a subroute.


When configuring a Subroute data processor in the main route, a subroute must first be delineated by using Parent route endpoints. With parent route you can see all sources and destinations, which allows you to understand the whole process. Subroutes provide deeper level of processing.


###### Component Settings


This component has only [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings.

---

<a id="rabbitmq-inbound"></a>

#### RabbitMQ Inbound

> 📄 Source: [RabbitMQ_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/RabbitMQ_Inbound.htm)

##### RabbitMQ Inbound


With this component, you can consume (read) messages from RabbitMQ servers.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



To see the list of macros supported by this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#RabbitMQ).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Activation Mode | Select a way to start the component: Route start. The component starts along with the route and scans the specified source queue. This activation mode allows you to only use system macros. By command. The component starts when it receives an XEServer message. In this mode, the inbound component obtains a component input called "data" for receiving XEServer messages. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Source Queue | Enter the name of the queue to read messages from. |
| Transaction Mode | This option controls the way the component sends acknowledgments to RabbitMQ servers after reading a message from the queue. The following options are available: None: The component sends an acknowledgement to a RabbitMQ server immediately after the message has been received from the queue. Regardless of message processing results further down on a route, a positive acknowledgement is sent and the message is deleted from the RabbitMQ server. Task Completion: The component sends an acknowledgement to RabbitMQ server only after the message has been received from the queue and has been processed on the route (XEServer Task is complete). If an error occurs during the processing of the received message further down on the route, a negative acknowledgement is sent to the RabbitMQ server and the message can be received again. |
| Batch Size | Enter the number of messages the component must read from a queue as one batch. Once the component reads the specified number of messages, the component sends the messages further down the route wrapping every receivedmessage in a separate XEServer message exchange. |
| Create queue if it does not exist | Select this option to create a new queue if the specified Source Queue does not exist. |

---

<a id="restful-web-service-inbound"></a>

#### RESTful Web Service Inbound

> 📄 Source: [RESTful_Web_Service_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/RESTful_Web_Service_Inbound.htm)

##### RESTful Web Service Inbound


When a [RESTful Web Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Restful_Web_Service.htm) receives an HTTP request, the service passes the request data to the RESTful Web Service Inbound component that is linked to the service. The component maps request data to an XEServer message exchange, and sends the exchange further down the route for processing.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | The name of the RESTful Web Service used by the component. |
| Relative path | A path that serves as a RESTful endpoint. This path is relative to the base URL specified in your RESTful Web Service. |



###### Auto Response tab


On this tab you can configure the component to send automatic responses on task completion or on task failure.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Send automatic response | Enables automatic response sending. |
| Response status | Select the response status code to send. |
| Mode | Define the response body type. None: the response has no body. Form encoded: use to define key-value pairs to be sent within the response. Text: use to provide a plain text message as a response body. |



Note Only one response should be sent - either an automatic response generated by the Restful Inbound component, or the response generated by the Restful Outbound component.


###### Request Mapping tab


On this tab, you can control the mapping of HTTP requests to XEServer message exchanges. The mapping process is different for application/x-www-form-urlencoded, Multipart/form-data, and Other content types:

- application/x-www-form-urlencoded: For every request of this content type, an empty message within a message exchange is created. You can control whether the form parameters from a request body should be converted to message properties.
- Other: For every request of this type a new message within a message exchange is created. The HTTP request body is converted to XEServer message body and the HTTP request headers are stored to the message properties in the HTTPHeaderName=HTTPHeaderValue format.
- Multipart/form-data: The request mapping behavior for this content type is defined by the Mapping Mode option.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Populate message properties from | |
| HTTP headers | Select this option to convert HTTP request headers to message properties in the HTTPHeaderName=HTTPHeaderValue format. |
| Query parameters | Select this option to convert query parameters to message properties. |
| Cookies | Select this option to convert the HTTP response cookies to message properties in the CookieName=CookieValue format. |
| Form parameters | When this option is enabled, all form parameters from a request body are converted to Name=Value pairs of a message properties file. |
|  Use custom mapping for multipart request | Select this option to specify the mapping strategy for multipart requests. By default, the option is cleared and the requests are mapped to exchange messages. |
| Mapping modes | |
| Convert each multipart body to exchange message | Select this option to convert the body of an HTTP request part to an XEServer message exchange. |
| Convert each multipart body to exchange property | Select this option to convert the body of an HTTP request part to XEServer message exchange properties. The name attribute of the Content-Disposition request header is used as the name of a message exchange property. |
| Custom mode | Select this option to specify mapping mode for each message part. Click New, then enter the Part ID (the name attribute of the request header) of the HTTP request part you want to be used for mapping and select the Mapping mode for this part. If there is no part with the specified name in the request, it is not mapped. |

---

<a id="sftp-inbound"></a>

#### SFTP Inbound

> 📄 Source: [SFTP_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Inbound.htm)

##### SFTP Inbound


This component is used to download files from an FTP server using the SFTP protocol. For each file detected on the FTP server, the component creates a new XEServer message, writes the file content into the message body, and sends the message further down the route attaching [FTP message descriptors](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#FTP) to each message.


To establish a connection with an FTP server, the component uses an [SFTP Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service_Dedicated.htm). Also, the component can establish the connection directly by using the component-level connection parameters.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.



###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use connection settings on component | Enable to use the connection settings provided on the component's Connection tab. If disabled, the component uses connection settings specified on the SFTP Service. |
| Use connection pool | Select this option to enable connection pooling. Opening and closing a physical connection with an FTP server is a costly operation. With connection pooling, the component opens several connections once and then reuses the connections when needed keeping them in the pool. If disabled, the component opens a new physical connection for every message that passes through the route component. |
| **Activation mode** | Select how the component processing starts: Route start: The component starts with the route and continuously scans the source location. In this mode, only system macros are allowed. By command. The component starts once it receives a trigger XEServer message. In this mode, the component gets an extra input for receiving the trigger message. This activation mode allows you to use route and system macros. |
| Default command | Available only for the By command activation mode.Select an action to perform when the component detects a file in the inbound location:Extract: Reads data from the file, submits the data further down the route for processing, and deletes the original file once processing is complete.Get: Reads data from the file and submits the data further down the route for further processing without deleting the file.Remove: Removes the file without reading and sending the data further down the route.Note If the file processing fails during the Extract or Get action, the file is moved to the folder defined by the system property com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.fs.recovery.template or to ${XESWorkspace}/error/{R_Name}/OriginalData/{C_Name}_{T_ID}{CDT}_{MD_File_Name} if the system property is not set. In case of Remove operation failure, the file remains in the inbound location. |
| **Service name** | Select an [SFTP service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service_Dedicated.htm) to be used by the component. The service is responsible for establishing a physical connection with an FTP server (unless the component is configured to use component-level connection settings). |
| **Source folder** | The path to the source folder to scan. |
| **Include masks**,**Exclude masks** | With these options, you can provide a file name mask to include or exclude specific files based on their name and extension. The following wildcards are supported: `**`: any folder with all its subfolders. `*` : any set of characters. `?`: any character. Note The Include masks field is case-sensitive. Example Mask: folder1/**/data?.dat Files matched: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/data1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/dataX.dat, and so on. Mask: 5010* Files matched: <SourceFolder>/5010_837P_x222_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_837D_x224_A2.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. To use multiple masks simultaneously, separate masks by using a comma or semicolon without the white space character. Example Masks: folder1/**/Test?.dat;5010* Files matched: <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/Test1.dat, <SourceFolder>/folder1/<AnySubfolder>/<AnySubfolder>/TestN.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010_270_x279_A1.dat, <SourceFolder>/5010.txt, and so on. By default, the value is set to `**/*` that picks up files with any name in all subfolders under the source directory. To use comma or semicolon in your file mask, double them: ,, or ;; in the Include masks field. For example, to pick up a single file with the name: Data;Test.txt or Data,Test.txt, the value for the Include masks field must be Data;;Test.txt or Data,,Test.txt. Examples Masks: Data1.dat,Test2.txt,Data3.dat,Test4.txt // the comma is used as the mask delimiter. Files matched: Data1.dat Test2.txt Data3.dat Test4.txt Masks: Data1,,Test2.txt,Data3,,Test4.txt // double commas is resolved as a regular character , in the file names. Files matched: Data1,Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt //commas for file names are escaped in the mask. Masks: Data1;;Test2.txt;Data3,,Test4.txt,Data5;;Test6.txt Accepted files: Data1;Test2.txt Data3,Test4.txt Data5;Test6.txt // the delimiters' mix is escaped. To use characters as delimiters instead of a comma or semicolon, define the property -Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.file_mask_delimiters on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) and specify the characters you need. To use the delimiter characters as regular characters in file names, double them in the Include masks field. Consider the following behavior for the explicit Include masks field (when the wildcard characters * and ? are not used): Include masks: *.txt: the FTP inbound component performs the LIST command first and iteratively matches each file name returned from the FTP server with the constraints specified in the fields Include masks, Exclude masks, Include files with size less, Include files with size greater, Inventory Size. Files that pass all the constraints are downloaded. Include masks: Data1.dat,Test1.txt: Since the mask has explicit file names (wildcard characters * and ? are not used), the FTP inbound endpoint skips the LIST operation and attempts to download Data1.dat first, then Test1.txt from a source folder. Other fields, such as Exclude masks, Include files with size less, Include files with size greater, Inventory Size, do not have any effect since they are used only in the LIST operation. |
| **Include files with size less** | Specify the maximum file size. The component picks up only files less than the size specified. You can set the file size in the following format: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. |
| **Include files with size greater** | Specify the minimum file size. The component picks up only files greater than the size specified. You can set the file size in the following format: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. |
| **Polling interval (ms)** | Set a time interval in milliseconds to scan the source folder. |
| **Inventory size** | Specify the size of the data buffer. You can set the number of files to be taken to the buffer from the source folder. |
| **Scan mode** | Select the mode to scan the source folder: Non-recursive: Scans only the directory defined as Source folder. Recursive. Recursively scans the file system starting from Source folder deep down the directory tree. |
| **Remove empty subfolders** | Select this option to remove empty subfolders under Source folder. |
| Create new missed folder | Select this option to allow XEServer to create Source folder and other directories if they do not exist on the FTP server. |
| Report error if no permission | When enabled, the component throws an exception if XEServer has insufficient permissions for files and folders on the FTP server. When disabled, the component ignores files and folders that have insufficient permissions. |



###### Connection tab


On this tab, you can specify connection parameters effective only for the current SFTP component. Component-level settings have a higher priority than the service-level settings. This tab becomes active after you select the Use connection settings on component option.


Note If you select to use component-level settings and do not provide connection settings on this tab, the service-level settings are used.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Host** | Enter the host name or the IP address of the server. |
| **Port** | Enter the port number to be used for the connection. |
| **User name** | Enter a user name to log in to the FTP server. |
| **Password** | Enter a password to log in to the FTP server. |
| **Retry attempt** | Specify the number of retries in case a connection with the FTP server cannot be established. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| Waiting time threshold | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) for the service to wait until an FTP server completes a requested operation. If the FTP server does not acknowledge that the operation has been completed within this timeout, the service interrupts the current operation and closes the connection with the FTP server. If the value is set to 0, the service does not interrupt the operation and waits until the FTP server returns the operation completion response. |



###### Security tab


Note SSH keys must be [generated](https://www.thedigitalcatonline.com/blog/2018/04/25/rsa-keys/#generating-key-pairs-with-openssh) using RSA key generation algorithm.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Server certificate store A server certificate store acts as a truststore - it holds certificates of FTP servers to which XEServer can connect. If you do not specify a certificate store, XEServer can connect to any FTP server. | |
| **Type** | The type of the keystore used as a server certificate store. |
| **Path** | The keystore location. |
| **Subject** | The location of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password used to access the keystore. |
| User certificate store A user certificate store acts as a regular keystore - it holds a private key used to connect to secured FTP server. | |
| **Type** | The type of the keystore used as a user certificate store. |
| **Path** | The keystore location. |
| **Subject** | The location of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password used to access the keystore. |
| **Primary key password** | The password to the private key. |
| **Authentication mode** | Select the type of authentication for XEServer to use when connecting to an FTP server. None. No authentication. The User value is ignored, and XEServer attempts to log in anonymously. Certificate. XEServer uses the User and Certificate values to authenticate the connection. The Certificate must have a private key available for this authentication method to succeed. Mixed. Allows XEServer to perform a multi-step authentication process. The client sends authentication data to the server based on the list of methods allowed for the current user and the authentication property values supplied. The client continues to send authentication data until the server acknowledges authentication success. If the server rejects an authentication step, an exception is thrown. Password. XEServer uses the User and Password values to authenticate the user session. |



###### Locking tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lock type | When working with FTP servers, you may encounter a situation where several instances of XEServer are trying to use the same file on the FTP server. To avoid using partially processed files, a lock functionality is provided. The following options are available: **None** If a file is used by an XEServer instance, current XEServer instance performs no action on this file. If a file is not used, XEServer locks the file. **Rename** If the file is not used by an XEServer instance, XEServer renames the file and starts processing the file. |



###### Manage SFTP Algorithms


You can enable (disable) cipher, key exchange, message authentication code (MAC), compressions, and signatures algorithms used by the SFTP Service to work with the SFTP protocol. For this, you have to define the supported algorithms as system properties on the [profile Startup tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeserver/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) or on the [global Startup tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeserver/9.4/Content/Startup_Tab.htm) for the [FTP Server Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeconnect/9.4/Content/Manage_SFTP_Algorithms.htm) and SFTP Inbound / SFTP Outbound components.


The properties are the following:

- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.ciphers
- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.mac
- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.kex
- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.compressions
- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.signatures


The following algorithms are supported by the SFTP Service:


Ciphers: none, 3des-cbc, aes128-ctr (default), aes192-ctr (default), aes256-ctr (default), aes128-cbc (default), aes192-cbc (default), aes256-cbc (default), aes128-gcm@openssh.com (default), aes256-gcm@openssh.com (default), arcfour128, arcfour256, blowfish-cbc, chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com (default)


MACs: hmac-md5, hmac-md5-96, hmac-sha1 (default), hmac-sha1-etm@openssh.com (default), hmac-sha1-96, hmac-sha2-256 (default), hmac-sha2-256-etm@openssh.com (default), hmac-sha2-512 (default), hmac-sha2-512-etm@openssh.com (default)


Key exchange algorithms: curve25519-sha256 (default), curve25519-sha256@libssh.org (default), curve448-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group1-sha1, diffie-hellman-group14-sha1, diffie-hellman-group14-sha256 (default), diffie-hellman-group15-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group16-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group17-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group18-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1, diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha256 (default), ecdh-sha2-nistp256 (default), ecdh-sha2-nistp384 (default), ecdh-sha2-nistp521 (default)


Compressions: none (default), zlib, zlib@openssh.com


Signatures: ssh-dss, ssh-dss-cert-v01@openssh.com, ssh-rsa (default), ssh-rsa-cert-v01@openssh.com, rsa-sha2-256 (default), rsa-sha2-256-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), rsa-sha2-512 (default), rsa-sha2-512-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp256 (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp256-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp384 (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp384-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp521 (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp521-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com (default), ssh-ed25519 (default), ssh-ed25519-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), sk-ssh-ed25519@openssh.com (default)


To define the algorithms to be used:

1. On the [Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeserver/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) tab of the profile with the SFTP Service or on the global [Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeserver/9.4/Content/Startup_Tab.htm) tab, set a system property with the values of algorithms you want to be used as on the example below:
2. Save the profile configuration.
3. Restart the profile.


To check which algorithms are enabled:

1. On the [Logger](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xeserver/Content/Logger_Tab.htm) tab of the profile with the SFTP Service, expand Advanced logger configuration.
2. In the Logger Name column, select FTPClient.
3. In the Log Level column, select Debug from the drop-down list.
4. Save the profile configuration and restart the profile.
5. On the System tab, expand your profile and double-click Log Viewer.
6. On the Outline tab, select \xes.ftp.client.log.
7. View the lists of enabled algorithms on the Log Viewer tab as on the example below:

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


Example




```
...
| List of selected ciphers: chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com,aes128-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes256-ctr,aes128-gcm@openssh.com,aes256-gcm@openssh.com,aes128-cbc,aes192-cbc,aes256-cbc
...
| List of selected KEX algorithms: curve25519-sha256,curve25519-sha256@libssh.org,curve448-sha512,ecdh-sha2-nistp521,ecdh-sha2-nistp384,ecdh-sha2-nistp256,diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha256,diffie-hellman-group18-sha512,diffie-hellman-group17-sha512,diffie-hellman-group16-sha512,diffie-hellman-group15-sha512,diffie-hellman-group14-sha256
...
| List of selected MAC algorithms: hmac-sha2-256-etm@openssh.com,hmac-sha2-512-etm@openssh.com,hmac-sha1-etm@openssh.com,hmac-sha2-256,hmac-sha2-512,hmac-sha1
...
| List of selected compression algorithms: none,zlib,zlib@openssh.com
...
| List of selected signature algorithms: ecdsa-sha2-nistp256-cert-v01@openssh.com,ecdsa-sha2-nistp384-cert-v01@openssh.com,ecdsa-sha2-nistp521-cert-v01@openssh.com,rsa-sha2-512-cert-v01@openssh.com,rsa-sha2-256-cert-v01@openssh.com,ecdsa-sha2-nistp256,ecdsa-sha2-nistp384,ecdsa-sha2-nistp521,sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com,rsa-sha2-512,rsa-sha2-256,ssh-rsa
```






Note Only the algorithms that are considered safe to use are enabled by default. To find which algorithms have been deprecated, refer to the [Apache MINA SSHD Documentation](https://github.com/apache/mina-sshd#implementedavailable-support). If you still want to include deprecated algorithms, you can define them using system properties.

<a id="web-service-inbound"></a>

#### Web Service Inbound

> 📄 Source: [Web_Service_Inbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Web_Service_Inbound.htm)

##### Web Service Inbound


The Web Service inbound endpoint uses the XEServer Web Service to receive data from a client application that is located outside of XEServer. Once received, this data can then be processed by any route within XEServer. The Web service that is used by this component must be the XEServer Web Service.


*[Image: Web Service Inbound Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


The following fields are available for the Web Service Inbound Endpoint component:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service Name** | The name of the XEServer Web Service. |
| **Full URL** | The URL for the XEServer Web Service. For example: http://localhost:9080/ws?wsdl. This setting is read-only. |



###### Outputs tab


The following output option is available on the [Outputs tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Components_Output_Properties.htm) for the inbound Web Service endpoint:


| Output | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **data** | Data is a routing component error output that streams data to another component in the route. |

---

<a id="amazon-s3-outbound"></a>

#### Amazon S3 Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Amazon_S3_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_S3_Outbound.htm)

##### Amazon S3 Outbound


With this component, you can upload data to the Amazon S3 storage. The component uses the [Amazon S3 Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_S3_Service.htm) to connect to an Amazon S3 server.


Note Use of this component requires an XEServer Module for AWS license. Contact [Edifecs Customer Support](https://support.edifecs.com/) to obtain the XEServer Module for AWS license.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



To see the list of macros supported by this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#AmazonS3).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Service Name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. | |
| Bucket Name | Enter the name of the bucket to upload data to. Click Load Buckets to fetch the list of available buckets. | |
| Object Key | Enter the object key. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. When you create an object, you specify the key name that uniquely identifies the object in the bucket. The following are examples of valid object keys: Development/Projects1.xls videos/2014/birthday/video1.wmv s3-dg.pdf For naming conventions and best practices, see [Object Key Naming Guidelines](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/UsingMetadata.html). Amazon S3 data model has a flat structure: you create a bucket, and the bucket stores the objects. There is no hierarchy of subbuckets or subdirectories like in traditional file systems. However, you can apply and infer logical hierarchy using key name prefixes and delimiters. See the following example: photos/2006/January/ | sample.jpg |
| | | |
| Object prefix | object name | |



This is an example of an object key whose prefix implicitly positions the corresponding object within the implicitly applied hierarchy. Using object key prefixes, you can group objects in Amazon S3 Storage in the same way as you would group files on your file system by putting them in the same directory. Key prefixes can be extremely useful when you retrieve objects from the storage: you can specify a key prefix and retrieve only those keys that match this prefix.






 If Object Exists




Select the action to perform if the target bucket already contains an object with the specified Object Key. The following options are available:

- error: The component reports an error and the data are not uploaded to the bucket.
- overwrite: The existing object is replaced with a new one.
- skip: The component does not upload the data and the route processing continues after the corresponding record is added to the log file.








Tagging


In this pane, you can set [Amazon Object Tags](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/object-tagging.html). Amazon S3 Tags are key-value pairs. Attaching a tag to the uploaded data allows you to categorize this data, and easily identify this data in the future. Take into consideration the following requirements when you add a tag:

- One object can have up to 10 tags associated with it.
- Tag keys can be up to 80 Unicode characters in length.
- Tag values can be up to 256 Unicode characters in length.
- Tag keys and values are case-sensitive.









###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |



Use this tab to store metadata associated with Amazon S3 objects. For more information about Amazon S3 object metadata, see [official documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/UsingMetadata.html).


###### Configure and Manage Checksum Algorithms for Amazon S3


Amazon S3 SDK supports response checksum validation to ensure the integrity of objects during download. This section explains how the SDK performs checksum validation and how to configure its behavior using system properties, environment variables, or AWS profile settings.


###### How Checksum Validation Works?


Amazon S3 SDK ensures data integrity by using checksums during both upload and download:

- Upload: The SDK calculates a checksum and stores it in the object’s metadata.
- Download: The SDK recalculates the checksum of the downloaded object and compares it with the stored value to verify that the object has not been altered.


###### Response Checksum Validation


You can control whether the SDK validates checksums during download using one of the following configuration options:


| Configuration Method | Setting Name | Priority |
| --- | --- | --- |
| System Property | aws.responseChecksumValidation | 1 |
| Environment Variable | AWS_RESPONSE_CHECKSUM_VALIDATION | 2 |
| AWS Profile Setting | response_checksum_validation | 3 |



By default, response checksum validation is enabled. To disable it, set any of the above to "WHEN_REQUIRED".

---

<a id="amazon-sqs-outbound"></a>

#### Amazon SQS Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Amazon_SQS_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Amazon_SQS_Outbound.htm)

##### Amazon SQS Outbound


With this component, you can send messages to Amazon SQS queues.


Note Use of this component requires an XEServer Module for AWS license. Contact [Edifecs Customer Support](https://support.edifecs.com/) to obtain the XEServer Module for AWS license.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



To see the list of macros supported by this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#AmazonSQS).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service Name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Queue type | Select the type of queue to which you want to send messages. Amazon SQS supports two types of queues: **Standard** (At-least-once Delivery) - Offers maximum throughput and does not retain the order of received messages. **FIFO** (First-In-First-Out Delivery) - Available in a limited number of regions: US East (N. Virginia), US East (Ohio), US West (Oregon), and EU (Ireland). Retains the order of incoming messages at the cost of limited throughput (300 transactions for every second). **Note** If the queue type is defined as **FIFO**, no need to include “.fifo” suffix in the **Queue name** field – it will be added dynamically in runtime. |
| Queue name | Enter the name of the queue you want to send messages to. Click Refresh to refresh the list of available queues. A queue name should adhere to the following requirements: Only alphanumeric characters, hyphens ('-'), and underscores ('_'). The maximum name length is 80 characters. Should be case-sensitive. **Note** When **Queue type**=**FIFO**, there is no need to add the ".fifo" suffix to the name of the queue. It will be added dynamically in runtime. To create a new queue, click New. For more information, see [Create a new Amazon Queue](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Queue.htm). |
| Message type | Select an option to send your message contents as plain text or as raw bytes. |
| Message encoding | Select the encoding of messages to be sent to the queue. |
| Message Group ID | This parameter is required for FIFO queues to combine messages into groups. The FIFO discipline is observed within the scope of a single group. So, messages that belong to the same group are processed in a strict order; however messages from different groups may be processed out of order. We recommend that you use a constant value for this field in order to reproduce the FIFO behavior. |
| Message Deduplication ID | This parameter is used to identify duplicate messages sent to a queue. The parameter is mandatory when you work with queues with disabled Content-Based Deduplication. For other cases, the parameter is optional. If you send a message with the Deduplication ID that was already used, the queue suspends this message for 5 minutes before exposing it to consumers. When working with FIFO Amazon queues, at least one of the following requirements must be met: The queue has Content-Based Deduplication enabled Message Deduplication ID is specified If both requirements are fulfilled, duplicate identification is carried out using Message Deduplication ID. We recommend that you use Message Deduplication ID as a way to identify duplicates preferably to Content-Based Deduplication since the latter may perform poorly during processing (hashing efforts on Amazon SQS side). |



###### Advanced tab


On this tab, you can configure the component's behavior if the queue with a specified name is not available. If Create Queue Automatically is selected and no queue with the specified name is found, the component creates a new queue using the following settings:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Queue settings | |
| Visibility Timeout | When a consumer receives and processes a message from a queue, the message remains in the queue and is not deleted for some time (In Amazon terminology, the message acquires the [Inflight status](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-visibility-timeout.html#sqs-inflight-messages)). Amazon SQS does not automatically delete such messages as there is no guarantee that a consumer can complete message receipt and processing within the specified time (the connection can break or the consumer can fail to process the message). Therefore, the consumer is responsible for deleting the message from the queue after the message receipt and processing are complete. The maximum value can be 43200 seconds (12 hours). After a message is received, it remains in the queue. To prevent other consumers from processing the message again, Amazon SQS uses the Visibility Timeout property that is a period of time during which Amazon SQS prevents other consumers from receiving and processing the current message. For a better understanding of how Amazon queues work, see [Amazon SQS message life cycle](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-message-lifecycle.html). |
| Message Retention period | The maximum time frame during which a message can be kept in a queue. After expiration, the message is automatically deleted from the queue. Supported values range: 60 seconds - 1,209,600 seconds (14 days). For more information, see [official documentation](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-message-lifecycle.html). |
| Maximum Message Size | The maximum size of messages for the queue. Supported values: 1KB - 256KB. Note XEServer converts message exchange properties to [Amazon message attributes](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-message-attributes.html) and sends them together with the message body to Amazon queues. The 256 KB limit is applied to the whole message including message body and attributes. |
| Delivery delay | The delay between the moment when a message is added to the queue and the moment this message becomes available for reading by customers. This parameter allows you to postpone message delivery by 0 to 900 seconds. |
| Receive Message Wait Time | The time during which an empty queue waits for new available messages. If no new messages get to the empty queue during this timeout, the queue sends an empty response. |
| Content-Based Deduplication | Select this option to detect message duplication based on message body hash. If a queue has the Content-Based Deduplication enabled and Message Deduplication ID is used, duplicate identification is performed using Message Deduplication ID. |
| Dead Letter Queue Settings A dead letter queue is an ordinary messaging queue that stores erroneous (failed to be consumed, with exceeded size limit, and so on) messages from other queues. You can bind one or more queues to a single dead letter queue. Typically, multiple queues are bound to a single dead message queue that accumulates failed messages from the bound queues. | |
| Queue Name | Select this option if you want to bind a queue automatically to a particular dead letter queue. |
| Dead Letter Queue | Enter the name of the dead letter queue to which erroneous data is reported. |
| Maximum Message Retrieved | Set the maximum allowed number of unsuccessful attempts to read a message from a queue before this message is redirected to a dead letter queue. |




###### Message Attributes


Due to certain [limitations](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-message-metadata.html) on the Amazon SQS side, such as limit of 10 attribute per message, the XEServer Amazon SQS components do not support reading arbitrary attributes from the SQS by default.


Instead, the attributes are handled as follows:

1. The SQS Outbound component serializes all XES message attributes in JSON format and puts them in a single SQS Message attribute Properties.
2. The SQS Inbound component reads the SQS message attribute Properties and deserializes the JSON value into XES message attributes.


You can explicitly specify the attributes to be added without being serialized to the SQS message and to be read from the message. To do this, add the amazon.sqs.message.properties.attributes parameter with the attributes name(s) on the [Profile Environment](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Environment_Tab.htm) tab (for example, amazon.sqs.message.properties.attributes=Attribute1,Attribute2,Attribute3), or on the [Profile Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) tab (for example, -Damazon.sqs.message.properties.attributes=Attribute1,Attribute2,Attribute3).

---

<a id="azure-blob-storage-outbound"></a>

#### Azure Blob Storage Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Azure_Blob_Storage_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Blob_Storage_Outbound.htm)

##### Azure Blob Storage Outbound


With this component, you can write [block blobs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/understanding-block-blobs--append-blobs--and-page-blobs#about-block-blobs) and [append blobs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/understanding-block-blobs--append-blobs--and-page-blobs#about-append-blobs) to Azure Blob storage, as well as create new blobs in a container. The component uses the [Azure Connection Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Connectivity_Overview.htm) to connect to an Azure server.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration Tab


| Field | Description | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. | |
| Container name | Select the name of your [blob container](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/storage-blobs-introduction#containers) to upload blobs to. Click to fetch the list of containers available in the Azure storage account you specified in the [Azure Connection Service settings](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Connection_Service.htm#Account_name). | |
| Blob prefix | Enter the blob prefix that uniquely identifies the blob in a container. If the blob with this name does not exist in the container, the component creates this blob with the Blob type you select. The following are examples of valid blob prefixes: Development/Projects1.xls videos/2014/birthday s3-dg.pdf For naming conventions and best practices, see [Introduction to Azure Blob storage](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/storage-blobs-introduction#blobs). The Azure Blob Storage data model has a flat structure: you create a container, and the container stores blobs. There is no hierarchy of subcontainers or subdirectories like in traditional file systems. However, you can infer logical hierarchy using blob name prefixes and delimiters. See the following example: photos/2006/January/ | sample.jpg |
| Blob prefix |   | |



This is an example of a blob name whose prefix implicitly positions the corresponding blob within the implicitly inferred hierarchy. Using blob prefixes, you can group blobs in Azure Blob Storage in the same way as you would group files on your file system by putting them in the same directory. Blob prefixes can be extremely useful when you retrieve blobs from the storage: you can specify a blob prefix and retrieve only those blobs that match this prefix.






 Blob type


 Select the blob type to be applied to the blob (either BlockBlob or AppendBlob). The blob type you choose also determines the Conflict resolution options.




 Conflict resolution




Select the conflict resolution strategy if the target container already has a blob with the specified Blob prefix.


For the AppendBlob type, the component adds data to the end of the existing blob.


For the BlockBlob type, the following options are available:

- Error: The component reports an error and the blob is not uploaded to the container.
- Overwrite: The existing blob is replaced with a new one.
- Skip: The component does not upload the blob and the route processing continues after the corresponding record is added to the log file.









###### Advanced Tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lease lock | When the component writes data to a blob of an AppendBlob type, it acquires a [lease](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/lease-blob) against the blob to prevent this blob from modification by other clients. You can disable this option to allow other clients to modify this blob when the component is writing to it. |
| Lock renew interval | When the component is writing to an append blob, this blob becomes locked for other clients during the time you specify in the Lock duration parameter. Specify the Lock renew interval at which the blob status is checked. If the blob is still locked, the lease lock time is prolonged (that is, the lock duration time starts from the beginning). |
| Lock duration | Specify for how long a blob remains locked for other clients when the component is writing to the blob. The possible value can be from 15 to 60 seconds. |
| Tagging In this pane, you can set [Azure blob index tags](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/storage-blob-index-how-to?tabs=azure-portal) in form of key-value pairs. Attaching a tag to the uploaded data allows you to categorize this data in your storage account and easily identify this data in the future. Refer to the [official Microsoft Azure documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/set-blob-tags#request-body) for the requirements you should follow when adding blob index tags. | |



###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |



The attribute parameters you add for a blob are passed as metadata headers in the format `x-ms-meta-name:string-value`. For more information on Azure blob metadata, refer to the [official Microsoft Azure documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/setting-and-retrieving-properties-and-metadata-for-blob-resources).

---

<a id="azure-queue-storage-outbound"></a>

#### Azure Queue Storage Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Azure_Queue_Storage_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Queue_Storage_Outbound.htm)

##### Azure Queue Storage Outbound


With this component, you can send messages to [Azure Queue Storage](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/queues/storage-queues-introduction).


By design, an Azure Queue storage message contains only a body with the actual message contents and do not carry any message attributes with it. Edifecs developed a proprietary message format that allows XEServer to embed message properties into the Azure message body when sending it to an Azure queue and to parse these attributes when [reading](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Queue_Storage_Inbound.htm) a message of this format.


Note Since Azure Queue storage message cannot exceed 64 KB, keep in mind that this limit is applied to the whole message, including the body and attributes, and that the message size is [increased](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Base64#encoded_size_increase) if you enable message encoding. If your messages cannot be uploaded to Azure Queue storage due to their size, consider adding the [Zip Component](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Zip_Components.htm) to the route that would compress messages before sending them to the Azure Queue Storage Outbound component.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Queue name | From the list of queues [created](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/queues/storage-quickstart-queues-portal#create-a-queue) in your Azure Storage account, specify the queue the component should send messages to. Click to fetch the list of queues available in your account (maximum 50 items are displayed). |
| Message format | Select the way the component should map an XEServer message with an Azure Queue Storage message. RAW: The component maps XEServer message body with Azure Queue message body as is. XEServer message attributes are ignored as not supported in Azure Queue messages. EDIFECS: The component transfers XEServer message body along with XEServer message attributes that you can include/exclude on the Attributes tab. The format can be represented as follows:[Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)EDIFECS message example ``` EDFXAAAAHw=={"color":"green","form":"oval"}success ``` where: `EDFX`: The format indicator occupying the first four bytes in a message. `AAAAHw==`: The Base64 encoded value of a four-byte integer that indicates the size of message attributes in bytes. In the example above, the length of JSON serialized string {"color":"green","form":"oval"} is 31, which corresponds to the four-byte 0x0000001F integer. The result of the 0x0000001F integer encoding is AAAAHw==, which occupies eight bytes. `{"color":"green","form":"oval"}`: The JSON serialized message attributes in UTF-8 encoding. The length of these attributes is indicated by the Base64 encoded value AAAAHw==. `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><my-transaction>success</my-transaction>`: The XEServer message body. |
| Message encoding | Select NONE or BASE64 to specify whether the component should encode messages before sending them to the queue. Keep in mind the following: Binary data and text messages containing special symbols must be encoded. Base64 encoding [increases](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Base64#encoded_size_increase) the data size. Make sure to [specify](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Azure_Queue_Storage_Inbound.htm#Encoding) the same encoding option in the Azure Queue Storage Inbound component settings for the messages to be consumed correctly. |
| Time to live | Define the expiration time for a message in the queue. If set to -1, the message never expires. |
| Visibility timeout | Define when a message should become visible to other consumers after the component adds the message to the queue. If set to 0 or -1, the message is visible immediately. |



###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |



Note If you want to add metadata to messages, select the EDIFECS format for messages to be sent. Otherwise, XEServer message attributes will be ignored as not supported in Azure Queue messages.

---

<a id="archival-service-outbound"></a>

#### Archival Service Outbound

> 📄 Source: [archival_Service_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/archival_Service_Outbound.htm)

##### Archival Service Outbound


With this component, you can upload data from XEServer routes to [Edifecs Archival Service](https://archive.edifecsfedcloud.com/). The component uses the XEServer [MFT](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MFT_Service.htm) service to establish a connection to the MFT Service server.


Note One [MFT activation code](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Set_Up_MFT_Connections.htm) can be used by one XEServer MFT service at a time, so if you want to read and send data using different [Smart Trading Accounts](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Create_Account.htm) in the same profile, ensure you have separate XEServer MFT service instances each using a different activation code.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service Name | The name of the XEServer MFT service associated with the current component. For more information on how to create a new service, see [Add a New Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Service.htm). |
| File Path | The name for the data file that is uploaded to the cloud. |



###### Metadata tab


On this tab, you can specify [MFT Transmission metadata](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Metadata_Exchange.htm). These are key-value pairs that can be utilized by the MFT Service.

---

<a id="bcounter-outbound"></a>

#### BCounter Outbound

> 📄 Source: [BCounter_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/BCounter_Outbound.htm)

##### BCounter Outbound


BCounter Outbound Endpoint is a route component that updates the counters. You can set BCounter Outbound properties on the Route component properties tab.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | The name of the service to be used. If none exists, click Create new. Click View to move to the Service settings tab. |
| Counter group | The name of the counter group to be used. |
| Counter ID | The IDs of the counters to be used. ID can be either auto-generated if this is the first time counter is incremented. Or some meaningful value that can be used for consequent counter increments (such as Task ID) must be used. Macros are supported for this field. |



Transacted functionality regulates when the BCounter Outbound component updates the information about the counter. If the check box is selected, the database is only updated after the successful completion of the task.


###### Commands


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Field Name | The name of the counter field to be incremented. |
| Increment | The value on which the counter is incremented. |



###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |
