<a id="batcher-outbound"></a>

#### Batcher Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Batcher_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Batcher_Outbound.htm)

##### Batcher Outbound


The Batcher Outbound component is designed to accept messages and determine which Batch Group they should be added to. With this component, you can change the Batcher Completion Strategy to be used during data processing.


Note Only one Batcher Outbound component on one profile must handle data items for the same channel.

 Click to see the processing diagram.


*[Image: Batcher Inbound Processing diagram]*






When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Displays the name of the service to be used. If none exists, click Create new. Click View to move to the Service settings tab. |
| Batcher Channel ID | Displays the ID of the Channel to which the data item is sent. |
| Batch Group Correlation ID | Displays the data item associated with the Batch Group according to the correlation ID. |



###### Advanced tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Message is not a control message | If this option is selected, the data is handled according to the completion strategy of the currently used Batcher configuration. |
| Update completion strategy | If this option is selected, the data is handled according to the completion strategy specified on the component. |
| Control message carries data | If this option is selected, the data file is uploaded and the Batcher Completion Strategy is updated. Otherwise, the strategy is updated without data upload. |
| Completion criteria | Displays the completion criteria. The following options are available:Size Timestamp Immediate |
| Completion Settings | |
| Completion datetime | Displays the completion datetime. This property contains macro expression. |
| Datetime format | Enables setting the date/time format. |
| Completion size | Specifies the number of items to be received before the Batch Group is finished processing. |



###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |

---

<a id="data-storage-outbound"></a>

#### Data Storage Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Data_Storage_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Data_Storage_Outbound.htm)

##### Data Storage Outbound


The component allows you to write message to a data storage.


*[Image: Data Storage Outbound Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service name** | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| **Destination path** | Provide path where you want to store the resulted data. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| **ID** | ID of messages that should be extracted. It can be exact id, mask, or empty value. Parameter is used if Activation mode parameter is Route start or the received command does not have this parameter. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Expiration timeout | Set the expiration timeout for items sent through this component. After the timeout expires, items become no longer available in the data storage. By default, the value is set to -1 which means that items never expire. To activate the purging mechanism of your storage, ensure that the following requirements are met: The Data Storage Outbound component's Expiration timeout is set to a positive value. The Expiry Control parameter of the Data Storage service is set to true. |
| Write only the properties file to data storage | Use this option to only store the properties file in data storage. |

---

<a id="email-outbound"></a>

#### Email Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Email_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Email_Outbound.htm)

##### Email Outbound


The Email Outbound Component is used to send the data about processed messages directly to an email address.


*[Image: Email Outbound Settings tab]*


To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Restful).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| To | Provide an email address of the recipient. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| CC | You can optionally provide other email addresses for other people to get copy of this email. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| BCC | Provide email address of people to get blind copy of this email. |
| From | Provide your email address. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Reply to | Add other people email addresses to include when replying. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Subject | Provide the subject of your email. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space.You can use a macro in this field. For example, the {M_SID} macro puts the message sequential id into the subject field. |
| Message body | Click Browse and provide message text or use macros to be resolved as message text. |
| Content type | Specify the content type for the message body:text/plain: the message body content is plain text;text/html: the message body is in HTML formal.This field also supports macros. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Send each message in separate e-mail | In case of receiving multimessage exchange all messages are sent out in one email (as attachments or in body).Note If you choose to send all messages in one email, the selected content type will only be applied to the first message. The rest of the messages will be in plain text format. |



###### Attachment tab


You can configure your email message to be sent as an attachment or you can set message properties to be sent in the file attached.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Send message as attachment | Click Yes, if you want your message to be sent as a file attached to the email message. |
| Send message properties as attachment | Click Yes, if you want a message properties file to be sent as a file attached to the email message. |
| Attachment name template | Use macro to provide the attachment name template. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |



###### Server Options tab


By using the Server options tab, you can provide server options to enable the Email Outbound component.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Connection security | Select a security type for your connection. The following types are available: None: A plain connection type is used. SSL/TLS: A TLS or SSL connection type is used to provide secure connection to the email server. Start TLS: Enables the STARTTLS mechanism for your connection. This option allows upgrading a plain connection to a secure one. For more information about the STARTTLS connection type, see STARTTLS documentation. |
| SMTP Server | Specify the server address to use. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Port | Specify the port number to be used by the Email Outbound component. |
| User name | Specify the user name to access the email server. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Password | Specify the password for the email server. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |



###### SSL tab


On the SSL tab, you can specify paths and passwords to your trusted and/or key stores. To do this, ensure that SSL/TLS connection security type is selected on the Server options tab and select the Use custom Trusted and(or) Key stores option. In case you disable the Use custom Trusted and(or) Key stores option, the certificates from a default Java trusted store are used.


| Section | Field | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Trusted store | Keystore | Specify a path to a key store with trusted certificates. |
| Password | Specify a password to a key store with trusted certificates. | |
| Key store (required for SSL with client authentication) | Keystore | Specify a path to a file which contains key store certificates. |
| Password | Specify a password to a file which contains key store certificates. | |
| Key password | Specify a password to a private key. | |



###### S/MIME tab


On the S/MIME tab, you can configure encryption and mail signature settings to provide maximum security level for your connection. An email signature is used to verify messages against its sender and receiver credibility to ensure that messages were not intercepted by a third party. The encryption allows authorized parties to exchange messages in an encrypted form.


| Section | Field | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|   | Create digital signature | Select this option to enable creating email signatures. |
| Create signature using a private key | Type | Select a key store format to be used. The JKS and the PKCS12 formats are supported. |
| Include certificate to signature | Select this option to insert a certificate into the signature. | |
| Keystore | Specify a path to a file which contains key store certificates. | |
| Password | Specify a password to a file which contains key store certificates. | |
| Key alias | Specify an alias which is used as an ID to select the required key. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. | |
| Key password | Specify a password to a private key. | |
|   | Encrypt mail | Select this option to encrypt outgoing messages. |
| Encrypt using a certificate | Type | Select a key store format to be used. The JKS and the PKCS12 formats are supported. |
| Keystore | Specify a path to a file that contains key store certificates. | |
| Password | Specify a password to a file that contains key store certificates. | |
| Common name | Specify a mask to filter certificates by their subject parameters. Commonly used parameters are common name (CN) and email address (emailAddress). Use the following syntax to filter certificates by these subject parameters: CN=www.example.com,emailAddress=emailbox@example.com Only the {EMAIL_To} macro is available for this field. Use the ',' character as a delimiter to specify more than one value for a subject parameter. For more information about certificate subject, see the [X.509 documentation](https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.1.1/man1/x509.html). | |



###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |

---

<a id="ftp-outbound"></a>

#### FTP Outbound

> 📄 Source: [FTP_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Outbound.htm)

##### FTP Outbound


This component is used to upload files onto an FTP server. To establish a connection with a target FTP server, the component can use [FTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Service.htm)/[SFTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service.htm)/[FTPS](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTPS_Service.htm) services as a bridge or can connect directly using the component-level settings.


*[Image: FTP Outbound Settings tab]*


To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#FTP).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use connection settings on component | Use this option to allow the component to connect to the target FTP server directly, without using [FTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Service.htm)/[SFTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service.htm)/[FTPS](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTPS_Service.htm) services. If selected, you have to provide connectivity parameters on the component's Connection tab. |
| Use connection pool | Select this option to enable connection pooling. Opening and closing a physical connection with an FTP server is a costly operation. With connection pooling, the service opens several connections and then reuses them when needed keeping the connections in the pool. If disabled, the service opens a new physical connection each time an FTP operation is requested. To configure connection pooling parameters, use the Pool configuration tab on the [FTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Service.htm)/[SFTP](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service.htm)/[FTPS](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTPS_Service.htm) service. If you select Use connection settings on component, the connection pooling is still available. In this case, to configure connection pooling parameters, you have to define the following properties on the profile [Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) tab. Click to see per-component connection pooling properties `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.maxActive`: The maximum number of objects that can be allocated by the pool at a time. If value < 0 the number of objects is unlimited. The default value is 4. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.maxIdle`: The maximum number of idle objects in the pool at a time. If value < 0 the number of objects is unlimited. The default value is 4. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.minIdle`: The maximum amount of time to wait for getting an object from the pool. The default value is 0. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.maxWait`: The maximum amount of time to wait for getting an object from the pool. The default value is 1000. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.minEvictableIdleTimeMillis`: The minimum amount of time for an idle object in the pool before it is eligible for eviction due to idle state. The default value is 60000. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.numTestsPerEvictionRun`: The number of objects to be examined during each run of idle eviction thread. The default value is 3. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.softMinEvictableIdleTimeMillis`: The minimum amount of time an object may remain idle in the pool before it is eligible for eviction by the idle evictor, with the extra condition that at least "minIdle" object remains in the pool. The default value is 1. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.testOnBorrow`: Defines whether objects pass a validation before being borrowed from the pool. If the validation fails, the object is dropped from the pool. The default value is true. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.testOnReturn`: Defines whether objects pass a validation before being returned to the pool. The default value is false. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.testWhileIdle`: Defines whether objects pass a validation by the idle object evictor. If the validation fails, the object is dropped from the pool. The default value is true. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.timeBetweenEvictionRunsMillis`: Defines whether objects pass a validation before being returned to the pool. The default value is 10000. `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.ftp.pool.whenExhaustedAction`: Select the behavior of getting an object from the pool if the pool is exhausted (the list number matches the selected value): 0 - throws NoSuchElementException. 1 - blocks according to the pool.maxWait value and throws NoSuchElementException after timeout exceeds. 2 - creates a new object and returns it. The pool.maxActive parameters becomes meaningless for such behavior. The default value is 1. |
| **Service name** | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| **Destination folder** | Provide path to the folder to put processed data to. |
| **File name template** | Choose the template to name the outgoing files. You can use macros to set the template name. For more information about supported macros, see [XEServer Route Macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm). |
| **Suppress data properties** | Only available for data properties. Properties file are not created in this mode. |
| Data mode | Select what you want to do if the file with the same name already exists in the provided destination folder. error: This mode throws an exception if file with the same name exists. overwrite: In this mode the file is overwritten with the new one. skip: This mode leaves existing file as it is. In case of properties mode, it skips creating properties file if properties file with such name already exists. Warning is displayed in the log file. |
| Data properties | Select what you want to do if the file with the same name already exists in the provided destination folder. error: This mode throws an exception if file with the same name exists. overwrite: In this mode the file is overwritten with the new one. skip: This mode leaves existing file as it is. In case of properties mode, it skips creating properties file if properties file with such name already exists. Warning is displayed in the log file. |
| Do additional operations | Select this option to perform the following actions for each of the data modes if there is a file with the same name on the FTP server: error: The error is raised. overwrite: If the Lock type is None, the existing file is deleted and the new file is uploaded. If the Lock type is Rename, the existing file is renamed with a specific backup prefix, and the uploaded file is saved with the temp prefix. Then, the file with the temp prefix is renamed to the original name, and the file with a specific backup prefix is deleted. skip: The file is not uploaded. If the uploading file does not exist on the FTP server, the following additional operations are performed: If the Lock type is Rename, files are uploaded to the FTP server with specific prefixes to prevent them from being used by other profiles or routes. Then, the files are renamed to original names. If the None lock type is set, an upload without any additional actions is performed. Disable this option to perform an upload of your files without any verification. |
| Create new missed folder | Select this option to allow XEServer creating the specified directory (Destination folder) if such directory does not exist on FTP server. |



###### Connection tab


On this tab, you can specify connection parameters effective only for the current component. Component-level settings have a higher priority than the service-level settings. This tab becomes active after you select the Use connection settings on component option.


Note If you select to use component-level settings and do not provide connection settings on this tab, the service-level settings are used.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Connection Protocol** | The box displays which service protocol is selected currently. To change from current FTP service type to another, select the corresponding protocol from the drop-down menu. |
| **Host** | Enter the host name or the IP address of the server. |
| **Port** | Enter the port number to be used for the connection. |
| **User name** | Enter the name used to log in to FTP Server. |
| **Password** | Enter the password used to log in to FTP Server. |
| **Retry attempt** | The number of attempts to restart the component when a failure occurs. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| **Transfer mode** | The mode for the data transfers. Mode selection is not available for SFTP Service. |
| Enable active mode | When the active mode is enabled, the FTP Service sends the connection details to the FTP Server and the FTP Server initiates a data connection that the FTP Service accepts. By default, the data connection is established in the passive mode and initiated by the FTP Service. |



###### Security tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Server certificate store** | |
| **Type** | The type of the keystore used in the Server keystore. |
| **Path** | The keystore location. |
| **Subject** | The address of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password used to work with the key from the keystore. |
| **User certificate store** | |
| **Type** | The type of the keystore used in the User keystore. |
| **Path** | The keystore location. |
| **Subject** | The address of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password used to work with the key from the keystore. |
| **Primary key password** | The password to the private key. |
| *Connection start mode | Select the FTPS method to provide the security. The following options are available: auto - If the remote port matches the standard plain text port, the explicit mode is used. In all other cases, the implicit mode is used. explicit - XEServer requests the SSL/TLS encryption type, and then SSL/TLS negotiation is started. implicit - A deprecated mode. XEServer connects to an FTP server, and SSL negotiation is started once the connection is established. none - The communication is performed in the plain text mode. |
| *Connection security mode | Select the SSL or TLS cryptographic protocol for communication with an FTP server. |
| *Authentication mode | Select the authentication mode for the SFTP protocol. The following options are available: certificate - The authentication is performed through a private key. mixed - An FTP server verifies the user private key, and then prompts the user password. password - The authentication is performed by entering the user name and the password. none - No authentication is performed. |



*Note The marked fields are available only for FTPS and SFTP protocols.


###### Locking tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lock type | When using FTP endpoints, you may encounter a situation where several instances of XEServer are trying to use the same file. To avoid using partially processed files, a lock functionality is provided. The following options are available: **None** If the file is being used by another process, the current process performs no action on this file. If a file is not used, it is locked for the current process. **Rename** If the file is not used by another process, it is renamed and the current process is started. |



###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |

---

<a id="fs-outbound"></a>

#### FS Outbound

> 📄 Source: [FS_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FS_Outbound.htm)

##### FS Outbound


The component allows you to write data files on your file system.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab The Settings tab includes the following tabs: #### Common tab The following common settings are available: Field Description Destination folder Specify a directory where to store the output data files. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. File name template Provide a template for naming the output files. Use XEServer Route Macros to provide dynamic names for output files. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. Data mode Specify the component behavior if a data file with the same name already exists in the destination folder. The following actions are available: error: The component throws an exception. overwrite: The component overwrites the existing data file. append: This mode appends new data to the existing data file. For this mode, you can specify a custom separator (a string or byte sequence) to split up appended data.Also, you can select to Create a backup copy before appending data. This option creates a copy of the existing file before appending the data and removes the backup copy after the appending is complete. This helps you to avoid corrupting files if XEServer goes down unexpectedly. skip: Retains the existing data file without creating a new one. In this case, a warning is logged to xes.system.log. rename: Creates a new data file named {existingFileName}.{ext}_{n} where {n} is a number, for example: 101.dat_1. If you choose to rename data files, the properties files can be either renamed too (in this case, for data file 101.dat_1, you get 101.dat_1.properties) or suppressed. Note that this option appends "_{n}" after the extension. rename and retain the extension: Creates a new data file named {existingFileName}_{n}.{ext} where {n} is a number, for example: 101_1.dat. Note that this option appends "_{n}" before the extension. Data properties Specify the component behavior if a property file (.properties) with the same name already exists in the destination folder. error: The component throws an exception. overwrite: The component overwrites the existing .properties file. append: Appends property pairs to the existing .properties file. If a property with the same name exists in the .properties file, this property is overwritten. This way several properties with the same name cannot exist in one file. skip: Retains the existing .properties file without creating a new one. rename: Creates a new .properties file named {existingPropFileName}_{n} where {n} is a number, for example: 101.dat_1.properties. suppress: No .properties file is stored in the destination folder in this mode. Override Temp Folder location Specify the path to the directory that temporarily holds files which are in the process of writing. After the last byte is written to a temporary file, this file is moved to the destination folder as a single atomic operation. To provide the atomicity, the Temp folder and the destination folder must belong to the same disk volume. If this requirement is violated, the file cannot be moved to the destination folder and the corresponding exception is logged. #### Advance tab The following advance settings are available: Field Description Buffer size This configuration controls the rate at which files are written. By default, the buffer size is set to 8K, but it can be adjusted to improve the speed of writing large files. For optimal performance with Network File System (NFS), a buffer size of 1M is recommended. The buffer size can be specified using one of the following formats: 1G: for gigabytes. 1M: for megabytes. 1K: for kilobytes. 1: for bytes. #### Locking tab When working with FS components, you may encounter a situation when several instances of XEServer are trying to write to the same file. To avoid using partially processed files, a lock functionality is provided. The following locking settings are available: Field Description **Lock type** write to temp and rename: If the file is used by another process, it is renamed and the current process cannot be started. For this option, files are renamed by adding corresponding prefix to their name. check locking service instance: File locks are controlled by the Locking service. In Custom properties, you can specify any attributes to be added to locks as meta information (for example, a trading partner name). These additional details are added to the record of a lock in the lock storage and reported by the Expired Lock Reporter component. You can also use macros for lock type configuration. To do this: In Lock type, add the macro ${LockingType}. On the Profile environment tab, add the environment property LockingType=<locking-type>. The following values are supported: none, locking.service. Lock timeout The period of time during which the component attempts to lock the file.   Cluster mode Available only for write to temp and rename lock type. Note You must turn on this mode if you use several XEServer instances that write to the same file(s). If the cluster mode is enabled, the component applies a lock (creates an empty .lock file) on the currently processed file. You should use this mode to prevent multiple XEServer instances from processing the same file concurrently. With the cluster mode enabled, the first XEServer instance to access the file fileName.** automatically creates the file *fileName.lock* when the processing begins. If another XEServer instance attempts to process the same file *fileName.**, XEServer does not create another *.lock* file with the same file name.


For more information on file locking capabilities and troubleshooting recommendations, view [File System Locking Considerations](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/File_System_Locking_Considerations.htm).









###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |



 


###### Files recovery


If the file processing is interrupted unexpectedly (for example, due to a JVM crash or power outage), the profiles that were not properly stopped have a recovery file in their corresponding ${XESProfile} folders. On the next profile start-up, XEServer detects this recovery file (it serves as an indicator where recovery is needed) and launches its build-in mechanism that recovers the files located in the inbound / outbound directories:

- For the FS Inbound endpoints, XEServer traverses source folders and removes the .inprogress_ prefixes in file names (for example, .inprogress_data.dat becomes data.dat). The traversal is recursive or non-recursive depending on the Scan mode[setting](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FS_Inbound.htm#Scan_mode).
- For the FS Outbound endpoints, XEServer recursively traverses destination folders and removes the .backup_ prefixes in file names (for example, .backup_data.dat becomes data.dat).


If you want to disable automatic data recovery for all FS Inbound and FS Outbound endpoints, set the system property `-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.fs.startup.recovery=false` on the profile [Startup tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) or `com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.fs.startup.recovery=false` on the profile [Environment tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Environment_Tab.htm). In this case, if you want XEServer to reprocess files that were under processing at the moment of crash, you will have to manually remove prefixes in file names in all source and destination folders prior to restarting XEServer profiles.


###### Container mode


If you run XEServer profile configurations in a containerized environment, read [this topic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_in_Containerized_Environments.htm) to learn about the required changes in settings.

---

<a id="hdfs-outbound"></a>

#### HDFS Outbound

> 📄 Source: [HDFS_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HDFS_Outbound.htm)

##### HDFS Outbound


With HDFS Outbound endpoint you can write data processed by an XEServer route to Hadoop Distributed file system clusters. Such clusters provide distributed data storage to ensure its reliability. You can configure the HDFS Outbound settings on the Route component properties tab.


*## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab #### Common tab Field Description Host Enter the host name or IP address of the Hadoop Distributed file system name node you want to connect to. Macros are available for this field. Port Enter the port number to be used for the connection. Connection Timeout The timeout in seconds for the connection to be established. Connection retry attempts The number of reconnection attempts if connection cannot be established. Destination folder Specify the path to the directory to which the resulting data is saved. File name template Provide a template to be used for naming the outgoing files. Macros are available for this field. For more information about supported macros, see XEServer Route Macros. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. Note Leaving this field empty results in assigning the message ID as a file name. Data mode Select an action to be performed in case outgoing files in your destination folder are duplicated. The following options are available: error: An error is reported and outgoing files are not saved. overwrite: The original data is replaced by newer outgoing files. rename: The outgoing files are saved with a prefix "_n*" appended to the original file name. skip: The outgoing files are not saved under the specified directory. #### Advanced tab | Field | Description | | --- | --- | | Permissions | By using this section, you can manage permissions for your outgoing files. Select an option that corresponds to the permission you want to grant to a User, a User group, or Other users. | | Replication | Specify the number of file copies to be saved in a cluster. File copies distributed in a cluster ensure reliability of stored data. | For more information about Apache Hadoop permissions and replication, see:

- [Apache Hadoop Permission Guide](http://hadoop.apache.org/docs/current/hadoop-project-dist/hadoop-hdfs/HdfsPermissionsGuide.html)
- [Apache Hadoop data replication](http://hadoop.apache.org/docs/r1.2.1/hdfs_design.html#Data+Replication)
- [hdfs-default.xml file description](http://hadoop.apache.org/docs/r2.7.1/hadoop-project-dist/hadoop-hdfs/hdfs-default.xml)



###### Security tab


For XEServer to connect to a [secured HDFS cluster](https://hadoop.apache.org/docs/r2.10.1/hadoop-project-dist/hadoop-common/SecureMode.html), use the Security settings tab to configure the HDFS components.


To configure a secure connection between XEServer and your name node, select Enable Kerberos Authentication and specify the following parameters:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Principal | Enter the Kerberos principal name of the name node (for example, hdfs/hdfs-client@EXAMPLE.COM). |
| Keytab File | Specify the path to the Kerberos keytab file for the name node (for example, ${ECRootPath}/hdfs.client.keytab). |
| RPC Protection | XEServer interacts with the name node using the Remote Procedure Call (RPC) protocol. Select an RPC protection option: authentication: Simple authentication using Kerberos (default option). integrity: Integrity check in addition to authentication. privacy: Data encryption with symmetric keys in addition to simple authentication and integrity check. |



If you want to use Kerberos authentication on XEServer side and the [Kerberos configuration properties file](https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-1.12/doc/admin/conf_files/krb5_conf.html) is not in the default location, set the JVM property java.security.krb5.conf on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) (for example,` -Djava.security.krb5.conf=C:\Edifecs\krb5.conf`). The default locations and file names for Kerberos configuration properties are the following:

- Windows: C:\windows\krb5.ini
- Linux: /etc/krb5.conf
- Other UNIX-based operating systems: /etc/krb5/krb5.conf


###### Locking tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lock timeout (ms) | Set a timeout within which XEServer attempts to access a file if it is locked. |

---

#### IBM WebSphere MQ Outbound

> 📄 Source: [IBM_WebShere_MQ_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/IBM_WebShere_MQ_Outbound.htm)

<a id="inter-route-outbound"></a>

#### Inter Route Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Inter_Route_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Inter_Route_Outbound.htm)

##### Inter Route Outbound


The XEServer Inter Route components allow you to transfer data from one route to another. The inbound and outbound Inter Route components are used in pairs and allow you to set up a unidirectional data stream (channel) between a source route and one or more target routes and transfer XEServer message exchanges with all their properties through this stream. The transfer mechanism is based on a traditional FIFO (first in, first out) queue which preserves the order of message exchanges that travel between the routes.


Note During the transition phase of XEServer messages between the routes, the data is not persisted but remains in the queue in the RAM. So, if the XEServer process terminates at the moment when there are messages in the queue, these messages are lost and cannot be recovered.


Unlike the [Subroute](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Subroute.htm) functionality, where data transferred between the routes is bound to an XEServer task, the Inter Route components exchange data in the send-and-forget mode - once the data reaches the destination route, the data has no correlation with the source route anymore.


The Inter Route Outbound component is a source endpoint that pushes data (current XEServer message exchange with all the nested messages and message exchange/message properties) to the target route. To send the data to the target route, the component puts the data into the queue and starts the timer during which the target route should pick up the data from the queue.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Target Route | The name of the route to send the data to. The drop-down lists the routes that contain the Inter Route Inbound component. |
| Sending Timeout | The timeout in milliseconds within which the data must be picked up by the target route. If the target route fails to receive the data, the selected QoS strategy comes into play, by attempting to re-deliver the data. |

---

<a id="jms-outbound"></a>

#### JMS Outbound

> 📄 Source: [JMS_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JMS_Outbound.htm)

##### JMS Outbound


JMS Outbound endpoint component allows to send messages to JMS server.


*## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab Field Description **Service name** Select the XEServer service associated with the current component. **Type** Select the type of destination to use. Options are: Queue: To use point-to-point messaging (one consuming receiver). Topic: To use publish and subscribe messaging (one or more consuming subscribers). XEServer JMS components support persistent/non-persistent queues and non-durable topics. Non-persistent queues and non-durable topics can be used to deliver data that does not affect the main processing flow (for example, statistics, events, real-time data, or any data that can be easily restored in case of its loss during processing). In XEServer, the session expiration timeout for JMS topics is set to 30 minutes to reduce the number of opened client sessions and avoid subscriber data and metadata overflow when a profile is stopped or failed. However, if data processing is not complete within this timeout, the messages not consumed will be lost. To avoid data loss due to non-durable subscription, you can increase the session timeout to keep JMS subscription active as long as the profile is working by setting the system property -Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.jms.session.expiration=120 (120 minutes in this example) on the Profile Startup Tab. However, if the profile is stopped unexpectedly, the messages that are not consumed will be lost. Using persistent queues ensures reliable data delivery, but performance is lower than in case of using topics and non-persistent queues. **Correlation ID** (Optional) Specify a unique identifier to be used in matching responses with requests. Use the {GUID} macro for XEServer to generate a unique ID automatically. **Hexadecimal format:** If the value starts with ID:, the remainder is interpreted as a hexadecimal byte string (e.g., ID:414D51205445535420202020202020207F3C896401010101*). This format is typically used when copying correlation IDs from IBM MQ tools or logs.




**Plain text format:** If no ID: prefix is present, the value is used as-is and converted to bytes using the default character encoding.




For more information, refer to the JMSCorrelationID section in *Java Message Service Specification.*






 **Message type**



 The message type describes the content of messages. The following options are available:

- **Byte Message**. The message payload is stored as an array of bytes. This type is useful when exchanging data in an application’s native format.
- **Text Message**. The message payload is stored as a string. This type is useful when exchanging simple text or XML.







 **Encoding**



 Provide the encoding of the message. Options can include:

- AutoDetect
- ASCII
- UTF-8
- UTF-16







 **Transacted**




Select one of the options below to decide if XEServer should exchange messages with the JMS server using transactions.

- None - Does not use transactions. Receives and sends each message independently. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, other messages are unaffected.
- Local - Several messages are transferred as a part of a single transaction. When there is an error during the transfer of a message, the entire transaction is rolled back and none of the messages is transferred.
- Shared - Uses a single transaction across the whole task. When there is an error in a transaction for the whole task, the entire transaction is rolled back.






 **Transaction name**



 This name is only used when the messages are sent as shared transactions. Outbound endpoints can identify the suitable transaction by its name to send the results out.






 **Priority**



 Set priority of the messages to be processed first.






 **Time to live (ms)**




Timeout in milliseconds for the messages to be kept in the queue.


Caution The Time to live property does not work if there is a difference in time on the machines where XEServer and JMS server are started.






 **Delivery mode**



 Click **Persistent** to store the message to stable storage. If you choose to use Default mode, then the value for the mode is taken from
							the JMS server connected to this endpoint. For example, if the mode is set to Persistent on the server, then Persistent mode
							is used for this endpoint. If you choose to use Default mode, then the value for the mode is taken from the Queue.







 Reply to

 A queue name to which the response should be sent.







###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |
