<a id="kafka-outbound"></a>

#### Kafka Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Kafka_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Kafka_Outbound.htm)

##### Kafka Outbound


**Note** Headers are not supported by the Kafka Outbound component. You can use keys to pass additional information in messages.


The Kafka Outbound component is used to connect to a Kafka cluster and publish messages to it.



To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Kafka).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Select the service to be used. Click View to go to the service settings page. If there are no services available on the page, click Create new to add a new service. |
| Topic | Specify the topic to publish new messages to. |
| Partitioning key | Specify the partition to which the message is appended. All messages in partitions are assigned a sequential id number (the offset) that uniquely identifies each message within the partition. The topic partition is calculated basing on this value and the number of partitions available for the topic. If left empty, the partition is determined basing on data key. |
| Key type | All messages published to Kafka clusters are stored in topics partitions with mandatory Kafka metadata - the key. Specify the type of the key to be generated by XEServer Kafka Outbound component: Custom: The serializer java class is used. The key is generated by the serializer java class. Note The key.serializer java class must be defined on the Service page. Properties: The key is generated from XEServer message properties. String: The custom string value is used as a Kafka key. |



###### Attributes tab


This tab contains message attributes information in the Name - Value pairs. You can manage your attribute parameters by using the command bar to the right of the Attributes parameters pane.


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |

---

<a id="mft-outbound"></a>

#### MFT Outbound

> 📄 Source: [MFT_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MFT_Outbound.htm)

##### MFT Outbound


With this component, you can push data from XEServer routes to [Edifecs Managed File Transfer (MFT) Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Welcome_to_Managed_File_Transfer.htm).


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*To see the list of macros supported for this component, see XEServer supported macros. ## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab #### Common tab Field Description Service Name Select the XEServer service associated with the current component. Note One MFT activation code can be used by one XEServer MFT Service at a time, so if you want to read and send data using different Smart Trading Accounts in the same profile, ensure you have separate XEServer MFT Service instances each using different Smart Trading Account activation codes. Partner Account ID Select the MFT partner to send data to. The list of available MFT partners (those MFT partners you have previously created an agreement with) is pulled from MFT Service at design time. Partner Inbox ID Select the MFT partner's inbox to send data to. The list of inboxes associated with the MFT partner (the one selected in the Partner Account ID field) is pulled from MFT Service at design time. File Path Specify a name for the data file (with extension) that is sent to the MFT partner. You can use "/" characters to emulate a file system structure. For example, the "foo/bar.dat" value creates the bar.dat file inside the foo folder when the data is delivered to your MFT partner. This way, you can categorize the data you send and be able to distinguish data files in future (for example, by parsing the path substring using the Groovy component). Use the macros MFT_FilePath* to specify names for several data files that are sent to the MFT partner.









###### Metadata tab


On this tab, you can specify [MFT Transmission metadata](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/MFT_Metadata_Exchange.htm). These are key-value pairs that can be utilized by the MFT Service.

---

<a id="parent-route-outbound"></a>

#### Parent Route Outbound

> 📄 Source: [Parent_Route_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Parent_Route_Outbound.htm)

##### Parent Route Outbound


A Parent Route Output endpoint marks the exit of a subroute. It reports the results of the subroute to the Subroute data processor in the main route. When configuring a Subroute data processor in the main route, a subroute must first be delineated by using Parent route endpoints.


###### Component Settings


This component has only [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings.

---

<a id="rabbitmq-outbound"></a>

#### RabbitMQ Outbound

> 📄 Source: [RabbitMQ_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/RabbitMQ_Outbound.htm)

##### RabbitMQ Outbound


With this component, you can send messages to RabbitMQ brokers. The component maps an XEServer message body to the RabbitMQ message payload, then sets RabbitMQ message headers, and then sends the assembled message to the broker.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Target Exchange | Enter the name of the [RabbitMQ exchange](https://www.rabbitmq.com/tutorials/amqp-concepts.html#exchanges) to send messages to. |
| Routing Key | Enter an identifier that is used by the RabbitMQ exchange to direct messages to a particular queue. Routing keys can be used only by the exchanges of type direct and topic. If the messages are sent to the exchange header, you can specify headers in this field in the following format: key1=value1, key2=value2.If the value is not specified, the "" (empty string) routing key is used. |
| Delivery Strategy | Select the strategy to deliver messages to a RabbitMQ broker. The following options are available: Send and forget: The component sends messages to the RabbitMQ broker asynchronously and the route processing continues without waiting for the acknowledgement from the RabbitMQ server. Guaranteed delivery: The component sends messages to the RabbitMQ broker and the route processing gets blocked waiting for the acknowledgement from the RabbitMQ server. This strategy ensures that the message is successfully received by the broker. |
| Store messages on RabbitMQ server side | Select this option if you want to persist sent messages on the file system of the RabbitMQ server. |
| Create exchange and queue if they do not exist | Select this option to create a new RabbitMQ exchange and a queue if the specified Target Exchange does not exist. If this option is selected and no exchange was found in the broker, a new exchange and a new queue are created. The created exchange will be of the type direct and both the exchange and the queue will have the same name (the one you enter in the Target Exchange field). The newly created exchange and queue are linked together using Routing Key. |



###### Message Settings tab


On this tab, you can set the [RabbitMQ message attributes](https://www.rabbitmq.com/tutorials/amqp-concepts.html#messages). Some attributes are used by the RabbitMQ broker and are mandatory, however, most of them are optional and are intended for use by receiving applications to identify specific messages during reading. In RabbitMQ terminology, such optional attributes are called headers. On this pane, you can specify the following optional headers, and if not specified, the RabbitMQ broker will use its default values.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Message ID | Enter the ID to identify the message in a queue. |
| Message Content Type | Select the content type of the message payload. RabbitMQ brokers do not use the Content Type header internally. The header is intended for producers and consumers to notify about the message content. |
| Message priority | Enter the message priority. The available range of values is 0-255. The higher the priority index, the sooner the message is going to be exposed by the RabbitMQ server for reading (assuming there are other messages with a lower priority index). |
| Time to live | Enter the number of seconds for a message to remain alive in a queue. When this time expires, the message cannot be read anymore. Setting the value to "0" allows messages to never expire. |
| Reply to Exchange | Enter the name of the queue from which the message sender expects a response. You may find this option useful when you want to send a message to initialize some long-term processing on the receiver side and after the work is done, you want to receive a message that carries information on processing results. With this option, you can bind the sending and receiving channels and a logical response to the initial request. For more information, see RabbitMQ [Direct reply-to](https://www.rabbitmq.com/direct-reply-to.html) feature. |
| Correlation ID | Enter an ID if you want to correlate the sent messages to the received messages. |
| Message Size Limit | Specify the maximum message size that can be sent to a RabbitMQ broker. The default value is 128 MB. Optimal Message Size To find an optimal message size threshold, take into account the total consumption of RAM for cases when multiple messages are sent to a broker in parallel and make sure that your Java heap has enough space for them. For example, if there are 10 messages N MB each to be sent in parallel, the total RAM consumption to handle these messages is 10*N MB in the worst case. Once the messages are sent, the memory is freed by the Java garbage collector. |

---

<a id="restful-web-service-outbound"></a>

#### Restful Web Service Outbound

> 📄 Source: [RESTful_Web_Service_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/RESTful_Web_Service_Outbound.htm)

##### RESTful Web Service Outbound


The RESTful Web Service Outbound component is used to return HTTP responses to the caller.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | The name of the RESTful Web Service. |
| Rest ID | An ID used to correlate requests with responses. By default, the {Prp[REST_ID]} macro is used. |
| Response status | Select the status code to be sent to the caller on task completion. |



Note Only one response should be sent - either an automatic response generated by the Restful Inbound component, or the response generated by the Restful Outbound component.


###### Response Header tab


On this tab, you can select from a list of HTTP headers to include in the response. To set custom HTTP headers, [use special Jetty properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Restful_Web_Service.htm#CustomHeaders).


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Include exchange properties | Sets all message exchange properties as response HTTP headers. |
| Include message properties | Sets all message properties as response HTTP headers. |



###### Response Body tab


On this tab, you can define the response body content type.


| Mode | Description | Additional options |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Form-encoded | Sets the content type to application/x-www-form-urlencoded. | Include as form parameters: select whether to include exchange/message properties as form parameters. Create message from: select XEServer Message to populate the response body from the XEServer message, or select Custom Template to enter form parameters as key =value pairs. |
| Multipart | Sets the content type to multipart/form-data. | Content-ID: specify the multipart attachment identifier. By default, the value is set to {M_SID}. Content-Type: select the attachment content type. Create message from: select XEServer Message to populate the response body from the XEServer message, or select Custom Template to specify custom Content-ID, Content-type, and the source for generating each message part (Part Body) - either XEServer message or custom template. |
| None | The response has no body. |   |
| Raw | Select to explicitly specify the content type from the list. | Content-Type - Select the response body content type. The following options are available:application/EDI-X12application/EDIFACTapplication/gzipapplication/json application/octet-streamapplication/soap+xmlapplication/xmlapplication/ziptext/htmltext/plaintext/xml Create message from - Select XEServer Message to create the response body from the XEServer message or select Custom Template to enter text data in the text box to be used as the response body. |



For more information about HTTP message content types, see the IANA official documentation.


###### Outputs Tab


On the [Outputs tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Components_Output_Properties.htm) of the component, you can add custom outputs with filters to direct the processing flow based on the response code received from the Web server.

---

<a id="sftp-outbound"></a>

#### SFTP Outbound

> 📄 Source: [SFTP_Outbound.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Outbound.htm)

##### SFTP Outbound


This component is used to upload files to an FTP server over the SFTP protocol. For each XEServer message that comes to the component, the component uploads a new file with the message body contents and (optionally) a .properties files with the message properties. The component uses the [SFTP Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service_Dedicated.htm) that is responsible for establishing a physical connection with the FTP server.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.



###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use connection settings on component | Enable to use the connection settings provided on the component's Connection tab. If disabled, the component uses connection settings specified on the SFTP Service. |
| Use connection pool | Select this option to enable connection pooling. Opening and closing a physical connection with an FTP server is a costly operation. With connection pooling, the component opens several connections once and then reuses the connections when needed keeping them in the pool. If disabled, the component opens a new physical connection for every message that passes through the route component. |
| **Service name** | Select an [SFTP service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SFTP_Service_Dedicated.htm) to be used by the component. The service is responsible for establishing a physical connection with an FTP server (unless the component is configured to use component-level connection settings). |
| **Destination folder** | Specify a directory on the FTP server to upload files. |
| **File name template** | Specify a file naming template for the uploaded files. You can use [macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm) to set the file names dynamically. |
| **Suppress data properties** | Specify whether to save message properties as separate .properties files on the FTP server. If set to true, properties files are not saved. |
| Data mode | Select an action to perform if a file with the same name already exists in the destination folder of the FTP server. error: the component throws an exception and does not upload the file. overwrite: the component uploads the file overwriting the existing file. skip: the component does not upload the file, leaves the existing file as-is, and adds a warning to the log. |
| Data properties | Select an action if a .properties file with the same name already exists in the destination folder of the FTP server. error: the component throws an exception and does not upload the .properties file. overwrite: the component uploads the file and overwrites the existing .properties file. skip: the component does not upload the .properties file, leaves the existing file as-is, and adds a warning to the log. |
| Do additional operations | Select to perform additional operations if there is a file with the same name on the FTP server and the mode is set to overwrite. In this case, if the Lock type is Rename, the component uploads files to the FTP server with specific prefixes to prevent them from being used by other FTP components. Then, the component renames files to the original names. If the None lock type is set, the component uploads files without additional renaming operations. Disable this option to perform an upload of your files without any verification. |
| Create new missed folder | Select this option to allow XEServer to create directories on the FTP server (**Destination folder** and subdirectories). |



###### Connection tab


On this tab, you can specify connection parameters effective only for the given SFTP component. Component-level settings have higher priority than the service-level settings. This tab becomes active after you select the Use connection settings on component option.


Note If you select to use component-level settings and do not provide connection settings on this tab, the service-level settings are used.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Host** | Enter the host name or the IP address of the server. |
| **Port** | Enter the port number to be used for the connection. |
| **User name** | Enter a user name to log in to the FTP server. |
| **Password** | Enter a password to log in to the FTP server. |
| **Retry attempt** | Specify the number of retries in case a connection with the FTP server cannot be established. |
| **Retry delay** | The delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. |
| Waiting time threshold | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) for the service to wait until an FTP server completes a requested operation. If the FTP server does not acknowledge that the operation has been completed within this timeout, the service interrupts the current operation and closes the connection with the FTP server. If the value is set to 0, the service does not interrupt the operation and waits until the FTP server returns the operation completion response. |



###### Security tab


Note SSH keys must be [generated](https://www.thedigitalcatonline.com/blog/2018/04/25/rsa-keys/#generating-key-pairs-with-openssh) using RSA key generation algorithm.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Server certificate store A server certificate store acts as a truststore - it holds certificates of FTP servers to which XEServer can connect. If you do not specify a certificate store, XEServer can connect to any FTP server. | |
| **Type** | The type of the keystore used as a server certificate store. |
| **Path** | The keystore location. |
| **Subject** | The location of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password used to access the keystore. |
| User certificate store A user certificate store acts as a regular keystore - it holds a private key used to connect to secured FTP server. | |
| **Type** | The type of the keystore used as a user certificate store. |
| **Path** | The keystore location. |
| **Subject** | The location of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password used to access the keystore. |
| **Primary key password** | The password to the private key. |
| **Authentication mode** | Select the type of authentication for XEServer to use when connecting to an FTP server. None. No authentication. The User value is ignored, and XEServer attempts to log in anonymously. Certificate. XEServer uses the User and Certificate values to authenticate the connection. The Certificate must have a private key available for this authentication method to succeed. Mixed. Allows XEServer to perform a multi-step authentication process. The client sends authentication data to the server based on the list of methods allowed for the current user and the authentication property values supplied. The client continues to send authentication data until the server acknowledges authentication success. If the server rejects an authentication step, an exception is thrown. Password. XEServer uses the User and Password values to authenticate the user session. |



###### Locking tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lock type | When working with FTP servers, you may encounter a situation where several instances of XEServer are trying to use the same file on the FTP server. To avoid using partially processed files, a lock functionality is provided. The following options are available: **None** If a file is used by an XEServer instance, current XEServer instance performs no action on this file. If a file is not used, XEServer locks the file. **Rename** If the file is not used by an XEServer instance, XEServer renames the file and starts processing the file. |



###### Manage SFTP Algorithms


You can enable (disable) cipher, key exchange, message authentication code (MAC), compressions, and signatures algorithms used by the SFTP Service to work with the SFTP protocol. For this, you have to define the supported algorithms as system properties on the [profile Startup tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeserver/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) or on the [global Startup tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeserver/9.4/Content/Startup_Tab.htm) for the [FTP Server Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeconnect/9.4/Content/Manage_SFTP_Algorithms.htm) and SFTP Inbound / SFTP Outbound components.


The properties are the following:

- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.ciphers
- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.mac
- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.kex
- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.compressions
- com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.sftp.signatures


The following algorithms are supported by the SFTP Service:


Ciphers: none, 3des-cbc, aes128-ctr (default), aes192-ctr (default), aes256-ctr (default), aes128-cbc (default), aes192-cbc (default), aes256-cbc (default), aes128-gcm@openssh.com (default), aes256-gcm@openssh.com (default), arcfour128, arcfour256, blowfish-cbc, chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com (default)


MACs: hmac-md5, hmac-md5-96, hmac-sha1 (default), hmac-sha1-etm@openssh.com (default), hmac-sha1-96, hmac-sha2-256 (default), hmac-sha2-256-etm@openssh.com (default), hmac-sha2-512 (default), hmac-sha2-512-etm@openssh.com (default)


Key exchange algorithms: curve25519-sha256 (default), curve25519-sha256@libssh.org (default), curve448-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group1-sha1, diffie-hellman-group14-sha1, diffie-hellman-group14-sha256 (default), diffie-hellman-group15-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group16-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group17-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group18-sha512 (default), diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1, diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha256 (default), ecdh-sha2-nistp256 (default), ecdh-sha2-nistp384 (default), ecdh-sha2-nistp521 (default)


Compressions: none (default), zlib, zlib@openssh.com


Signatures: ssh-dss, ssh-dss-cert-v01@openssh.com, ssh-rsa (default), ssh-rsa-cert-v01@openssh.com, rsa-sha2-256 (default), rsa-sha2-256-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), rsa-sha2-512 (default), rsa-sha2-512-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp256 (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp256-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp384 (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp384-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp521 (default), ecdsa-sha2-nistp521-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com (default), ssh-ed25519 (default), ssh-ed25519-cert-v01@openssh.com (default), sk-ssh-ed25519@openssh.com (default)


To define the algorithms to be used:

1. On the [Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeserver/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) tab of the profile with the SFTP Service or on the global [Startup](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xeserver/9.4/Content/Startup_Tab.htm) tab, set a system property with the values of algorithms you want to be used as on the example below:
2. Save the profile configuration.
3. Restart the profile.


To check which algorithms are enabled:

1. On the [Logger](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xeserver/Content/Logger_Tab.htm) tab of the profile with the SFTP Service, expand Advanced logger configuration.
2. In the Logger Name column, select FTPClient.
3. In the Log Level column, select Debug from the drop-down list.
4. Save the profile configuration and restart the profile.
5. On the System tab, expand your profile and double-click Log Viewer.
6. On the Outline tab, select \xes.ftp.client.log.
7. View the lists of enabled algorithms on the Log Viewer tab as on the example below:

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


Example




```
...
| List of selected ciphers: chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com,aes128-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes256-ctr,aes128-gcm@openssh.com,aes256-gcm@openssh.com,aes128-cbc,aes192-cbc,aes256-cbc
...
| List of selected KEX algorithms: curve25519-sha256,curve25519-sha256@libssh.org,curve448-sha512,ecdh-sha2-nistp521,ecdh-sha2-nistp384,ecdh-sha2-nistp256,diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha256,diffie-hellman-group18-sha512,diffie-hellman-group17-sha512,diffie-hellman-group16-sha512,diffie-hellman-group15-sha512,diffie-hellman-group14-sha256
...
| List of selected MAC algorithms: hmac-sha2-256-etm@openssh.com,hmac-sha2-512-etm@openssh.com,hmac-sha1-etm@openssh.com,hmac-sha2-256,hmac-sha2-512,hmac-sha1
...
| List of selected compression algorithms: none,zlib,zlib@openssh.com
...
| List of selected signature algorithms: ecdsa-sha2-nistp256-cert-v01@openssh.com,ecdsa-sha2-nistp384-cert-v01@openssh.com,ecdsa-sha2-nistp521-cert-v01@openssh.com,rsa-sha2-512-cert-v01@openssh.com,rsa-sha2-256-cert-v01@openssh.com,ecdsa-sha2-nistp256,ecdsa-sha2-nistp384,ecdsa-sha2-nistp521,sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com,rsa-sha2-512,rsa-sha2-256,ssh-rsa
```






Note Only the algorithms that are considered safe to use are enabled by default. To find which algorithms have been deprecated, refer to the [Apache MINA SSHD Documentation](https://github.com/apache/mina-sshd#implementedavailable-support). If you still want to include deprecated algorithms, you can define them using system properties.

---

<a id="outbound-endpoints-attributes"></a>

#### Outbound Endpoints Attributes

> 📄 Source: [Outbound_Endpoints_Attributes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Outbound_Endpoints_Attributes.htm)

##### Outbound Endpoints Attributes


The Attributes tab is available for some route components, mostly for outbound endpoints. On this tab, you can define metadata to be passed with the outgoing data and store it to a destination location.


*[Image: Attributes tab]*


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |

---

<a id="xprocess-event"></a>

#### XProcess Event

> 📄 Source: [XPM_Event.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XPM_Event.htm)

##### XPM Event Component


With this component, you can send events to [XProcess Management](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XProcess_Management_Overview.htm). An XProcess Management event is a message that notifies XProcess Management that a certain monitored object has transitioned to a new state. The component communicates with XProcess Management using the XEServer [XProcess service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XProcess_Service.htm).


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Event tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Enter the name of the XProcess Service service to be used by the component. If you do not have a service name, click **Create new**. For more information about how to create a new service, see [Add a New Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Service.htm). |
| Process | Select a process to which the event should be sent. An XProcess Management process is a representation of a real-life business process that depicts a data processing route with all possible variants of business logic flow. In XProcess Management, processes are represented in the form of BPMN diagrams. |
| Business types | An abstract data type that represents objects and phenomena from the real-life world (for example, an electronic transaction, a text document, a payment to be paid to a party, and so on). Instances of business types (business items) travel across processes and depict the progress of the monitored business process. Business types can contain any number of fields to describe the entity they represent. You can query XProcess Management by these fields to obtain precise information about the current state of a monitored item. Enter a name of business type associated with the selected Process. One XProcess Management process can have only one business type associated with it. |
| Object ID | An ID used to identify the business item (instance of a business type) which state should be updated. It can be any unique combination. For example, the value from the CLM01 segment of a HIPAA File, or some synthetic value - a concatenation of Claim ID, Date, Subscriber ID, and so on. |
| Producer | A name of the system that sends the event to XProcess Management. |
| State | A unique place in the business process workflow that is a logical result of previous states and will or may become a source for upcoming states. The state has the explicit state name being updated, for example, Start, <State_Update_Name>, End. This name is used in the body of the event when it is submitted. |
| Date/Times | The time and date when the event occurred. The default time zone XProcess Management uses is UTC (Coordinated Universal Time). If you use the format {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]} to express the local time, XProcess Management converts the time and date to the UTC time and date. To indicate another time zone, specify a time zone offset in hours and minutes in the following format {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSSZZZZ]}, where 'ZZZZ' = '+hh:mm' or '-hh:mm'. Possible formats: Timestamp - Time in milliseconds. When XEServer sends this value to XProcess Management, the value is stored in the XProcess Management default time format (UTC). Route macros ({Prp[name]}) and system macros (${name}) are available for this field. {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS]} {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSSZZZZ]}, where 'Z' is a UTC designator. 'ZZZZ' = '+hh:mm' or '-hh:mm' {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]} {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ssZZZZ]}, where 'Z' is a UTC designator. 'ZZZZ' = '+hh:mm' or '-hh:mm' {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS]} {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZZZZ]}, where 'Z' is a UTC designator. 'ZZZZ' = '+hh:mm' or '-hh:mm' {CDT[yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss]} {CDT[yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZZZZ]}, where 'Z' is a UTC designator. 'ZZZZ' = '+hh:mm' or '-hh:mm' You can also use the POSIX format to express the local time. |



The Properties pane displays a list of business type fields associated with the selected XProcess Management process. You can use these properties to pass additional data to XProcess Management along with the event. In this pane, you can only provide values for existing properties and you cannot create new property pairs. The set of available properties is pulled in from XProcess Management at the design time and includes only the fields of the selected business type. To add a new property, you can add a new business type field on the XProcess Management Business Types tab.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Ignore unresolved property | Select this option if you want to remove a property pair from the event to be sent if the value of this property is an unresolved macro. |



Note If a metadata field has a date and time value, XProcess Management assumes that all of those received date/time metadata fields are in the UTC time zone. You cannot specify a time zone offset for metadata field values. The issue may have been already resolved in XProcess Management, double-check the XProcess Management help documentation.

---

<a id="endpoints-command-mode"></a>

### Endpoints Command Mode

> 📄 Source: [Endpoints_Command_Mode.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Endpoints_Command_Mode.htm)

#### Endpoints Command Mode


Supported command parameters


There are several types of endpoints available on the Route Editor. All types of endpoints are represented by inbound and outbound endpoints. The following endpoint types are supported:


###### Inbound Endpoints

- Amazon S3. Used to fetch data from an Amazon S3 storage.
- Amazon SQS. Used to read messages from an Amazon SQS queue.
- BCounter. Monitors the counters and generates an empty XEServer message with properties that consists of all information about the state of the counter when certain events take place.
- Batcher. Takes out all Batch Groups that match the trigger criteria and sends them for further processing in for of a .zip archive.
- Data Storage. Scans the specified location in data storage and extracts messages from it.
- Email. Scans the specified email box for email messages.
- Error Channel. Accumulates the errors from all the spare error outputs of all the components on the route.
- FTP. Lets you specify masks that cause files and folders to be included in and excluded from the FTP server download.
- File System.Serves as inbound connectors scanning your file system.
- HDFS. Serves as a connector to Hadoop distrubuted file system clusters to read data from it.
- HTTP. Allows a route to receive HTTP messages from an external source through a Jetty web server running on XEServer.
- IBM WebSphere MQ. Allows to receive messages from IBM WebSphere MQ by using native MQ interfaces.
- JMS. Allows to receive messages from a JMS server.
- Kafka. Allows you to subscribe to a Kafka broker and read messages from it.
- Parent Route. Marks the entry point of a subroute and registers the subroute so that it can be called by a main route.
- RabbitMQ. Used to receive messages from RabbitMQ brokers.
- RESTful Web Service. Receives processed data from RESTful server in a form of HTTP(s) responses.
- Task Reporter. Monitors the events on the route and collects task-related statistic.
- Web Service. Uses the XEServer Web Service to receive data from a client application that is located outside of XEServer.


###### Outbound Endpoints

- Amazon S3. Used to upload data to an Amazon S3 storage.
- Amazon SQS. Used to send messages to an Amazon SQS queue.
- BCounter. Updates the balance counters.
- Batcher. Accepts messages and determines which Batch Group they should be added to.
- Data Storage. Allows you to remove, update, and put data in data storage.
- Email. Allows you to connect to a mail server and send messages to email boxes.
- FTP. Lets you provide a file name template and destination folder for uploads to an FTP server.
- File System. Serves as an outbound connector for the process.
- HDFS. Serves as a connector to Hadoop distrubuted file system clusters to write data to it.
- HTTP. Receives HTTP message processing results from the route and sends the response, through the Jetty web server, to the external source that sent the original message.
- IBM WebSphere MQ. Allows to send messages to IBM WebSphere MQ by using native MQ interfaces.
- JMS. Allows to send messages to a JMS server.
- Kafka. Allows you to connect to a Kafka broker and publish messages to it.
- Parent Route. Reports the results of the subroute to the Subroute data processor in the main route.
- RabbitMQ. Used to send messages to RabbitMQ brokers.
- RESTful Web Service. Sends data for further processing to the REST server in a form of an HTTP(s) requests.
- Web Service. Uses the XEServer Web Service to return data to a client application that is located outside of XEServer.
- XProcess Event. Used to send events to Edifecs XProcess Management.


Most of the incoming endpoints can work in two modes:

- Route start: The component starts with the route and is continuously scanned. This activation mode allows you to use only system macros.
- By command: The component starts when it receives an XEServer message. In this mode the inbound component obtains an input for receiving messages. This activation mode allows you to use only the route macros.


To set commands, use the following formats:

- For File System endpoints: cmd.fs.command - see the following table for the available commands
- For Date Storage endpoints: cmd.ds.command - see the following table for the available commands


The following commands can be used:


| Command | Description | Required parameters | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| File System Inbound | get.data* | Allows you to load messages from the file system. Messages are reported to output data. This method is already obsolete. The same functionality can be achieved by using the File System Inbound properties settings. It is recommended to use File System Inbound properties settings as it's easier in terms of understanding and tracking. For more information, see [File System Inbound Properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FS_Inbound.htm). |   |
| Data Storage Inbound | get.data | Allows you to get a single message from data storage. The message is reported to output data. | path and id, or uidExample: path;id  |
| get.group | Allows you to get all messages from a group. All messages are reported one by one to output data. | path; others are ignored | |
| extract.data | Allows you to get a single message from data storage. The message is removed from data storage and is reported to output data. | path and id, or uidExample: path;id | |
| extract.group* | Allows you to get all messages from a group. All messages are reported one by one to output data. The group is removed when all messages are received and reported. | path; others are ignored | |
| Data Storage Outbound | remove.data | Allows you to remove a data item from data storage | path and id, or uidExample: path;id |
| remove.group | Allows you to remove a group with all data items that belong to this group. | path; others are ignored | |
| update.group.prop | Allows you to update group properties. The current collection is replaced by a new collection. | path | |
| put.data* | Allows you to store received data in DS. | path and id, or uid Example: path;id | |



Notes (*) indicates the default value. Separate parameters with a ";" Example: path;id


>  


###### Supported command parameters


To set command parameters, use the following formats:

- For File System endpoints: cmd.fs.params - see the following table for the available command parameters:
- For Date Storage endpoints: cmd.ds.params - see the following table for the available command parameters:


|   | Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| File System | dir | Directory path in File System. If 'dir' paths are read from a file, a backslash must be replaced with forward slash or it should be doubled (escaped). This parameter is equal to Source folder in Common Settings of the File System Inbound component. |
| include.fileMasks | Specifies wildcards for files that should be processed by FS inbound endpoint. Use comma "," to separate multiple wildcards. This parameter is equal to Include masks in Common Settings of the File System Inbound component. | |
| exclude.fileMasks | Specifies wildcards for files that should be ignored by FS inbound endpoint. Use comma "," to separate multiple wildcards. This parameter is equal to Exclude masks in Common Settings of the File System Inbound component. | |
| recursive | Specifies whether or no traversing of directory should be recursive (that is traverse sub-directories recursively). This parameter is equal to Scan Mode in Advanced Settings of the File System Inbound component. | |
| Data Storage | path | Path to group in DS. |
| id | Data item ID in scope of group. | |
| uid | Data item unique ID that identifies item in scope of DS path/ID. If uid is specified, path and id are ignored. | |



You can configure output parameters for the Data Storage to receive the command. See the following configuration example:


*[Image: JMS Inbound Output propetries tab]*

<a id="bcounter-search"></a>

#### BCounter Search

> 📄 Source: [BCounter_Search.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/BCounter_Search.htm)

##### BCounter Search


BCounter Search is a route component that enables retrieval of information from a certain Balance Counter database by its state and/or ID. You can also specify a time interval for the search. The same functionality can be performed by using Balance Counter Search or the command line tool. For more information about this tools, see [Balance Counter Search Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Balance_Counter_Search_Overview.htm) and [BCounter Search Command Line Tool](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Balance_Counter_Search_Command_Line_Tool.htm).


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| Counter group | Enter a list of Balance Counter Groups separated by comma to search data. Use * wildcard to perform search in all available groups. |
| Date format | The date format. The default value is yyyyMMdd-HHmmss.SSS. |
| Report results | Select how search results are sent back to the route for further processing: As multimessage exchange: Information about every found counter is reported as a message within a multimessage exchange. Single message: Information about every found counter is reported as independent message exchange that contains one message. |



###### Search tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Counter ID | The ID of the Balance Counter. If no ID is specified, the search is performed based on other specified parameters(From date, To date, Counter State). If you want to get information about all the counters of the corresponding group, leave this field empty. |
| From date | The lower date bound to perform the search. |
| To date | The lower date bound to perform the search. |
| Counter state | The state of the counter. |
| Maximum number of search results | The maximum number of counters returned as one search result. |

---

<a id="base64-components"></a>

#### Base64 Components

> 📄 Source: [Base64_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Base64_Components.htm)

##### Base64 Components


With the Base64 Encoding and the Base64 Decoding components, you can encode binary data to text strings and decode Base64 text strings to binary data correspondingly. This format is used for transferring binary data (for example .png X-Ray images, .pdf clinical attachments, and so on) over the Internet in the form of text strings.


###### Component Settings


These components have only [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings and do not require any explicit configuration as the only function they perform is encoding/decoding of Base64 contents.


Note that if the incoming Base64 buffer is invalid, an exception is reported on the error output (Outputs tab).

---

<a id="business-rules"></a>

#### Business Rules

> 📄 Source: [Book_Business_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Business_Rules.htm)

##### Business Rules


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="business-rules-overview"></a>

##### Business Rules Overview

> 📄 Source: [Business_Rules_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Business_Rules_Overview.htm)

###### Business Rules Overview


With the Business Rules component, you can apply the Drools rules to your data exchange process flow. [Drools](https://www.drools.org/) is a Business Logic Integration Platform that allows you to create custom logic for data processing. A Drools rule is a .drl file that contains a set of when (...) - then {...} statements. The Drools engine checks a condition(s) in the when() block and if it is satisfied, performs the corresponding action(s) from the then {} block.


A typical Drools rule has the following structure:


<import statements>


rule <rule_name>


  <attribute> <value>


when


   <conditions>


then


   <actions>


end


For more information about the Drools technology, see [official documentation](https://www.drools.org/learn/documentation.html).


The Drools engine (7.17.0 Drools version) that resolves and processes rules is embedded into XEServer, so you do not have to make external calls or use third-party software. Rules files are compiled and loaded to the memory at runtime, so if a rule file is updated, XEServer invokes the latest available version of that rule. You must keep all .drl files you want to execute in a special directory - Rules Root. The component does not load all rules from this directory, but only those that are required for incoming data.

---

<a id="business-rules-component"></a>

##### Business Rules Component

> 📄 Source: [Business_Rules_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Business_Rules_Component.htm)

###### Business Rules Component


The Business Rules component expects input data in two formats: XML or JSON. When the component receives a message, it does the following:

1. Constructs a Java object model from the incoming data (deserialization). Throughout its lifetime, the object exists only in RAM and is not stored on disk. You have to provide either an XML schema or a compiled Root JSON class to perform the deserialization.
2. Executes Drools rules and updates the object's fields. To determine which rules to apply for a specific trading partner, the component has several [configuration methods](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Configuration_Methods.htm).
3. Transforms the modified object back to the XML or JSON notation (serialization), and sends it back through the component's output.


When the component is added to the route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Rules Root | A root directory for rules files (.drl). You can reference rules using relative paths beginning from this folder. Depending on the trading partner and criteria of the input data, all rules or a part of rules are applied to the input data. |
| Configuration method | Select a method that the component uses to apply rules to incoming data. The following options are available: Crosswalk: The component picks the appropriate rule based on the trading partner / criteria row match in a crosswalk table. You can create and edit crosswalk files (.crsw) using Edifecs SpecBuilder which provides an efficient way of maintaining crosswalk correlation tables. Explicit folder: The component applies all the rules in the directory Rules Root one by one regardless of the trading partner / criteria information. You can use this method for simple cases when rules are insensitive to the incoming data contents and should be applied to every message. Explicit List: The component picks the appropriate rule based on a special configuration file received as a part of the XEServer multi-message exchange (the first message in the exchange) together with business data. This method overrides all possible trading partner / criteria settings and forces the component to execute only the provided rules. External file: The component picks the appropriate rule based on the trading partner / criteria match in a special JSON file. This file has a special format (see further down in this section) and provides the same information as with crosswalks described above, but in the form of a JSON file. For detailed description of configuration methods, see [Configuration Methods](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Configuration_Methods.htm#Configuration). |
| Configuration File Path | Used with the External File configuration method. Enter the path to the JSON configuration file. |
| Crosswalk Name | Used with the Crosswalk configuration method. Enter the name of the crosswalk from where the component must fetch rules configuration. |
| Trading Partner Name | Used with the Crosswalk and External File configuration methods. Enter the name of the trading partner to pick the relevant rule files. |
| Criteria Name | Used with the Crosswalk and External File configuration methods. Enter the criteria string to pick the relevant rule files. |
| Input Format | Select the data format the component would expect on the input. For the XML format, the component constructs the Java object model based on the specified XML Schema file. For the JSON format, the component constructs the Java object model based on a pre-compiled Java class that you must provide explicitly. |
| XML Schema Path | Enter the path to the XML Schema file (.xsd) that is used as a template for constructing the Java object model from incoming XML data. Note If you want to bind your custom class names to the specified XML Schema, use the binding.jaxb file located at %XEServer%\features\route-engine\components\business-rules\resources\. For example, the following .jaxb file allows you to control method naming when constructing an object from a schema file. Sample.jaxb <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <jaxb:bindings xmlns:jaxb="http://java.sun.com/xml/ns/jaxb" version="2.1"> <jaxb:globalBindings underscoreBinding="asCharInWord"/> </jaxb:bindings> This sample .jaxb file allows you to name methods in by the following pattern: test_name -> getTest_name(); Note Some versions of JDK apply a restriction on accessing external XSD schemas which does not allow to access external files by default. If your JDK applies such a restriction, you may observe the following exception in error logs: java.lang.AssertionError: org.xml.sax.SAXParseException: "Failed to read external schema document <pathToXSDFile> ... ". To fix this issue, define the -Djavax.xml.accessExternalSchema=all property on the EAM [Environment](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Environment_Tab.htm) tab and restart the profile. |
| Root Namespace | Enter a root namespace for compiling the Java object model. This name serves as a prefix to the Java package names for the generated Java object Models. All classes with this namespace are generated in the same package. |
| Validate data structure against schema | Select this option to validate incoming data against the specified schema file. Clear this option to allow the component to pass data that is potentially non-compliant with the schema file. |
| Root Json Class | Enter the fully qualified name of the class used to construct the Java object model. The JAR file that contains this class must be placed at ${XEServer}\platform\lib.user\business-rules\. You can use the route macro {Prp[RootJSONClass]} to dynamically resolve Root JSON Class at run time. Note The component relies on the Gson Java library to perform object (de)serialization. When you provide your class, be sure that it is compliant with Gson. You can use generic classes to perform deserialization, for example: Test.java [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);) Test.java ``` package com.edifecs.generated; public class Test { private T parameterizedField; // ... deserialization logic here } ``` To use the above class parameterized by a `String`, you have to enter the class name com.edifecs.generated.Test<java.lang.String> as shown below: Note You have to use a fully qualified class name for the generic type argument (even for those classes of the `java.lang` package). |
| Json Array on Root Level | Select if the first element of the incoming JSON data is an JSON array. This option is required when the root object model provided as Root JSON Class is used in an array instead of a single root object. If this option is selected, then the input JSON array is mapped to java.util.List<Root JSON Class>.  Apart from selecting this option, you can also wrap a value of the parameter Root JSON Class with java.util.List and not with an array. For example, you can use the following two equivalent ways to pass the array of the strings in the JSON format: If the option is selected, then the value of the parameter Root JSON Class is java.lang.String. If the option is not selected, then the value of the parameter Root JSON Class is java.util.List<java.lang.String>. |



Note Most of the properties described above, such as a trading partner, a criteria, a path, and so on, can accept message / exchange macros.

---

<a id="configure-the-business-rules-component"></a>

##### Configure the Business Rules Component

> 📄 Source: [Book_Configure_Business_Rules_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Configure_Business_Rules_Component.htm)

###### Configure the Business Rules Component


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="configuration-methods"></a>

##### Configuration Methods

> 📄 Source: [Configuration_Methods.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Configuration_Methods.htm)

###### Configuration Methods


When the Business Rules component receives data on input and constructs a Java object model, it should also get instructions on what rule files to invoke for the constructed object. In this section, when we refer to instructions, we refer to a set of configuration properties which the component uses to apply the appropriate rule.


A typical instruction looks like the following:


Example


[


   {


      "path": "Generic/birth_date_check_rule.drl",


      "sequence": 1,


      "disabled": false,


      "properties": {


         "prop2": "Prop2",


         "prop1": "Prop2"


       }


   }


]


This instruction includes the following configuration properties:


| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| path | String | The property is mandatory. Defines a path to the rule file (.drl) that must be invoked for the current object model. If the path refers to a directory, all rule files in this directory are invoked in alphabetical order. |
| sequence | Integer | The property is optional. Defines the order of invoking rule files if multiple rules are applied to the same object model. If several rules have the same sequence value, they are executed in alphabetical order. If the value is not specified, the current rule is applied last in the sequence. |
| disabled | Boolean | The property is optional. Defines whether the current rule should be applied. If set to TRUE, the current rule is not executed. If the value is not specified, the default value FALSE is used. |
| properties | JSON Object | The property is optional. Defines a set of custom properties that can be passed to the constructed Java model object. These properties can be referenced during rules execution and are included in the Business Rules XML reports. |



The Business Rules component can obtain these instructions from the following sources: a crosswalk file, an XEServer message as part of a multimessage exchange, and an external JSON file. You can use the following configuration methods to set the source that the component uses to get instructions on applying particular rules:

- Crosswalk: This method allows the component to retrieve instructions on what rules to apply from a crosswalk. For more information, see [Crosswalk Configuration Method](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Configuration_Method.htm).
- Explicit folder: This method applies all rules located in the Rules Root directory. For this method, all .drl files are invoked recursively in alphabetical order. This is a simple method if you do not care about trading partner and criteria values combination and just need to apply all rules to all incoming data. Since there is no external execution order configuration and all rules are executed in ascending alphabetical order, you can use the corresponding file naming strategy to control rules execution order.
- Explicit List: This method presumes that instructions on what rules to apply are received as a part of XEServer multimessage exchange. For more information, see [Explicit List Configuration Method](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Explicit_List_Configuration_Method.htm).
- External File: This method allows the component to obtain instructions from an external JSON file. For more information, see [External File Configuration Method](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/External_File_Configuration_Method.htm).

---

<a id="crosswalk-configuration-method"></a>

##### Crosswalk Configuration Method

> 📄 Source: [Crosswalk_Configuration_Method.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Configuration_Method.htm)

###### Crosswalk Configuration Method


This method allows the component to retrieve instructions what rules to apply from a crosswalk. You can create and edit crosswalk files (.crsw) using Edifecs SpecBuilder which provides an efficient way of maintaining crosswalk correlation tables. The Business Rules component has direct access to the crosswalk functionality, so you do not have to add additional components to retrieve the information - all you need is to enter the required crosswalk name. For this method, you need a crosswalk that has the following structure and meets the listed requirements:


Crosswalk structure:


|   Source columns |   Target columns |
| --- | --- |
| Trading Partner | Criteria |
|   |   |






| Rule Path | Sequence | Disabled |
| --- | --- | --- |
|   |   |   |










Crosswalk requirements:

- In the source columns, name the first and second columns "Trading Partner" and "Criteria" respectively.
- In the source columns, enable the Use column for run-time lookup column property for Trading Partner and Criteria columns. To change column properties, use the Column Properties dialog in Edifecs SpecBuilder Crosswalk Browser as shown in the image (this window is triggered using the Column Properties menu item on a crosswalk column):
- You can add any number of additional columns to source and target columns (for example, a "Description" column, and so on). The only requirement is that these added columns must have their Use column for run-time lookup options cleared.


If a crosswalk's source columns contain a row with matching Trading Partner Name and Criteria Name values, it returns the corresponding row from Target columns. Use the "*" wildcard for the Trading Partner Name and Criteria cells to apply certain rules regardless of the Trading Partner Name and Criteria values. This way, you can have rules that are always applied to every chunk of incoming data.


Example


Sample Crosswalk:



Lookup parameters:


   Trading Partner = "GreatHospital";


   Criteria = "Correction";


Crosswalk return value:


[


   {


      "Path":"GH/update_postal_code_correction.drl",


      "Sequence": 1,


      "Disabled": false,


      "Property": "test"


   },


   {


      "Path":"Generic/birth_date_check_rule.drl",


      "Sequence": 1,


      "Disabled": false,


      "Property": "teststs"


   },


   {


      "Path":"GH/update_postal_code.drl",


      "Sequence": 1,


      "Disabled": false,


      "Property": "test"


   }


]


Note When the Business Rules component accesses a crosswalk, the crosswalk's Refreshable cache type acts in a way that is identical to Full. It means that, when the component loads a crosswalk file to the memory on the profile startup, it is not refreshed until you restart the profile.

---

<a id="explicit-list-configuration-method"></a>

##### Explicit List Configuration Method

> 📄 Source: [Explicit_List_Configuration_Method.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Explicit_List_Configuration_Method.htm)

###### Explicit List Configuration Method


This configuration method presumes that the Business Rules component expects an XEServer multimessage exchange on input where the first message contains JSON instructions on what rules to apply and the other messages contain actual XML/JSON business data.


The body of the first XEServer message with JSON instructions looks like the following:


Example


 [{


      "path": "Generic/birth_date_check_rule.drl",


      "sequence": 1,


      "disabled": false,


      "properties": {


         "prop2": "Prop2",


         "prop1": "Prop2"


       }


   }]


You should use this configuration method when the list of rules is dynamically determined by some Trading Partner Management system.

---

<a id="external-file-configuration-method"></a>

##### External File Configuration Method

> 📄 Source: [External_File_Configuration_Method.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/External_File_Configuration_Method.htm)

###### External File Configuration Method


This method allows the component to read an external JSON configuration file and select the corresponding rule by matching Trading Partner and Criteria values. You can consider this method as an alternative to the Crosswalk configuration method when an external JSON file is easier to maintain and the user-friendly user interface of SpecBuilder Crosswalk Browser is not required.


A typical configuration file looks like the following:


Example


{


 "partnerRules": {


  "<TradingPartnerName>": [


   {


    "criteria": "*",


    "rules": [


     {


      "path": "GH/update_postal_code_all.drl",


      "sequence": 1,


      "disabled": false,


      "properties": {


       "prop2": "Prop2",


       "prop1": "Prop2"


      }


     }


    ]


   }


  ]


 }


}


Use the "*" wildcard for the Trading Partner Name and Criteria JSON values to apply certain rules regardless of the Trading Partner Name and Criteria values. This way, you can have rules that are always applied to every chunk of incoming data with the same logic of application.

---

<a id="logging-and-reporting"></a>

##### Logging And Reporting

> 📄 Source: [Logging_and_Reporting.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Logging_and_Reporting.htm)

###### Logging and Reporting


The Business Rules component allows you to generate XML reports that contain information about rules execution. The component sends reports through the report output and includes the following information:

- The date and time of rule execution.
- The component configuration: A Rules Root path, a configuration method, an input format, and so on.
- A list of invoked rule files.
- Event statistics: Information on the occurred events at every severity level.
- A description of the event occurred.
- Context properties: A list of the context properties that were passed to the component.


The Business Rules Report looks like the following:


<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>


<report>


   <ExecutionDate>03/30/2017 05:44:08 PM</ExecutionDate>


   <ExecutionTime>404ms</ExecutionTime>


   <Configuration>


      <RulesRoot>C:/Edifecs/XEServer/profiles/Business Rules Engine_1/config/config-files/Business Rules/GH</RulesRoot>


      <ConfigurationMethod>Explicit Folder</ConfigurationMethod>


      <InputFormat>XML</InputFormat>


   </Configuration>


   <ExecutableRulePaths>


      <ExecutableRulePath>update_postal_code.drl</ExecutableRulePath>


      <ExecutableRulePath>update_postal_code_correction.drl</ExecutableRulePath>


   </ExecutableRulePaths>


   <EventStatistics>


      <CriticalEventCount>0</CriticalEventCount>


      <ErrorEventCount>1</ErrorEventCount>


      <InformationEventCount>0</InformationEventCount>


      <WarningEventCount>0</WarningEventCount>


   </EventStatistics>


   <Events>


      <Event>


         <ID>1</ID>


         <Description>path=update_postal_code_correction, text=Rule Compilation error $claimarty cannot be resolved, line=7, column=0


         </Description>


         <Severity>ERROR</Severity>


         <Properties/>


      </Event>


   </Events>


</report>


In addition to XML reports generated for every chunk of incoming data, XEServer logs all events in the business.rules.log file located at %XEServer%\profiles\<ProfileName>\log\. You can define the event severity level (ERROR, WARNING, INFO, and so on) at the profile level as for any other log file.

---

<a id="business-rules-api"></a>

##### Business Rules API

> 📄 Source: [Business_Rules_API.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Business_Rules_API.htm)

###### Business Rules API


The Business Rules API includes the following base Java classes. Instances of these classes are automatically available within the body of each rule (with the corresponding imported package) and instances like Context (see below) allow the component to carry information across multiple rules when necessary.


| Class | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Class Context | You can use objects of this class to allow sharing data (name-value pairs) between different compiled rules. Package: com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api Constructor: Context(Map<String,Object> properties) Methods: Map<String,Object> getCustomData() - Returns custom data objects passed to the Context object, if any. Map<String,Object> getProperties() - Returns context properties passed to the Context object, if any. To use the Context object in your rules, you should do the following: Import the com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api.Context class to your rule file. Create a reference to the Context object in the rule's when() block:...whencontext: Context()     // Create the referenceString data = (String)context.getProperties().get("CrosswalkName")     // Call the method...When an instance of the Context class is created, it includes XEServer message exchange/message headers (properties). |
| Class EventCollection | You can use objects of this class to add events to rules execution. The events that you add are included in the Business Rules reports. Package: com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api Constructor: EventCollection() Methods: void addEvent(Event event) - Adds a new event type. Collection<Event> getEvents() - Returns a collection of available events. |
| Class Event | You can use objects of this class to create custom rule events for registration in the EventCollection. Package: com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api Methods: String getBrief() - Returns a brief description of the event. String getErrorData() - Returns the error data of the event. String getDescription() - Returns a description of the event. String getId() - Returns the event ID. Map<String,String> getProperties() - Returns the event properties Event.ESeverity getSeverity() - Returns the severity of the event. static Event.Builder newBuilder() - Creates and returns a reference to the new Event object. |
| Enum Event.ESeverity | You can use objects of this class to define the severity of events. Package: com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api Enum Constants: CRITICAL ERROR INFORMATION WARNING Methods: static Event.ESeverity valueOf(String name) - Returns the enum constant with the specified name. static Event.ESeverity[] values() - Returns an array of enum constants in the order they are declared.   |
| Class Logger | Objects of this class are used for logging information about business rules execution. Package: com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api Constructor: Logger() Methods: public void write (String message) - Writes the message string to the log file. Messages are logged at the INFO level. |

---

<a id="work-with-rule-files"></a>

##### Work with Rule Files

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Rule_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Work_with_Rule_Files.htm)

###### Work with Rule Files


This section describes base operations with built-in Business Rules API classes, such as Logger, EventCollection, Context, and so on. The highlighted comments in the sample code blocks describe the rule execution.


All sample rules described in this section refer to the same Java Object Model:

 Sample Java Object Model


public class Provider


{


private String provider_ID;


private String criteria;


private String tP;


private List<Line> lines = null;


public String getProviderID()


{


return provider_ID;


}


public void setProviderID(String provider_ID)


{


this.provider_ID = provider_ID;


}


public String getCriteria()


{


return criteria;


}


public void setCriteria(String criteria)


{


this.criteria = criteria;


}


public String getTP()


{


return tP;


}


public void setTP(String tP)


{


this.tP = tP;


}


public List<Line> getLines()


{


return lines;


}


public void setLines(List<Line> lines)


{


this.lines = lines;


}


}


public class Line {


private String city;


private String state_Code;


private String postal_Code;


private String birth_Date;


private String qualifier;


private String code;


private String sequence_Number;


private String birth_Date_Format_Code;


public String getCity() {


return city;


}


public void setCity(String city) {


this.city = city;


}


public String getStateCode() {


return state_Code;


}


public void setStateCode(String state_Code) {


this.state_Code = state_Code;


}


public String getPostalCode() {


return postal_Code;


}


public void setPostalCode(String postal_Code) {


this.postal_Code = postal_Code;


}


public String getBirthDate() {


return birth_Date;


}


public void setBirthDate(String birth_Date) {


this.birth_Date = birth_Date;


}


public String getQualifier() {


return qualifier;


}


public void setQualifier(String qualifier) {


this.qualifier = qualifier;


}


public String getCode() {


return code;


}


public void setCode(String code) {


this.code = code;


}


public String getSequenceNumber() {


return sequence_Number;


}


public void setSequenceNumber(String sequence_Number) {


this.sequence_Number = sequence_Number;


}


public String getBirthDateFormatCode() {


return birth_Date_Format_Code;


}


public void setBirthDateFormatCode(String birth_Date_Format_Code) {


this.birth_Date_Format_Code = birth_Date_Format_Code;


}


}






###### Work with the EventCollection and Event objects


The following rule file illustrates how to add a custom event in a rule file.

 SampleRule1.drl


import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api.EventCollection; // Ensure that the required class is imported to the rule.


import com.edifecs.generated.Provider;


import com.edifecs.generated.Line;


import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api.Context;


import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api.Event;


import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api.Logger;


import java.math.BigInteger;


import java.util.Map;


rule "SampleRule1"


when


$provider: Provider (getLines().size()>0)


$line: Line (getQualifier() == 1) from $provider.getLines()


collection: EventCollection() // Get a reference to the EventCollection object from Drools engine.


context: Context()


then


System.out.println("-------------- Rule2 - Seq: 2 ---------");


Event.Builder b = Event.newBuilder(); // Create a new Event Builder object.


b.id("ID:1"); // Asign the property "ID" = 1 pair to the Event object.


b.description("Rule2 - Seq: 2"); // Asign the "description" property to the Event object.


b.severity(Event.ESeverity.valueOf("WARNING")); // Set the event severity level.


collection.addEvent(b.build()); // Add the created Event to EventCollection object.


end






###### Work with the Logger object


The following rule file illustrates how to write data to the log file using the Logger object.

 SampleRule2.drl


import com.edifecs.generated.Provider;


import com.edifecs.generated.Line;


import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api.Logger;


import java.math.BigInteger;


rule "SampleRule2"


when


logger: Logger() // Get a reference to the Logger object from Drools engine.


$provider: Provider (getLines().size()>0)


$line: Line (getQualifier() == 1) from $provider.getLines() // If the condition (getQualifier() == 1) is true, the rule's then block executes.


then


logger.write(“found - ” +$line); // Write the message to the log file.


end






###### Work with Context object


The following rule file illustrates how to set properties to the Context object that is used for carrying information across multiple rules.

 SampleRule3.drl


import com.edifecs.generated.Provider;


import com.edifecs.generated.Line;


import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api.Context;


import java.math.BigInteger;


rule "SampleRule3"


when


context: Context() // Get a reference to the Context object from Drools engine.


$provider: Provider (getLines().size()>0)


$line: Line (getQualifier() == 1) from $provider.getLines()


then


context.getProperties().put(“foundLine”,$line.toString()); // Add a new field to the Context object "foundline"="$line". To access this property from a different rule, use


end                                             // context.getProperties().get(“foundLine”);






###### Work with List object


The following rule file illustrates data handling if the incoming data has a JSON format and the component's JSON Array on Root Level is selected (an array of JSON objects is expected on the input).

 SampleRule4.drl


import com.edifecs.generated.Provider;


import com.edifecs.generated.Line;


import java.math.BigInteger;


import java.util.*;


import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.business.rules.api.Logger;


rule "SampleRule4"


when


logger: Logger()


$providers: List(size()>0) // Get reference to the List object that holds an array of objects.


$provider: Provider (getLines().size()>0)from $providers // Iterate through the List array.


then


logger.write($provider.toString()); // Write information about the provider to the log file.


end

<a id="cloud-archive-components"></a>

#### Cloud Archive Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_Cloud_Archive_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Cloud_Archive_Components.htm)

##### Cloud Archive Components
[Redirect to: {href}](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Components_Overview.htm)

---

<a id="cloud-archive-components-overview"></a>

##### Cloud Archive Components Overview

> 📄 Source: [Cloud_Archive_Components_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Components_Overview.htm)

###### Cloud Archive Components Overview


XEServer provides you with convenient and versatile Cloud Archive route components for interaction with [Smart Trading Archive Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Welcome_to_Archival_Service.htm). With these components, you can perform the following operations directly from XEServer routes:

- Search files
- Upload files
- Download files


The route components use the [Cloud Archive Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Service.htm) to establish a connection with the Smart Trading Archive Service.


Note Before you start working with the Smart Trading Archival Service, create a [Smart Trading account](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Create_Account.htm) and enable the Archival service subscription. For more information, see the [help documentation](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Welcome_to_Archival_Service.htm) of this service.


###### Smart Trading Cloud domain


By default, XEServer connects to the Smart Trading Account service hosted on [https://edifecsfedcloud.com](https://edifecsfedcloud.com/).
