If you want to work with the Smart Trading Cloud services hosted on a different domain (for example, www.cоllablynk.com), uncomment the required domain name in ${ECRootPath}\Common\cloud.properties. After you update the file, EAM restart is required.


###### Cloud Archive interaction logs


The archival components have a dedicated logger Cloud Archive that logs the process of communication between XEServer and the Smart Trading Archive service to a separate log file located at {XESRoot}/profiles/<profileName>/log/cloud.archive.log. By setting the [log level](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Logger_Tab.htm) to Trace or Debug, you can view detailed information about each request-response exchange.

---

<a id="cloud-archive-download"></a>

##### Cloud Archive Download

> 📄 Source: [Cloud_Archive_Download_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Download_Component.htm)

###### Cloud Archive Download


This component is used to download data from the [Smart Trading Archival service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Welcome_to_Archival_Service.htm). The component uses the XEServer [Cloud Archive service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Service.htm) to connect to the Smart Trading Archival service. The component's functionality can be used in the following cases:

- To reprocess files in case of a processing failure and the file does not reach the expected disposition.
- For audit purposes.


To download a file from the Smart Trading Archival service, the component must provide a special file ID. To get this ID, use the [Cloud Archive Search](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Search_Component.htm) component that can pass file ID and other metadata to Cloud Archive Download in the form of message properties.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service Name | Select a [Cloud Account Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Service.htm) used to establish a connection with the Smart Trading Archival service. |
| File ID   | The ID of the file to be downloaded from the Smart Trading Archival service. By default, the component is configured to extract the file ID from the search results received from the [Cloud Archive Search](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Search_Component.htm) component ({MD_CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_ID}). |

---

<a id="cloud-archive-search"></a>

##### Cloud Archive Search

> 📄 Source: [Cloud_Archive_Search_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Search_Component.htm)

###### Cloud Archive Search


This component is used to search for files in the [Smart Trading Archival service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Welcome_to_Archival_Service.htm) and returns metadata of the found files. The component uses the XEServer [Cloud Archive service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Service.htm) to connect to the Smart Trading Archival service.


For each file found, the component creates one XEServer exchange with a single message carrying the ID of the found file in the message body. For each exchange, the component sets the following message properties with the file metadata (can be accessed through {Prp[...]}).

- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_Total_Files_Count: the total files count that matched the search request.
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_Total_Files_Size: the total size of all files that matched the search request
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_Creation_Date
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_Size
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_Name
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_Business_Object_Type
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_Metadata
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_ID
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_Path
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_User_ID
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_Namespace
- CLOUD_ARCHIVE_File_Timestamp


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


On the Settings tab, you can specify the search parameters.


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Select a [Cloud Accound Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Service.htm) used to establish a connection with the Smart Trading Archival Service. |
| Namespaces | Specify the namespace. |
| Business types | Specify the type of the business item. |
| File name | Specify the name of the file. |
| Tracking number | Specify the file tracking number. |
| Creation date | |
| From | Specify the start date for the creation time frame for the search. |
| To | Specify the end date for the creation time frame for the search. |



###### Advanced tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Submission Date | |
| From | Specify the start date for the submission time frame for the search. |
| To | Specify the end date for the submission time frame for the search. |
| Size | |
| From | Specify the minimum file size for the search. |
| To | Specify the maximum file size for the search. |
| Search limit | Specify the number of files for the search limit. |
| Metadata Query | Specify additional metadata query name-value pairs for the search. |

---

<a id="cloud-archive-upload"></a>

##### Cloud Archive Upload

> 📄 Source: [Cloud_Archive_Upload_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Upload_Component.htm)

###### Cloud Archive Upload


This component is used to upload data to the [Smart Trading Archival service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Welcome_to_Archival_Service.htm). The component uses the XEServer [Cloud Archive service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Service.htm) to connect to the Smart Trading Archival service. As the result of the upload, the component returns the original data along with the metadata generated by the Archival service.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Select a [Cloud Accound Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Cloud_Archive_Service.htm) used to establish a connection with the Smart Trading Archival service. |
| Namespace | Select the namespace for the file. |
| Business type | Select the business type for the file. |
| File name | Specify the name of the file to be uploaded. |
| Tracking number | Specify the file tracking number. |
| Creation Date | Specify the file creation date. |



###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can manage key-value metadata transferred to the endpoint destination: for example, to a directory on a file system, web server, JMS queue, and so on. Depending on the endpoint destination, metadata are stored in the format that is suitable for a certain destination environment. For example, for FS components attributes are stored in the form of key-value pairs in .property files, for JMS components, attributes are mapped to queue message metadata, and so on.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select this option to send exchange properties for further processing. |
| **Include message properties** | Select this option to send message properties for further processing. |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane, you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to the metadata. |

---

<a id="db-components"></a>

#### DB Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_DB_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_DB_Components.htm)

##### DB Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="db-reader"></a>

##### DB Reader

> 📄 Source: [DB_Reader.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Reader.htm)

###### DB Reader


Note Use of this routing component requires a Professional XEServer license. In order to unlock these capabilities, please obtain the Professional license through Edifecs Customer Support.


The DB Reader component is used to read data from a database and direct the fetched data to the route for processing. The component uses XEServer [DB Connection Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Connection_Service.htm) to establish the connection to a database. The component retrieves data from a database, and forwards this data to the route in one of the three formats: an XEServer message exchange (returns a separate message for each row or a batched multimessage exchange), a delimited flat file (DFF), or XML file.


**Note** If [jTDS](http://jtds.sourceforge.net/) driver is used and successful database connectivity has already been tested via other components, disable the **Connection check on start** setting in [DB Connection Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Connection_Service.htm) component to allow the DB Connection Service to start. This is due to the fact that the jTDS driver does not support the latest features of MS SQL Server. For more information, see [JDBC Connectivity Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JDBC_Connectivity_Overview.htm#jTDS).


All input message properties from input message are copied to outgoing messages properties of DB Reader component. The following is a sample representation of database in XML and DFF formats:

 XML:

```




			123
			TEST DATA
			03/12/2010


			123
			TEST DATA
			03/12/2010


			123
			TEST DATA
			03/12/2010



```





 DFF:

```
123~TEST DATA~03/12/2010
123~TEST DATA~03/12/2010
123~TEST DATA~03/12/2010
123~TEST DATA~03/12/2010
```






When the component is added to a route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: DB Reader Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service Name** | Select the service used to establish the connection to a database. |
| Import database configuration from SpecBuilder   | Click to navigate to the location of SpecBuilder .ecs file, which contains DB configuration. Note All existing SQL statements are replaced while import. |
| Transaction In this section, you can specify database transaction settings. | |
| **Local** | Select Local if you don't use this component between [DB Transaction Open](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Open_Overview.htm) and [DB Transaction Close](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Close_Overview.htm) components in one route, and specify the isolation level for component's SQL transactions: TRANSACTION_READ_COMMITTED: At the Read Committed isolation level, the transaction sees only data committed by concurrent SQL transactions before the transaction execution began. It doesn't see uncommitted data and changes committed during transaction execution. TRANSACTION_READ_UNCOMMITTED: At the Read Uncommitted isolation level, transactions don't issue shared locks when modifying data and are also not prevented from reading data that is being modified by other transactions. This means that transactions can see uncommitted changes made by other concurrent transactions. TRANSACTION_REPEATABLE_READ: At the Repeatable Read isolation level, the transaction holds locks on the rows it references, preventing other transactions from reading, updating or deleting these rows. TRANSACTION_SERIALIZABLE: At the Serializable isolation level, concurrent transactions commit changes one after another, thus appearing to be executed serially (one at a time). The first completed transaction wins, making other transactions start their execution from the beginning. Note Before selecting the SQL transaction isolation level for the component, refer to the documentation of the database you use to make sure this level is implemented in your database. If Use auto-commit is enabled, the automatic commit for a single batch (or individual record if batching is disabled) is performed. Use this option to improve the performance, but note that it affects data integrity. For example, if the connection to your database is lost, or an internal database error occurs, the data that has already been written to the database by the moment of the error is not rolled back as it would be in the case with the auto-commit option disabled. |
| **Shared** | Select Shared when the component is part of a DB transaction containing multiple DB Reader or DB Writer components between [DB Transaction Open](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Open_Overview.htm) and [DB Transaction Close](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Close_Overview.htm) components. In the box next to the Shared setting, provide the transaction ID property value that is specified in the DB Transaction Open component and shared among the DB transaction's components. For example, this property could be expressed as {Prp[DBConnectorTransactionID]}. |



###### SQL Statements tab


On this tab, you can declare context variables and enter SQL queries that should be performed by the component.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Statements context** | Use this pane to [add local context variables](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Variables_in_SQL_Requests.htm) that can be used in your SQL expressions in future. |
| **Statements groups** | Use this pane to enter SQL expressions that should be performed by the component. For more information about creating or editing SQL statements, see [Configure SQL Statements](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Configure_SQL_Statements.htm). |



###### Output Handling tab


On this tab, you can define a data format to which data retrieved from a database should be converted before sending to a component's output. Only the data which is retrieved in the form of a result set is processed and converted on this tab.


| Field | Description | Parameters |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Batch results to one exchange | Select this option to accumulate records from a database and to send them within one XEServer message exchange once all the records of a result set are processed. If this option is cleared, each result retrieved from the database is sent for further processing as a separate XEServer message exchange. |   |
| Edifecs Database DFF Format | Select this option to convert results retrieved from the database into a delimited flat file. Each record of a result set is converted into a single line of a DFF where the values are split by a separator character. ExampleSampleGroup,John,Smith,12345SampleGroup,Tom,Johnson,54321Data is sent through one of the component outputs as an XEServer message that has a body with a flat file. | encoding: Select a character set to be used. Escape: Select a character for escaping characters. If the value is provided (the default value is \), then the component escapes all special characters (comma, double quotes) with the specified escape character (e.g., \). If the parameter Escape is removed from the configuration, XEServer uses RFC 4180 field quotation behavior. If a field value contains a comma or double quotes, this field should be quoted, meaning that the double quotation character is used as the start and end marker of the field. In addition, if a field contains double quotes characters, the embedded double quotes characters must be represented by a pair of double quotes characters. Note The parameter Escape is not mandatory starting from EAM/XEServer 9.2.2 Hotfix 2. Header in first line: Set the value to TRUE if you want the first line of the generated DFF file to be a header that contains a list of field names. Quote: Select a character to be used as a quote. Separator: Select a character to be used as a separator. Only single character is allowed. For this option, only one non-printable tab character "\t" (Tab) can be used. table name in first column - Adds a name of an SQL statement group to the first position of each DFF line. If the value is set to TRUE, the name of a statement group is added.For example: Eligibility,1,875439,Max,SeattleEligibility,2,123456,Dorothy,NYThe marked field "Eligibility" is the name of the Statement group that performed a query to a database, thus it is added to the beginning of DFF lines. |
| Edifecs Database XML Format | Select this option to convert data from the database to XML.Example<DataSet><SQL_Statements name="SampleGroup"><Row><Name>John</Name><Surname>Smith</Surname><ZIP>12345</ZIP></Row><Row><Name>Tom</Name><Surname>Johnson</Surname><ZIP>54321</ZIP></Row></SQL_Statements></DataSet>Data is sent through one of the component outputs as an XEServer message that has a body with generated XML contents. | encoding: Select a character set to be used. |
| No Output | Select this option if you do not need content from the database. |   |
| XEngine Server Exchange | Select this option if you are going to pass extracted data in the form of XEServer Exchange.For each row in a record set, a separate XEServer message exchange with one XEServer message inside of it is generated. These message exchanges are sent to output only when all records of a record set are processed.If the Batch results to one exchange option is selected, all messages are sent within one XEServer message exchange when all records of a record set are processed.Note Accumulation of messages for very large result sets may lead to a java.lang.OutOfMemoryError exception.The Send message on creation option allows you to read large volumes of data from a database with efficient RAM consumption.To send an XEServer message as soon as the current row processing finishes, and to not wait until the rest of the rows in a result set are processed, select the Send message on creation option. However, this option has a lower priority to the Batch results to one exchange option. In other words, if Batch results to one exchange is selected, the Send message on creation value is ignored. | Column name for message content: Define the name of the column whose values should be set to an XEServer message body in the form of binary code. All other values are set as ColumnName-ColumnValue pair to XEServer message headers. Header prefix: Enter a prefix that is added to XEServer headers carrying ColumnName-ColumnValue pair. Headers as strings: Select whether content from a database column should be converted to String data type. |

---

<a id="db-transaction-open"></a>

##### DB Transaction Open

> 📄 Source: [DB_Transaction_Open_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Open_Overview.htm)

###### DB Transaction Open


Note Use of this routing component requires a Professional XEServer license. In order to unlock these capabilities, please obtain the Professional license through Edifecs Customer Support.


The DB Transaction Open component lets the route contain multiple DB Reader and Writer in the context of a single database transaction. For example, a route with a database transaction could use a DB Reader component to retrieve data, an XEngine component to process the data, and a DB Writer component to update a database record. Use the DB Transaction Close component to end the database transaction.


*[Image: DB Transaction Open Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service Name** | The name of the DB Connection Service. |
| **Transaction ID** | Macros used to specify the database connector identifier of the transaction. This ID is unique and is a property that is attached to the output of the DB Transaction Open component. |
| **Expiration (ms)** | The length of time (in milliseconds) that transpires after which the DB transaction is considered expired and any database updates are rolled back. |
| **Isolation** | Specify the level of isolation for the SQL transaction. The level is used for each DB Writer and DB Reader component in the transaction: TRANSACTION_READ_COMMITTED: At the Read Committed isolation level, the transaction sees only data committed by concurrent SQL transactions before the transaction execution began. It doesn't see uncommitted data and changes committed during transaction execution. TRANSACTION_READ_UNCOMMITTED: At the Read Uncommitted isolation level, transactions don't issue shared locks when modifying data and are also not prevented from reading data that is being modified by other transactions. This means that transactions can see uncommitted changes made by other concurrent transactions. TRANSACTION_REPEATABLE_READ: At the Repeatable Read isolation level, the transaction holds locks on the rows it references, preventing other transactions from reading, updating or deleting these rows. TRANSACTION_SERIALIZABLE: At the Serializable isolation level, concurrent transactions commit changes one after another, thus appearing to be executed serially (one at a time). The first completed transaction wins, making other transactions start their execution from the beginning. Note Before selecting the SQL transaction isolation level for the component, refer to the documentation of the database you use to make sure this level is implemented in your database. |

---

<a id="db-transaction-close"></a>

##### DB Transaction Close

> 📄 Source: [DB_Transaction_Close_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Close_Overview.htm)

###### DB Transaction Close


Note Use of this routing component requires a Professional XEServer license. In order to unlock these capabilities, please obtain the Professional license through Edifecs Customer Support.


The DB Transaction Close component closes a database transaction that contains multiple DB Reader and Writer components in the context of a single database transaction.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service Name** | The name of the DB Connection Service. |
| **ID** | Provide the transaction ID property value that is specified in the DB Transaction Open component and shared among the DB transaction's components. For example, this property could be expressed as {Prp[DBConnectorTransactionID]}. |

---

<a id="db-writer"></a>

##### DB Writer

> 📄 Source: [DB_Writer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Writer.htm)

###### DB Writer


Note Use of this routing component requires a Professional XEServer license. In order to unlock these capabilities, please obtain the Professional license through Edifecs Customer Support.


With the DB Writer component, you can perform INSERT and UPDATE operations with a database.


*[Image: DB Writer Settings tab]*


**Note** If [jTDS](http://jtds.sourceforge.net/) driver is used and successful database connectivity has already been tested via other components, disable the **Connection check on start** setting in [DB Connection Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Connection_Service.htm) component to allow the DB Connection Service to start. This is due to the fact that the jTDS driver does not support the latest features of MS SQL Server. For more information, see [JDBC Connectivity Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JDBC_Connectivity_Overview.htm#jTDS).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service Name** | The name of the DB Connection Service. |
| Import database configuration from SpecBuilder | Click to navigate to the location of SpecBuilder .ecs file, which contains DB configuration. Note All existing SQL statements are replaced while import. |
| **Transaction (Local)** | Select Local if you don't use this component between [DB Transaction Open](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Open_Overview.htm) and [DB Transaction Close](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Close_Overview.htm) components in one route, and specify the isolation level for component's SQL transactions: TRANSACTION_READ_COMMITTED: At the Read Committed isolation level, the transaction sees only data committed by concurrent SQL transactions before the transaction execution began. It doesn't see uncommitted data and changes committed during transaction execution. TRANSACTION_READ_UNCOMMITTED: At the Read Uncommitted isolation level, transactions don't issue shared locks when modifying data and are also not prevented from reading data that is being modified by other transactions. This means that transactions can see uncommitted changes made by other concurrent transactions. TRANSACTION_REPEATABLE_READ: At the Repeatable Read isolation level, the transaction holds locks on the rows it references, preventing other transactions from reading, updating or deleting these rows. TRANSACTION_SERIALIZABLE: At the Serializable isolation level, concurrent transactions commit changes one after another, thus appearing to be executed serially (one at a time). The first completed transaction wins, making other transactions start their execution from the beginning. Note Before selecting the SQL transaction isolation level for the component, refer to the documentation of the database you use to make sure this level is implemented in your database. If Use auto-commit is enabled, the automatic commit for a single batch (or individual record if batching is disabled) is performed. Using this option can improve the performance, but at the cost of data integrity. For example, if the connection to your database is lost, or some internal database error occurs, the data that was already written to the database by the moment of the error is not rolled back as it would be in the case without the auto-commit option enabled. |
| **Transaction (Shared)** | Select Shared when the component is part of a DB transaction containing multiple DB Reader or DB Writer components between [DB Transaction Open](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Open_Overview.htm) and [DB Transaction Close](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Transaction_Close_Overview.htm) components. In the box next to the Shared setting, provide the transaction ID property value that is specified in the DB Transaction Open component and shared among the DB transaction's components. For example, this property could be expressed as {Prp[DBConnectorTransactionID]}. |



###### Input Handling tab


On this tab, you can define a data format that is expected by the DB Writer component on input.


| Field | Description | Parameters |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Edifecs Database DFF Format | Select this option if the DFF format is expected on input. You can use [index-based variables](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Column_Index_in_SQL_Requests.htm) to access DFF fields and reuse them in SQL expressions. When the DB Writer component receives a DFF file on input, the component performs every SQL Statement in every Statement group one time for every DFF line in the order these SQL Statements/Statements groups are defined in the Statement groups pane. | encoding: Select the character set used. Escape: Select the character used for escaping characters. If the value is provided (the default value is \), then the component escapes all special characters (commas, double quotes) with the specified escape character (e.g., \). If the parameter Escape is removed from the configuration, XEServer uses RFC 4180 field quotation behavior. If a field value contains a comma or double quotes, this field should be quoted, meaning that the double quotation character is used as the start and end marker of the field. In addition, if a field contains double quotes characters, the embedded double quotes must be represented by a pair of double quotes characters.Note The parameter Escape is not mandatory starting from EAM/XEServer 9.2.2 Hotfix 2. Header in first line: Set the value to TRUE if the first line of incoming DFF file is a header that contains a list of field names. Quote: Select the character used as a quote. Separator: Select the character used as a separator. Only single character is allowed. For this option, only one non-printable tab character "\t" (Tab) can be used. table name in first column - Select whether incoming DFF files should contain a name of SQL statement group at the first position of each DFF line.For example: Eligibility,1,875439,Max,SeattleEligibility,2,123456,Dorothy,NYThe marked field "Eligibility" is the name of the Statement group, thus for correct parsing of DFF file, the table name in first column parameter should be set to TRUE. |
| Edifecs Database XML Format | Select this option if the XML format is expected on input.To make the component write data to a database, the names of SQL Statement Groups should strictly match the names of nodes inside the <DataSet></DataSet> node in incoming Edifecs Database XML files. On the following example, such nodes are marked green.Example<DataSet><Claims><Row>...</Row>...</Claims>...</DataSet>For this sample .xml file, the SQL Statement Group named Claims should be created. In this case, all SQL Statements within the Claims SQL Statement Group are performed for every <Row></Row> node inside <Claims></Claims>.If a node has name attribute, for example <Claims name="test">, the name of SQL Statement Group should be "test".If SQL Statement Groups do not match, the component still forwards original data to an output, however the data is not written to the database. | encoding: Select the character set used. |
| XEngine Server Exchange | Select this option if the component expects input data in the form of XEServer Exchange properties. |   |



By using the option Empty string is NULL, you can define how to handle empty strings on input. If selected, empty DFF fields/XML nodes are written to the database as NULL values.


With the Parse "NULL" string as NULL option, you can define whether to transform the "null" string (case insensitive) to the NULL database value. If selected, DFF fields/XML nodes that contain "NULL"/"Null"/"null" strings are written to the database as NULL values.


###### SQL Statements tab


On this tab, you can declare context variables and enter SQL queries that should be performed by the component.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Statements context** | Use this pane to [add local context variables](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Variables_in_SQL_Requests.htm) that can be used in your SQL expressions in future. |
| **Statements groups** | Use this pane to enter SQL expressions that should be performed by the component. For more information about creating or editing SQL statements, see [Configure SQL Statements](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Configure_SQL_Statements.htm). |



###### SQL Statement Batching


With this option, you can allow the component to make several SQL queries to a database within a single transaction instead of making a separate call for every SQL expression. The Batch size option is used to set the number of SQL Statements that should be batched before calling a database.


When working with the DB Writer component that expects DFF data format on the input (Input Format = "Edifecs Database DFF Format"), there is one peculiarity in batching process at which you should pay attention. There can be two types of behavior depending on the Table name in first column property:

1. If the property is set to FALSE, the batching is performed in context of the whole incoming DFF file. In other words, every SQL statement is performed one by one for every DFF record, and as soon as the number of records reaches the batch size, the transaction is committed to database.
2. If the property is set to TRUE, the batching is performed in context of Statement groups. In other words, every SQL statements is performed one by one for every DFF record, and as soon as the number of records that belong to the same Statement group (the first field in a DFF record) reaches the batch size, the transaction is committed to database.


Example


| Table name in first column = FALSE | Table name in first column = FALSE |
| --- | --- |
| Batch: On Batch Size: 3 Input DFF file:   John, Smith, 548624, WA Alex, Anderson, 541241, CA Christina, Tyler, 276123, NY Lindsey, Adams, 54084, AR Luke, Tyler, 127231, TX // Incomplete batch | Batch: On Batch Size: 3 Input DFF file:   Group1, John, Smith, 548624, WA Group1, Alex, Anderson, 541241, CA Group1, Christina, Tyler, 276123, NY Group1, Luke, Tyler, 127231, TX Group1, Shane, Smith, 246889, OH // Incomplete batch Group2, James, Wane, 987520, MS Group2, Ryan, Woodley, 127231, TX Group2, Richard, Jackson, 145231, TX |
| //After processing the marked DFF records, a batch is formed and submitted to a database. | //After processing the marked DFF records, a batch is formed and submitted to a database. |



If a batch cannot be formed (the number of remaining DFF records is less than the batch size), an incomplete batch for remaining records is submitted to a database.

---

<a id="db-output-handling-tab"></a>

##### DB Output Handling Tab

> 📄 Source: [Output_Handling_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Output_Handling_Tab.htm)

###### DB Output Handling Tab


On this tab, you can define a data format to which data retrieved from a database should be converted before sending to a component's output. Only the data which is retrieved in the form of a result set is processed and converted on this tab.


When the component is added to a route, the following options are available on the Output handling tab.


| Field | Description | Parameters |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Batch results to one exchange | Select this option to accumulate records from a database and to send them within one XEServer message exchange once all the records of a result set are processed. If this option is cleared, each result retrieved from the database is sent for further processing as a separate XEServer message exchange. |   |
| Edifecs Database DFF Format | Select this option to convert results retrieved from the database into a delimited flat file. Each record of a result set is converted into a single line of a DFF where the values are split by a separator character. ExampleSampleGroup,John,Smith,12345SampleGroup,Tom,Johnson,54321Data is sent through one of the component outputs as an XEServer message that has a body with a flat file. | encoding: Select a character set to be used. Escape: Select a character for escaping characters. Quote: Select a character to be used as a quote. Separator: Select a character to be used as a separator. Only single character is allowed. For this option, only one non-printable tab character "\t" (Tab) can be used. table name in first column - Adds a name of an SQL statement group to each line of the DFF. If the value is set to TRUE, the name of a statement group containing the SQL statement or SQL procedure which returned the current result set, is added. For example: Eligibility,1,875439,Max,Seattle Eligibility,2,123456,Dorothy,NY The marked field "Eligibility" is the name of the Statement group that performed a query to a database, thus it is added to the beginning of DFF lines. |
| Edifecs Database XML Format | Select this option to convert data from the database to XML.Example<DataSet><SQL_Statements name="SampleGroup"><Row><Name>John</Name><Surname>Smith</Surname><ZIP>12345</ZIP></Row><Row><Name>Tom</Name><Surname>Johnson</Surname><ZIP>54321</ZIP></Row></SQL_Statements></DataSet>Data is sent through one of the component outputs as an XEServer message that has a body with generated XML contents. | encoding: Select a character set to be used. |
| No Output | Select this option if you do not need content from the database. |   |
| XEngine Server Exchange | Select this option if you are going to pass extracted data in the form of XEServer Exchange.For each row in a record set, a separate XEServer message exchange with one XEServer message inside of it is generated. These message exchanges are sent to output only when all records of a record set are processed.If the Batch results to one exchange option is selected, all messages are sent within one XEServer message exchange when all records of a record set are processed.Note Accumulation of messages for very large result sets may lead to a java.lang.OutOfMemoryError exception.The Send message on creation option allows you to read large volumes of data from a database with efficient RAM consumption.To send an XEServer message as soon as the current row processing finishes, and to not wait until the rest of the rows in a result set are processed, select the Send message on creation option. However, this option has a lower priority to the Batch results to one exchange option. In other words, if Batch results to one exchange is selected, the Send message on creation value is ignored. | Column name for message content: Define the name of the column whose values should be set to an XEServer message body in the form of binary code. All other values are set as ColumnName-ColumnValue pair to XEServer message headers. Header prefix: Enter a prefix that is added to XEServer headers carrying ColumnName-ColumnValue pair. Headers as strings: Select whether content from a database column should be converted to String data type. |



The parameters mentioned in the table have default values which satisfy the majority of business cases. However, you can change them to fit your business environment.

---

<a id="configure-sql-statements"></a>

##### Configure SQL Statements

> 📄 Source: [Configure_SQL_Statements.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Configure_SQL_Statements.htm)

###### Configure SQL Statements


When working with the DB Reader or DB Writer components, you can use the SQL Statements tab to declare context variables and add SQL statements that should be performed.


When you open the SQL Statements tab on the Settings tab of DB Reader/DB Writer, you can view the following settings:



###### Statements context


In this pane, you can declare local name-value variables that can be used in your SQL expressions in future. Such named variables can be addressed in SQL expression by using the following syntax: ?VariableName.


###### Statement groups


In this pane, you can enter SQL queries that should be performed by the component.


An SQL Statement is an actual SQL expression to query a database. All SQL Statements are created inside Statement groups - named containers used for grouping multiple SQL statements.


When DB Reader/DB Writer components receive an XEServer message (for example, a DFF file), the components perform every SQL Statement in every Statement group once for every DFF record in the order these SQL Statements/Statements groups are defined in the Statement groups pane. For this reason, you can use Move Up/Move Down to arrange the order of execution of your SQL expressions:



When you add a new SQL Statement, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: New SQL statement dialog box]*


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SQL Statement type | An SQL Statements can be of three types: Select, Insert, and Update. Use the required type to perform corresponding SQL queries. |
| Batch | Note This option is used to optimize the process of making calls to a database, thus its usage is efficient only when working with the DB Writer component. For more information about on batching SQL Statements, see the [DB Writer](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Writer.htm#SQL) section. |
| Output parameters | With this option, you can define how to handle the data returned by a database as a response to a query. The list of possible output types depends on the SQL statement type used. #### DB Reader usage: Select Result Set: All rows from a table are extracted. The extracted data are forwarded through the data component output as XEServer message body. Single Row: A single row is extracted from the table. If Add result to exchange properties is selected, the extracted data are set to XEServer message exchange properties in the following way: Column1=ColumnValue1, Column2=ColumnValue2, ColumnN=ColumnValueN. #### DB Writer usage: Insert None: The response from the database is ignored. RowCount: The number of inserted rows is returned. This value is added to SQL Statement context and can be accessed by SQL Statements that follow the current statement by using the ?RowCount syntax. Update Result Set: All rows from a table are extracted. The extracted data are forwarded through the data component output as XEServer message body. RowCount: The number of inserted rows is returned. This value is added to SQL Statement context and can be accessed by SQL Statements that follow the current statement by using the ?RowCount syntax. |
| SQL Expression | Enter an SQL query you want to perform. |



When working with the DB Reader or DB Writer settings, on the SQL Statements tab, you can create one of the following:

- SQL Statement group Used to create a named container for one or multiple SQL Statements. DB Reader usage:The name of the container is used to identify the result records when working with DB Reader. If different Statement Groups are logically related to multiple tables (entities), the output (if it is Edifecs Database DFF or Edifecs Database XML format) contains the name of the group either as a first field (in case of DFF), or as a special table tag (in case of XML). For simple cases, when this naming is not planned to be used later, the name of the Statement Group can be any and does not affect the further workflow. DB Writer usage:To make the component write data to a database, the names of SQL Statement Groups should strictly match the names of nodes inside the <DataSet></DataSet> node of incoming Edifecs Database XML Format files. For more information, see [DB Writer Component](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DB_Writer.htm).

- SQL Statement. Used to create actual query to a database and configure request/response parameters.
- SQL Stored Procedure. Used to make calls to SQL procedures stored in a database. For more indormation, see [SQL Stored Procedures](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SQL_Stored_Procedures.htm).

---

<a id="sql-stored-procedures"></a>

##### SQL Stored Procedures

> 📄 Source: [SQL_Stored_Procedures.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SQL_Stored_Procedures.htm)

###### SQL Stored Procedures


With the DB Reader and DB Writer components, you can invoke SQL procedures stored in a database and receive results from the procedure. All the examples provided in this section refer to the DB Reader component.


A typical SQL procedure receives input parameters, queries a database using these input parameters, and returns result back to the user. Depending on the database used, the process of calling a stored SQL procedure differs. The XEServer DB components work with major popular databases, such as Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, H2, Postgre SQL, and so on.


To configure stored procedures:

1. Add the DB Reader component to a route. For more information, see [Add a Component](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Work_with_Components.htm).
2. On the Settings tab of the component, go to the SQL Statements tab.
3. In the Statements groups pane, click New.
4. In the Select an action dialog box, select SQL Stored Procedure, and then click Next. The following dialog box is displayed. | Field | Description | | --- | --- | | Is Oracle DB | Select this option for an Oracle database. | | Procedure Name | Enter the name of the stored procedure to be executed. | | Result type | Select the format in which data should be returned from the database to XEServer:Named values: Data is returned in the form of key-value pairs where the key is the parameter name, for example `param_1`, and the value is the actual data fetched from the corresponding output of the stored procedure. The extracted value pairs are set to the component's context and can be reused.Result set: Data is returned in the form of a set of database records. For more information on retrieving record sets from Oracle database, see Get a result set from an Oracle database. | | Add results to exchange headers | Available only for the Named values result type. Select this option to set procedure results as message exchange properties.Note You should be careful when setting named values as message exchange properties. If two or more statement groups have stored procedures with the same parameter name, the last added property overwrites the previous values. | | Parameter Name | Available if the Is Oracle DB check box is selected and the Result type is set to ResultSet. Use this option to select the required result set that should be used by XEServer when a stored procedure returns several result sets. For more information , see notes.  |


###### Procedure parameters


A stored procedure expects input parameters to be passed and returns some output parameters to you. Every parameter should have a name, mode, type, and value (optional). In the following pane, you can enter parameters that match the structure of your procedure:



Note The number of parameters defined in this pane should strictly match the number of parameters expected by the stored procedure.


| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | Enter a name for the parameter. This name is further used as a key in a key-value pair of data returned from the database. |
| Mode | Select the mode of the parameter:IN: The parameter to be passed to a stored procedure.OUT: The parameter to be returned by a stored procedure.INOUT: The parameter which can be used for both input and output. |
| Data Type | Select a data type carried by the parameter. |
| Value | Enter the value of a parameter. |



###### Get a Result Set from the Oracle Database


Due to design differences between Oracle and Microsoft databases, to get a result set (a set of records from the database) from an Oracle database, consider the following:

- You should have at least one output parameter (mode set to "OUT").
- In Data type, the "CURSOR" value should be selected. This data type is only available when working with Oracle databases and in Oracle database terminology stands for a result set.
- If a procedure returns several result sets, in Parameter name, select the result set you want XEServer to work with.

---

<a id="use-variables-in-sql-requests"></a>

##### Use Variables in SQL Requests

> 📄 Source: [Use_Variables_in_SQL_Requests.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Variables_in_SQL_Requests.htm)

###### Use Variables in SQL Requests


When working with database components, you can use context variables in your SQL queries. There are three ways of creating a new variable in components context:

1. You can declare a name-value variable by using the Statements context pane on the SQL Statements tab of DB Reader or DB Writer components:Such variables can be used anywhere in your SQL expressions, and can be addressed by using the following syntax: ?VariableName .
2. A name-value variable can be created as a result of execution of some SQL Statement. Since SQL Statements are executed in the order they are presented on the SQL Statements tab, such a variable is only available for all SQL Statements that follow the statement that created this variable. For example, if an SQL Statement with the RowCount output parameter performs some UPDATE operation, a database responds with a number of modified rows. This value can be reused in those SQL Statements that follow the original UPDATE SQL Statement. Another example is execution of some SELECT SQL Statement that has the output parameter set to Single row. In this case, for every column a new <Name>-<Value> variable is created where <Name> is the name of the row column and <Value> is the value of this row column. The following is a sample database row fetched by a SELECT SQL Statemet with the RowCount output parameter: After execution of such SQL Statement, the four variables are added to context and can be accessed using ?ID, ?Name, ?PATIENTID, ?ADDRESS1 syntax. Note If an SQL Statement creates a column-named variable and this name coincides with a variable defined in the Statements context pane (see Point 1), the column-named variable overwrites those defined in the Statements context pane.
3. When working with DB Writer and the DFF (Edifecs Database DFF Format) on input, index-based variables can be used. For more information, see [Use Column Index in SQL Requests](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Column_Index_in_SQL_Requests.htm).


###### Variable Usage Scenario


In this scenario, a sample database is used. Use the following SQL code to create and populate the database:


SQL


CREATE TABLE Eligibility


(ID INTEGER NOT NULL,


NAME varCHAR(30),


PATIENTID INTEGER NOT NULL,


ADDRESS1 VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,


ADDRESS2 VARCHAR(30),


CITY varchar(30),


STATE varchar(30),


ZIP INTEGER,


SERVICETYPE INTEGER NOT NULL,


COVERAGELEVEL varCHAR(30),


INSURANCETYPE INTEGER NOT NULL);


 


INSERT INTO Eligibility VALUES ( 1, 'Anthony Bishop', '875439', '123th Ave NE', 'D123', 'Seattle', 'WA', 98004, 30, 'EMP-SPS', 1);


INSERT INTO Eligibility VALUES ( 3, 'Alison Brown', '31323', '23Pl N', 'A111', 'Kent', 'WA', 98034, 1, 'EMP', 2);


In this scenario, the DB Reader component extracts a record by a special Patient ID. Data that comes from the FS Inbound contains message property "PatientID".


A sample route should be created for this scenario:


*[Image: DB Reader route]*


To use a variable in SQL query:

1. Open the sample route.
2. Select the DB Reader component, and then go to the Settings tab.
3. On the Output handling tab, set Output format to Edifecs Database XML Format.
4. On the SQL Statement tab, click New in the Statements context pane to declare a new name-value variable.


*[Image: SQL Statement tab]*

1. In the Name field, type MyID.
2. In the Value field, type {Prp[PatientID]}. The macro is used to resolve the "PatientID" XEServer message property.


*[Image: Specifying a new variable]*

1. Click OK.
2. In the Statements groups pane, click New.
3. Click SQL Statement, and then click Next.
4. In the SQL Expression field, enter the following SQL query:


SELECT*from Eligibility WHERE PatientID=?MyID


Note A named variable can be referenced in SQL statements using the following syntax: ?<VariableName> .

1. Click Finish.


To verify the results:

1. Start the sample profile.
2. Provide any data file and .properties file that should contain the PatientID=875439 pair to the FS Inbound source location.
3. Verify the results in the FS Outbound destination. The result data should look like the following:


*[Image: SQL Result]*

---

<a id="use-column-index-in-sql-requests"></a>

##### Use Column Index in SQL Requests

> 📄 Source: [Use_Column_Index_in_SQL_Requests.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Column_Index_in_SQL_Requests.htm)

###### Use Column Index in SQL Requests


When working with DB Writer and the DFF (Edifecs Database DFF Format) on input, you can access DFF record fields by using index-based variables (_1, _2, _N) in SQL queries.


Note To get values of DFF fields and reuse them in SQL expressions, only index-based (_1, _2, _N) variable syntax is allowed. The positioning of DFF fields in a record starts from 1, thus the variable "_1" stands for the first DFF field in a row.


###### Variable Usage Scenario


In this scenario, a sample database is used. Use the following SQL code to create and populate the database:


SQL


CREATE TABLE Eligibility


(ID INTEGER NOT NULL,


NAME varCHAR(30),


PATIENTID INTEGER NOT NULL,


ADDRESS1 VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,


ADDRESS2 VARCHAR(30),


CITY varchar(30),


STATE varchar(30),


ZIP INTEGER,


SERVICETYPE INTEGER NOT NULL,


COVERAGELEVEL varCHAR(30),


INSURANCETYPE INTEGER NOT NULL);


 


INSERT INTO Eligibility VALUES ( 1, 'Anthony Bishop', '875439', '123th Ave NE', 'D123', 'Seattle', 'WA', 98004, 30, 'EMP-SPS', 1);


INSERT INTO Eligibility VALUES ( 3, 'Alison Brown', '31323', '23Pl N', 'A111', 'Kent', 'WA', 98034, 1, 'EMP', 2);


 


In this scenario, the DB Writer component updates a table record with a new Patient ID. The sample data that comes from the FS Inbound component is in DFF format, where the second field represents a Patient ID. The third and the fourth columns represent patient's name and patient's city. The first DFF field ("Eligibility") represents a table name, since the Table name in first column parsing parameter is set to TRUE.


DFF


Eligibility,1,875439,Max,Seattle


Eligibility,2,123456,Dorothy,NY


A sample route should be created for this scenario:


*[Image: DB Writer Route]*


To use an index-based variable in SQL query:

1. Open the sample route.
2. Select the DB Writer component, and then go to the Settings tab.
3. On the Input handling tab, set Input format to Edifecs Database DFF Format.


*[Image: Input handling tab]*

1. On the SQL Statements tab, in the Statements Group pane, click New and then add a new SQL Statement Group.
2. In the SQL Statement Group Name field, enter Eligibility.


Note This name must match the first field in the DFF file.


*[Image: SQL Statement Group]*

1. Click OK.

1. In the Statements groups pane, click New and add a new SQL Statement.


*[Image: New SQL Statement]*

1. In the SQL statement type, select Update.
2. In SQL Expression, enter the following query:


UPDATE Eligibility Set Name=?_3, CITY=?_4 where PatientID=?_2


In this query, the variables like "?_3" define the DFF fields from which data should be taken.

1. In the Output parameters type, click RowCount.


*[Image: Create an SQL statement]*

1. Click Finish.


To verify the results:

1. Start the sample profile.
2. Provide the sample DFF file to the FS Inbound source location.
3. Verify the database for the results. The affected table row should look like the following:


*[Image: Resulted Database]*

---

<a id="ftp-command-component"></a>

#### FTP Command Component

> 📄 Source: [Book_FTP_Command_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_FTP_Command_Component.htm)

##### FTP Command Component


[Redirect to: {FTP_Command}](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command.htm)

---

<a id="ftp-command"></a>

##### FTP Command

> 📄 Source: [FTP_Command.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command.htm)

###### FTP Command Component


With this component, you can send FTP commands to an FTP server. The component acts as a low-level FTP interface that allows you to submit any command to the FTP server. The only requirement is that the target FTP server has to support the required command.


###### Connect to an FTP Server


The component can connect to an FTP server either directly or through the [FTP Connection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Connection_Service.htm) service.


If your route has a single FTP Command component that performs several operations, you can specify the FTP server connectivity parameters directly in the component's configuration. The component will open a new connection to the FTP server for each received message.


However, if your route has multiple FTP Command components that work with the same FTP server, a good practice is to connect to the FTP server through the service [FTP Connection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Connection_Service.htm). Using several FTP Command components in combination with the service FTP Connection significantly improves the performance as the service opens the connection only once and shares this connection among multiple FTP Command components, reducing the overhead on handling the TCP/IP connection each time.


###### Component Settings


The component has a script-driven configuration design. That is, you define the actual FTP operation(s) to send to the FTP server in a Groovy script. For more information, see [FTP Command Groovy API](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command_Groovy_API.htm) and [Groovy Samples](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command_Groovy_Samples.htm).


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.



###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use connection settings from component | Select this option if you want the component to establish a connection with the FTP server directly, without using the service [FTP Connection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Connection_Service.htm). In this case, the component uses the connection settings specified on the component's Connection tab. If disabled, the component connects to the FTP server using the service FTP Connection. |
| Request shared connection for | Specify the shared connection ID. When the service FTP Connection is used, the component can request the service to provide a connection by a specific ID. If the requested connection object exists, the service returns the shared connection so that the component can reuse the same TCP/IP connection to perform an operation (or creates a new connection otherwise). For example, to allow several FTP Command components to use the same connection, this value should be identical for all the FTP Command components within the profile. Tip The macro {T_ID} (task ID), used by default, produces a unique ID that is the same for all the FTP Command components running within one task. |
| Commands | Provide a Groovy script (either in-memory or an external file) with the FTP operation logic. In Parameters, you can declare key-value parameters and reference them in your Groovy code. For more information, see [FTP Command Groovy API](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command_Groovy_API.htm) and [Groovy Samples](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command_Groovy_Samples.htm). |



###### Connection tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Connection Protocol** | Select the protocol to work with the FTP server. The following options are available: FTP FTPS SFTP |
| **Host** | Enter the host name or the IP address of the server. |
| **Port** | Enter the port number to be used for the connection. |
| **Retry attempt** | Enter the number of connection retry attempts. |
| **Retry delay** | Enter the delay (in milliseconds) between the retry attempts. |
| **User name** | Enter the name used to log in to the FTP server. |
| **Password** | Enter the password used to log in to the FTP server. |



###### Security tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Server certificate store | |
| **Type** | The server keystore type. |
| **Path** | The keystore file location. |
| **Subject** | The address of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password to access the key in the keystore. |
| User certificate store | |
| **Type** | The user keystore type. |
| **Path** | The keystore file location. |
| **Subject** | The address of the key in the keystore. |
| **Password** | The password to access the key in the keystore. |
| **Primary key password** | The password to the private key. |
| *Connection start mode | Select the FTPS method for invoking security. The following options are available: auto - If the remote port matches the standard plain text port, the explicit mode is used. In all other cases, the implicit mode is used. explicit - XEServer requests the SSL/TLS encryption type, and then SSL/TLS negotiation is started. implicit - The deprecated mode. XEServer connects to an FTP server, and SSL negotiation is started once the connection is established. none - The communication is performed in the plain text mode. |
| *Authentication mode | Select the authentication mode for the SFTP protocol. The following options are available: certificate - The authentication is performed through a private key. mixed - An FTP server verifies the user's private key and then prompts the user password. password - The authentication is performed by entering the user name and the password. none - No authentication is performed. |



*Note The marked fields are available only for FTPS and SFTP protocols.

---

<a id="groovy-api"></a>

##### Groovy API

> 📄 Source: [FTP_Command_Groovy_API.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command_Groovy_API.htm)

###### FTP Command Groovy API


This section describes the Groovy API for [FTP Command](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command.htm) components. Using the Groovy objects and methods presented in this section, you can create a custom logic to perform operations on an FTP server.


###### Core FTP Classes


In addition to the standard XEServer [Groovy API](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Groovy_API.htm), you can use the following classes in your Groovy code to interact with an FTP server.


| Class | Default object name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FTPConnection | ftp | Used to work with FTP protocol. |
| FTPSConnection | ftps | Used to work with FTPS protocol. |
| SFTPConnection | sftp | Used to work with SFTP protocol. |
| FTPCommandLogger | logger | Used to log FTP Command events. |



When you create a Groovy script for the FTP Command component, an instance of each class above is available in your script by default. You can reference these objects by using their default object names, for example:

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)
 defaultFtpObjects.groovy


```
FTPCommandLogger logger = logger
FTPConnection ftp = ftp

ftp.connect()
logger.write("Connection established")
// Further FTP actions ...
```






###### FTPConnection / FTPSConnection / SFTPConnection


The objects `ftp`, `ftps`, and `sftp` have the following common methods:

 void connect()


Opens a connection to the FTP server using the corresponding protocol (FTP/FTPS/SFTP).





 void close()


Logs out and closes the connection to the FTP server.





 void downloadFile(OutputStream stream, String file)


Downloads a file identified by `file` from the FTP server to the provided OutputStream.





 void delete(String path)


Removes a file at the given path.





 String getConnectionId()


Returns the ID of the connection.





 String getLastReply()


Returns the last reply from the FTP server.


Note This method is not supported for SFTP.





 String getPermissions(String filePath)


Returns permissions of a file or a directory identified by `filePath` in the form of a 32-bit integer containing a POSIX-compatible file permission bitmask, for example: 16877.


Note The method is supported only for SFTP.





 String getPermissionsOctal(String filePath)


Returns permissions of a file or a directory identified by `filePath` in the form of an octal string that contains a POSIX-compatible file permission bitmask, for example "100644".


Note The method is supported only for SFTP.





 String getWorkingDirectory()


Returns the current working directory on the FTP server.





 boolean isConnected()


Indicates whether the connection with the FTP server is active.





 boolean isFileExists(String path)


Indicates whether a file exists at the given `path`.





 List<FTPDirEntry> listDirectory()


Returns a list of `FTPDirEntry`s that represent files and directories available in the FTP working directory. `FTPDirEntry` is an internal XEServer data structure that can be represented as follows:

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


FTPDirEntry.java




```
public class FTPDirEntry {
    public final String dirEntry;
    public final String fileName;
    public final boolean isDir;
    public final long fileSize;
    public final String fileTime;
}
```









 List<FTPDirEntry> listDirectoryNames()


Supported for FTP and FTPS protocols. Returns a list of `FTPDirEntry`s that represents files/directories present in the current FTP directory. `FTPDirEntry` is an internal XEServer data structure that can be represented as follows:

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


FTPDirEntry.java




```
public class FTPDirEntry {
    public final String dirEntry;
    public final String fileName;
    public final boolean isDir;
    public final long fileSize;
    public final String fileTime;
}
```






This method differs from `listDirectory()` since it sends the command NLST as opposed to LIST for `listDirectory()` case.





 void logon()


Performs the logon operation on the FTP server using the credentials set by `setUser(String userName)` and `setPassword(String password)`. This method is not supported for SFTPConnection.





 void makeDirectory(String path)


Creates a new directory at the given `path`.





 void removeDirectory(String path)


Removes a directory at the given `path`.





 void renameFile(String path, String newFileName)


Renames a file at the given `path` to `newFileName`.





 void setUser(String userName)


Sets `userName` to be used for the connection with FTP server.





 void setPassiveMode(boolean passiveModeON)


Specifies whether FTP passive mode should be used for the connection.


Note The method is not supported for SFTP.





 void setPassword(String password)


Sets the user password.





 void setPermission(String filePath, int permissions)


The method is supported only for SFTP. Sets permissions for a file or directory identified by `filePath`. The permissions argument must be a 32-bit integer containing a POSIX-compatible file permission bitmask, for example: `setPermissions("test.txt", 16877)`.


Note The corresponding SFTP server must support POSIX permissions.





 void setPermissionsOctal(String filePath, String permissions)


The method is supported only for SFTP. Sets permissions for a file or directory identified by `filePath`. The permissions argument must be an octal string containing a POSIX-compatible file permission bitmask, for example `setPermissionsOctal("test.txt", "100644")`.


Note The corresponding SFTP server must support POSIX permissions.





 void setHost(String host)


Specifies the host name of the FTP server to connect to.





 void setPort(int port)


Specifies the port number to be used for the connection.





 void setTransferMode(Enum transferModeType)


Sets the transfer mode type where `transferModeType` can be the following:

- BINARY: creates a byte-for-byte identical copy of the transferred file.
- ASCII: intended for transferring files that contain plain text.
- ServerDefault: select the mode that is set by default on the FTP server.





 String sendCommand(String ftpCommand)


Sends an arbitrary FTP command (for example, SITE) to the FTP server and returns the FTP server's response.


Note The method is not supported for SFTP.





 void setWorkingDirectory(String path)


Changes the current working directory to `path`.





 void uploadFile(InputStream stream, String file)


Uploads a file from an InputStream to a file on the FTP server identified by `file`.






###### FTPCommandLogger


The object `logger` has the only method:

 void write(String format, Object... arguments)


Logs an event to the log file. The method accepts zero or more `Object` arguments and invokes `toString()` for each of them.

---

<a id="groovy-samples"></a>

##### Groovy Samples

> 📄 Source: [FTP_Command_Groovy_Samples.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Command_Groovy_Samples.htm)

###### Groovy Samples


The section contains Groovy examples that show you basic operations with an FTP server using FTP Command components.


The following script gets the listing of the FTP directory and downloads all the files from that directory. This sample script does not open a connection to the FTP server as it assumes that the connection is provided by the service [FTP Connection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/FTP_Connection_Service.htm).

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


sample1.groovy




```
import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.ftp.command.*

// Make default objects typed
FTPCommandLogger logger = logger
FTPConnection ftp = ftp

try {
    // create directory
    ftp.makeDirectory("base_dir")
    // get list of files/directories in the current directory
    def dirEntries = ftp.listDirectory()
    // output directory listing to log
    logger.write("{}", dirEntries)
    // send the command NOOP and log the server's reply
    String reply1 = ftp.sendCommand("NOOP")
    logger.write("1. reply=" + reply1)
}
catch (Exception ex) {
    logger.write("1. ex=" + ex)
    return
}

try {
    // send the command HELP and get the server's reply
    String reply2 = ftp.sendCommand("HELP")
    logger.write("2. reply=" + reply2)
}
catch (Exception ex) {
    logger.write("2. ex=" + ex.message)
}

// create another directory
ftp.makeDirectory("test")
// change the working directory
ftp.workingDirectory = "/test"
logger.write("workingDirectory=" + ftp.workingDirectory)
// upload the string "abc" as a byte array to the file "test.txt" in the current working directory
ftp.uploadFile(new ByteArrayInputStream("abc".getBytes()), "test.txt")
// loop through the directory/file entries in the current directory
for (FTPDirEntry dirEntry in ftp.listDirectory()) {
    logger.write("dirEntry=" + dirEntry.dirEntry)
    // if the directory entry is a file ... 
    if (!dirEntry.isDir) {
        OutputStream outputStream = new FileOutputStream("Temp/" + dirEntry.fileName);
        // download the file
        try {
            ftp.downloadFile(outputStream, dirEntry.fileName)
        } finally {
            outputStream.close()
        }
    }
}
```






The following script gets the listing of the FTP directory and creates a new XEServer message exchange with one message carrying the file name for each file in the directory. The message leaves the component through the data output.

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


sample2.groovy




```
import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.ftp.command.*

// make default objects typed
FTPCommandContext context = context
FTPCommandLogger logger = logger
FTPConnection ftp = ftp

// disable FTP passive mode
ftp.passiveMode = false

// change current working directory
ftp.workingDirectory = "/test"
logger.write("workingDirectory=" + ftp.workingDirectory)

// loop through the directory/file entries in the current directory
for (FTPDirEntry dirEntry in ftp.listDirectory()) {
    logger.write("dirEntry=" + dirEntry.dirEntry)
    // if the directory entry is a file ...
    if (!dirEntry.isDir) {
        // create an XEServer message. The message body holds the file name retrieved from the FTP server.
        // The message headers list is empty.
        def message = context.createNewMessage(dirEntry.fileName, null)
        // create an XEServer message exchange with one message and send the exchange to the "data" output 
        // of the "FTP Command" component.
        context.createExchangeResponse(message)
    }
}
```






The following script reads file names from the incoming XEServer messages (each message carries a file name) and downloads the corresponding file from the FTP server for each incoming message.

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


sample3.groovy




```
import com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.component.ftp.command.*

// make default objects typed
FTPCommandContext context = context
FTPCommandLogger logger = logger
FTPConnection ftp = ftp
// get the first message of the message exchange that comes to the component input
def message = context.exchange.messages.first()
// read the message body as String
def fileName = message.bodyAsString
OutputStream outputStream = new FileOutputStream("Test/" + fileName)
try {
    // download the file from the FTP server using the file name in the input XEServer message
    ftp.downloadFile(outputStream, fileName)
    logger.write("downloadFile=" + fileName)
} finally {
    outputStream.close()
}
```

---

<a id="encryption-and-decryption"></a>

#### Encryption and Decryption

> 📄 Source: [Book_Encryption_and_Decryption.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Encryption_and_Decryption.htm)

##### Encryption and Decryption


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="encryption-component"></a>

##### Encryption Component

> 📄 Source: [Encryption.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Encryption.htm)

###### Encryption Component


With the Encryption component, you can encrypt and sign the data that is going to be sent to an outer system from a route. The Encryption and [Decryption](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Decryption.htm) components are intended to be used in pairs, however you can use them separately in your routes since the components implement popular encryption and signing mechanisms.


Note If you observe the illegal key size error exception when applying a strong encryption level (using 128-bit or longer keys), this indicates that your JDK installation lacks [Java Cryptography Extension (JCE) Unlimited Strength Jurisdiction Policy Files 8 files](http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/downloads/jce8-download-2133166.html) which are not distributed with the default JDK installation. These files regulate the usage of admissible cryptography strength for some countries. After you download the archive, extract the .jar files and put them to %JAVA_HOME%/jre/lib/security/.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Algorithm type | The type of an algorithm to be used to encrypt data. You can select to work with symmetric algorithms that imply exchanging a single secret key between the Encryption and Decryption components, or you can select the asymmetric Open PGP technology to use a pair of public/private keys. |
| Generate key | Click to open the Key Generation dialog box. In this dialog box, you can generate new keys by using [symmetric algorithms](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Generate_New_Key_Sym.htm) and [Open PGP technology](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Generate_New_Key_PGP.htm). |
| Validate | Click to verify the keys to be used for encryption/signing. The component checks the following criteria: Algorithm type: Symmetric If the keystore file exists and can be loaded from the specified path. If the specified alias name exists in the keystore. If the key can be loaded. If the key can be used for symmetric encryption. Algorithm type: Open PGP If the public key exists and can be loaded from the specified path. If the private key can be extracted using the specified password. |
| Algorithm type: Symmetric | |
| Keystore path | The path to the keystore file on your file system. |
| Keystore password | The password to the keystore. |
| Key alias | The alias name of the key in the keystore. |
| Key password | The password to the key. |
| Encodes message body with Base64 prior to encryption | When you select this option, the component encodes the message body to the Base64 format before encrypting the message body. |
| Algorithm type: Open PGP | |
| Encryption tab: | |
| Public key ring | The path to the file with the public key. |
| Encodes message body with Base64 prior to encryption | If selected, the component encodes the message body to the Base64 format prior to the encryption. |
| Key selection strategy | Specifies how the component should pick the key for encryption from the Public key ring file. The following options are available: Take first matching: The component uses the first key that has the 'Encrypt' usage flag. By Key ID: Enter the ID of the key that has the 'Encrypt' usage flag. By Key Fingerprint: Enter the fingerprint of the key that has the 'Encrypt' usage flag. Tip You can use the {Prp[ ]} macro to dynamically set the key ID or key fingerprint. Note If the key you specify for encryption does not have the 'Encrypt' usage flag, the component throws an exception. If you need to use the key regardless of its usage type(s), add the property COM_EDIFECS_XESERVER_PGP_IGNORE_FILTER_KEYS_BY_TYPE=true to your system environment variables. |
| Source file name | Provide a name for the resulting encrypted file or use the macro {MD_File_Name} to have the same name as the original source file. If no file name is provided, the default is PGPOpen. For the decrypted file to have the same name as the original file, use the macro {MD_DECRYPTOR_SourceFileName} on the outbound component where Decryption component saves decrypted file to. |
| Signature tab: | |
| Sign | Select whether the data that the component encrypts should be signed with your private key. |
| Private key | Specify the path to the file with your private key that has the 'Sign' usage flag to sign the outgoing data. To view the Key ID and Key Fingerprint of your private key, click Details. Note If the key you specify for signing does not have the 'Sign' usage flag, the component throws an exception.
