If you need to use the key regardless of its usage type(s), add the property COM_EDIFECS_XESERVER_PGP_IGNORE_FILTER_KEYS_BY_TYPE=true to your system environment variables. |
| Key password | Enter the passphrase to your private key. |



###### Troubleshooting


When encountering issues during data encryption and/or signing, you can [start a debug session](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Start_Debugger.htm) and view the log records that contain exception messages, IDs of the keys used, and more.

---

<a id="decryption-component"></a>

##### Decryption Component

> 📄 Source: [Decryption.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Decryption.htm)

###### Decryption Component


With the Decryption component, you can decrypt the data that is coming from an outer system in the encrypted form, as well as verify the sender's signature. The [Encryption](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Encryption.htm) and Decryption components are intended to be used in pairs, however you can use them in your routes separately since the components implement popular encryption and signing mechanisms.


Note If you observe illegal key size error exception when applying a strong encryption level (using 128-bit or longer keys), this indicates that your JDK installation lacks [Java Cryptography Extension (JCE) Unlimited Strength Jurisdiction Policy Files 8 files](http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/downloads/jce8-download-2133166.html) which are not distributed with the default JDK installation. This files regulate the usage of admissible cryptography strength for some countries. After you download the archive, extract the .jar files and put them to %JAVA_HOME%/jre/lib/security/.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Algorithm type | Select the type of algorithms to work with. You can select to work with symmetric algorithms that imply exchanging a single secret key between the Encryption and Decryption components, or you can select the asymmetric Open PGP technology that uses a pair of public/private keys. |
| Generate key | Click to open the Key Generation dialog box. In this dialog box, you can generate new keys by using [symmetric algorithms](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Generate_New_Key_Sym.htm) and [Open PGP technology](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Generate_New_Key_PGP.htm). |
| Validate | Click to verify the keys or signature to be used for decryption. The component checks the following criteria: Algorithm type: Symmetric If the keystore file exists and can be loaded from the specified path. If the specified alias name exists in the keystore. If the key can be loaded. If the key can be used for symmetric decryption. Algorithm type: Open PGP If the private key exists and can be loaded from the specified path. If the private key can be extracted using the specified password. |
| Algorithm type: Symmetric | |
| Keystore path | Enter the path to your keystore. Click to browse your file system. |
| Keystore password | Enter the password for accessing the keystore. |
| Key alias | Enter the alias name of the key in the keystore. |
| Key password | Enter the password for accessing the key. |
| Base64 | Select this option if incoming data is encoded in Base 64 format. |
| Algorithm type: Open PGP | |
| Decryption tab: | |
| Private key | Enter the path to the private key file on your system to be used for decryption. Note If the key you specify for decryption does not have the 'Encrypt' usage flag, the component throws an exception. If you specify the key ring file and the data was encrypted with a sub-key that does not have the 'Encrypt' usage flag, the component picks up another sub-key that has the 'Encrypt' usage flag, which also leads to the exception. If you need to use the key regardless of its usage type(s), add the property COM_EDIFECS_XESERVER_PGP_IGNORE_FILTER_KEYS_BY_TYPE=true to your system environment variables. |
| Password | Enter the password for accessing the private key. |
| Signature tab: | |
| Verify | Specify whether the component should verify the sender's signature of the incoming encrypted data. If signature verification fails, the data will not be decrypted. If you select the autodetect option, the component checks whether the incoming file is signed or not, then verifies the signature for the signed files and passes the unsigned files without signature verification. This option is not recommended since it may introduce a security breach in your system. |
| Public key ring | Enter the path to the file with the public key to be used for signature verification. |
| Key selection strategy | Specifies how the component should pick the key for signature verification from the Public key ring file. The following options are available: Take first matching: The component uses the first key that has the 'Sign' usage flag. By Key ID: Enter the ID of the key that has the 'Sign' usage flag. Key Fingerprint: Enter the fingerprint of the key that has the 'Sign' usage flag. Tip You can use the {Prp[ ]} macro to dynamically set the key ID or key fingerprint. Note If the key whose ID or fingerprint you specify does not have the 'Sign' usage flag, the component throws an exception. If you select the Take first matching selection strategy and the data was signed with a sub-key that does not have the 'Sign' usage flag, the component picks up another sub-key that has the 'Sign' usage flag, which also leads to the exception. If you need to use the key regardless of its usage type(s), add the property COM_EDIFECS_XESERVER_PGP_IGNORE_FILTER_KEYS_BY_TYPE=true to your system environment variables. |
| Note For the Open PGP algorithm, the detection of Base 64 format is performed automatically.   | |



###### Troubleshooting


When encountering issues during data decryption and/or signature verification, you can [start a debug session](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Start_Debugger.htm) and view the log records that contain exception messages, IDs of the keys used, and more.

---

<a id="generate-new-key-open-pgp"></a>

##### Generate New Key (Open PGP)

> 📄 Source: [Generate_New_Key_PGP.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Generate_New_Key_PGP.htm)

###### Generate New Key (Open PGP)


In this dialog box, you can generate public and private key pair for encryption/decryption using the Open PGP algorithm:



| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Public key | Enter a path to the file on your system where the generated public key should be stored. |
| Private key | Enter a path to the file on your system where the generated private key should be stored. |
| Key password | Enter a passphrase to be used to encrypt your private key on your disk. |
| Generate keys in ASCII-Armoured format | Select this option to encode the generated keys in the Base64 format. |
| Use for | If you generate keys for Encryption component: Specify whether the component should use the newly generated keys for data encryption or for signing. If you generate keys for Decryption component: Specify whether the component should use the newly generated keys to decrypt data or verify the sender's signature. |



After the new keys are generated, their file paths are automatically populated in the corresponding components' configurations.

---

<a id="generate-new-key-symmetric"></a>

##### Generate New Key (Symmetric)

> 📄 Source: [Generate_New_Key_Sym.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Generate_New_Key_Sym.htm)

###### Generate New Key (Symmetric)


Use this dialog box to generate new keys for encryption/decryption by using symmetric algorithms:



| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Keystore path | Enter a path to the keystore to which a new key should be added. If a keystore already exists under the entered path, it is extended with a new generated key. If there is no keystore under the entered path, a new keystore is created. Click to browse your file system. |
| Keystore password | Enter the password for accessing the existing keystore or set a new password if you create a new keystore. Click Generate to generate a random password. |
| Algorithm | Select an algorithm to use for encrypting the key. The following algorithms are available:3DES AES DES |
| Key alias | Enter an alias name for the generated key in the keystore. |
| Key password | Enter a password for the generated key. Click Generate to generate a random password. |

---

<a id="data-scrubber"></a>

#### Data Scrubber

> 📄 Source: [Data_Scrubber.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Data_Scrubber.htm)

##### Data Scrubber


With the Data Scrubber component, you can clean up data files. The component allows you to perform the following operations:

- Replace certain values with different ones.
- Remove characters.
- Insert characters at a specified position.
- Apply scrubbing operations on a specific file.


*## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab Field Description Encoding Specify the file encoding. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. Instructions List of Scrubber instructions based on the Groovy script language. Default value is {Prp[Scrubber.Instructions]} which means that Scrubber instructions are picked up from incoming message property Scrubber.Instructions. If property value is empty or the property does not exist, then no data transformation is applied but a warning is reported. You can set the instruction here by using special commands. For more information on how to write scrubber instructions, see the section below. ## Data Scrubber Instructions The following instructions are available for Data Scrubber: Instruction Description Example remove(source) Removes a sequences of bytes. remove([0x0D,0x0A] as char[]) remove (source, position, direction) Removes all occurrences of {source} moving forwards or backwards from the specified {position}. remove([0x0D,0x0A] as char[], 10, forward) remove(source, delimiter, direction) Removes all occurrences of {source} moving forwards or backwards from the {delimiter} remove(' ',[0x0A] as char[], backward) replace([source : target]) Replaces the specified {source} with {target} values. replace(["'":"\r\n"]) replace([source : target], delimiter, direction) Replaces the specified {source} characters with {target} values starting from the {delimiter} and moving in the specified {direction}. replace (['AB':'CD'], [0x2A] as char[], forward) replace (['AB': [0x43, 0x44] as char[]], [0x2A] as char[], forward) insert(source, position, direction) Inserts the specified {source} characters into a specified {position} moving in the specified {direction}. insert([0x0D,0x0A] as char[], 80, forward) insert(source, delimiter, direction) Inserts the specified {source} characters after/before the found {delimiter} depending on the {direction}. insert([0x0D] as char[],[0x0A] as char[], backward) block(size, delimiter, pad) Blocks results into a specified number of characters with a specified delimiter for sending back to the mainframe. block (80, 0x2A,0x2E) deblock(delimiter, pad) Removes all padding symbols before the delimiter and at the end of source data, and then removes all delimiters from source data. deblock ([0x0D,0x0A] as char[], 0x20) removeLine(pattern, delimiter, deleteDelimiter) Removes a line based on regular expression {pattern}. The {delimiter} argument defines the end of line character, and the {deleteDelimiter} boolean flag defines whether the delimiter should be removed together with the line. removeLine ('^999.*', [0x0D,0x0A] as char[], true) - removes lines beginning with "999" and followed by any text up to a \n. removePattern(pattern, maxBlockSize) Removes a sequence of characters that matches the {pattern} regular expression. removePattern('!.*#',5) - removes all characters starting with '!' following up to '#'. MaxBlockSize is set to 5. removePattern(pattern, delimiter, maxBlockSize) Removes a sequence of characters that matches the {pattern} regular expression using the {delimiter}. removePattern('!.*#','%',5) - removes all characters starting with ! following up to # with delimiter set to %. MaxBlock size is set to 5. replacePattern(pattern, target, maxBlockSize) Replaces the character sequence matching the specified pattern with {target}. replacePatter('!.*#', '~00', 8) - replaces all characters beginning with '!' following up to '#' with ~00. MaxBlock size is set to 8. replacePattern(pattern, target, delimiter, maxBlockSize)   replacePattern('!.*#','~00','%',8) - replaces all character sequences beginning with '!' following up to '#' with '~00'. Delimiter is set to '%'. MaxBlock size is set to 8. #### Instruction arguments source – hex codes of symbols in a garbage string. For example, "0x0A0x0D". It is possible to specify more than one source only for remove operations. For example: remove([[0x0D, 0x0A] as char[], [0x2A] as char[]]) position – a numeric value, specifies the position. Scrubber functions are applied after or before the specified position. After applying, scrubber moves forward from current position to number of symbols that is specified by “position”. delimiter – specifies delimiter string as hex codes. Scrubber functions are applied after or before the specified delimiter. Scrubber searches the specified delimiter, applies a function, and then searches for the next delimiter. direction – parsing direction, either forward, or backward. pad - padding symbol. pattern – regular expression pattern. maxBlockSize – specifies max block size that is loaded to memory for applying regular expression. Data that is loaded to memory is limited by delimiter or by maxBlockSize. Default size is 128 KB. Note All the operations must be separated by semicolon. Example: remove([0x0D,0x0A] as char[]); insert([0x0D,0x0A] as char[], 80, forward); block (80, 0x0D,0x0A); Data Scrubber works with the **STRING** data type, that always has encoding. It is not intended to do operations against byte sequences unless those sequences are proper characters in a given char set. Make sure that you defined an appropriate encoding data type when using Data Scrubber. For example, there is a data file with the custom encoding ISO-8859-1 without the auto-detected BOM mechanism. In this case, by default, the encoding is considered to be ASCII. The file contains a garbage byte 0x93* that needs to be replaced with a white space character. The *0x93* byte is not a proper ASCII character and is not replaced with a white space. Thus, you need to specify the proper encoding in the Data Scrubber component and use *replace([0x93:0x20])* to change *0x93* to white space.

---

<a id="groovy"></a>

#### Groovy

> 📄 Source: [Book_Groovy.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Groovy.htm)

##### Groovy


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="groovy-component"></a>

##### Groovy Component

> 📄 Source: [Groovy_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Groovy_Component.htm)

###### Groovy Component


With the Groovy component, you can execute groovy scripts. Every time the component receives an XEServer message, it runs the script. The component uses the Groovy engine version 2.5.19.


When the component is added to a route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: Groovy Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Script | Click Edit to enter a groovy script you want to execute. You can use def _onGroovyServiceStart() and def _onGroovyServiceStop() methods to control the lifecycle of Groovy script execution. For more information, see [Edit Groovy Script](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Edit_Groovy_Script.htm). |
| Script path | Enter a path to an external .groovy file you want to execute. Click to navigate to select the file you need. Click Edit to edit the file. You can also edit Groovy files which reside in your Smart Trading Cloud Repository. For more information, see [Edit Groovy Script](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Edit_Groovy_Script.htm#You). |
| Groovy script parameters | Enter name-value parameters that can be resolved and used during the Groovy script execution. |



For more information about groovy API, see [Groovy API](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Groovy_API.htm).

---

<a id="edit-groovy-script"></a>

##### Edit Groovy Script

> 📄 Source: [Edit_Groovy_Script.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Edit_Groovy_Script.htm)

###### Edit Groovy Script


When you work with Groovy components, you can enter in-memory Groovy scripts or a path to an external .groovy file you want to execute.


To edit a Groovy script:

- Select a Groovy component you want to work with, and then open the Settings tab.
- Click Edit next to the Script option. The Edit Groovy Script dialog box is displayed.


*[Image: Edit Groovy Script dialog box]*

- Enter the script you want to execute


-or-

1. Click Load Sample, and in the Load Sample Groovy Script dialog box, select the sample script, and then click OK. For more information, see [Sample Groovy Scripts](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Sample_Groovy_Scripts.htm).
2. Click Save.


You can also edit the scripts located in the Smart Trading Cloud Repository. To do this, enter a path to the Groovy file in the repository. On the Settings tab, click next to the Script path field. In the File Path dialog box, select Cloud repository, and then navigate to the file you need. The selected file is pulled from the repository during run-time.


To edit a Groovy script in the Smart Trading Cloud repository:

1. Select the corresponding Groovy component, and then open the Settings tab.
2. Click Edit next to the Script path field. The Edit Groovy Script dialog box is displayed.


1. Enter the script you want to execute.


-or-

1. Click Load Sample, and in the Load Sample Groovy Script dialog box, select the sample script, and then click OK. For more information, see [Sample Groovy Scripts](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Sample_Groovy_Scripts.htm).
2. Click Save.

---

<a id="sample-groovy-scripts"></a>

##### Sample Groovy Scripts

> 📄 Source: [Sample_Groovy_Scripts.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Sample_Groovy_Scripts.htm)

###### Sample Groovy Scripts


The following sample groovy scripts are available:


| Sample script's name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| base_operations.groovy | This sample shows basic operations with the generic XEServer object "context". |
| generate_mitimessage_result.groovy | This sample shows how to process a message exchange and split it into individual messages by using the newline character '\n'. This sample script reads the body of the incoming message with the java.io.InputStream. Then it splits the message content into several messages by using the newline character '\n' as a delimiter. The resulting messages are created in the form of the byte arrays. In addition, this sample illustrates how to create a multi-message exchange. |
| groovy_component_lifecycle.groovy | This sample shows how to use the global 'componentContext' object accessible from the Groovy component, which can transfer the information between the component execution scopes. The code iterates through all the messages of the incoming message exchange, extracts data from them, and modifies the counters map. The counters map is placed into the context on the component start. This enables the lifecycle of this object to be as big as the lifecycle of the route or the profile. |
| process_message_stream.groovy | This sample shows how to process a message exchange that contains a single message and report results with a new message content. Message body is read as with the java.io.InputStream. This sample reads body of the incoming message from InputStream; It writes the whole content of the message to the SmartStream, and then appends arbitrary string 'Processed by groovy on Wed Jun 01 10:34:19 GMT 2011' at the end. Then it creates the resulting message based on new InputStream. |
| process_message_string.groovy | This sample shows how to process a message exchange with a single message as a String and report results with a new message content. Namely it reads message body of incoming message exchange as a String, then creates new String by appending the newline character and 'Processed by groovy on Wed Jun 01 10:34:19 GMT 2011'. Then it creates response message exchange based on this assembled String. |
| process_multimessage.groovy | This sample shows how to process a multimessage exchange. The following code iterates through all the messages of the incoming message exchange and generates a single message that contains the content of all messages separated by the newline character. The SmartStream class is used to store intermediate results. |
| update_header.groovy | This sample shows how to add message exchange properties to the incoming exchange. Namely it sets property with the name 'GroovySample' to the value 'Processed by groovy on Wed Jun 01 10:34:19 GMT 2011'. |



Sample groovy scripts can be accessed from both Groovy Service and Groovy component.


To access the sample scripts:

1. Start Edifecs Application Manager.
2. On the System tab, expand XEServer, and then expand Profiles.
3. Expand the profile you need and double-click Configuration.
4. On the Outline tab, do one of the following:

1. Expand Services, and then click Groovy Service.
2. On the right side of the Configuration tab, next to Script, click Edit.


-or-

1. Expand Routes, and then click the route containing Groovy component.
2. In the Route Viewer, click the Groovy component.
3. On the Route component properties tab, click Settings.
4. On the right side of the Settings tab, next to Script, click Edit.

1. Click Load sample.
2. Select a sample script, and then click OK.


Note The loaded sample script overwrites the existing one.

---

<a id="groovy-api"></a>

##### Groovy API

> 📄 Source: [Groovy_API.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Groovy_API.htm)

###### Groovy API


Context object methods Exchange object methods Message object methods


Edifecs Application Manager is distributed with several sample groovy scripts.


To access the sample scripts:

1. Create a new route or open an existing one.

2. On the Palette, in the Data Processors sectopn, click Groovy, and place component in the Route Viewer. The Groovy component is displayed in the Route Viewer.

3. Select the Groovy component and open the Settings tab.

4. Click Edit next to the Script field, and in the Edit Groovy script dialog box, click Load sample.

5. In the List of samples section, select a script you want to insert, and then click Save. The selected script is inserted into the Groovy component.


###### Context object methods

 getExchange()


###### Syntax


getExchange();


###### Return value


Returns the incoming exchange object.





 createNewMessage()


###### Syntax

- createNewMessage(byte[] data, Map<String, Object> messageHeaders); Creates a new message of the data byte array and the messageHeaders.
- createNewMessage(InputStream data, Map<String, Object> messageHeaders); Creates a new message of the data input stream and the messageHeaders.
- createNewMessage(SmartStream data, Map<String, Object> messageHeaders); Creates a new message of the data smart stream and the messageHeaders.
- createNewMessage(String data, Map<String, Object> messageHeaders); Creates a new message of the data string, converted to byte array by using platform default encoding and the messageHeaders.


###### Return value


Returns the message object that was created.





 createExchangeResponse()


Creates a new message from the data input stream and the messageHeaders


###### Syntax

- createExchangeResponse(Map<String, Object> exchangeProperties, ProxyMessage... messages); Creates and registers a new outgoing single or multimessage exchange of the exchangeProperties and previously created messages.
- createExchangeResponse(Map<String, Object> exchangeProperties, byte[] data); Creates and registers a new outgoing single-message exchange of the exchangeProperties and a message that is created internally of the data byte array without headers.
- createExchangeResponse(Map<String, Object> exchangeProperties, InputStream data); Creates and registers a new outgoing single-message exchange of the exchangeProperties and a message that is created internally of the data input stream without headers.
- createExchangeResponse(Map<String, Object> exchangeProperties, String data); Creates and registers a new outgoing single-message exchange of the exchangeProperties and a message that is created internally of the data string, converted to byte array using platform default encoding and no headers.


###### Return value


Returns the exchange object that was created.





 getResponseIDs()


###### Syntax


getResponseIDs();


###### Return value


Returns a set of IDs of all registered outgoing message exchanges.





 getResponse()


###### Syntax


getResponse(String ID);


###### Return value


Returns a registered outgoing message exchange by its ID.






###### Exchange object methods

 getId()


###### Syntax


getId();


###### Return value


Returns a unique String ID of this message exchange.





 getMessages()


###### Syntax


getMessages();


###### Return value


Returns the array of the messages of this message exchange.





 getProperties()


###### Syntax


getProperties();


###### Return value


Returns a modifiable Map of the message exchange properties.





 getTaskID()


###### Syntax


getTaskID();


###### Return value


Returns String ID of the current task.





 getTaskContextKeys()


###### Syntax


getTaskContextKeys();


###### Return value


Returns Set of keys of items that currently are in the task context.





 getTaskContextItem()


###### Syntax


getTaskContextItem(String key);


###### Return value


Returns an item Object from the task context by the given key.





 removeTaskContextItem()


Removes an item Object from the task context by the given key.


###### Syntax


removeTaskContextItem(String key);


###### Return value


Returns the Object that was removed.





 setTaskContextItem()


Associates the specified value with the specified key in the current task context. If the task context previously contained a mapping for a key, the old value is replaced by the specified one.


###### Syntax


Method setTaskContextItem(String key, Object value);


###### Return value


Returns the previous value associated with a key, or null if there was no mapping for a key.






###### Message object methods

 getMessageId()


###### Syntax


getMessageId()


###### Return value


Returns a unique String ID of the message.





 getBody()


###### Syntax


getBody();


###### Return value


Returns the body of the message as an object: either an instance of SmartStream or any other Object (for messages that carry Java objects such as UCF).





 getBodyAsBase64()


###### Syntax


getBodyAsBase64();


###### Return value


Returns a String object that represents the body of the message encoded in Base64. In case this message carries a Java object as a body, the object is serialized to the byte array before encoding.





 getBodyAsSmartStream()


###### Syntax


getBodyAsSmartStream();


###### Return value


Returns the SmartStream object that contains message bytes. In case this message carries a Java object as body, the object is serialized and is written to the SmartStream before it is returned.





 getBodyAsString()


###### Syntax


getBodyAsString();


###### Return value


Returns a String that represents a body of the message encoded in the platform default encoding.





 getBodySize()


###### Syntax


getBodySize();


###### Return value


Returns the length of the message body in bytes. In case this message carries a Java object as a body, 0 is returned.





 getHeader()


###### Syntax


getHeader(String name, Class<T> type);


###### Return value


Returns the value of the message header by a name casted to a type.





 getHeaders()


###### Syntax


getHeaders();


###### Return value


Returns a copy of the message headers Map.





 setHeader()


###### Syntax


setHeader(String name, Object value);


###### Return value


Sets a header to the message. Once the header is set it cannot be removed, only updated.

---

<a id="hash-calculator"></a>

#### Hash Calculator

> 📄 Source: [Hash_Calculator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Hash_Calculator.htm)

##### Hash Calculator


The Hash Calculator component is designed to calculate a hash value from string type content of the incoming message by using one of the provided algorithms. By default, the generated hash code is written to the XEServer "Hash" message property, however you can specify any other property name on the Outputs Tab.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Hash algorithm | Select the algorithm to be implemented for hash code calculation. The following options are available: MD2 MD5 SHA1 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 |
| Use message content for hash calculation | Select this option to calculate the hash code by using the body of an incoming XEServer message. |
| Use additional information for hash calculation | Select this option to calculate the hash code by resolving one or more macros that you can specify in the Content field and appending it to the XEServer message content. |



Note If both options are selected, then the component behavior is following:

1. The XEServer message body is resolved and its content and the value of the Content field are concatenated into a single string.
2. The hash code is generated from the resulting string.


###### Outputs Tab


| Output | Description |
| --- | --- |
| original | Original data and its property files are directed to this output. By default, the hash code is available in the "Hash" message property. |
| error | Data that cannot be used for generating a hash code (for example, invalid hash algorithm specified) is directed to this output. |

---

<a id="json-components"></a>

#### JSON Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_JSON_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_JSON_Components.htm)

##### JSON Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="json-to-xml-component"></a>

##### JSON to XML Component

> 📄 Source: [JSON_To_XML.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JSON_To_XML.htm)

###### JSON to XML Component


With the JSON to XML component, you can convert JSON data to the UTF-8 encoded XML documents.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Virtual root | Enter a name for a root XML node that encompasses all the data converted from JSON. |
| Artificial array node name | If the root element of the input JSON file is an array (input JSON starts with the "[" character), use this option to mark the array items in the output XML data. The following JSON file is processed with the option Artificial array node name = testArray: Input JSON [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);) ``` [ { "stringValue" : "abc", "numericValue" : 123, "boolValue" : true }, { "stringValue_1" : "def", "numericValue_1" : 987, "boolValue_1" : false } ] ``` Output XML ``` abc 123 true def 987 false ``` In the output XML file, note that the highlighted <testArray></testArray> elements wrap each item of the JSON root array. |
| Skip namespaces | Select this option to exclude namespaces from the output XML file. |
| Namespaces properties file | Specify a path to the file that contains namespace properties. With this option, you can replace namespace prefixes with those defined in your property file. The following namespaces.properties file removes xml namespace prefixes that have the http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope key and replaces them with the prefix newPrefix. [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);) Namespaces.properties ``` newPrefix=http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope ``` [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);) Original JSON example ``` { "soap1:Header" : { "@xmlns:soap1" : "http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope", "$" : "TestValue" } } ``` [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);) Converted XML example (with namespaces included) ``` TestValue ``` [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);) Converted XML example (with namespaces skipped) ``` TestValue ``` |
| Namespace prefix separator | Specify a single character separator to be placed between the namespace and its prefix. By default, the value is set to ":" (colon). |
| Skip XML declaration | By default, the component adds the XML declaration `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>` as the first line in the output XML file. If this line is not expected in further processing, enable this option to skip XML declaration. |

---

<a id="json-value-extractor"></a>

##### JSON Value Extractor

> 📄 Source: [JSON_Value_Extractor.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JSON_Value_Extractor.htm)

###### JSON Value Extractor


With JSON Value Extractor component, you can extract data from JSON-encoded documents and set it to XEServer message properties. By using the JSON Path expression language, you can access JSON fields and extract its values. The output of this component is the original JSON document and a property file, containing extracted values. The JSON Value Extractor component validates the parsed file against JSON standards before actual processing and if it is valid, extracts data from it. If the document has an invalid JSON structure, the exception is thrown and the original file is redirected to the error channel.


The extracted data is stored as a value of the <Outbound properties prefix>+<Name of JSONPath variable> property in the message property file.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Outbound properties prefix | Specify a prefix to be prepended to an XEServer message property name. |
| Return first item of collection | Select the way for values to be handled in case there are two or more results, satisfying the JSONPath expression in the document: TRUE: Only the first value is returned. FALSE: All values that match the JSONPath expression are returned. |
| Ignore JSONPath not found error | Select TRUE to continue processing data on a route if no result matching your JSONPath expression was found. Select FALSE to make XEServer throw the exception and stop data processing. |
| List separator | Specify the delimiter to separate values in a result list, if multiple values were extracted from JSON data. |
| Decorate results | Select the action to be performed after the data is extracted: Unquote string value: Removes quotes if the extracted value is a single string, for example, value1. No decoration: The extracted data is returned without additional decoration, for example, “value1”. Collections are returned in square brackets. Remove brackets for collection values: The extracted list of values is returned without brackets, for example “value1, value2, value3”. Single values are returned in square brackets. |
| JSON Path | Specify Name and Value of JSON Path expressions to access fields of the JSON file and extract data from it. |



The following JSON Path expressions illustrate extracting data from the sample JSON document:


| Sample JSON file contents | JSONPath expression | Extracted Data |
| --- | --- | --- |
| { "field1": "key1", "field2": [ { "item1" : "itemValue1" }, { "item2" : "itemValue2" }, { "itemN" : "itemValueN" } ], "field3": { "property1": "value1", "property2": "value2", "propertyN": "valueN" } } | $['field2'][1] | { "item2" : "itemValue2" } |
| $['field2'][0]['item1'] | itemValue1 | |
| $['field3'] | {"property1" : "value1", "property2" : "value2", "propertyN" : "valueN"} | |
| $..[?(@.itemN)] | { "itemN" : "itemValueN" } | |



###### Memory consumption


During the processing, incoming JSON data is loaded to RAM. The following table displays the ratio of JSON data size to the amount of RAM consumed by the JSON Value extractor component for processing.


| JSON File Size | Memory Consumption |
| --- | --- |
| 1 MB | 25 MB |
| 2 MB | 29 MB |
| 3 MB | 52 MB |
| 4 MB | 69 MB |
| 5 MB | 48 MB |
| 10 MB | 82 MB |
| 15 MB | 148 MB |
| 20 MB | 171 MB |
| 30 MB | 246 MB |
| 40 MB | 367 MB |
| 50 MB | 391 MB |

---

<a id="process-call"></a>

#### Process Call

> 📄 Source: [Process_Call.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Process_Call.htm)

##### Process Call


Use the Process Call component to start an external process. This component waits for results and sets return code as a property on the original message.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: Process Call Components Setings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Command line | Command line to execute. |
| Working directory | Directory the command is executed in. |
| Execute command for each message | Execute command for each message of incoming multimessage exchange. If set to 'false' then command is executed once per message exchange. |
| Wait for process | Wait for completion of initiated process. |
| Report error if exit code doesn't equal 0 | Specifies if exit code returned by the process should be verified. If this parameter is set to 'true' and exit code supplied by process is not equal to 0 then original message is redirected to output 'error'. This parameter takes effect only if parameter 'Wait for process' is set to 'true'. |
| Pass inbound message | Pass inbound message to process. Message body is passed through standard input stream of the process. |
| Create message from output stream | Create message from the standard output stream of the process. |
| Message separator bytes | Separator bytes to use for splitting the standard output stream of the process to several output messages. This parameter takes effect only if parameter 'Create message from output stream' is set to 'true'. |
| Create message from error stream | Create message from the error output stream of the process. |
| Process environment | Use this pane to enter environment variables for starting an external process. |

---

<a id="rest-client"></a>

#### REST Client

> 📄 Source: [REST_Client.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/REST_Client.htm)

##### REST Client


Work with proxy servers


The component allows you to send HTTP requests to a RESTful server endpoint and get responses by using the synchronous connection type. For example, if you have to delete a record in a database (or perform some other action) that runs on a RESTful server, then the best way is to use REST Client to make a call to the RESTful server and pass action and record ID as parameters.


*To see the list of macros supported for this component, see XEServer supported macros. ## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab #### Connection tab Field Description URI Specify a URI that points to a RESTful server endpoint where you want to send the HTTP requests. HTTP Method Select an HTTP method to be used to send the requests. Connection Timeout (ms) A timeout in milliseconds within which the component should establish a connection with the specified server. If the timeout time expires, the component throws an exception. Read Timeout (ms) A timeout in milliseconds within which the component should receive a response. If the timeout time expires, the component throws an exception. Chunked transfer threshold (MB) To send requests with a relatively big payload (tens or hundreds of megabytes), you may want to enable HTTP Chunked Transfer Encoding. To do this, you can specify a threshold for the messages to be chunked. If the size of an XEServer message body to be sent as an HTTP request exceeds the threshold, the request is chunked and the request headers contain the HTTP header Transfer-Encoding: chunked. If the XEServer message body size is below the specified threshold, the payload is buffered and the corresponding content length is set as the HTTP header. Setting the value to -1 disables message chunking. Setting the value to 0 enables chunking for all messages, regardless of the body size. Enable auto redirect This option allows you to control the auto-redirect functionality. Note that the auto-redirect option is only available for the following HTTP response codes:301 Moved Permanently302 Moved Temporarily307 Temporary Redirect URI query parameters Use this pane to specify the URI query parameters. You can also specify the query parameters in the URI field, for example https://localhost:11123/rest**?name1=value1;name2=value2;***.


If you use a macro to specify a query parameter value and this macro is not resolved, the message processing fails. If this macro is resolved to an empty value, the parameter is added to the request without any value. To exclude such parameters from the request, select Exclude parameters with unresolved values and/or Exclude parameters with empty values.


Note Some characters are not permitted in the URI field, for example "%" or " "(white space). Query parameters with such characters should be specified in the URI query parameters pane.









###### Request Header tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Cookies | You can specify cookie parameters in the *<name>=<value>* format. Use ';' or '\r\n' characters as a separator. |
| Include to HTTP Headers | |
| Include exchange properties | Select this option to set all message exchange properties as request HTTP headers. |
| Include message properties | Select this option to set all message properties as request HTTP headers. |
| HTTP Headers | |
| In this pane, you can explicitly add custom HTTP request headers. **Note** For implementation steps on how to create custom HTTP request headers to work during the authorization phase, see [OAuth 2.0 Integration Scenarios](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/OAuth2.0_Integration_Scenarios.htm). | |



###### Authorization tab


Select the type of authorization that is used on the target server. The following options are available:

- Basic: In this mode, the user name and password are encoded in a Base64 string and sent to the server within the Authorization header.
- Digest: In this more complex and secure mode of authorization, the user name is sent as a plain text, and the password is sent as a hash code.
- None: No authorization is performed.
- OAuth 2.0: In this mode, the client (XEServer) requests an authorization server (a remote server) to issue a unique temporary access token to access resources on a resource server. The authorization server returns a unique access token. This token is then embedded in every request made by the REST Client component to the target URI. #### Caching of Access Token In order to avoid redundant token requests and hitting rate limits imposed by authorization servers, the REST Client component provides a build-in token caching functionality for the OAuth 2.0 authorization type. By default, any access token issued for a given service URL is cached and reused until it expires, based on the **expires_in** field provided in the token response. If the **expires_in** field is not present, the client applies a default expiration time of one hour. This default expiration time can be customized by defining the following system property (value in seconds) on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm):-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.oauth.token.expiresIn=3600To use the OAuth 2.0 authorization mechanism, you have to specify the following parameters: | Field | Description | | --- | --- | | Protocol Flow | Select the method of getting the access token from the authorization server. XEServer supports the following flows:[Resource Owner Password Credentials Grant](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-4.3)[Client Credentials Grant](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-4.4)**Note** See [OAuth 2.0 Integration Scenarios](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/OAuth2.0_Integration_Scenarios.htm) to explore solutions for using Username and Password with the Client Credentials Grant protocol when sending claims to the FOX Adjudication System. | | Access token | | | Access Token Endpoint URL | Enter the [endpoint URL](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-3.1) of the authorization server. The REST Client component makes a request to this URL in order to obtain the access token. | | Access Token Scope | Enter the [scope parameter](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-3.3) for the access token to be issued. The scope defines the exact type of access (for example: read, write) the client should obtain. | | Client | | | Identity | Enter a unique string to identify XEServer during the registration on the authorization server. | | Secret | Enter a password to protect the client's Identity. | | Credentials Location | Select a way for the client credentials (Identity and Secret) to be sent to the authorization server. The following options are available:Authorization Header (Basic Authentication Scheme): If selected, the client credentials are sent embedded within the authorization header in the basic authentication scheme.Form Fields (URL Encoded Form): Select if you want client credentials to be sent embedded within the body of the request. | | Resource OwnerAvailable for the Resource Owner Password Credentials Grant protocol flow only. | | | Username | Enter the user name for authorization on the resource server. | | Password | Enter the password for authorization on the resource server. |


###### Request Body tab


On this tab, you can define the representation of your request body. The additional options depend on the Mode you select.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Mode: Form-encoded In this mode, the request body content type is set to application/x-www-form-urlencoded. | |
| Create message from | Select XEServer Message to create the request body from the XEServer message, or select Custom Template to specify the form parameters as Name=Value pairs. |
| Include as form parameters | Select whether to include XEServer message exchange/message properties in the request body as form parameters. |
| Mode: Multipart In this mode, the request body content type is set to multipart/form-data. Note To establish a connection to XEConnect, the Multipart mode is used. | |
| Content-ID | Specify the multipart attachment identifier. By default, the value is set to {M_SID} |
| Content-Type | Select the attachment content type. |
| Create message from | Select XEServer Message to create the request body from the XEServer message, or select Custom Template to enter custom Content-ID, Content-type, and a source for generating each message part (Part Body) - either an XES message or a custom template. |
| Mode: Raw In this mode, you can set the data-specific content type. | |
| Content-Type | Select the request body content type. The following options are available:application/EDI-X12application/EDIFACTapplication/gzipapplication/json application/octet-streamapplication/soap+xmlapplication/xmlapplication/ziptext/htmltext/plaintext/xml |
| Create message from | Select XEServer Message to create the request body from the XEServer message or select Custom Template and enter the request body in the text box. |
| Mode: None Select this mode to exclude the body from the HTTP request. | |



Note Only requests with PUT and POST HTTP methods can have a body.


###### Response Mapping tab


On this tab, you can specify how HTTP responses map to XEServer message exchanges. The mapping process is different for application/x-www-form-urlencoded, Multipart/form-data, and Other content types:

- application/x-www-form-urlencoded: For each response of this type, the component creates a new XEServer message exchange with one empty XEServer message. All form parameters from the HTTP response body are set as message properties.
- Other: For each response of this type, the component creates a new XEServer message exchange with one XEServer message. The HTTP response body is mapped to the XEServer message body and the HTTP response headers are set as message properties in the HTTPHeaderName=HTTPHeaderValue format.
- Multipart/form-data: For each HTTP response of this type, the component creates a new XEServer message exchange with a separate XEServer message for each response part. The body of each response part can be mapped to either XEServer message body or to a message exchange property, depending on the Mapping Mode you select. Responses of multipart/alternative, multipart/mixed, and multipart/related content types are mapped in the same way as responses with Other content type.


Note Ensure that [boundaries](https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc1341/7_2_Multipart.html) in the multipart responses are properly specified. If not, the response parts cannot be properly parsed and converted to XEServer objects.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Populate message properties from | Select whether the component should map HTTP headers and Cookies to XEServer message properties. |
| Use custom mapping for multipart response | Select this option to specify a custom mapping strategy for multipart responses. If the option is cleared, each multipart HTTP response creates one XEServer message exchange and each response part produces a separate XEServer message within this message exchange. The following modes are available: Convert each multipart body to exchange message: For each HTTP response part, maps the part's body to the XEServer message body. Convert each multipart body to exchange property: For each HTTP response part, maps the part's body to an XEServer message exchange property. The name attribute of the Content-Disposition response header is used as the name for the message exchange property. Custom mode: In this mode, you can specify how to map each individual HTTP response part: either to an XEServer message body or an message exchange property. To identify a response part, use the name attribute of the response header in the Part ID field. If there is no part with the specified name in the response, that part is not mapped.   |



###### SSL tab


On this tab, you can configure SSL parameters for the secure connection with the server.


Warning: DSA certificates are not supported because of the migration to Java 17. For SSL connections, you have to [use RSA certificates](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DSA_Certificates_Support.htm) instead.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use default Java trust store | Select this option to get trusted certificates from the currently used Java installation. |
| Enable hostname validation | If selected, the component compares the server's host name with the host name in the certificate (the certificate that the server sends to XEServer). If the names do not match, the SSL connection is not established. |
| Trusted store The TrustedStore is a KeyStore file that contains public keys (certificates) of the external hosts that you trust. | |
| Path | Enter the path to the TrustedStore with trusted certificates. |
| Password | Enter the password to the TrustedStore. |
| Check certificate expiration | If set to True, the expiration date of the trust certificate is checked and an exception is thrown if an expired certificate is encountered. |
| Key store (required for SSL with client authentication) If [client authentication](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E19424-01/820-4811/aakhe/index.html) is enabled on the server, this KeyStore kicks in. The KeyStore must contain XEServer key pairs (a private key and a certificate with a public key) used by the RESTful server to validate the XEServer's identity. | |
| Path | Enter the path to the KeyStore file that contains the key pair entries. |
| Password | Enter the password to the KeyStore. |
| Key password | Enter the password to the key pair. |



###### Preview tab


On this tab, you can preview the request to be sent with all the parameters you have specified.


###### Work with proxy servers


You can configure the component to communicate with a remote server through a proxy. To work with a proxy server, define the following properties on the [Profile Startup Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Profile_Startup_Tab.htm) and prefix each property with "-D", for example:


-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.enabled=true


| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.enabled | Indicates whether to use a proxy server - can be either true or false. If set to false, the properties listed below will not come into effect. |
| com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.host | The IP address or the host name of the proxy server to be used. |
| com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.port | The port number to be used for the connection. |
| com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.authorization.type | The authorization type to be used when connecting to the proxy server. The following values are allowed: None: No authorization is performed on the proxy server. Basic: A basic authorization (Base64-encoded name and password). Digest: A more complex authorization (plain text user name and a hashed password). [NTLM](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/desktop/secauthn/microsoft-ntlm): A Windows-specific authorization based on user name and a password. |
| com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.username | The user name to connect to the proxy server. Note For [NTLM](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/desktop/secauthn/microsoft-ntlm) authorization, the user name must contain a domain name, for example: someDomain\Administrator. |
| com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.password | The user password to connect to the proxy server. |



Here is how a sample configuration that works with the xx.xx.xx.xx proxy server looks like:


-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.enabled=true


-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.host=xx.xx.xx.xx


-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.port=80


-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.authorization.type=NTLM


-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.username=domain\Administrator


-Dcom.edifecs.etools.xeserver.rest.client.proxy.password=123456

---

<a id="soap-call"></a>

#### SOAP Call

> 📄 Source: [SOAP_Call.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/SOAP_Call.htm)

##### SOAP Call


Note Use of this routing component requires a Professional XEServer license. In order to unlock these capabilities, please obtain the Professional license through Edifecs Customer Support.


The Simple Object Access Protocol (SOAP) Call component picks data from a route, sends it in the form of the SOAP message request to a web service, and receives a SOAP message response by using a synchronous connection type. The transfer of data happens through the HTTP protocol. Depending on your business needs, SOAP Call can be considered as an alternative to the [REST Client.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/REST_Client.htm)



To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#SOAP).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### SOAP Request tab


On this tab you can configure SOAP message request parameters.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| WS URL | Enter the URI to the SOAP web service. The URI schema is used for enabling/disabling the SSL connection type. Use **https** to activate SSL for your connection. |
| SOAP message template | Select the template for generating SOAP requests. Select Default to generate SOAP requests of 1.1 or 1.2 versions. Or select Custom and enter the path to the file with the custom template that is used for request generation. By engaging custom templates, you can generate requests with more complex structure, thus adopting SOAP for your business requirements. This field supports referencing to Smart Trading Cloud Repository. The templates used for 1.1 and 1.2 SOAP versions are located at the xes-soap-call-9.3.0.jar archive under ${XESRoot}\features\route-engine\components\soap-call\lib directory. |
| SOAP version | Select the supported SOAP version. To get more information about SOAP versions, see [SOAP 1.1](http://www.w3.org/TR/2000/NOTE-SOAP-20000508/) and [SOAP 1.2](http://www.w3.org/TR/soap12/) specifications. |
| Template Path | Specify the absolute path to the custom template. The {Prp[]} and {M_Body} macros are available for this field. |
| Set SOAP action to header | Select this option to append the [SOAP action](http://www.w3.org/TR/2000/NOTE-SOAP-20000508#_Toc478383528) to HTTP request headers. The SOAP action parameter is a unique URI to a web service method. It is used to define which of the web service methods should be used to process the current request. |
| SOAP action | Enter the URI to the required method. If the value is an empty string, the system looks for a SOAP action in the request URI. Note This mechanism is mostly used with the version SOAP 1.1. |
| Encoding | Select the encoding to be used for reading content from a custom template and from incoming XEServer messages. The selected value is set as the "charset" attribute to the Content-Type HTTP header. |
| Connection Timeout | The timeout in milliseconds to establish a connection to a web service. |
| Read Timeout | The maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a response. |
| Report whole SOAP response body | Select this option to receive the whole SOAP response content including envelope, header, and body tags. If disabled, then only the data extracted from the body is received. |



###### SSL tab


On this tab, you can configure connection security parameters.


Warning: DSA certificates are not supported because of the migration to Java 17. For SSL connections, you have to [use RSA certificates](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DSA_Certificates_Support.htm) instead.


| Section | Field | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|   | Use default Java trust store | Select this option to get trust store certificates from currently used Java installation when establishing SSL connection. |
|   | Enable hostname validation | Select this option to ensure security by validating the host name. |
| Trusted store | Path | Enter the path to the key store with trusted certificates. |
| Password | Enter the password to the key store with trusted certificates. | |
| Check certificate expiration | If set to True, the expiration date of the trust certificate is checked and an exception is thrown if an expired certificate is encountered. | |
| Key store | Path | Enter the path to the file which contains key store certificates. |
| Password | Enter the password to the file which contains key store certificates. | |
| Key password | Enter the password to the private key. | |



###### Outputs Tab


| Output Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| response | Messages with SOAP responses are directed to this output. |
| fault | Messages that contain SOAP fault codes in their body are directed to this output. |
| original | Original messages are directed to this output. |
| error | Messages that could not be processed due to system, route, or component errors. |

---

<a id="salesforce-components"></a>

#### Salesforce Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_Salesforce_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Salesforce_Components.htm)

##### Salesforce Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="salesforce-search-component"></a>

##### Salesforce Search Component

> 📄 Source: [Salesforce_Search_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Salesforce_Search_Component.htm)

###### Salesforce Search Component


With the Salesforce Search component, you can make queries to Salesforce objects. The component works only with SOQL which is a proprietary Salesforce query language. It has similar syntax as SQL, but does not support any statements except SELECT. For more information about SOQL syntax, see [Salesforce Object Query Language](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.soql_sosl.meta/soql_sosl/).


When the component is added to a route, you can view the following settings:



To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Salesforce).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use connection settings from component | Select this option to unbind the component form the currently used Salesforce Service and use custom connectivity settings for this particular component. If you chose to select this option, enter the connection settings on the Connection tab. |
| Service name | Select the service to use. If none exists, click Create New. Click View to go the service configuration page. |
| Response content type | Select the data format in which the component sends responses to a route. |
| Import configuration from SpecBuilder | Click to load pre-saved configurations from SpecBuilder. By default, the configuration file is located at ${ECRootPath}/Common/SalesforceConfig.json. Note Loading a configuration from SpecBuilder overwrites your query and the Response content type value. |



Use the text area to enter SOQL statements.


###### Connection tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| API version | Select the API version you are going to work with. For more information on getting the latest API version, see [official documentation](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/dome_versions.htm). |
| Authorization type | Select the type of authorization. The OAuth type requires you to enter the fields Client ID and Client secret. Since Salesforce supports several OAuth authentication flow types, note that XEServer implements the Username-Password OAuth Authentication Flow. |
| Username | Enter the login to your account. |
| Password | Enter the password to your account. |
| Check Authentication | Click to verify the correctness of the entered credentials. |
| Load Credential | Click to select the Salesforce account that should be used by the component. By default, the configuration file with available accounts resides at ${ECRootPath}/Common/SalesforceConfig.json. For more information, see [Import Salesforce Credentials](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Import_Credential.htm). Note Loading credentials overwrites all values on the Connection tab. |
| Security token | Enter your account security token. This token is issued by Salesforce to allow third-party software to connect to the Salesforce Restful API. You can reset the token on the Personal Settings page at the Salesforce portal. |
| Client ID | Required for the OAuth authorization type. Enter your Salesforce consumer key. |
| Client secret | Required for the OAuth authorization type. Enter your Salesforce consumer secret. |



###### Outputs Tab


SOQL query results retrieved from Salesforce are sent to a route as XEServer message exchange that has one XEServer message containing records in the XML or JSON format. If Salesforce returns only a part of the query results (Salesforce can send a limited number of records within one response), a separate message exchange is generated for the remaining records.


Use the {MD[SF_QueryDone]} macro to verify the search completion status (returns TRUE if all query results are received from Salesforce) and the {MD[SF_QueryRecordsCount]} macro to get the total number of query results.

---

<a id="salesforce-component"></a>

##### Salesforce Component

> 📄 Source: [Salesforce_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Salesforce_Component.htm)

###### Salesforce Component


With the Salesforce route component, you can perform CRUD operations with Salesforce objects. The component works only with XML and JSON data formats, however, the input and output data formats can differ. For example, the component can expect XML on the input and generate JSON as output.


When the component is added to a route, you can view the following settings:



To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Salesforce).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Import configuration from SpecBuilder | Click to load the Salesforce settings from SpecBuilder. By default, the configuration file is located at %ECRootPath%/Common/SalesforceConfig.json. Note Loading a configuration from Specbuilder overwrites the Response content type, Object type, and Object fields values. |
| Use connection settings from component | Select this option to unbind the component from the currently used Salesforce Service and use custom connectivity settings for this particular component. If you choose to select this option, enter connection settings on the Connection tab. |
| Service name | Select the service to use. If none exists, click Create New. Click View to go to the service configuration page. |
| Operation | Select one of the following operations to perform with a Salesforce object:CreateDeleteRetrieveUpdateUpsert (Update or Insert)Note that the Upsert operation initially attempts to update a record, and if the required record does not exist, a new record is inserted. If several records match an upsert operation request, the response with the "300" status code and a list of matching records is returned. Also, you can perform this operation only by the primary object ID type. |
| Object type | In the Salesforce cloud storage, an object implies a database table which contains logically related information, such as accounts, personal data, applications, and so on. Select an object to perform the operation. Click Load Objects to see the list of available objects in storage. |
| Object ID | Select the type of id (can be either primary or external) and enter a unique ID of the Salesforce object you want to work with. |
| Object fields | Available only for the Retrieve operation. Enter the Salesforce object columns that should be retrieved. Click to select the columns available for the currently selected Object type. The fields should be separated by a comma. If the value is empty, all existing fields are fetched. |



###### Connection tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| API version | Select the API version you are going to work with. For more information on getting the latest API version, see [official documentation](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/dome_versions.htm). |
| Authorization type | Select the type of authorization. The OAuth type requires you to enter the fields Client ID and Client secret. Since Salesforce supports several OAuth authentication flow types, note that XEServer implements the Username-Password OAuth Authentication Flow. |
| Username | Enter the login to your account. |
| Password | Enter the password to your account. |
| Check Authentication | Click to verify the correctness of the entered credentials. |
| Load Credential | Click to select the Salesforce account that should be used by the component. By default, the configuration file with available accounts resides at ${ECRootPath}/Common/SalesforceConfig.json. For more information, see [Import Salesforce Credentials](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Import_Credential.htm). Note Loading credentials overwrites all values on the Connection tab. |
| Security token | Enter your account security token. This token is issued by Salesforce to allow third-party software to connect to the Salesforce Restful API. You can reset the token on the Personal Settings page at the Salesforce portal. |
| Client ID | Required for the OAuth authorization type. Enter your Salesforce consumer key. |
| Client secret | Required for the OAuth authorization type. Enter your Salesforce consumer secret. |



###### Outputs Tab


Data sent through the Salesforce component outputs depends on the operation performed:


| Operation | Output Data |
| --- | --- |
| CREATE | The original XEServer message. Exchange headers on the original output contain a property with the fixed name SalesforceObjectID that matches the ID of the created Salesforce object. You can access the property by using the {Prp[SalesforceObjectID]} macro. |
| DELETE, UPDATE | The original XEServer message. Message properties contain a status code of the operation that was performed. Use the {MD[SF_StatusCode]} macro to get the response status code. |
| RETRIEVE | An XEServer message with data retrieved from a Salesforce object in the XML or JSON format. |
| UPSERT | If a new record was added, message exchange headers on the original output contain a property with the fixed name SalesforceObjectID that matches the ID of the created Salesforce object. If a record was updated, the original XEServer message is returned and message properties contain a status code of the operation that was performed. Use the {MD[SF_StatusCode]} macro to get the response status code. |

---

<a id="subroute"></a>

#### Subroute

> 📄 Source: [Subroute.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Subroute.htm)

##### Subroute


The Subroute component is used to pass control to a linked subroute. Using subroutes allows you to simplify complex routes by splitting them into separate routes.


The following are some important recommendations when using a subroute data processor:

- Add the Parent Route endpoints to the target route before linking the Subroute component to existing routes.
- When creating a subroute, use the Parent Route endpoints to mark entry points in a subroute.
- The subroute must have input fields that correspond to the Subroute processor specified on the main route.
- The best practice is to return all outbound data back to the parent route.
- JMS and Data Storage outbound endpoints are supported for subroutes, but we do not recommend that you use them with this type of routes.
- Subroute errors are reported in the corresponding subroutes. At the same time, other errors are reported in the parent route that contains the Subroute component. This errors indicate that a task wasn’t successfully completed on a subroute. We do not report the details of the error because multiple errors are also possible.
- You can double-click the component icon on the route editor to open the target route.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the Subroute Configuration wizard:



In this wizard, you should select whether you want to link the current Subroute component to a new route or to existing one. For the New route option, enter the name of the new route to be created, and for Existing route select the route you want to link to from the list of available routes.


Note The list of existing routes includes only routes that already have the [Parent Route Input](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Parent_Route_Inbound.htm) component.


After you link the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: Subroute Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Subroute name** | The name of the route linked to the Subroute processor. You must create the subroute in Edifecs Application Manager for the subroute to appear in the list of available subroutes. |
| **Service timeout ms** | The timeout threshold for the subroute service in milliseconds. |
| Ignore subroute failures | When enabled, the Subroute component prevents the parent route from failing, thereby avoiding excessive "java.lang.Exception: One or more sub tasks are failed" errors. If the subroute includes an error channel component, this property allows you to customize the behavior to bypass failures accumulating in logs. |

---

<a id="tracking-and-duplication"></a>

#### Tracking and Duplication

> 📄 Source: [Tracking_and_Duplicates.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Tracking_and_Duplicates.htm)

##### Tracking and Duplicates Component


With XEServer Tracking and Duplicates route component, you can pick up data from a route and send it to the Tracking and Duplication Client Service (which will send data to the Tracking and Duplication Server Service) associated with the current component. To be precise, the component does not pass entire XEServer message to the Tracking and Duplication Client Service. Instead, the component passes a set of user-defined keys with values calculated from the current message and message exchange properties. Using these key-value sets, XEServer can uniquely identify a certain XEServer message.


After verification is complete, the component directs the incoming message to one of the outputs, depending on the verification results.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab #### Configuration tab Field Description Service name Select the Tracking and Duplication Client Service to be used. Click View to go to the service settings page. If there are no services available on the page, click Create new to create a new service. Namespace Type a namespace to group items by a criterion that makes a group easily distinguishable. For example, you can group items by trading partners. Check mode Select the data processing mode: Check and create new: Allows you to register data in OpenSearch storage if the data file is processed for the first time. Check only: Allows you to only verify incoming data against that already existing in the storage and prevents the service from registering new data. Report original properties on duplicate Select this option to receive a list of attributes of an original item (the one previously registered in storage) if this item has already been processed. The attributes are extracted in the JSON format. Prefix Specify a prefix to be prepended to properties of the original message if the current message is duplicated. By default, the value is set to dup_{C_Name}_*. Ignore unresolved values Select this option to continue verifying items even if one of key properties values cannot be resolved. Key properties Specify the Name and Value pairs to define the key pairs used for calculation of the message identifier. Macros are allowed for the Value field.


Note If a macro is not resolved and Ignore unresolved values is cleared, the message processing fails.









###### Technical Duplicates tab


When working with the [Checkpoint](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Checkpoint.htm) component, you may encounter the following situation:



Step 1: The Checkpoint component receives an XEServer message (for example, a claim).


Step 2: The Tracking and Duplication component receives the claim and registers the claim in the OpenSearch storage since the claim has been processed for the first time.


Step 3: The Unique Claims Web Service Outbound component receives the claim through the Tracking and Duplication component's unique output. However, due to connectivity issues, XEServer is unable to access the required Web service host temporarily.


Step 4: In this case, the Checkpoint Service rolls back the failed operation and XEServer attempts to process the current claim again.


Step 5: The Tracking and Duplication component receives the claim again but at this time the claim is marked as duplicate since it was previously registered in the OpenSearch storage.


Step 6: The claim is directed to the Duplicates Web Service Outbound component which is an incorrect destination.


To handle such situations, the concept technical duplicate comes into play. A technical duplicate is a piece of data which is marked as duplicate due to a failure in delivering this data to the destination endpoint and thereby is submitted to OpenSearch storage by the Checkpoint Service repeatedly.


To distinguish real and technical duplicates, the two properties are passed with every message to the Tracking and Duplication component:

- User Task ID
- Task ID


Technical duplicates are those, that have identical "User Task ID" but different "Task ID" properties.
