The following settings are displayed on the Technical Duplicates tab:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Detect technical duplicates | Select this option to verify all duplicates against technical duplication. |
| User Task ID | Set the scope of messages that should be checked for technical duplication. Only messages with the entered "User Task ID" value but different "Task ID" property values are marked as duplicates. |
| Technical duplicate marker property name | Enter a name for the message exchange property which is generated only for technical duplicates. The property value is set to TRUE. |



###### Attributes tab


On this tab, you can control metadata that is transferred with processing results.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Attribute parameters | In this pane you can specify a custom name-value pair to be appended to metadata. Name: Enter the name of the attribute parameter. Value: Enter the value of the attribute parameter. |

---

<a id="velocity"></a>

#### Velocity

> 📄 Source: [Book_Velocity_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Velocity_Components.htm)

##### Velocity Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="velocity-component"></a>

##### Velocity Component

> 📄 Source: [Velocity_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Velocity_Component.htm)

###### Velocity Component


With this component, you can transform data by providing a [Velocity template](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/1.7/user-guide.html#velocity-template-language-vtl-an-introduction). The component interprets [Velocity Template Language](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/1.7/user-guide.html#velocity-template-language-vtl-an-introduction) by using the embedded Apache Velocity Engine (version 2.3).


Note Starting from XEServer 9.2.3, you may need to upgrade your templates to work with a new version of the embedded Apache Velocity Engine that has been upgraded in XEServer 9.2.3 to the 2.3 version. To view the list with breaking changes, click [here](https://velocity.apache.org/engine/2.3/upgrading.html#upgrading-from-velocity-16x-to-velocity-17x).


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: Velocity Configuration tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Velocity template** | Specify a path to the Velocity template file. The template file can be of any extension but should adhere to the Velocity Template Language syntax. For more information, see [Select a Velocity Template](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Select_and_Edit_Velocity_Template.htm). |
| Template encoding | Specify the encoding of the template file. |
| Output encoding | Specify the encoding of the output file. |
| Parameters | Use this pane to define variables that can be referenced within a template file. In this pane, you can use macros for the Value field to dynamically update the template file. Note The field Name is case-sensitive. To reference these variables within the template file, you can use the shorthand notation $<variableName> (for example, $myVar) and the [silent shorthand notation](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/1.7/vtl-reference.html) $!{variableName} (for example, $!{myVar}) |
| Use exchange and message properties as parameters | Select this option if you want to automatically add message exchange and message properties to the list of variables that can be referenced inside the template file. |



###### Tools tab


On this tab, you can configure the component to use [Velocity GenericTools](http://velocity.apache.org/tools/devel/generic.html#tools). Velocity GenericTools is a set of helper functions that provide a basic infrastructure for working with Velocity templates, such as formatting dates, numbers, text, and so on. For more information on using Velocity Tools, see [Velocity Tools](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Velocity_Tools.htm).

---

<a id="select-a-velocity-template"></a>

##### Select a Velocity Template

> 📄 Source: [Select_and_Edit_Velocity_Template.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Select_and_Edit_Velocity_Template.htm)

###### Select and Edit a Velocity Template


When you configure the Velocity component, you should use a template used for transformation. The template file can be of any extension but should adhere to Velocity Template Language syntax.


To select a Velocity template:

- In the Route Viewer, click the Velocity component you want to configure.
- On the Route component properties tab, click Settings.
- On the Configuration tab, click Browse next to the Velocity template box. The File Path dialog box is displayed.

- In the File box, enter the corresponding file path, or select one from the List of files box.
- Click OK.


To edit a Velocity template:

1. Select the corresponding Velocity template, and then on the Configuration tab, click Edit next to the Velocity template box. The Edit dialog box is displayed.


- View and edit the Velocity template.
- Click Save.


Inside a velocity template, you can reference variables defined in the Parameters pane, global exchange object, and those Velocity tools you activated on the Tools tab. To do this, use the "$" notation inside your template file, for example: $MyVar, $exchange, $date.long, and so on.


You can also select templates from the Smart Trading Cloud Repository. To do this:

1. Specify a path to a template file you want to edit in your repository.
2. On the Configuration tab, click Browse next to the Velocity template field.
3. In the File Path dialog box, select Cloud repository, and then navigate to the file you need. The selected file is pulled in from the repository to the Velocity template field.


To edit a file from the repository:

1. After you have selected a file from the repository, on the Configuration tab, click Edit next to the Velocity template field. The Edit dialog box is displayed.
2. View and edit the file from the repository.
3. Click Save.

---

<a id="velocity-tools"></a>

##### Velocity Tools

> 📄 Source: [Velocity_Tools.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Velocity_Tools.htm)

###### Velocity Tools


Velocity Tools are helper functions that provide a basic infrastructure for working with Velocity templates, such as sorting, formatting dates, URL building, and so on. XEServer comes with [Velocity GenericTools v 2.0](http://velocity.apache.org/tools/devel/generic.html).


*[Image: Velocity Tools tab]*


| Column Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Alias | A name used to reference the tool object with the Velocity template. Use the shorthand notation $aliasName or the [silent shorthand notation](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/1.7/vtl-reference.html) $!{aliasName} to call the tool's methods. |
| Class Name | The fully qualified name (including package name) of the class that implements the tool's functionality. The compiled class must be available at run time using the class loading mechanism. |



###### Velocity Tools Example


To format date and time using DateTool (comes with XEServer), you have to define the tool class org.apache.velocity.tools.generic.DateTool with the alias Date on the Tools tab of the component's settings.


Example


// Formatting date and time using the DateTool


$Date             -> Oct 19, 2017 9:54:50 PM


$Date.long        -> October 19, 2017 9:54:50 PM PDT


$Date.medium_time -> 9:54:50 PM


XEServer comes with the following [Generic Tools](http://velocity.apache.org/tools/devel/generic.html#tools).


| Verlocity Tool Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| AlternatorTool | Used for creating Alternators to easily alternate over a set of values. |
| ClassTool | Simplifies reflective lookup of information about Classes and their fields, methods and constructors. |
| ContextTool | Used for convenient access to Context data and meta-data. |
| ConversionTool | Used for converting String values to richer object types. |
| DateTool, ComparisonDateTool | Used for manipulating, formatting, and comparing dates. |
| DisplayTool | Used for controlling display of references (e.g. truncating values, "pretty printing" lists, and displaying alternates when a reference is null). |
| EscapeTool | Used for common escaping needs in Velocity templates (e.g. escaping html, xml, javascript etc.). |
| FieldTool | Provides easy access to static fields in a class, such as string constants. |
| LoopTool | A convenience tool to use with #foreach loops. It wraps a list with a custom iterator to provide greater control, allowing loops to end early, skip ahead and more. |
| LinkTool | Used for creating and manipulating URIs and URLs. The API for this tool is designed to closely resemble that of the VelocityView tool of the same name. |
| ListTool | Used for working with arrays and lists, treats both transparently the same. |
| MathTool | Used for performing math functions. |
| NumberTool | Used for formatting and converting numbers. |
| RenderTool | Used to evaluate and render arbitrary strings of VTL, including recursive rendering. |
| ResourceTool | Simplifies access to ResourceBundles for internationalization or other dynamic content needs. |
| SortTool | Used to sort collections (or arrays, iterators, etc) on any arbitary set of properties exposed by the objects contained within the collection. |
| XmlTool | Used for reading/navigating XML files. This uses dom4j under the covers and provides complete XPath support. |

---

<a id="add-custom-velocity-tool"></a>

##### Add Custom Velocity Tool

> 📄 Source: [Add_Custom_Velocity_Tool.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Add_Custom_Velocity_Tool.htm)

###### Add Custom Velocity Tool


Apart from using the built-in [Apache GenericTools](http://velocity.apache.org/tools/devel/generic.html), you can implement your own tools and use them within templates. To do this, you have to create a Java class that implements the logic, compile the class, and add the compiled class to XEServer class loading. These steps are described below.


To use a custom Velocity tool:

1. Create the following Java class: package com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.velocity.tool; public class HelloTool {    public String hello(String name) {       return "Hello, " + name;    }   @Override    public String toString() {       return hello("Tool");    } }
2. Compile the .java file.
3. Put the .class file to the ${XESRoot}/platform/lib.user/shared directory. This adds the class to XEServer class loader thus, making it available during the run time.
4. In EAM Route Editor, select the Velocity component you want to work with.
5. On the component's Settings tab, on the Tools tab, define the new tool as shown in the image:
6. Create a new .vlct template file.
7. On the component's Settings tab, on the Configuration tab, in Velocity template field, enter the path to the created template file.
8. Next to the Velocity template field, click Edit.
9. Add the following expression to the template: $Hello.hello($Name) This expression invokes the method Hello.hello() method with the parameter $Name.
10. Save the profile configuration.
11. Submit a data to the Velocity component's input to view the results.


For more information on creating custom Velocity tools, see [Apache Velocity documentation](http://velocity.apache.org/tools/devel/creating-tools.html).

---

#### Visualizer Reporter

<a id="web-service-call"></a>

#### Web Service Call

> 📄 Source: [Book_Web_Service_Call.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Web_Service_Call.htm)

##### Web Service Call


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="web-service-call-overview"></a>

##### Web Service Call Overview

> 📄 Source: [Web_Service_Call_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Web_Service_Call_Overview.htm)

###### Web Service Call Overview


Note Use of this routing component requires a Professional XEServer license. In order to unlock these capabilities, please obtain the Professional license through Edifecs Customer Support.


The Web Service Call data processor route component can request data from an external Web service and invoke its methods to process data so that the returned data can be processed by a route.


The Web Service Call component can be used to integrate the functionality of a Web service into the processing behavior of a route. This component can be configured to connect to any Web Service, including the XEServer Web Service. Once a connection to the Web service has been established, the component must be configured so that the Web Service Call component handles the SOAP request and response messages.


Some important considerations for when to use a Web Service Call processor include:

- A Web Service should be available to the route; if this service is not available to the route, then it must be added and enabled. The XEServer Web Service may be used.
- The corresponding Service Settings should be configured. For more information, see [Web Service General Settings](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Web_Service_Call_Component.htm).
- The WSDL should be able to send requests to the Web Service Call component as a SOAP message that can be understood by the processor component.
- The Web Service Call sends SOAP responses back. The response that is sent should be configured to be understood by the client application.


*[Image: Web Service Call]*

---

<a id="web-service-call-component"></a>

##### Web Service Call Component

> 📄 Source: [Web_Service_Call_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Web_Service_Call_Component.htm)

###### Web Service Call Component


When you add the component to the route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: Web Service Call Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Service tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **WSDL URL** | View the full URL to the WSDL description of the external Web service. |
| **WS Url** | Select the URL of the external Web service operation to perform. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+[spacebar]. If a Web Service Call component and XEServer Web Service are used in the same profile, you must specify a port number in WS URL. To get the port number which is currently used by XEServer Web Service (if the service is started in the Platform Jetty mode), use the ${com.edifecs.etools.xeserver.profile.http.port} macro or go to Admin Console and check the HTTP column of your profile. |
| **WS Binding** | Select the binding interface to use from the drop-down menu. This setting requires that the WSDL URL be specified first. |
| **Method to Call** | Select the method of the external Web service that the Web Service Call uses. |
| Set SOAP action to header | Select this option to append the [SOAP action](http://www.w3.org/TR/2000/NOTE-SOAP-20000508#_Toc478383528) to HTTP request headers. The SOAP action parameter is a unique URI to a Web service method. It is used to define which of the Web service methods should be used to process the current request. |
| SOAP action | Enter the URI to the required method. If the value is an empty string, the system looks for a SOAP action in the request URI. Note This mechanism is mostly used with the version SOAP 1.1. |
| **Connection Timeout** | Enter the length time (in milliseconds) that the connection to an external Web service remains open while the route waits for a response. |
| **Read Timeout** | Enter the length time (in milliseconds) that a read operation requested from the Web Service Call waits for a successful response from an external Web service. |



###### Request tab


On this tab you can set up the structure of request messages to be sent to a remote Web Service.


For detailed information on how to set up a request, see [Set up the Request Message](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Set_Up_the_Request_Message.htm).


###### Response tab


On this tab you can set up the structure of response messages to be received from a remote Web Service.


For detailed information on how to set up a response, see [Set up the Response Message](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Set_Up_the_Response_Message.htm).


###### SSL tab


On this tab, you can configure connection security parameters.


Warning: DSA certificates are not supported because of the migration to Java 17. For SSL connections, you have to [use RSA certificates](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/DSA_Certificates_Support.htm) instead.


| Section | Field | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|   | Use default Java trust store | Select this option to get trust store certificates from currently used Java installation when establishing SSL connection. |
|   | Enable hostname validation | Select this option to ensure security by validating the host name. |
| Trusted store | Path | Specify the path to the key store with trusted certificates. |
| Password | Specify the password to the key store with trusted certificates. | |
| Check certificate expiration | If set to True, the expiration date of the trust certificate is checked and an exception is thrown if an expired certificate is encountered. | |
| Key store | Path | Specify the path to the file which contains key store certificates. |
| Password | Specify the password to the file which contains key store certificates. | |
| Key password | Specify the password to the private key. | |



###### WS-Security tab


On this tab, you can provide user authentication with a user name and password for Web Service.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Authorization type** | The type of authentication to be used with a server. The following options are available: None: No authentication to be used with a server. Digest: The encrypted user name and the hashed password to be used for the Web Service Security. Text: The user name and password in plain text to be used for the Web Service Security. |
| User name | Enter a user name for the Web Service Security. |
| Password | Enter a password for the Web Service Security, Click to show the password. |

---

<a id="set-up-the-request-message"></a>

##### Set Up the Request Message

> 📄 Source: [Set_Up_the_Request_Message.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Set_Up_the_Request_Message.htm)

###### Set Up the Request Message


To set up the request message:

1. Add the Web Service Call processing component to a route.
2. Ensure that the corresponding Web service is configured.
3. On the Request tab, click Edit.
4. In the SOAP Message dialog box, define the structure of the message and add additional messages and header groupings as required. Define the required values for each of the message, header, name, and value elements within the SOAP message. Macros can be used to define these values.


*[Image: Request message settings]*


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **XPath** | Select an element from the Node pane, and then click Set XPath to set an XPath expression to the elements that you require in the response. |
| **Source** | Click this tab to display the SOAP message code that is sent to the external Web Service. |
| **Table** | Provide values for the SOAP message parameters to send to the external Web Service. To enter or edit values, click Edit to open the SOAP Message Dialog box. |


1. When finished, click OK.

---

<a id="set-up-the-response-message"></a>

##### Set Up the Response Message

> 📄 Source: [Set_Up_the_Response_Message.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Set_Up_the_Response_Message.htm)

###### Set Up the Response Message


Responses received from a remote Web Service are located in a SOAP message. This message should be configured so that it is transformed properly into the format required by an external client application.


There are two options for formatting the SOAP response:

- Simple mode. You can define the data type for saving responses (either XML or text), indicate whether the response should be added to the header and enter a name for that header. All returned data is included with the header of the message that was processed.
- Advanced mode. You can suse XPath expressions to define how the message, body, and header should be processed, the number and type of matches that should be resolved (as part of the response), and what types of separators should be used for headers. The message, body, and header should be mapped specifically to the structure of the SOAP message.


To set up the response message: 



After you add the Web Service Call component to a route, the following settings are available for configuration:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Body (tab)** | Use the Body pane to construct requests and responses. For requests, the Body pane is available for editing XPath expressions and their values. For responses, click on an element in the Body pane, and then click Set XPath to enter the XPath expression. |
| **Body (advance response)** | Enter a string to use for the response body or click Edit to enter an XPath. |
| **Body Base64** | Specify whether the message is encoded by using a Base64 scheme. |
| **List Separator** | When Match Resolution is All, enter a character or string to delimit the list of results in the response header. |
| **Match Resolution** | Select an option to accept the First result in the response message, the Last result, or All result values. When the match resolution is set to All, parameter names in the response are appended with _1,_2, _3, and so on, to distinguish the returned name/value pairs. When returning values and match resolution is set to List, the body of the response message is a delimited list of values. Set the delimiter by using Separator String or Separator Bytes. |
| **Message XPath** | An expression that locates the results within the response message. |
| **Message body as constant message** | The response message body is the contents of the string specified by the Message XPath. |
| **Name (Response Header)** | Enter the name of the header field to be added to the response message. |
| **Response Serialization Mode** | Select the format of the Web service response that the Web Service Call returns to the data output stream of the component. Available modes are XML or text. |
| **Separator Bytes** | Enter the byte value to use to delimit multiple results. |
| **Separator String** | Enter the string to use to delimit multiple results. |
| **Set XPath** | Select an element from the Node pane, and then click Set XPath to set an XPath expression to the elements that you require in the response. |
| **Value (Response Header)** | Enter the value of the header field to be added to the response message. Click Edit to enter an XPath to a result value in the message body. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+[spacebar]. |

---

<a id="xml-components"></a>

#### XML Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_XML_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_XML_Components.htm)

##### XML Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="xml-value-extractor"></a>

##### XML Value Extractor

> 📄 Source: [XML_Value_Extractor.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Value_Extractor.htm)

###### XML Value Extractor


XML Value Extractor is a routing data processor component that allows you to extract data from XML document nodes and build complex properties. By using the XPath expression language, you can access XML document nodes and extract values. The output of this component is the original XML document and a property file, which contains values. For more information on practical use of the component, see [Work with XML Value Extractor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Work_with_XML_Value_Extractor.htm).


*[Image: XML Value Extractor Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Extraction priority** | Select either message Header or Message data as the priority for property extraction. When the header has priority and the header contains a property to be extracted, the message data is not searched for the property found in the header. | |
| **Prefix for result properties** | Optionally, specify a prefix that is applied to extracted properties in the results output. | |
| **List separator** | Specify a delimiter for the extracted properties in the results output. A comma is the default separator. | |
| **Scenario ID** | Specify a scenario, such as Claim, which provides namespace, XPath, and complex property settings for common format documents. | |
| Auto detect namespaces | Automatically detects and provides the list of prefixed namespaces. | |
| Namespaces | The list of XML namespaces. To add prefixed namespaces, click **New**, and define the **Name** (prefix) and the **Value** for the namespace. When creating namespaces with the scope to query XML documents using XPath expressions, keep in mind that XPath doesn't automatically recognize default namespaces. Therefore, users must define a prefix for the default namespace and use it in XPath expressions. This ensures that your XPath expressions correctly match elements and attributes in XML documents that use namespaces. Default namespaces in XML files Default namespaces apply to elements without prefixes. Such elements cannot be directly referenced in XPath expressions unless a prefix is explicitly defined. For example: Given an XML with a default namespace: <root xmlns="http://example.com">  <Order>     <Patient>       <Name>John</Name>    </Patient>  </Order> </root> Adding the following prefixed namespace: Name | Value |
| pnt | http://example.com | |



allows the user to query any element using XPath expressions that include the `pnt` prefix. For example:


//pnt:Order/pnt:Patient/pnt:Name/text()





 Prefixed namespaces in XML files


Prefixed namespaces are used with prefixed elements (e.g., ns:element), and must be referenced using the assigned prefix in XPath expressions.


For example:


Given an XML with prefixed namespaces:


<ns:order xmlns:ns="http://patients.com">


 <ns:patientlist id="1">


  <ns:firstName>John</ns:firstName>


 </ns:patientlist>


</ns:order>


Adding the following prefixed namespace:


| Name | Value |
| --- | --- |
| ns | http://patients.com |



allows the user to query any element using XPath expressions that include the `ns` prefix. For example:


//ns:order/ns:patientlist/ns:firstName/text()


**Note** To automatically detect prefixed namespaces, check the **Auto detect namespaces** check-box .













###### XPath tab


By using the XPath tab, you can define XPath expressions and select multiple match resolution options.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Name** | Type a name for an XPath expression. |
| **Multiple match resolution** | Select the way for values to be handled in case there are more than one node value, satisfying the XPath expression in the document.The options for multiple matching are: **first** - returns only the first value found **last** - returns only the last value found **all** - returns all values that match the XPath **list** - concatenates all matched values into one string, by using the separator character between values |
| **Value** | Specify the XPath expression used for accessing XML nodes. To extract a node value, use the XPath text() function, for example /elementName/text(). To get a value of an XML element attribute, append @attributeName expression, for example /elementName/@attributeName. |



###### Complex Properties tab


Complex properties are used to aggregate multiple values into one value, delimited by your custom text when generating an output.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Name** | Type a name of the complex property. |
| **Value** | Specify a value of the complex property. Use the {Prp[Name]} macro, where Name is the Name of required XPath expression. |

---

<a id="xml-transformer"></a>

##### XML Transformer

> 📄 Source: [XML_Transformer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Transformer.htm)

###### XML Transformer


By using XML Transformer you can operate the following transformation engines:

- XSLT is an Extensible Stylesheet Language Transformer. For more information, see [Work with XSLT](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Work_with_XSLT.htm).
- STX is a Streaming Transformations for XML. For more information, see [Work with STX](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Work_with_STX.htm).


These transformation engines transform results generated by XEngine according to the template provided.


*[Image: XML Transformer Settinga tab]*


Note The Transaction Management tab is not displayed for this component unless you have Transaction Management installed.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


To configure XML Transformer:

1. In the Route Viewer, click the XML Transformer component.
2. On the Route component properties tab, click Settings tab.
3. Set the following properties:

- Transformation template: Provide a path to the template to be used in transformation. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space.
- Encoding: Specify the file encoding.To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space.
- Transformer: Select what transformation engine you want to use. The following engines are available: XSLT:Saxon, XSLT:Xalan, STX:Joost.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Transformation template | Provide a path to the template to be used in transformation. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| **Encoding** | Specify the file encoding. Options can include: Auto DetectUTF-16UTF-8ASCIIEBCDIC-CP-US |
| **Transformer** | Choose what transformation engine you want to use. The following engines are available: XSLT:Saxon, XSLT:Xalan (deprecated), STX:Joost. Note The Apache Xalan XSLT library support has been deprecated to address the security vulnerability issue CVE-2022-34169 and will be fully removed on 8/25/2023. Edifecs recommends that you use the Saxon XSLT library instead. You can still use the Xalan XSLT library by downloading and putting it along with the serializer library to the folder ${XESRoot}\features\route-engine\components\xmltransformer\lib at your own risk. |
| **Include exchange properties** | Select to include the message exchange properties file to the outgoing data. |
| **Include message properties** | Select to include message properties with the file or message. |
| Include prefix | Include the prefix separating message exchange and message properties. |
| Parameters | On this pane, you can add XEServer message properties to the component's context to allow an XSLT engine to extract XEServer message property values. For example, to add the "Test" message property to the component context, add a new parameter named "Test" and set the parameter value to "{Prp[Test]}". This adds the property to the context and allows an XSLT engine to get the "Test" property value. Additionally, your .xslt file should contain the node:...<xsl:param name="Test"/>...The file also should contain a node that performs a transforming operation with the message property value.For example, the following template node makes an XSLT engine replace the value of the original <SomeName> node with the value extracted from the "Test" message property:...<Property Name="SomeName"><xsl:value-of select="$Test"/></Property>...  |

---

<a id="configure-an-xpath-property"></a>

##### Configure an XPath Property

> 📄 Source: [Configure_an_XPath_Property.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Configure_an_XPath_Property.htm)

###### Configure an XPath Property


When configuring an XML component settings, you can define specific XPath properties. This property is used to define the path to an element that you want to extract.


To add or edit a property:

- On the XML component Settings tab, click the XPath tab section, and then click New or Edit.


*[Image: New XPath Property dialog box ]*

- In the New XPath Property dialog box, enter the following information:
- **Name**. Name for an XPath property.
- **Value**. The corresponding value.
- **Multiple match resolution**. The options for multiple matching are:
- **first** - returns only the first property value found
- **last** - returns only the last property value found
- **all** - returns all values that match the XPath
- **list** - concatenates all matched values into one string, by using the separator character between values
- Click OK.


The property is displayed in the Parameters section.

---

<a id="xml-batcher"></a>

##### XML Batcher

> 📄 Source: [Book_XML_Batcher.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_XML_Batcher.htm)

###### XML Batcher


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="xml-batcher-component"></a>

##### XML Batcher Component

> 📄 Source: [XML_Batcher.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Batcher.htm)

###### XML Batcher


XML Batcher is a routing component used to accept XML documents with the same set of nodes to be compared and merged and batch them into one or more documents based on the key elements retrieved from accepted documents. Incoming data should be a multimessage exchange, where each message is an XML document to be batched. XML batching can be performed at multiple hierarchical levels. Input information must be represented in ECF.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: XML Batcher Settings tab]*


Note The Transaction Management tab is not displayed for this component unless you have Transaction Management installed.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Initial Parameters tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Name** | The name of the property of initial parameters. |
| **Value** | The value of the property of initial parameters. |



###### Configuration Parameters tab


Use this tab to select a preconfigured batcher scenario. To do this, double-click Batcher scenario in the Name field. In the Edit Property dialog box, enter a value in the Value field. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. Click OK. The Batcher scenario is configured.


The following names and values are available on the Configuration Parameters tab:


| Name | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Batcher scenario | The name of the custom XML batching scenario. |
| Namespace aware | Allows you to set the namespace mode to distinguish elements and attributes that have the same name but belong to different namespaces. |
| Output encoding | Encodes an output data file. |

---

<a id="xml-batcher-sample-usecase"></a>

##### XML Batcher Sample Usecase

> 📄 Source: [XML_Batcher_Sample_Use_Case.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Batcher_Sample_Use_Case.htm)

###### XML Batcher Sample Use Case


The following sample demonstrates how to perform batching of .xml files by using the XML Batcher component.


Rules that control the batching process are defined in configuration files with the .template.xml extension. You need to create a new configuration file sample_scenario.template.xml and insert the contents of sample configuration file there.


The configuration files must be located under the ${XESRoot}\features\route-engine\components\xmlbatcher\resources directory and have the following structure:


Sample Configuration File


<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>


<!DOCTYPE preferences


SYSTEM "http://java.sun.com/dtd/preferences.dtd">


<preferences EXTERNAL_XML_VERSION="1.0">


<root type="system">


<map />


<node name="xeserver">


<map />


<node name="templates">


<map />


<node name="extensions">


<map />


<node name="batcher">


<map />


<node name="batch-scenarios">


<map />


<node name="batch-ClaimAck">


<map />


<node name="ClaimBatch">


<map>


<!-- Defining the "ClaimAcknowledgementBatch" node. The lookup is performed relatively to this node. -->


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="ClaimAcknowledgementBatch" />


<!-- Getting the "value" attribute of the "ClaimBatchID" node. The nodes with identical "value" attributes are considered equal and merged during the batching. -->


<entry key="lookup-xpath-ClaimID" value="ClaimBatchID/@value" />


</map>


<node name="ClaimGroup">


<map>


<entry key="name" value="ClaimGroup" />


<!-- Defining the "ClaimGroup" node to be compared. The lookup is performed relatively to this node. -->


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="ClaimGroup" />


<!-- Getting the "value" attribute of the "ClaimBatchID" node. If identical, the nodes are treated as equal and merged. -->


<entry key="lookup-xpath" value="ClaimGroupID/@value" />


</map>


<node name="Claim">


<map>


<!-- Defining the "Claim" node to be compared. Since no attributes are set for comparison, every incoming node is treated as unique and is not merged. -->


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="Claim" />


</map>


</node>


</node>


</node>


</node>


</node>


</node>


</node>


</node>


</node>


</root>


</preferences>


To batch sample .xml documents:

1. Open Edifecs Application Manager and connect to XEServer.
2. Create a new XEServer profile, expand it, and click Configuration. For more information, see [Create a New Profile](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Profile.htm).
3. Create a new route. For more information, see [Create a New Route.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Route.htm)
4. Add the following components to your route: - FS Inbound - Aggregation - XML Batcher - FS Outbound
5. Connect the components using the Synchronous connection type in the sequence they are listed in step 4.
6. In the Route Editor, select the Aggregation component and click the Settings tab.
7. On the Correlation tab of the Settings tab, in Expression, set the value to 1 to correlate your test data.
8. On the Completion tab, select Number of messages received and set the value to 3. This makes the Aggregation component send data to XML Batcher when 3 messages are received.
9. On the Aggregation tab, select the Create multimessage exchange strategy. This allows sending a multimessage exchange to the XML Batcher component.
10. On the Route Editor, select the XML Batcher and click the Settings tab.
11. On the Configuration parameters tab of the Settings tab, select the Batcher scenario item, and click Edit.
12. From the Value drop-down list, select batch-ClaimAck.


Note Ensure that the sample_scenario.template.xml has the correct structure and is located under the ${XESRoot}\features\route-engine\components\xmlbatcher\resources directory. Otherwise, the Value drop-down list does not contain any options.

1. Save your profile and start it from the Admin Console.
2. Go to ${XESRoot}/profiles/%YourProfileName%/workspace/test-data/inbound and drop 3 files expected for strategy completion with different names but identical content to the directory. The following is a sample .xml input document:Sample Input Document<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><ClaimAcknowledgementBatch><ClaimBatchID value="GPG12345"/><ClaimGroup><ClaimGroupID value="12345"/><ProviderInfo><AddressInfo></AddressInfo></ProviderInfo><Claim></Claim></ClaimGroup></ClaimAcknowledgementBatch>
3. Go to the ${XESRoot}/profiles/%YourProfileName%/workspace/test-data/inbound directory for results.


###### Expected results


The result file should have the following content:


Sample Result File


<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><ClaimAcknowledgementBatch>


<ClaimBatchID value="GPG12345"/>


<ClaimGroup>


<ClaimGroupID value="12345"/>


<ProviderInfo>


<AddressInfo>


<node1></node1>


</AddressInfo>


</ProviderInfo>


<Claim>


<node1>Test</node1>


</Claim>


<Claim>


<node1>Test</node1>


</Claim>


<Claim>


<node1>Test</node1>


</Claim>


</ClaimGroup>


</ClaimAcknowledgementBatch>


The result .xml file has the <ClaimAcknowledgementBatch> node merged because original files have identical <ClaimBatchID value="GPG12345"/> nodes. The <ClaimGroup> nodes are also merged due to identical <ClaimGroupID value="12345"/>. However, the nodes <Claim>Test</Claim> were not merged, but duplicated.

---

<a id="xml-batcher-predefined-stratagies"></a>

##### XML Batcher Predefined Stratagies

> 📄 Source: [XML_Batcher_Predefined_Stratagies.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Batcher_Predefined_Stratagies.htm)

###### XML Batcher Predefined Strategies


Some XML Batcher strategies are distributed with Claim Management solution and are stored in the {ECRootPath} \EUO\Common\components\batcher\config\batcher.config.xml.


The strategies described in this topic are those, distributed with Claim Management. For information about XML Batching use case, see [XML Batcher Sample Use Case.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Batcher_Sample_Use_Case.htm)


There are several levels that define strategy and its rules in the batcher.config.xml file. Strategies are defined in the <node name="batch-scenarios"> section. There are three predefined strategies for XML batching.

- batch-ClaimAck
- batch-ClaimPaymentInfo
- batch-ClaimPaymentGroup

 Batching ClaimAck


According to this strategy batching is performed based on:

- ClaimBatchID
- ClaimGroupID


Data used in batching may look like following:


<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>


<ecahcf:ClaimAcknowledgementBatch>


<ecahcf:Packaging>


...


</ecahcf:Packaging>


<ecahcf:ClaimBatchID value="XXXXX"/>


<ecahcf:ClaimSubmitterInfo>


...


</ecahcf:ClaimSubmitterInfo>


<ecahcf:ClaimReceiverInfo>


...


</ecahcf:ClaimReceiverInfo>


<ecahcf:ClaimGroup>


<hcfd:ClaimGroupID value="XXXXX"/>


<hcfd:ProviderInfo>


...


</hcfd:ProviderInfo>


<hcfd:ProviderType value="BillingProvider"/>


<hcfd:Claim>


<hcfd:SubmittedClaimID value="1111111"/>


...


</hcfd:Claim>


</ecahcf:ClaimGroup>


</ecahcf:ClaimAcknowledgementBatch>


Then following steps are performed:

1. Search for <ClaimAcknowledgementBatch> is performed.
2. Search for <ClaimBatchID> is performed. The values of this node are compared for all input data.
3. Search for <ClaimGroup>with the same <ClaimBatchID> is performed.
4. Search for <ClaimGroupID> is performed. The values of this node are compared.
5. All <Claims> inside the <ClaimGroup> are combined for all inputs with the same <ClaimGroupID>.
6. If <ClaimGroupID>s are different, output file contains several Claim Groups (one for every unique ClaimGroupID) with all correspondent claims inside


All other data is the data from the first input.





 Batching ClaimPaymentInfo


According to this strategy batching is performed based on:

- ClaimRemittanceID
- ClaimPaymentGroupID


Data used in batching may look like the following:


<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>


<ecrhcf:ClaimRemittance >


<ecrhcf:Packaging>


...


</ecrhcf:Packaging>


<ecrhcf:ClaimRemittanceID value="2010122010239"/>


<ecrhcf:PayerInfo>


...


</ecrhcf:PayerInfo>


<ecrhcf:PayeeInfo>


...


</ecrhcf:PayeeInfo>


<ecrhcf:HandlingCode value="I"/>


<ecrhcf:TotalPaymentAmount value="4000"/>


<ecrhcf:CreditDebitFlag value="C"/>


<ecrhcf:PaymentMethod value="BOP"/>


<ecrhcf:PayerAccountInfo>


...


</ecrhcf:PayerAccountInfo>


<ecrhcf:PayeeAccountInfo>


...


</ecrhcf:PayeeAccountInfo>


<ecrhcf:PaymentIssueDate value="2010-11-07"/>


<ecrhcf:CheckorEFTNumber value="1574893748"/>


 


<ecrhcf:ProductionDate value="2010-11-06"/>


<ecrhcf:ClaimGroup>


<hcfd:ClaimPaymentGroupID value="2010122010239000001"/>


<hcfd:ClaimPaymentInfo>


...


</hcfd:ClaimPaymentInfo>


</ecrhcf:ClaimGroup>


</ecrhcf:ClaimRemittance>


The following steps are performed:

1. Search for <ClaimRemittance> is performed.
2. Search for <ClaimRemittance ID> is performed. The values of this node are compared for all input data.
3. Search for <ClaimGroup> for data with the same <ClaimRemittance ID> is performed.
4. Search for <ClaimPaymentGroupID> is performed. The values of this node are compared.
5. All <PaymentInfo> are combined inside the <ClaimGroup> for all inputs with the same <ClaimPaymentGroupID>.
6. If <ClaimPaymentGroupID>s are different, the output file contains several Claim Groups (one for every unique ClaimPaymentGroupID) with all corresponding payment info inside.


All other data is the data from the first input.





 Batching ClaimPaymentGroup


According to this strategy batching is performed based on ClaimRemittanceID.


Data used in batching may look like the following:


<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>


<ecrhcf:ClaimRemittance >


<ecrhcf:Packaging>


...


</ecrhcf:Packaging>


<ecrhcf:ClaimRemittanceID value="2010122010239"/>


<ecrhcf:PayerInfo>


...


</ecrhcf:PayerInfo>


<ecrhcf:PayeeInfo>


...


</ecrhcf:PayeeInfo>


<ecrhcf:HandlingCode value="I"/>


<ecrhcf:TotalPaymentAmount value="4000"/>


<ecrhcf:CreditDebitFlag value="C"/>


<ecrhcf:PaymentMethod value="BOP"/>


<ecrhcf:PayerAccountInfo>


...


</ecrhcf:PayerAccountInfo>


<ecrhcf:PayeeAccountInfo>


...


</ecrhcf:PayeeAccountInfo>


<ecrhcf:PaymentIssueDate value="2010-11-07"/>


<ecrhcf:CheckorEFTNumber value="1574893748"/>


<ecrhcf:ProductionDate value="2010-11-06"/>


<ecrhcf:ClaimGroup>


<hcfd:ClaimPaymentGroupID value="2010122010239000001"/>


<hcfd:ClaimPaymentInfo>


...


</hcfd:ClaimPaymentInfo>


</ecrhcf:ClaimGroup>


</ecrhcf:ClaimRemittance>


The following steps are performed:

1. Search for <ClaimRemittance> is performed.
2. Search for <ClaimRemittance ID> is performed. The values of this node are compared for all input data.
3. Search for <ClaimGroup> for data with the same <ClaimRemittance ID> is performed.
4. All <ClaimGroup> inside the <ClaimRemittance> for all inputs with the same <ClaimRemittanceID> are combined.
5. If <ClaimRemittanceID>s are different, several outputs are produced.






You can also implement your own batching strategy. For more information about how to do it, see [Create a Custom Batcher Strategy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_Custom_Batcher_Strategy.htm).

---

<a id="create-a-custom-batcher-strategy"></a>

##### Create a Custom Batcher Strategy

> 📄 Source: [Create_a_Custom_Batcher_Strategy.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_Custom_Batcher_Strategy.htm)

###### Create a Custom Batcher Strategy


There some predefined XML batcher strategies that go together with the Claim Management application. However, you can implement your custom strategy that meets your business needs. The configuration file is located at: {ECRootPath}\EUO\Common\components\batcher\config\batcher.config.xml.


To create a custom strategy:

1. Define a name of a new strategy and place correspondent tag inside the <node name="batch-scenarios"> section.


<node name="MyStrategy”>


<map />


</node>

1. Define sections and values to compare (lookups for merging).


<node name=" MyStrategy ">


<map>


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="MySection" />


<entry key="lookup-xpath-ClaimID" value="SomeMyID/@value" />


</map>


</node>

1. Define section that should be placed in the output merged file (from input files). Under the section where values for merging were described.


<node name=" MyStrategy ">


<map>


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="MySection" />


<entry key="lookup-xpath-ID" value="SomeMyID/@value" />


</map>


<node name="MyGroup">


<map>


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="SomeNestedSection" />


</map>


</node>


</node>


There are several levels that define strategy and its rules in this file. Strategy must be defined inside the <node name="batch-scenarios"> section. See a following example of one of the strategies:


Example


<node name="batch-ClaimPaymentInfo">


<map />


<node name="ClaimRemittance">


<map>


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="ClaimRemittance" />


<entry key="lookup-xpath-ClaimID" value="ClaimRemittanceID/@value" />


</map>


<node name="ClaimGroup">


<map>


<entry key="name" value="ClaimGroup" />


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="ClaimGroup" />


<entry key="lookup-xpath-ClaimID" value="ClaimGroupID/@value" />


</map>


<node name="ClaimPaymentInfo">


<map>


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="ClaimPaymentInfo" />


</map>


</node>


</node>


</node>


</node>


First node defines the name of the strategy. In this example it is batch-ClaimPaymentInfo. All the nodes that follow define sections to be merged. There are three nested sections. This means that in the input XML (which must be merged) application looks for the same nesting. Inside the <node> section lookup parameters must be described. There is such a section for “ClaimRemittance” node in the example displayed earlier.


There are two keys that are used. First of all the ClaimRemittance section inside the input XML is checked. After this the <ClaimRemittanceID> is checked, and then its value is compared against the same value inside other input XML (several XML are merged). If values inside input XML are the same, the application places all nested sections inside the output XML. For the following example all ClaimGroup sections from all input XML are placed inside the ClaimRemittance section of the output merged file:


Example


<node name="ClaimRemittance">


<map>


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="ClaimRemittance" />


<entry key="lookup-xpath-ClaimID" value="ClaimRemittanceID/@value" />


</map>


<node name="ClaimGroup">


<map>


<entry key="node-location-xpath" value="ClaimGroup" />


</map>


</node>


</node>


For more information about sample XML Batching, see [XML Batcher Sample Use Case](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Batcher_Sample_Use_Case.htm).

---

<a id="xml-to-json-component"></a>

##### XML to JSON Component

> 📄 Source: [XML_To_JSON_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_To_JSON_Component.htm)

###### XML To JSON Component


With the XML to JSON component, you can convert XML data to the JSON data format. JSON is a language-independent, human-readable data format that allows data transmission in the form of key-value pairs.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Virtual root | Enter a case-sensitive name for the node to be deleted from the output file. Only root nodes can be removed, for example <rootNode></rootNode>:<rootNode><node1>...</node1><node2/></rootNode> |
| Auto primitive | Select this option to identify number, Boolean, and null values as primitives. If this option is disabled, the primitives are converted to string values. |
| Skip namespaces | Select this option to exclude namespaces from the output JSON file. |
| Namespaces properties file | Specify a path to the file that contains namespace properties. With this option, you can replace namespaces prefixes with those, defined in your property file. The following namespaces.properties file removes json namespace prefixes that have the http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope key and replaces them with the prefix newPrefix. Namespaces.properties file contents: newPrefix=http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope Example Original XML: <?xml version="1.0"?> <soap1:Header xmlns:soap1="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope">TestValue</soap1:Header>   Converted JSON: {      "soap1:Header" : {           "@xmlns:soap1" : "http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope",           "$" : "TestValue"       } } If the Skip namespaces option is enabled, then the converted JSON is as follows: {      "Header" : "TestValue" } |
| Namespace prefix separator | Specify a single character separator to separate a namespace and its prefix. By default, the value is set to ':'. |



###### Advanced tab


Use this tab to configure the behavior of the component if your input XML files have sibling nodes with the same name.



To convert XML sibling nodes that have the same name to a JSON array, use the following options:

- All sibling elements with the same name: The component converts all sibling nodes to a JSON array. This option is an efficient solution when you receive XML files of moderate size in the input (<10 MB). However, this option has several drawbacks: - The component caches the entire XML document to RAM that may be resource- and time-consuming when you convert large XML files. If you notice that your route fails with java.lang.OutOfMemoryError while converting voluminous XML files, consider changing to a different option for handling sibling XML nodes. - This option does not generate empty arrays or arrays with a single element.
- Evaluated by XPath list defined in the file: This option converts only those sibling nodes that match the XPath expressions in the configuration file. For this option, enter a path to a file that contains a list of XPath expressions. The component converts matching XPath nodes to a JSON array. Note The configuration file should contain only XPath expressions, each defined on a new line. You cannot enter comments or any other text in this file.
- Evaluated by list of XPaths: This option converts only those sibling nodes that match XPath expressions defined in the XPath list pane.


All sibling XML nodes with the same name are converted to a JSON array. For example:


| Input XML file | Resulting JSON file |
| --- | --- |
| <rootNode>    <node1>value1</node1>    <node1>value2</node1> </rootNode>   | {    rootNode : {       "node1" : ["value1", "value2"]    } } |

---

<a id="zipunzip-components"></a>

#### Zip/Unzip Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_Zip_Unzip_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Zip_Unzip_Components.htm)

##### Zip/Unzip Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="zip-component"></a>

##### Zip Component

> 📄 Source: [Zip_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Zip_Components.htm)

###### Zip Component


This component allows you to compress the content of XEServer messages.


*## Component Settings Apart from generic settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings. ### Settings tab Field Description Multimessage compression mode The following options specify the behavior when a multimessage exchange comes to the component's input. Compress all messages to one. Creates a single archive file with all the incoming messages compressed in it. Compress each message separately. Creates a separate archive file for each message withing the multimessage exchange. Multimessage compression mode Select the compression mode. ZIP: Uses the DEFLATE compression algorithm. Produces .zip* archive on the output. TAR.GZ: Uses the GZIP compression algorithm. Produces .tar.gz package on the output. Compression level Specify the compression level in range 1-9. The maximum compression level is 9. File name template The template used for the result archive file name generation. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space.Example
Default template is {EID}-{M.IDX}.{M.EXT} - where

{EID} - exchange ID

{M.IDX} - message index within the exchange (1,2,...)

{M.EXT} - message extension





 Add exchange properties




Click to add message exchange properties to properties file in zip archive.






 Add message properties




Click to add message properties to properties file in the zip archive.

---

<a id="unzip-component"></a>

##### Unzip Component

> 📄 Source: [Unzip_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Unzip_Components.htm)

###### Unzip Component


This component allows to unzip the content of XEServer messages.


*[Image: Unzip Settings tab]*


To find the Unzip Component macros used for file name template, see [Unzip Component Macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


The following fields are available for the Unzip data processor component:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Unzip mode** | The following options specify the behavior when a [multimessage exchange](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Terms.htm#MultimessageExchange) comes to the component's input**Create a new exchange for each message**. Creates a separate XEServer message exchange for each message in the zip archive. **Create one multimessage exchange for zip**. Creates a single multimessage exchange for the entire zip archive. |
| **Populate unzip properties on each message** | Select to read message properties from the archive (if any). |

---

<a id="mock-velocity"></a>

#### Mock Velocity

> 📄 Source: [Mock_Velocity.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Mock_Velocity.htm)

##### Mock Velocity


The Mock Velocity component is a design-time tool that emulates functionality that has not yet been fully determined or developed. Mock components can also be used to aid integration testing. Missing functionality is emulated primarily by transforming messages by using Velocity XML transformations.


*[Image: Mock Velocity Initial parameters]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Initial Parameters tab


The Mock Velocity component can be configured by using the following parameters:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **message.encoding** | Provide the encoding of the message that the Mock component receives. Options can include: UTF-16 UTF-8 ASCII |
| **output.encoding** | Provide the encoding of the result that the Mock component generates. Options can include: UTF-16 UTF-8 ASCII |
| **processing.mode** | Choose what mode you want to use for processing incoming messages. The following modes are available: SingleMessage MultiMessage |
| **template.encoding** | Provide the encoding of the Velocity template that the Mock component uses. Options can include: UTF-16 UTF-8 ASCII |
| **template.path** | Provide the path to the Velocity template that the Mock component uses. |



###### Context tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Name** | The name of the property of context. |
| **Value** | The value of the property of context. |

---

<a id="mongodb-components"></a>

#### MongoDB Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_MongoDB_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_MongoDB_Components.htm)

##### MongoDB Components
[Redirect to: {MongoDB_Command.htm}](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Command.htm)

---

<a id="mongodb-command"></a>

##### MongoDB Command

> 📄 Source: [MongoDB_Command.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Command.htm)

###### MongoDB Command


The MongoDB Command component allows you to execute [commands](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/) on MongoDB databases. The component interacts with MongoDB databases through the [MongoDB Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Service.htm) that provides connectivity with a MongoDB server.


On the input, the component expects a valid [BSON](https://www.mongodb.com/basics/bson)-formatted command that arrives either within an XEServer message or is defined as a static template. The component runs the command for every message received. On the output, the component returns a [MongoDB document](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/core/document/) depending on the performed operation.


A sample MongoDB command below:



```
{
    find: 'test_users', 
    projection: {
        _id: 0, user: 1
    }, 
    sort: {
        user: 1
    }, 
    limit: 10
}
```






For details about MongoDB syntax, see [Commands Syntax Reference](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/command/#command-syntax). For more information on live examples of using MongoDB commands with XEServer, see [Command Examples](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Command_Examples.htm).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.



###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service Name | Select the [MongoDB Connection Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Service.htm) to be used by the component. |
| Source | Select the commands source. The component can receive MongoDB commands from two sources: From a message body. In this case, each incoming XEServer message must carry a valid command string. From the component settings. In this case, you provide a static command in Command Template and the component runs this command for each message passing through the component. Regardless of the selected source, your commands can contain macros to reuse data from message processing context. |



###### Transaction tab


On this tab, you can set the component to run commands in transactions. If the component expects a [multi-message exchange](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Terms.htm#MultimessageExchange) on the input, use Local [transaction type](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Transaction_Components.htm) to execute commands for each message as a single atomic transaction. For other cases, use Shared transactions as described in [MongoDB Transactions](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Transaction_Components.htm).


###### Command Macros


You can use macros in your commands as shown below.

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)

```
{
    find: '?{Prp[command_find]}', 
    ...
}
```






In the sample command above, the find expression is taken from the `command_find` XEServer message property. Using command parameters, you can have a command template where separate fields are populated from the message properties. For more example of using macros, see [Command Examples](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Command_Examples.htm).


The component supports the following types of command macros:

- `?{MacroName[propertyKey]}`
- `{MacroName[propertyKey]}`

---

<a id="mongodb-transactions"></a>

##### MongoDB Transactions

> 📄 Source: [MongoDB_Transaction_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Transaction_Components.htm)

###### MongoDB Transactions


You can wrap commands in [transactions](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/core/transactions/) using the [MongoDB Transaction Open](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Transaction_Open.htm) and [MongoDB Transaction Close](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Transaction_Close.htm) components.


Note MongoDB transactions are [available](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/transactions/) in a cluster mode only.


###### Transaction types: Shared vs Local


There are two types of transactions described below.


###### Shared transactions


This is the recommended choice for most cases when you decide to work with transactions. In this mode, `transactionStart` and `transactionCommit` events occur in separate Transaction Open/Transaction Close route components as shown in the image:



By default, when the MongoDB Transaction Open component creates a new transaction, it sends an ID of the created transaction further down the route. Using this transaction ID, the component MongoDB Transaction Close can close the transaction later on the route.


###### Local transactions


In this mode, `transactionStart` and `transactionCommit` events occur within the same [MongoDB Command](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Command.htm) component. This transaction type is useful if you expect a multi-message exchange on the MongoDB Command input and you want to execute several commands for each message as a single atomic transaction.

---

<a id="mongodb-transaction-open"></a>

##### MongoDB Transaction Open

> 📄 Source: [MongoDB_Transaction_Open.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Transaction_Open.htm)

###### MongoDB Transaction Open


With the MongoDB Transaction Open component, you can open a shared MongoDB transaction.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service Name | Select the [MongoDB Connection Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Service.htm) to be used by the component. |
| Transaction ID | Specify an ID for the transaction. The ID must be unique in the context of one route. Thus, consider using a combination of macros such as `{R_Name}` (route name), `{C_Name}` (component name) to ensure uniqueness. The component attaches this ID as a property to every message that leaves the component through the original output. |
| Read Preference | Select the [Read Preference](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/read-preference/#read-preference) mode that describes how MongoDB clients must route read operations to the members of a replica set. |
| Max commit time | Specify the maximum time (in milliseconds) allotted for a MongoDB server to complete the `commitTransaction` operation. If the server fails to close the transaction within the timeout, the component performs a rollback, logs this event, and the data leaves the component through the error output. Setting the value to 0 removes the limit. |
| Consistency level (Mongo DB Concerns) | |
| Read | Select the [Read Concern](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/read-concern/) level that allows you to control the consistency and isolation properties of the data read from replica sets and replica set shards. |
| Write | Select the [Write Concern](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/write-concern/) level that describes the level of acknowledgment requested from MongoDB for write operations. |



###### Outputs tab


By default, when you add the MongoDB Transaction Open component to a route, the component sets the `MongoDBTransactionID` property on every message that leaves the component through the original output. Using this ID, you can close the transaction later on the route using the MongoDB Transaction Close component.

---

<a id="mongodb-transaction-close"></a>

##### MongoDB Transaction Close

> 📄 Source: [MongoDB_Transaction_Close.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Transaction_Close.htm)

###### MongoDB Transaction Close


With the MongoDB Transaction Close component, you can close a shared MongoDB transaction.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.



###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service Name | Select the [MongoDB Connection Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Service.htm) to be used by the component. |
| Transaction ID | Specify an ID of the transaction to close. Tip The `MongoDBTransactionID` property is set on [MongoDB Transaction Open](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Transaction_Open.htm) components and carries a transaction ID. |

---

<a id="command-examples"></a>

##### Command Examples

> 📄 Source: [MongoDB_Command_Examples.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/MongoDB_Command_Examples.htm)

###### Command Examples


This section demonstrates sample MongoDB commands for CRUD operations.


###### Read document(s)


The sample command below gets practitioners (`firstName`, `lastName`, and `npi` fields) whose specialty is "Ophthalmology". The maximum number of results to read from MongoDB is taken from the `find_limit` message property.



```
{
    find: "practitioners",
    filter: { specialty: "Ophthalmology" },
    projection: { firstName: 1, lastName: 1,npi: 1 },
    limit: '?{Prp[find_limit]}'
}
```






In this example, the `find` command returns a MongoDB cursor with matched results set.


###### Insert a single document


The command below can be used as a template for adding new practitioners to a database. Information about each practitioner (`firstName`, `npi`, and so on) is populated from the message properties.



```
{
  insert: 'practitioners', 
  documents: [
    {
      firstName: '?{Prp[practitioner_fName]}',
      lastName: '?{Prp[practitioner_lName]}',
      npi: '?{Prp[practitioner_npi]}',
      specialty: '?{Prp[practitioner_spec]}'
    }
  ]
}
```






###### Insert multiple documents


The following command inserts multiple user entries into the `test_users` collection.



```
{
    insert: 'practitioners',
    documents: [
            {
                firstName: 'John',
                lastName: 'Drew',
                npi: '111112222',
                specialty: 'Ophtalmology'
            },
            {
                firstName: 'Peter',
                firstName: 'Larris',
                npi: '222223333',
                specialty: 'Dermatology'
            }
    ]
}
```






###### Update a document


The following command updates the `testUsr` record in the `test_users` collection.



```
{
    update:'test_users',
    updates:[{
        q: {
                user:'testUsr',
                pwd:'testPwd'
           },
        u: {
                 user:'testUsr',
                 pwd:'newPwd'
           },
        upsert:true
      }
    ]
}
```






###### Delete a document


The following command removes all users from the collection.



```
{
    delete: 'test_users',
    deletes: [
        { q: { }, limit: 1 }
    ]
}
```

---

<a id="json-batcher"></a>

#### JSON Batcher

> 📄 Source: [Book_JSON_Batcher.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_JSON_Batcher.htm)

##### JSON Batcher


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="component"></a>

##### Component

> 📄 Source: [JSON_Batcher_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JSON_Batcher_Component.htm)

###### JSON Batcher


JSON Batcher is a routing component used to accept JSON documents (of the same schema) and batch them into one document based on the array of elements retrieved from accepted documents. An incoming data should be a multimessage exchange, where each message is a JSON document from which the data is selected and batched based on a JSON Batcher scenario.


The JSON Batcher scenario defines the batching rules that consist of the following parts:

- Comparison criteria: the definition of elements on how to decide what should be batched into one resulting node, for example, a JSON path to the elements to be equal.
- Elements (objects) to be batched: where to start an array of such elements.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:


*[Image: XML Batcher Settings tab]*


Note The Transaction Management tab is not displayed for this component unless you have Transaction Management installed.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


Use this tab to select a custom JSON Batcher scenario.


The following is available on the Settings tab:


Batcher scenario: The name of the custom JSON bathcer scenario.


The configuration file should have the following structure:

- Match criteria: one or more JSON paths to the elements that must be equal to match the nodes.
- Batch criteria: a JSON path to the element that must be batched.


For example,

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)
 Example


```
{
    "selector": {
        "batchNode": "ACK_999.Loop_2000_TransactionSetResponseHeader",
        "matchFields": [
            "AK2_TransactionSetResponseHeader.AK201",
            "AK2_TransactionSetResponseHeader.AK202"
        ],
        "selector": {
            "batchNode": "Loop_2100_ErrorIdentificationsection"
        }
    }
}
```






Where:


selector: Describes a JSON path in a structure of input JSON files. This means how to collect a resulting node from incoming documents. The JSON Batcher component checks each entry in a document and batches entries that are matched by a type.


batchNode: a JSON path to the element that must be batched.


matchFields: The fields that are used to search for matches.

---

<a id="example"></a>

##### Example

> 📄 Source: [JSON_Batcher_Example.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JSON_Batcher_Example.htm)

###### JSON Batcher Example


For example, to batch the following two files, the comparison criteria is a JSON path to the elements to be equal:


`999_ACK.Loop_2000_TransactionSetResponseHeader.AK202` and


`999_ACK.Loop_2000_TransactionSetResponseHeader.AK201`


The elements (objects) that should be batched into one array is `Loop_2100_ErrorIdentificationsection`.

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


File 1




```
{ 

            "999_ACK" : { 

                "ISA" : { 

                        "ISA01" : "00", 

                }, 

                "GS" : { 

                        "GS01" : "FA", 

                }, 

                "ST" : { 

                        "ST01" : "999", 

                }, 

                "AK1" : { 

                        "AK101" : "HC", 

                }, 

                "Loop_2000_TransactionSetResponseHeader" : [ { 

                        "AK2_TransactionSetResponseHeader" : { 

                            "AK201" : "837", 

                            "AK202" : "1001", 

                            "AK203" : "005010X222A1" 

                        }, 

                        "Loop_2100_ErrorIdentificationsection" : [ { 
            
                            "IK3_ErrorIdentification" : { 

                                    "IK301" : "BHT", 

                                    "IK302" : "2", 

                                    "IK303" : "", 

                                    "IK304" : "3" 

                            },
```






 

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


File 2




```
{ 

            "999_ACK" : { 

                "ISA" : { 

                        "ISA01" : "00", 

                }, 

                "GS" : { 

                        "GS01" : "FA", 

                }, 

                "ST" : { 

                        "ST01" : "999", 

                }, 

                "AK1" : { 

                        "AK101" : "HC", 

                }, 

                "Loop_2000_TransactionSetResponseHeader" : [ { 

                        "AK2_TransactionSetResponseHeader" : { 

                                "AK201" : "837", 

                                "AK202" : "1001", 

                                "AK203" : "005010X222A1" 

                        }, 

                        "Loop_2100_ErrorIdentificationsection" : [ { 

                                "IK3_ErrorIdentification" : { 

                                        "IK301" : "ABC", 

                                        "IK302" : "5", 
    
                                        "IK303" : "", 

                                        "IK304" : "7" 

                                },
```





 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


Result File




```
{ 

        "999_ACK" : { 

              "ISA" : { 

                    "ISA01" : "00", 

                }, 

                "GS" : { 

                    "GS01" : "FA", 

                }, 

                "ST" : { 

                    "ST01" : "999", 

                }, 

                "AK1" : { 

                    "AK101" : "HC", 

                }, 

                "Loop_2000_TransactionSetResponseHeader" : [ { 

                        "AK2_TransactionSetResponseHeader" : { 

                            "AK201" : "837", 

                            "AK202" : "1001", 

                            "AK203" : "005010X222A1" 

                    }, 

                    "Loop_2100_ErrorIdentificationsection" : [ 

 

{

                            "IK302" : "2", 

                            "IK303" : "",

                            }, 

 {
