<a id="xengine-components"></a>

### XEngine Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_XEngine_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_XEngine_Components.htm)

#### XEngine Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="crosswalk-components"></a>

#### Crosswalk Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_Crosswalk_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Crosswalk_Components.htm)

##### Crosswalk Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="crosswalk-components-overview"></a>

##### Crosswalk Components Overview

> 📄 Source: [Crosswalk_Components_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Components_Overview.htm)

###### Crosswalk Components Overview


To facilitate interoperability between code sets of different standards, XEngine offers a sophisticated data conversion mechanism that works with crosswalks. A crosswalk is a correlation table that consists of source and target columns with equivalent elements that are mapped. You can query a crosswalk with a value available in the source column to get the corresponding value from the target column.


To work with crosswalks, you can use the following components available on the XEngine section of the Palette:

- [Crosswalk Lookup component](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Lookup_Component.htm)
- [Crosswalk Update component](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Update_Component.htm)


To [create and edit](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Create_a_New_Crosswalk.htm) crosswalk tables, you can use SpecBuilder.


The crosswalk files are stored in a dedicated directory at {ECRootPath}\repository. From this directory, XEngine automatically picks up and scans crosswalks. If you store your crosswalks at another location, you must [copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Update_Component.htm#Copy) the crosswalks to the repository using the Crosswalk Update component.


There are two types of crosswalks:

- System: Read-only crosswalks that are stored in a sub-folder of a corresponding standard, for example {ECRootPath}\STANDARDS\HIPAA\Crosswalks. If you install system crosswalks that do not correspond to any of the standards, the installer creates a new destination in the Standards directory, for example {ECRootPath}\STANDARDS\GovernmentEditsCrosswalk\Crosswalks and adds a new record to the StandardsRegistry.xml file.
- User: Crosswalks that you create and modify based on your requirements. You can create user crosswalks using system crosswalks as templates. User crosswalks are stored at {ECRootPath}\repository\User\Crosswalks


Note You cannot copy user crosswalks to the folder with system crosswalks and in the reverse.


If you create crosswalks with the same Name parameter, for the correct crosswalk to be selected at runtime, you must set the Trading Partners and Criteria parameters by using the [Crosswalk Update](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xeserver/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Update_Component.htm) component. If a repository contains crosswalks with the same Name, Trading Partners, and Criteria parameters, these crosswalks extend each other at runtime by default and XEngine applies them one by one depending on the file path order. If you want a specific crosswalk override other crosswalks with the same parameters, you can set the Override merge policy for this crosswalk by using the [Crosswalk Update](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xeserver/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Update_Component.htm) component.


With XEngine 9.2 and later, you do not have to register and unregister crosswalks, as all the crosswalk properties are incorporated in the crosswalk file structure. This makes crosswalk files self-sufficient and easily portable from one environment to another, which drastically simplifies crosswalks' distribution and maintenance. All the registered crosswalks that you created before the 9.2 update are automatically migrated to the new format and moved to {ECRootPath}\repository\User\Crosswalks during XEngine 9.2 installation with their folder structure retained. If you have unregistered crosswalks, you must migrate them using the [Crosswalk Update](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xeserver/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Update_Component.htm) component. If required, you can find the backup copy of the Crosswalk Registry file at {ECRootPath}\Common

---

<a id="crosswalk-lookup"></a>

##### Crosswalk Lookup

> 📄 Source: [Crosswalk_Lookup_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Lookup_Component.htm)

###### Crosswalk Lookup


The Crosswalk Lookup component provides access to the Crosswalk functionality that helps to define a workflow based on configurable crosswalk tables. Crosswalk tables can carry any type of business information, such as names, addresses, ZIP codes, NPIs, and so on. The component searches for property values in source columns of a Crosswalk and returns corresponding matching values from the target columns.


Note Use Edifecs SpecBuilder to conveniently [create and edit](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Create_a_New_Crosswalk.htm) crosswalks.


The Crosswalk Lookup component can be configured on the Route component properties tab.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Crosswalk Name | Enter a name of a crosswalk you want to work with. |
| TP Name | The trading partner name. If there are several crosswalks with the same name, TP name can be used to pick up the right crosswalk. |
| Result Prefix | Enter a string to be prepended to retrieved data. Use this property to avoid mismatch between the retreived properties. Each returned property looks the following way: CrosswalkResult_1_1, CrosswalkResult_1_2, CrosswalkResult_2_1, and so on. The first index indicates a Crosswalk row, the second index indicates a column within that row. |
| Date | Lookup date in yyyyMMdd format. |
| Search Method | The following search methods are available: Best Match: This method returns values from target columns if at least one lookup key matches a value in one of the source columns. Full Match: This method returns values from target columns only if all lookup keys for all available source columns match. For example, if a Crosswalk has three source columns (Name, Address, ZIP) and the three specified lookup keys match all three values, then Crosswalk returns the value of target columns. |
| Multiple Results | Define the strategy in case multiple crosswalks match the search criteria. The following options are available: Report Error: Error is reported in case multiple crosswalks match the search criteria. Take All: All the results that match the search criteria are included in the output. Take First: Only the first result that matches the search criteria is included in the output. |
| Lookup keys | Lookup information, which is used to find matching records in Crosswalk. Crosswalk tables consist of the left side (source) which contains Lookup fields and the right side, which are the results. Lookup values are passed to a crosswalk in the order they are indicated in SpecBuilder (using source column indexes, not the names). Unresolved macro action functionality defines the behavior in case the macro failed to resolve. The following options are available: Continue Search Report Error Skip Search |



###### Criteria tab


Crosswalk criteria are key-value pairs that are used as conditions to access a crosswalk. If a criterion which is entered on this tab matches the criterion of Crosswalk, the access to the Crosswalk is granted.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Skip criteria with unresolved macro | Enables skipping the criteria with unresolved macro. |
| Name | The name of the criterion property. |
| Value | The value of the criterion property. |

---

<a id="crosswalk-update"></a>

##### Crosswalk Update

> 📄 Source: [Crosswalk_Update_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Update_Component.htm)

###### Crosswalk Update


With the Crosswalk Update component, you can:

- Make changes to crosswalk tables (add and remove rows),
- Add properties to the crosswalk files to update their structure to a new format,
- Copy crosswalks from your file system to the crosswalk repository and remove them from there.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


You can configure the following operations with crosswalks:

- Copy - Copies a crosswalk file stored on your local drive to the crosswalk repository with the specified name.
- Delete - Removes a crosswalk from the crosswalk repository.
- Delete Row - Removes rows from a crosswalk.
- Insert - Inserts new rows into a crosswalk.
- Update - Adds properties that are required for working with crosswalks into crosswalk files.


To configure the Copy operation:

1. On the Settings tab of the Crosswalk Update component, in Crosswalk operation, select Copy.
2. In Target crosswalk, specify a name for the crosswalk after you copy it to the repository.
3. In Crosswalk file, navigate to the .crsw file on your file system that you want to copy to the repository.
4. Select Override existing if you want to replace a file with the same name in the repository.


To configure the Delete operation:

1. On the Settings tab of the Crosswalk Update component, in Crosswalk operation, select Delete.
2. In Target crosswalk, specify the name of the crosswalk you want to remove from the repository.
3. Select Keep backup file if you want to retain a backup copy of the removed crosswalk.


To configure the Delete Row operation:

1. On the Settings tab of the Crosswalk Update component, in Crosswalk operation, select Delete Row.
2. In Target crosswalk, specify the name of a crosswalk in the crosswalk repository you want to delete row(s) from.
3. In the Lookup Keys pane, to identify a row to remove, define the Lookup value for every Source column of the crosswalk. If your crosswalk has two source columns, you should define values for these two columns as shown below:


To configure the Insert operation:

1. On the Settings tab of the Crosswalk Update component, in Crosswalk operation, select Insert.
2. In Target crosswalk, enter the name of a crosswalk in the crosswalk repository you want to insert row(s) into.
3. Select the format of data expected by the component to be inserted into a crosswalk. The available data formats are:


To configure the Update operation:

1. On the Settings tab of the Crosswalk Update component, in Crosswalk operation, select Update.
2. In Target crosswalk, navigate to the crosswalk stored on your file system or in the repository you want to add properties to.
3. In Crosswalk name, specify the name property to be inserted into the crosswalk file.
4. In TP names, specify the trading partners' names to be inserted into the crosswalk file.
5. In Cache type, select the way XEngine uploads this crosswalk to the memory:
6. In Merge policy, select the way a crosswalk is handled if the repository contains crosswalk(s) with the same name:
7. In Criteria, specify the name-value pairs you want to insert to the crosswalk file.

---

<a id="external-code-list-components"></a>

#### External Code List Components

> 📄 Source: [Book_ECL_Components.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_ECL_Components.htm)

##### External Code List Components


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="external-code-list-lookup"></a>

##### External Code List Lookup

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Lookup_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ECL_Lookup_Component.htm)

###### External Code List Lookup


With this component, you can search for codes in external code list repositories during a route's run time. The component verifies whether the specified code is available in the code list and returns search results in the form of XEServer message exchange properties set on the component's original output.


Note You can use the Edifecs SpecBuilder ECL Browser to view the external code lists.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Connect to ECL Service with port | This option allows the component to interact with the ECL database file through XEServer [ECL Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ECL_Service.htm). Using this option and the XEServer ECL service, you can have multiple ECL components that can safely interact with the same ECL database. To force the component to interact with the ECL database file through the ECL Service, select the Connect to ECL Service with port option and specify the port number defined on the ECL Service Configuration page. |
| Code List Name | Enter the name of the code list that contains the code to be verified. |
| TP Name | Enter the name(s) of the Trading Partner(s) that was used during the registration of the above Code List in the ECL registry. To specify multiple Trading Partners, separate names by commas. If you leave this field empty, the component attempts to find the required code list regardless of its Trading Partner value. |
| Result prefix | Enter a prefix that is prepended to the name of XEServer message exchange property carrying the code verification results. |
| Code | Enter the ID of the code to verify. If the value is not set, the component only checks if the specified code list is available and sets only one XEServer message exchange property "ECLResultsCount=0". |
| Date | Codes are issued for a certain period of time and each code has Start Date and End Date properties. Enter the date when you want to verify the code. |
| Multiple Results | Select an action if multiple codes have been found. The following options are available: Take First: Returns the first found code. For this option, the number of search results can be 0 or 1. Take All: Returns all found codes. Report Error: Logs an error and directs data to the error output. |
| Unresolved macro action | Select an action if a macro cannot be resolved. The following options are available: Continue Search: Continue searching for codes. Report Error: Logs an error and directs data to the error output. Skip Search: Aborts the search operation and returns 0 search results. |



###### Criteria tab


On this tab, you can enter the criteria pairs to identify the required code list.


The criteria are key-value pairs that are used as conditions to access a code list. If all the criteria pairs entered on this tab match the criteria of the code list, then access to the code list is granted.


###### Outputs Tab


The component has the following outputs:


| Output | Description |
| --- | --- |
| original | Original input data is directed to this output. Code search results are set in the form of the following XEServer message exchange properties: ECLResultsCount: Total number of codes retrieved. If the search returns "0", other properties are not set. <ResultPrefix>_<CodeOrdinalNumber>_Name: The name of the code retrieved. <ResultPrefix>_<CodeOrdinalNumber>_Description: The description of the code retrieved. where <CodeOrdinalNumber> is an integer identifying a single code in the returned result set. |
| error | Messages that could not be processed because of system, route, or component errors. |

---

<a id="external-code-list-update"></a>

##### External Code List Update

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Update_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ECL_Update_Component.htm)

###### External Code List Update


With this component, you can perform the following operations on External Code Lists:

- register new user code lists
- insert codes into an existing user code list
- delete codes from an existing user code list
- unregister existing user code lists


The component expects an XEServer message exchange with one or more XEServer messages (multimessage exchange) on input. For Register and Unregister operations, the number of XEServer messages in the incoming message exchange can be ignored since these operations can register/unregister one Code List for every incoming XEServer message exchange. For Insert Code and Delete Code operations, the component attempts to insert/remove n codes in the code list where n is the number of XEServer messages in the incoming XEServer message exchange.


The component does not produce any data, it only passes the original data further on the route through the original or error outputs.


Warning If you need to update an ECL database from different XEServer profiles, use the External Code List Update components in combination with the XEServer [ECL Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ECL_Service.htm) to update the ECL database safely. If two or more profiles simultaneously attempt to update the same ECL database directly (without using XEServer [ECL Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ECL_Service.htm)), this may lead to the corruption of ECL database file.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### External Code List tab


| Field | Description | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Connect to ECL Service with port | This option allows the component to interact with the ECL database file through XEServer [ECL Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/ECL_Service.htm). Using this option and the XEServer ECL service, you can have multiple ECL components that can safely interact with the same ECL database. To force the component to interact with the ECL database file through the ECL Service, select the Connect to ECL Service with port option and specify the port number defined on the ECL Service Configuration page. | |
| Code List Name | Enter the name of the code list to perform an operation with. | |
| TP name | Enter the name(s) of the Trading Partner(s) that was used during the registration of the above Code List in the ECL registry. To specify multiple Trading Partners, separate names by commas. If you leave this field empty, the component attempts to find the required code list regardless of its Trading Partner value. | |
| ECL operation | Select an operation to perform with the code list. The available operations are: Delete code: Deletes codes from the existing code list. Insert code: Inserts new codes into an existing code list. Register: Registers a new user code list in the ECL registry. Unregister: Unregisters an existing code list from the ECL registry. | |
| ECL operation: Delete code Deletes codes from the existing user code list. For each XEServer message in the incoming XEServer message exchange, the component attempts to remove a single code from the code list. When looping through XEServer messages in the message exchange, on every iteration, the component gets access to the body and headers of the current message. Thus, you can dynamically extract data from messages in the message exchange to remove multiple codes per one XEServer message exchange. | | |
| Interrupt processing if the code does not exist | Select this option if you want the component to fail the entire route processing if the specified code does not exist in the specified code list. Otherwise, the corresponding message is logged in xes.system.log and the route processing continues. | |
| Input data | Select the source of input data to identify codes you want to delete. The following options are available: DFF File: Use this option if you expect incoming XEServer message body to contain a DFF file. Records in the DFF file should have the following structure: <CodeID>,<Date> where: <CodeID> : The ID of the code you want to delete. This parameter is mandatory. <Date> : The date in the yyyyMMdd format to delete only those codes that match the specified date. If the entered date falls between the code's Start Date and End Date values, the code is deleted from the code list. Example codeID2Delete, 20180624 Use the following parameters to parse the DFF file: encoding: Select the character set used. Escape: Select the escape character. Quote: Select the character used as a quote. Separator: Select the character that is used to separate DFF fields. Only a single character is allowed. For this option, only one non-printable tab character "\t" (Tab) can be used. XEngine Server Exchange: With this option, you can dynamically extract required data from XEServer message exchange properties. Code | Enter the ID of the code you want to delete. |
| Date | Codes are issued for a certain period of time, and every code has the Start Date and End Date properties. Enter the date in the yyyMMdd format to delete only those codes that match the specified date. If the entered date falls between the code's Start Date and End Date values, the code is deleted from the code list. | |











ECL operation: Insert code








Inserts new codes into the existing user code list. For each XEServer message in the incoming XEServer message exchange, the component attempts to insert a single code into the code list. When looping through XEServer messages in the message exchange, on every iteration, the component gets access to the body and headers of the current message. Thus, you can dynamically extract data from messages in the message exchange to insert multiple codes per one XEServer message exchange.






 Interrupt processing if the code already exists


 Select this option if you want the component to fail the entire route processing if the specified code already exists in the specified code list. Otherwise, the corresponding message is logged in xes.system.log and the route processing continues.




 Input Data




Select the source of input data to identify codes you want to insert. The following options are available:

- DFF File: Use this option to extract code-specific data from the XEServer message body that carries a DFF file. Records in the DFF file should have the following structure: <CodeID>,<CodeName>, <CodeDescription>, <DateRange>, <isExcluded>, <NoteLabel>, <NoteValue> where: - <CodeID> : The ID for the new code. This parameter is mandatory. - <CodeName> : The name for the new code. This parameter is mandatory. - <CodeDescription> : A description for the new code. - <DateRange> : The time frame during which the code should be considered as valid.The <DateRange> DFF field is a complex field that should have the following structure:..., <startDate>-<endDate>-<Type>; ...;, ...where: - <startDate> : The start date of the time frame in the yyyyMMdd format. - <endDate> : The end date of the time frame in the yyyyMMdd format. - <Type> : The Boolean flag that defines whether the code range is Effective (true) or Not used (false). If the code range is Effective, this means that the code should be considered as valid within the time frame. If the code range is Not Used, this means that the code should be considered as invalid within the time frame. The following are examples of valid DFF records with different <DateRange> fields:Example:// Both start date and end dates are set.foo,bar,description,20181201-20181210-true,false,noteLabel,noteValue// Srart date set only:foo,bar,description,20171201--true,false,noteLabel,noteValue// Neither Start date nor End date are set (the code is always valid):foo,bar,description,--true,false,noteLabel,noteValue// Two Date Ranges separated with a semicolon are set. In this example, the new code is invalid from 01/12/2017 to 10/12/2017 and is valid from 01/12/2018 to 10/12/2018foo,bar,description,20171201-20171210-false;20181201-20181210-true,false,noteLabel,noteValue - <isExcluded> : The Boolean flag that defines whether the new code should be enabled (FALSE) or disabled (TRUE). - <NoteLabel> : The label that is used to identify a single Note property of the new code. Typically, Notes store specific implementation details for a code that are company- or industry-specific. - <NoteValue> : The value of the Note property. Example foo,bar,description,20171201-20171210-false;20181201-20181210-true,false,noteLabel,noteValue Use the following parameters to parse the DFF file: - Date elements separator: Select the character used to separate the <StartDate>, <EndDate>, <Type> fields within the <DateRange> field. - Date range separator: Select the character used to separate multiple <DateRange> fields. - encoding: Select the character set used. - Escape: Select the escape character. - Quote: Select the character used as a quote. - Separator: Select the character that is used to separate DFF fields. Only a single character is allowed. For this option, only one non-printable tab character "\t" (Tab) can be used.
- XEngine Server Exchange: With this option, the data for inserting a new code is retrieved from every XEServer message that is a part of a message exchange: Note You can set only one Date range for a new code using the XEServer Exchange source type.






 Code


 Enter the ID of the code you want to insert. Do not use the ASCII character 0x27 (') in the code ID.




 Name


 Enter the name for the new code.




 Excluded


 Select whether the new code should be used (FALSE). If set to TRUE, the new code will not be used.




 Description


 Enter the description for the new code.




 Date Range


 Select if you want to specify a time frame during which the code should be considered as valid(or invalid) . If you do not specify the date range, the new code will be always valid.




 Type


 Select the type of the date range. Use the Effective date range type to specify a time frame during which the new code should be considered as valid. Use the Not Used date range type to specify a time frame during which the new code should be considered as invalid.




 Start


 Enter the start point of the date range.




 End


 Enter the end point of the date range.






ECL operation: Register


Registers a new user code list in the ECL registry. The component registers a single code list per one received XEServer message exchange.






 Interrupt processing if the code list already exists


 Select this option if you want the component to fail the entire route processing if the specified code is already registered in ECL. Otherwise, the corresponding message is logged in xes.system.log and the route processing continues.




 Description


 Enter a description for the new code list.




 Version


 Enter the code list version. This property is used only for informative purpose and may help you to distinguish code lists in future.




 Standard


 Enter the standard of the code list. Code lists structure may slightly differ depending on the associated standard. For example, code lists created for NCPDP contain Version Range properties (equivalent to Date Range properties of code lists created for HIPAA).




 Cache type




Select a cache type of the code list you want to register. The cache type property defines the way XEngine will load this code list to the memory in the future.

- Full: Once XEngine loads the code list to the memory, it is not reloaded.
- None: The code list is not loaded to the memory. Every call to such code lists returns up-to-date data from the code list database.
- Refreshable: XEngine caches the code list on startup, and if the code list changes, it is reloaded to the memory without stopping XEngine.






 Override System




Select this option if you want your code list to override the system code list during data processing. This means that if a code cannot be found in a user code list that has the Override System property set to TRUE, the search for this code is not performed in the system code list. If Override System is FALSE, the code list extends the system one, that is, if a code cannot be found in the user code list, then the code will be searched in the system code list too.






 Commentary


 Enter the commentary to the code list.






ECL operation: Unregister


Unregisters an existing code list from the ECL registry. The component unregisters a single code list per one received XEServer message exchange.






 Interrupt processing if the code list does not exist


 Select this option if you want the component to fail the entire route processing if the specified code list is not registered in ECL. Otherwise, the corresponding message is logged in xes.system.log and the route processing continues.




 Remove DB file




Select if you want to remove the corresponding H2 database file from the file system.









###### Criteria tab


On this tab, you can enter criteria to identify the required code list.


The criteria are key-value pairs that are used as conditions to access a code list. If all criteria entered on this tab match the criteria of the code list, then access to the code list is granted.

---

<a id="jdata-processing-component"></a>

#### JData Processing Component

> 📄 Source: [JData_Processing_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JData_Processing_Component.htm)

##### JData Processing Component


With the JData Processing component, you can validate incoming JData and convert it to a Native data format.


JData is a JSON-based format developed by Edifecs that can be considered as an alternative to Edifecs XData. For more information, see [JData Processing Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/JData_Processing_Overview.htm) and [JData File Structure](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/JData_File_Structure.htm).


For more information, see also the JData section of XEngine User Guide.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


On this tab, you should enter the name of XEngine Service to be used by the component.


###### Data Source tab


On this tab, you can specify the encoding and parser parameters for incoming data.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Encoding | Select the encoding of the input data. |
| Parameters | Use this pane to enter additional parser parameters. The following parameters are available: Parser.DataNode.AddItem Parser.Delimiter.DecimalSeparator Parser.Delimiter.ElementDelimiter Parser.Delimiter.ReleaseCharacter Parser.Delimiter.RepeatingSeparator Parser.Delimiter.SegmentDelimiter Parser.Delimiter.SubelementDelimiter For more information, see [Parser parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Parser_Parameters.htm). |



###### Selector tab


On this tab, you can enter selector parameters.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use internal selector | Select if you want to use the default XML Selector, specified on the XEngine Service Selectors tab. |
| Selector name | Select the XEngine selector for building data node structure. |
| Parameters | Use this pane to enter additional selector parameters. For more information, see [Use Selector](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Use_the_Selector.htm). |



###### Processing Configuration tab


On this tab, specify the JData processing options.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert | Select to convert JData to native format. |
| Validate | Select to validate incoming JData. |
| Encoding | Select the component's output encoding. The following encodings are supported: ASCII UTF-16 UTF-16BE UTF-16LE UTF-8 UTF-8 Without BOM |
| Parameters | Use this pane to enter additional processing parameters. For more information, see [XEngine Configuration Parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Configuration_Parameters.htm). |



###### Reports tab


To receive reports from XEngine route components you must configure the XEngine route component and add the Task Reporter Inbound component to your route.


To configure the Task Reporter component:

1. Add the Task Reporter Inbound endpoint component to your route.
2. On the Task Reporter's Settings tab, ensure that the corresponding JData Processing component is selected in the Watched components list.


To configure reports settings for an XEngine component:

1. Ensure that the Watched by task reporter option is selected on the Overview tab of the JData Processing component.
2. Specify report types and formats by using the Reports tab in the Settings section:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Advanced | Select to display the advanced view that allows you to add properties to different report types. |
| Audit report | Select the corresponding check boxes to generate Audit and Summary reports in a specified format. You can also create a multi-page report in HTML format. |
| Summary report | |



###### External Document Handlers tab


External document handlers allow you to extend and modify the business rules in XEngine processes. You can provide your custom Document Handler Factory service to be used for a particular XEngine process.


Note External document handler service is not among those services created by default.


For more information, see [Use Custom Document Handler](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Custom_Document_handler.htm).


###### Outputs Tab


Filtering is available for this component. Use expression language to customize the output results to accommodate your business requirements. For more information, see [Expression language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm).


| Output Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| native | Native data are directed to this output. |
| reports | Reports specified on the Reports tab are directed to this output. |
| original | Original JData is directed to this output. |
| error | Messages that could not be processed due to system, route, or component errors. |

---

<a id="native-processing"></a>

#### Native Processing

> 📄 Source: [Book_Native_Processing.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Native_Processing.htm)

##### Native Processing


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="native-processing-component"></a>

##### Native Processing Component

> 📄 Source: [Native_Processing_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Native_Processing_Component.htm)

###### Native Processing Component


The Native Processing component is a core XEngine component that can process raw EDI data by accessing XEngine through the exposed API. For more information, see [Native Data Processing Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/Native_Data_Processing_Overview.htm).



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


On this tab, you can define the [XEngine Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Service.htm) to be used by the component.


###### Data Source tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Parser schema | Specify a parser schema to be used. If you do not specify a schema file, XEngine picks up one of the default parser schemas. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Guideline | Specify a guideline to be used. If you do not specify the guideline file, XEngine picks up the guideline according to the selector configuration. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Encoding | Specify the file encoding.To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Tag Schema | Provide the path to the tagging schema you want to use. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. For more information, see [Tagging Data](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_XE_Tagging_Data.htm). |



###### Selector tab


On this tab, you can specify the XEngine selector to be used by the component. For more information, see [Selector settings](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Selector_Settings.htm).


###### Processing Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert to Avro Data | Select to convert native data to Avro Data (the Avro output format) in the following serialization formats: Binary: a binary data with a schema generated based on a current guideline. Generic Binary: a binary data with a schema generated based on a standard Avro specification. Generic JSON: a JSON data with a schema generated based on a standard Avro specification. JSON: a JSON data with a schema generated based on a current guideline. For more information on the Avro format, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/xengine/9.4/Content/Avro_Data_Processing_Overview.htm). |
| Convert to JData | Select to convert native data to JData. |
| Convert to XData | Select to convert native data to XData. |
| Split | Select to split native data. |
| Tags | Select to generate tags in XData, JData, and LDNS Report. Tags allow you to define logical markers to specific raw data elements. For more information, see [Tagging Data](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_XE_Tagging_Data.htm). |
| Validate | Select to validate native data. |
| Split rules | Provide a path to the data split rules file. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space.  Example ${XERoot}/config/ldns/DataSplitRules.xml |
| Encoding | Select the component's output encoding. The following encodings are supported: ASCII UTF-16 UTF-16BE UTF-16LE UTF-8 UTF-8 Without BOM |
| Acknowledgment | Select the appropriate check box or boxes to indicate the type of acknowledgment to create. |
| Big data file support | XEServer allows you to set the threshold limit for the incoming data that enables the BDF mode. In this case, all files less than the threshold limit are processed in memory with better performance. Bigger files are processed in the BDF mode with memory preservation. Note that the threshold limit does not always correlate with the size of data node structure footprint in memory, since large data can only have few data nodes. The BDF mode allows you to process large data files. When XEngine processes data, the main memory consumer is a data node structure that consists of instances, for example, Interchanges, Groups, Transactions, Providers, Claims, and so on. XEngine swaps data nodes to the hard drive storage if they are not in use, and restores them back in memory when necessary. In this case, memory stores only those data nodes that are currently being processed. It allows you to greatly reduce processing memory load with some trade-off in performance. |



You can set additional XEngine parameters in the Parameters section on the right. For more information about supported parameters, see [XEngine Configuration Parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Configuration_Parameters.htm).


###### Reports tab


To receive reports from XEngine route components, you should configure the corresponding XEngine route component and add the Task Reporter Inbound component to your route.


To configure the Task Reporter component:

1. Add the Task Reporter Inbound endpoint component to your route.
2. On the Task Reporter's Settings tab, ensure that the corresponding Native Processing component is selected in the Watched components list.


To configure reports settings for an XEngine component:

1. Ensure that the Watched by task reporter option is selected on the Overview tab of the Native Processing component.
2. Specify report types and formats by using the Reports tab in the Settings section.


On the Reports tab, you can select the following reports to be generated:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Advanced | Display advanced view that allows you to add properties to the different report types. |
| Audit report | This report shows the results of a submitted data file validated against a guideline. If there are errors, you must fix the application that created the data file, and then generate and submit a new data file. Additionally you can set format for each type of report. It is also possible to create a multi-page report in HTML format. |
| COBA Dispute report | This report shows the information about logical data nodes and events in a compact form (DFF format). COBA report is created for the highest level with errors or as specified for node on DETAIL record in 837 transactions. |
| Data with errors report | a) Report that shows data with error. b) Only create reports in case of error. c) Create separate reports for each Interchange. d) Name reports based on information in ISA, GS, ST. |
| Logical Data Node structure report | An LDNS report. This report shows the results of a separating the good business items from bad business items from an EDI data file. Selecting DFF check box enables creating Logical data node structure DFF report. It is also possible to create a multi-page report in HTML format. |
| Summary report | This report shows the summary results of a submitted data file validated against a guideline. Summary error report is designed to help you quickly and determine which violations are occurring the most number of times. Use the detail reports if you would like to know exactly where each violation occurred. |
| Tag report | Tag DFF report. This report shows the information about tags from applied Tag Schema and values from a data file these tags refer to. For more information, see [Tagging Data](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_XE_Tagging_Data.htm). |



For example, to generate Logical Data Node Structure reports in the DFF format, you should do the following:

1. On the Route Components Properties tab, click the Reports tab, for the Logical Data Node structure report value, select DFF:
2. On the component's Output tab, click New to create a new output.
3. Enter a name of the output, and from the Predefined outputs list, select DFF Logical Data Node structure reports. Note The new LDNS DFF output should have a position above the reports output as shown on the image: Click Move Up or Move Down to move an output within the pane.


###### External Document Handler tab


External document handlers allow you to extend and modify the business rules in XEngine processes. You can provide your custom Document Handler Factory service to be used for a particular XEngine process.


Note External document handler service is not among those services created by default.


For more information, see [Use Custom Document Handler](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Custom_Document_handler.htm).


###### Outputs Tab


Filtering is available for this component. Use expression language to customize the output results to accommodate your business requirements. For more information, see [Expression language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm).


| Output Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| xdata | XData are directed to this output. |
| jdata | JData are directed to this output. |
| split.good | Transactions that passed validation are directed to this output. |
| split.bad | Transactions that failed validation are directed to this output. |
| reports | Reports specified on the Reports tab are directed to this output. |
| acks | Acknowledgments are directed to this output. |
| original | Original native data are directed to this output. |
| error | Messages that could not be processed due to system, route, or component errors. |

---

<a id="selector-settings"></a>

##### Selector Settings

> 📄 Source: [Selector_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Selector_Settings.htm)

###### Selector Settings


On this tab, you can specify the XEngine selector to be used by the component and pass additional selector properties. For detailed information about XEngine Selectors, see [XEngine Selectors](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Selector_Overview.htm).


Select either the default XEngine XML selector or your custom selector:


*[Image: Settings tab]*


On the Parameters tab, you can override the selector parameters defined in your ${XERoot}\extensions\Selector\config\XERegistry.xml. This file lists the available selectors and stores selector parameters in the following format:


<Key name="Selectors">


   <Key name="XMLSelector">


      <!-- Mandatory -->


      <Item name="Config">${XERoot}/config/XESelectorConfig-generic.xml</Item> <!-- Mandatory DB / XML-->


      <Item name="Type">XML</Item>


      <!-- Optional Default "*" -->


      <Item name="TPName">*</Item>


   ...


</Key>


The following Selector parameters are available:


| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Config | For XML Selectors, this parameter defines a path to the XML Selector file. For DB Selectors, this property defines a path to the database connection .properties file. |
| Type | The type of Selector used. This can be either "XML" or "DB." |
| TPName | Optional parameter. The name of the Trading Partner. This can be any string. |
| Structure | The structure of the Selector. This can be either "Tree" or "Flat." |
| OutputLevel | The Selector's output level. This can be "Commit," "NoOutput," or "EchoAll." |

---

<a id="external-document-handlers-settings"></a>

##### External Document Handlers Settings

> 📄 Source: [External_Document_Handlers_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/External_Document_Handlers_Settings.htm)

###### External Document Handlers Settings


External document handlers provide the ability to extend and modify the business rules supplied by XEngine processes. You have to provide your custom Document Handler Factory service to be used for the particular XEngine process.


*[Image: External Document Handlers tab]*


To provide a document handler:

1. In the Route view, click one of the XEngine components.
2. On the Route component properties tab, click Settings tab, and then click External document handlers tab.
3. In the XE Document Handler section, click New on the right side of the tab.


*[Image: External document handlers tab]*


The New External document Handler window is displayed.

1. In the External document handler name field, provide a name of the new document handler.
2. In the Service name field, click the service that you want to use to provide an external document handler.


Note External document handler service is not among those services created by default. You have to add this service if you do not have it set. For more information on how to add the service, see [Supported Services](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Supported_Services.htm).


After the new external document handler is provided and service for it is set, you can add additional custom properties to the handler.


To add custom properties:

1. In the Properties section, click New on the right side of the tab.
2. On the New property attribute window, provide property name and its value.
3. Click OK.

---

<a id="use-custom-document-handler"></a>

##### Use Custom Document Handler

> 📄 Source: [Use_Custom_Document_handler.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Custom_Document_handler.htm)

###### Use Custom Document Handler


This section illustrates a basic usage model of implementing a custom document handler in XEngine.


To use a custom document handler:

1. Create a class that implements the com.edifecs.xengine.xeobjects.IXEDocHandler interface. Example public class EventLoggerDochandler implements IXEDocHandler { private OutputStream originalStream; private BufferedWriter output;   public boolean onStart(XEProcContext xeProcContext, XEDataNode xeDataNode) throws Exception { originalStream = xeProcContext.getStream(null, "EventLogRecords", null); output = new BufferedWriter(new OutputStreamWriter(originalStream, Charset.forName("UTF-8"))); return true; }   public void onEvent() { output.write(xeEventViewer.getSeverity() + " - " + xeEventViewer.getBrief() + " - " + xeEventViewer.getDescription()); output.newLine(); }   public boolean onFinish(XEProcContext xeProcContext, XEDataNode xeDataNode) throws Exception { output.close(); xeProcContext.closeStream(originalStream); return true; } } This implementation logs all events in the format <Severity> - <Brief> - <Description>. To get the output stream, the com.edifecs.xengine.xeobjects.XEProcContext.getStream(null,"EventLogRecords",null) method is called. The second parameter is a result type and is used by the XEServer XE Native component to filter the output of the custom document handler. To release the stream, the com.edifecs.xengine.xeobjects.XEProcContext.closeStream(stream) method is called.
2. Create a class that implements the com.edifecs.xengine.extensions.IXEDocHandlerFactory interface and generates an instance of the EventLoggerDochandler class. Example public class EventsLoggerDocHandlerFactory implements IXEDocHandlerFactory {   public IXEDocHandler getXEDocHandler(Properties prop) throws Exception { return new EventLoggerDochandler(); }   public void init(Properties props) throws Exception {   } }  
3. Compile the two classes and put the .jar file at the ${XESRoot}/platform/lib.user/shared location.
4. Create a route that looks like the following:
5. Register the document handler class in the XEServer XEngine Service:
6. Add the document handler to the XEServer XE Native route component:
7. To get the document handler results, add a new output to the XE Native component. This output should have a filter that passes only messages with the "EventLogRecords" result type: The {MD_XE_RT} macro used in the filter expression is resolved to the result type name that was set during the xeProcContext.getStream(null, "EventLogRecords", null) method call.


The public OutputStream getStream(String enginename, String typename, String name) and public void closeStream(OutputStream stream) methods of the XEProcContext class are introduced with the Hotfix v.2 for Edifecs XEngine v.8.8.1 . With these methods, you can obtain new streams by name and close them. You do not have to close all open streams manually since XEngine does this automatically after data processing is complete.


After the steps above, you can start your route and supply test data to the FS Inbound component to verify your custom document handler activity.

---

<a id="translator"></a>

#### Translator

> 📄 Source: [Book_Translator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Translator.htm)

##### Translator


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="translator-component"></a>

##### Translator Component

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Translator_Component.htm)

###### Translator Component


The component allows you to use the XEngine Translator engine functionality. For more information, see [Translator Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/Translator_Overview.htm) and the Translator Engine section in XEngine help.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


On this tab, you should enter the name of XEngine Service to be used by the component.


###### Data Source tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Parser schema | Provide parser schema that you want to use. If you do not set the schema file, XEngine picks up one of the default parser schemas. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Map | Specify the map file (.ems) to use for data translation. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Main Source Name | Specify a name of the inbound route component (placed before the Translator on the route) whose data should be treated as the main data source. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Encoding | Select the encoding of the input data. |
| Parameters | You can set additional XEngine parser parameters. For more information about supported parameters, see [Parser Parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Parser_Parameters.htm). |



###### Selector tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use internal selector | Select if you want to use the default XML Selector, specified on the XEngine Service Selectors tab. |
| Selector name | Select the XEngine selector for building data node structure. |
| Parameters | Use this pane to enter additional selector parameters. For more information, see [Use Selector](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Use_the_Selector.htm). |



###### Processing Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Validate Target | Select this check box if you want to validate the target guideline during data processing. |
| Encoding | Select the component's output encoding. The following encodings are supported: ASCII UTF-16 UTF-16BE UTF-16LE UTF-8 UTF-8 Without BOM |
| Format | Select the result format. After the Translator component finishes processing, it generates results in one of the following formats: MData: MData is a partially native result type wrapped in an XData-like structure that requires further processing by the XData to native component to create a fully native result. MData results can be processed individually or in a batch. Native: Native results are fully functional native data that can be consumed by any application. NativeBatch: Use this option to group native results in a batch combining relevant transaction under the same parent data node. For more information about Translation engine result formats, see Map Engine Result Overview in XEngine help. |
| Big data file support | XEServer allows you to set the threshold limit for the incoming data that enables the BDF mode. In this case, all files less than the threshold limit are processed in memory with better performance. Bigger files are processed in the BDF mode with memory preservation. Note that the threshold limit does not always correlate with the size of data node structure footprint in memory, since large data can only have few data nodes. The BDF mode allows you to process large data files. When XEngine processes data, the main memory consumer is a data node structure that consists of instances, for example, Interchanges, Groups, Transactions, Providers, Claims, and so on. XEngine swaps data nodes to the hard drive storage if they are not in use, and restores them back in memory when necessary. In this case, memory stores only those data nodes that are currently being processed. It allows you to greatly reduce processing memory load with some trade-off in performance. |
| Parameters | You can set additional XEngine parameters. For more information about supported parameters, see [XEngine Configuration Parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Configuration_Parameters.htm). |



###### Reports tab


After translating guidelines you can generate a translator report. This report contains information about the source file, map, execution start time, execution end time, target file which was created, overall status of the translation, any errors encountered during translation, any debug messages saved to the log file. Translator report can be generated in XML or HTML format.


Note A result Translator report may contain a lot of target data nodes. You may want to know which source node produced a certain target. To do that, source node’s GUID is published to the target node as an attribute in the report. You can also use the API to traverse the result data nodes (sources and targets). Being on target data node, you are able to retrieve the ID of corresponding source data node by executing the macro {dns.sourceID}, that returns the GUID of the source data node.


###### External Document Handler tab


External document handlers allow you to extend and modify the business rules in XEngine processes. You can provide your custom Document Handler Factory service to be used for a particular XEngine process.


Note External document handler service is not among those services created by default.


For more information, see [Use Custom Document Handler](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Custom_Document_handler.htm).


###### Outputs Tab


Filtering is available for this component. Use expression language to customize the output results to accommodate your business requirements. For more information, see [Expression language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm).


| Output Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| result | Result data are directed to this output. |
| reject | Data that failed to be translated are directed to this output. |
| reports | Reports specified on the Reports tab are directed to this output. |
| original | Original native data are directed to this output. |
| error | Messages that could not be processed due to system, route, or component errors. |

---

<a id="dynamic-parser-schema-pickup-scenario"></a>

##### Dynamic Parser Schema Pickup Scenario

> 📄 Source: [Dynamic_Parser_Schema_Pickup_Scenario.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Dynamic_Parser_Schema_Pickup_Scenario.htm)

###### Dynamic Parser Schema Pickup Scenario


This section shows you how to configure the XE Translator component to dynamically pick up a parser schema when working with multisource maps. A multisource map is a map that allows you to define multiple source guidelines, thereby allowing you to convert different types of incoming data formats into one target format using the same map file.


A sample route:



On this route, the input data may come in, in different formats: X12 or DFF. To select a suitable parser schema, use the following approach:


Step 1: The FS Inbound components X12 and DFF set the CustomParserSchema=<pathToParserSchemaFile> XEServer property where <pathToParserSchema> is the path to the appropriate X12 or DFF parser schema file.


Step 2: For each data file that comes in as input (for every XEServer message exchange), the Translator component retrieves the path to the appropriate parser schema file from the CustomParserSchema XEServer property using the {Prp[CustomParserSchema]} macro:



This approach has one important advantage: if you do not know the exact parser schema to use, you can let XEngine automatically select one from the set of parser schemas distributed with XEngine (${XERoot}\config\schema). To do this, you simply have to set the CustomParserSchema XEServer property as described earlier in this section, and leave the property value empty - this makes XEngine automatically detect the required parser schema for you. This is possible because of the following XEngine behavior: if the explicitly-provided parser schema file does not match the input data (or the provided parser schema cannot be found at all), then XEngine attempts to select a different parser schema from the list of default parser schemas. This way, when you specify a parser schema explicitly, you do not override XEngine’s choice, but simply extend the list of parser schemas distributed with XEngine


The modified sample route:



This route has been slightly modified - it has a new FS Inbound endpoint Other. This endpoint sets the CustomParserSchema = ''(with empty value) XEServer property. This means that all the data files submitted to the Other endpoint are converted using the XEngine's parser schema auto-detect mechanism.

---

<a id="create-multiple-source-mapping"></a>

##### Create Multiple Source Mapping

> 📄 Source: [Create_Multiple_Source_Mapping.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_Multiple_Source_Mapping.htm)

###### Create Multiple Source Mapping


Multiple source mapping uses the Translator component and a multiple source map, which specifies two or more guidelines to be used in a single translation process. Upon completion of this scenario, you are able to:

- Create a route that aggregates the messages from two sources to create a multimessage exchange.
- Input the multimessage exchange to Translator to transform X12 messages into PFF result.


Here is the route that aggregates two sources and uses a multiple source map for translation.


*[Image: Example Aggregation route]*


Before you go through this scenario, you should do the following:

- Create a new profile. For more information, see [Create a New Profile](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Profile.htm).
- Create a new route. For more information, see [Create a New Route](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Route.htm).

- Add the route component that are present on the preceding image and connect them the same way.


To configure the route components:

1. Click the FS Inbound endpoint.
2. On the Overview tab, rename the component to Source 1.
3. Repeat Step 1 for the second FS Inbound endpoint and rename it to Source 2.

1. On the Outputs tab, on the right side of the tab, click New to add a new property. New Output Properties dialog box is displayed.

1. In the Name field, type xes_msg_srcname.
2. In the Value field, for the Source 1 FS Inbound endpoint type Source 1.
3. Repeat Steps 4 and 5 for the Source 2 FS Inbound endpoint and in the Value field, type Source 2.
4. Click the Aggregation component, and then click the Settings tab.
5. On the Completion tab, make sure that all the setting coincide with the following ones:


*[Image: Aggregation Completion tab]*


Note The Transaction Management tab is not displayed for this component unless you have Transaction Management installed.

1. On the Correlation tab, type 1 in the Expression field. In our example Correlation IDs are not specified for the sample data files being used, so we need to make sure that all the incoming messages are correlated.


*[Image: Aggregation Correlation tab]*

1. On the Aggregation tab, specify the aggregation strategy as Create multimessage exchange.


*[Image: Create multimessage exchange]*

1. Click the Translator component, and then click Settings tab.
2. On the Data source tab, specify the path to the multiple source map file to be used in the Translator component. The Map.ems file for this scenario is located at ${XERoot}/samples/standards/Mapper/Multisource.


*[Image: Translator Data source tab]*

1. On the Processing configuration tab, change the target format from MData to native.


*[Image: Translator Processing configuration tab ]*

1. Click Save () or press CTRL+S to save changes.


To start the profile:

- On the EAM System tab, expand XEServer.
- Double-click Admin Console.
- On the EAM Outline tab, expand Profiles, and select the profile you need.
- On the Admin Console tab, click Start.
- Verify that all the profile's routes and services have been started


To process the data files:

1. Go to ${XERoot}samples\standards\X12\data.
2. Copy the 101.dat file and rename it to 101_2.dat so that you have two similar files in native format with different names.
3. Copy two data files to the source folder for the inbound endpoints. By default, it is located at ${XESRoot}\profiles\[ProfileName]\workspace\test-data\inbound.


###### Expected results:


By default, the results are located at ${XESRoot}\profiles\[ProfileName]\workspace\test-data\outbound. The outbound folder should contain a file in PFF format. A report in XML format is generated by default.


Note The resulting data file contains random dummy data. It does not contain valid business data and is only shown for the purpose of demonstrating how to use the Translator configuration.

---

<a id="xdata-processing-component"></a>

#### XData Processing Component

> 📄 Source: [XData_Processing_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XData_Processing_Component.htm)

##### XData Processing Component


With the XData Processing component, you can validate incoming XData and convert it to a native data format. For more information, see [XData Processing Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/XData_Processing_Overview.htm).


XData is a proprietary XML-based format developed by Edifecs. For more information, see [JData Processing Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/JData_Processing_Overview.htm).


For more information, see also the XData section in the XEngine User Guide.


When you add the component to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


On this tab, you should enter the name of XEngine Service to be used by the component.


###### Data Source tab


On this tab, you can specify the encoding and parser parameters for incoming data.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Encoding | Select the encoding of the input data. |
| Parameters | Use this pane to enter additional parser parameters. The following parameters are available: Parser.DataNode.AddItem Parser.Delimiter.DecimalSeparator Parser.Delimiter.ElementDelimiter Parser.Delimiter.ReleaseCharacter Parser.Delimiter.RepeatingSeparator Parser.Delimiter.SegmentDelimiter Parser.Delimiter.SubelementDelimiter For more information, see [Parser Parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Parser_Parameters.htm). |



###### Selector tab


On this tab, you can enter selector parameters.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use internal selector | Select if you want to use the default XML Selector, specified on the XEngine Service Selectors tab. |
| Selector name | Select the XEngine selector for building data node structure. |
| Parameters | Use this pane to enter additional selector parameters. For more information, see [Use Selector](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Use_the_Selector.htm). |



###### Processing Configuration tab


On this tab, you can enter selector parameters.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert | Choose what modes you want to use in the data processing. |
| Validate | Choose what modes you want to use in the data processing. |
| Encoding | Select the component's output encoding. The following encodings are supported: ASCII UTF-16 UTF-16BE UTF-16LE UTF-8 UTF-8 Without BOM |
| Parameters | You can set additional XEngine parameters. For more information about supported parameters, see [XEngine Configuration Parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Configuration_Parameters.htm). |



###### Reports tab


To receive reports from XEngine route components you must configure the XEngine route component and add the Task Reporter Inbound component to your route.


To configure the Task Reporter component:

1. Add the Task Reporter Inbound endpoint component to your route.
2. On the Task Reporter's Settings tab, ensure that the corresponding XData Processing component is selected in the Watched components list.


To configure reports settings for an XEngine component:

1. Ensure that the Watched by task reporter option is selected on the Overview tab of the XData Processing component.
2. Specify report types and formats by using the Reports tab in the Settings section:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Advanced | Select to display the advanced view that allows you to add properties to different report types. |
| Audit report | Select the corresponding check boxes to generate Audit and Summary reports in a specified format. You can also create a multi-page report in HTML format. |
| Summary report | |



###### External Document Handler tab


External document handlers allow you to extend and modify the business rules in XEngine processes. You can provide your custom Document Handler Factory service to be used for a particular XEngine process.


Note External document handler service is not among those services created by default.


For more information, see [Use Custom Document Handler](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Custom_Document_handler.htm).


###### Outputs Tab


Filtering is available for this component. Use expression language to customize the output results to accommodate your business requirements. For more information, see [Expression language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm).


| Output Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| native | Native data are directed to this output. |
| reports | Reports specified on the Reports tab are directed to this output. |
| original | Original JData is directed to this output. |
| error | Messages that could not be processed due to system, route, or component errors. |

---

###### Processing Configuration tab


On this tab, you can enter selector parameters.


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert | Select what modes you want to use in the data processing. |
| Validate | Select what modes you want to use in the data processing. |
| Encoding | Select the component's output encoding. The following encodings are supported: ASCII UTF-16 UTF-16BE UTF-16LE UTF-8 UTF-8 Without BOM |
| Parameters | You can set additional XEngine parameters. For more information about supported parameters, see [XEngine Configuration Parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Configuration_Parameters.htm). |



###### Reports tab


To receive reports from XEngine route components you must configure the XEngine route component and add the Task Reporter Inbound component to your route.


To configure the Task Reporter component:

1. Add the Task Reporter Inbound endpoint component to your route.
2. On the Task Reporter's Settings tab, ensure that the corresponding component Avro Data Processing is selected in the Watched components list.


To configure reports settings for an XEngine component:

2. Specify report types and formats by using the Reports tab in the Settings section:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Advanced | Select to display the advanced view that allows you to add properties to different report types. |
| Audit report | Select the corresponding check boxes to generate Audit and Summary reports in a specified format. You can also create a multi-page report in HTML format. |
| Summary report | |



###### External Document Handler tab


External document handlers allow you to extend and modify the business rules in XEngine processes. You can provide your custom Document Handler Factory service to be used for a particular XEngine process.


Note External document handler service is not among those services created by default.


For more information, see [Use Custom Document Handler](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_Custom_Document_handler.htm).


###### Outputs Tab


Filtering is available for this component. Use expression language to customize the output results to accommodate your business requirements. For more information, see [Expression language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm).


| Output Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| native | Native data are directed to this output. |
