<a id="data-replacement-component"></a>

#### Data Replacement Component

> 📄 Source: [Data_Replacement_Component.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Data_Replacement_Component.htm)

##### Data Replacement Component


With the Data Replacement component, you can make minor non-structural data transformation based on a set of custom replacement rules. Data Replacement is an alternative to the translation mechanism that works like a search and replace function for several values and allows you to make a lot of changes to your data file quickly and efficiently.


With the Data Replacement component, you can:

- Automatically replace confidential information (for example, social security numbers, credit card numbers, protected healthcare information - PHI) with some generic values
- Swap values in your data file
- Add new segments and automatically recalculate their number
- Replace delimiters
- Generate user-friendly XML and HTML reports.


To configure the data replacement process, the following files are required:

- Replacement rules file (an XML-based configuration file with user-defined replacement rules)
- Guideline (a hierarchically-structured standard specification with structural and logical rules)
- Parser schema (to identify standards and to build a basic data node structure that is common to the specific standard).


For information on how to configure replacement rules file and for information on typical cases where Data Replacement can be used, view [Data Replacement Rules](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/Data_Replacement_Rules.htm) and [Data Replacement Use Cases](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/Data_Replacement_Use_Cases.htm).


Note Data Replacement supports the following standards: X12 (including HIPAA), HL7, JSON, XML, Positional flat data, Delimited flat data.


By default, XEngine picks up guidelines and parser schemas automatically based on the transaction type of your data file. If required, you can specify the paths to these configuration files in the component's settings.


The Data Replacement component can be configured on the Route component properties tab.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field/Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use XEngine service | (Optional) Select this option to define the [XEngine Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Service.htm) to be used by the component. |
| Service name | (If the Use XEngine service option is enabled) Define the [XEngine Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Service.htm) to be used by the component. You can also view the selected service's configurations and create a new XEngine service. |
| Use internal selector | Select if you want to use the default XML [Selector](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/Selector_Overview.htm) specified on the [XEngine Service Selectors](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Service.htm#Selectors) tab. |
| Selector name | (If the Use internal selector option is disabled) Select the [XEngine selector](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEngine_Service.htm#Selectors) configuration file from where XEngine retrieves the path to the guideline and/or to the replacement rules file. To specify the paths to the guidelines and/or replacement rules files in a [selector file](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/Selector_Overview.htm), in a section that corresponds to your transaction, add the ecsFilePath property and/or the replaceScenarioFilePath property as shown in the following example: Example <property type="ecsFilePath">${XERoot}/config/guideline/997.ecs</property> <property type="replaceScenarioFilePath">%ECRootPath%/repository/User/XEngine/user-configs/rules.xml</property> You can add the paths to the replacement rules files for each level. For the rules to be applicable to all the levels, you can add the rule path only to the Interchange level. If you want to have different rules, for example, some for the Interchange level and some for the Transaction level, the rules you specify at the Interchange level are applicable for the Interchange and FunctionalGroup levels when the Transaction level refers to a different set of rules under a different path. |
| Encoding | Select the input data encoding. By default, XEngine detects the encoding automatically. XEngine supports the following encodings: ASCII EBCDIC-CP-US (not detected automatically, must be specified) UTF-16 UTF-8 UTF-8 Without BOM |
| Selector Parameters | You can override the selector parameters defined in your ${XERoot}\extensions\Selector\config\XERegistry.xml. The following Selector parameters are available: Config: For XML Selectors, this parameter defines a path to the XML Selector file. For DB Selectors, this property defines a path to the database connection .properties file. Type: The type of Selector used can be either "XML" or "DB." TPName: Optional parameter. The name of the Trading Partner can be any string. Structure: The structure of the Selector can be either "Tree" or "Flat." OutputLevel: The Selector's output level can be "Commit," "NoOutput," or "EchoAll." |



###### Processing Configuration tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Replacement rules | (Mandatory if not specified in selector) Specify the path to the file with [replacement rules](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/Data_Replacement_Rules.htm). If you do not specify the rules, then XEngine retrieves the path from the replaceScenarioFilePath property in the selector file based on the transaction type of your data. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Guideline | Specify the path to the guideline that is used to traverse input data. If you do not specify the guideline, then XEngine retrieves the guideline path from the ecsFilePath property in the selector file based on the transaction type of your data. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Parser schema | Specify the path to the [parser schema](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/go/xengine/9.4/Content/Parsing_Overview.htm) that must be used to identify standards and to build a basic data node structure. If you do not specify the parser schema, then XEngine picks up the parser schema automatically based on the transaction type of your data file. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |
| Output Encoding | Select the component's output encoding. XEngine supports the following encodings: ASCII UTF-16 UTF-16BE UTF-16LE UTF-8 UTF-8 Without BOM |



###### Reports tab


On the Reports tab, select the required report formats (XML and/or HTML).


The generated Data Replacement report displays the original and the replaced data, the places where the data was replaced, and the rule description. The following is an example of the generated Data Replacement report in the HTML format:



###### Outputs Tab


Filtering is available for this component. Use expression language to customize the output results to accommodate your business requirements. For more information, see [Expression language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm).


The Data Replacement component has the following outputs:


| Output Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| data | The component directs replaced data through this output. |
| reports | The component directs the reports specified on the Reports tab through this output. |
| original | The component directs the original native data through this output. |
| error | The component directs the messages that could not be processed due to system, route, or component errors through this output. |

### Enterprise Integrated Patterns

> 📄 Source: [Book_Enterprise_Integrated_Patterns.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Enterprise_Integrated_Patterns.htm)

#### Enterprise Integrated Patterns


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="aggregation"></a>

#### Aggregation

> 📄 Source: [Book_Aggregation.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Aggregation.htm)

##### Aggregation


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="aggregation-overview"></a>

##### Aggregation Overview

> 📄 Source: [Aggregation_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Aggregation_Overview.htm)

###### Aggregation Overview


The Aggregation component can be used to gather several messages into one or it can send a message when all related messages are received. Using an aggregation component you can:

- Archive all messages
- Batch messages
- Merge properties
- Create a multimessage exchange
- Create a Report message
- Use a groovy script to perform any other actions
- Use a pluggable template (UCF rules aggregator, HIPAA merge, or XML Batch)


You can configure any process within aggregation object by using Route component properties tab. Select the object in the Route Viewer, to see its properties on the Properties tab.

---

<a id="aggregation-common-properties"></a>

##### Aggregation Common Properties

> 📄 Source: [Aggregation_Common_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Aggregation_Common_Properties.htm)

###### Aggregation Common Properties


On the Route component properties tab, click Settings tab and Common tab to set one of the following:


*[Image: Aggregation Common Settings tab]*


###### Settings tab


###### Common tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Max number of open aggregations | Maximum number of aggregation processes within this aggregation available at the same time. Maximum allowed number is 1000. |
| Create a new task | Creates a new task when aggregation starts. This task is used to set completion method. |
| Report error for empty aggregation | Reports an error when aggregation is empty. |
| Aggregation expiration time (ms) | Time limit used for all aggregations to come. In case the last aggregation is not received in the provided time limit, an error is reported. |
| Reset aggregation timeout after new message | Reset the timeout between aggregations after the next in turn message is received. |
| Timeout expiration action | Complete Aggregation. Report timeout expiration error. |

---

<a id="aggregation-correlation-properties"></a>

##### Aggregation Correlation Properties

> 📄 Source: [Aggregation_Correlation_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Aggregation_Correlation_Properties.htm)

###### Aggregation Correlation Properties


Using Correlation you can correlate incoming messages. All incoming messages are combined together basing on correlation ID that is calculated by an expression based strategy.


*[Image: Correlation tab]*


Note The Transaction Management tab is not displayed for this component unless you have Transaction Management installed.


###### Settings tab


###### Correlation tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Expression: | For all incoming data the specified expression is calculated and result is treated as correlation ID. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. Example {PN@CorrelationID} - according to this condition, all messages are checked for the CorrelationID property and messages with the same ID are correlated. |

---

<a id="aggregation-completion-messages"></a>

##### Aggregation Completion Messages

> 📄 Source: [Aggregation_Completion_Messages.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Aggregation_Completion_Messages.htm)

###### Aggregation Completion Messages


Completion functionality specifies how the system decides that all incoming messages are received. The following strategies for completion are available:

- XEServer expression
- Groovy expression
- Task completion


*[Image: Completion tab]*


Note The Transaction Management tab is not displayed for this component unless you have Transaction Management installed.


###### Settings tab


###### Completion tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Choose strategy | |
| XES expression | Applies XEServer expressions to all incoming messages. XEServer supports predefined expressions and allows to specify custom one. |
| Last message contains property | Specify a property name. If the property is present in the incoming message, it is treated as the last message. Last message strategy activates aggregation when the last message is received. Last message can be detected by some property. Additional option is provided that indicates if the last message should be included to the aggregation or not. |
| Number of received messages reached | Specify number of messages that constitute the entire message. |
| Custom expression | Specify a custom expression. If expression returns true, then message is treated as last. |
| Do not aggregate last message | If selected, the last message is treated as a marker and is not added to the result message. |
| Groovy expression | Runs a custom groovy script on each incoming message, and then calls it for getting the completion message. For more information about the groovy API, see [Groovy API](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Groovy_API.htm). |
| **Script** | Provide a groovy script for the component. You can edit a groovy script. To do this, click Edit next to the Script field. |
| **Script path** | Enter a path to an external .groovy file you want to execute. You can edit a file with a groovy script. To do this, click Edit next to the Script path field. You can also edit a file from the repository. To do this, you need to provide a path to the file from the repository first. Click Browse next to the Script path box. In the File Path dialog box, select Cloud repository, and then navigate to the file you need. The selected file is pulled from the repository to the Script path field. To edit a file from the repository, click Edit next to the Script path field. |
| **Groovy script parameters** | Provide parameter and a value pairs to be resolved. |
| Task completion | Task completion listens to the events of XEServer. When a task is considered to be ready, XES reports corresponding event and aggregation component calls for aggregation strategy for getting aggregated results. It means that then all messages with the same TaskID are already completed. Aggregator usually prepares results in scope of the same task, so for using this strategy Aggregation component should be configured to create new task. |
| Task originator is completed on component | Choose one of the components on the Route that serves as marker for aggregation completion. |
| Task originator is completed | A new task is created on aggregation start and when this task is completed then the aggregation is completed. |

---

<a id="aggregation-properties"></a>

##### Aggregation Properties

> 📄 Source: [Aggregation_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Aggregation_Properties.htm)

###### Aggregation Properties


Select the way you want the messages to be aggregated.


*[Image: Aggregation tab]*


###### Settings tab


###### Aggregation tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Archive all messages | Archives (zips) all messages. Select archive level, and then click properties if you want to include message properties in the archive. |
| Batch all messages | Batches all files into one (append). The specified batch separator is added between messages. Default separator is 0x0D0x0A. Message properties are merged to one collection. |
| Merge properties | This strategy merges the properties of all messages and message exchanges according to aggregation settings. The resulting single message exchange has the content of the last received message and contains all properties of all processed messages. If the property with the same name already exists, the last received property overwrites the existing one. |
| Create multimessage exchange | Creates an XEServer message exchange with aggregated messages. The exchange properties are added to each message. It can be useful when it is necessary to start batching XData processing or it is necessary to send messages to JMS in one transaction. |
| Groovy expression | Runs a custom groovy script on each incoming message, and then calls it for getting the completion message. For more information about the groovy API, see [Groovy API](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Groovy_API.htm). You can edit a custom groovy script. To do this, click Edit next to the Script or Script path field. You can also edit a file from the repository. To do this, you need to provide a path to the file from the repository first. Click Browse next to the Script path box. In the File Path dialog box, select Cloud repository, and then navigate to the file you need. The selected file is pulled from the repository to the Script path field. To edit a file from the repository, click Edit next to the Script path field. |
| Pluggable | Select to provide a custom template to perform aggregation. Available templates are [XML Batch Strategy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Batcher_Predefined_Stratagies.htm) and [HIPAA Merger](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HIPAA_Merger_Overview.htm). |

---

<a id="hipaa-merger"></a>

##### HIPAA Merger

> 📄 Source: [Book_HIPAA_Merger.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_HIPAA_Merger.htm)

###### HIPAA Merger


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="hipaa-merger-overview"></a>

##### HIPAA Merger Overview

> 📄 Source: [HIPAA_Merger_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HIPAA_Merger_Overview.htm)

###### HIPAA Merger Overview


HIPAA Merger is an embedded XEServer module that is used to merge native X12 transactions. This module is used as a pluggable strategy for the Aggregation component.


There is a difference between merging transactions through HIPAA Merger and the XEngine Native Processing component. HIPAA Merger compares and merges identical logical levels within a transaction. Native Processing batches transactions within an interchange, without traversing the logical levels of the transactions. Thus, HIPAA Merger analyzes an interchange much deeper by comparing logical levels of a transaction, such as Submitters, Receivers, and so on.


To configure HIPAA Merger:

1. On the Aggregation tab of the XEServer Aggregation component, in the Template drop-down list, select HIPAA Merger.
2. On the Initial parameters tab, define the following mandatory parameters.


| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| create.log | Set the value to TRUE to log the merging process. |
| create.report | Set the value to TRUE to create a report with merging results. |
| scenario | The base configuration parameter. Specify the type of transactions to be merged. HIPAA Merger comes with the following [scenarios](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HIPAA_Merger_Scenarios.htm): HIPAA271 HIPAA277CA HIPAA834 HIPAA834_5010X220A1_NO_QTY HIPAA834_5010x220A1_NO_QTY_W_NM1 HIPAA834_W_QTY HIPAA837I HIPAA837I_W_BHT HIPAA837P HIPAA837P_W_BHT |
| input.encoding | Specify the encoding of incoming files to be merged. Note the following behavior peculiarities: If the parameter is not specified (input.encoding == null) or is set to auto, the system detects UTF-16LE, UTF-16BE, UTF-8 by BOM and sets the same encoding and BOM for output files. If input data doesn't contain BOM, it is handled as ASCII. All input files should have the same encoding, otherwise an exception is thrown. If the parameter is specified explicitly (input.encoding != null), the specified input encoding is used for output files. BOM is added to output files if input files contain it. If an input file cannot be properly decoded using the "auto-detect" or any other encoding string, the default java "CodingErrorAction.REPLACE" field is used to replace all unmappable characters with a default replacement character. If input.encoding == "UTF-16", and the input file does not contain BOM, the input data is handled as "UTF-16BE". Output files have the same "UTF-16BE" encoding and no BOM added to them. If BOM does not match the specified encoding, an exception is thrown. |

---

<a id="hipaa-merger-scenarios"></a>

##### HIPAA Merger Scenarios

> 📄 Source: [HIPAA_Merger_Scenarios.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HIPAA_Merger_Scenarios.htm)

###### HIPAA Merger Scenarios


Before processing your EDI data, you can merge several EDI files to process a single file instead of many. HIPAA Merger works with native data files and can merge different transaction types based on special scenarios. A scenario is a base HIPAA Merger setting that defines the type of EDI transactions to merge. The scenario definition is stored in special [configuration files](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HIPAA_Merger_Configuration_Files.htm) (${XESRoot}/features/route-engine/components/aggregator-hipaa/config/) that define which transaction segments should be compared and merged.


Below you can view the available HIPAA Merger scenarios.


**Note** Due to the specifics of HIPAA835 Transactions, merging them is not supported by default. For more information, see the HIPAA835 section below.


###### HIPAA834


This scenario merges several 834 ST/SE files into one ST/SE file. There are two types of processesing this scenario follows:

- Takes all segments from the beginning of the transaction (ST) up to the INS segment in the first file and inserts them into the output file.
- Takes all the data from the files to be merged starting from the INS segment up to the end (SE) and inserts them into the output file one after another.


In the following example, there are two similar files. All segments up to the INS segment are inserted into the result file.

 Click to view sample data files


Input Data


ISA*00*Authorizat*00*Security I*ZZ*Interchange Sen*ZZ*Interchange Rec*030326*1037*U*00401*000031033*0*T*:


GS*BE*Sample Sen*Sample Rec*20030326*1037*123456*X*004010X095A1


ST*834*123456


BGN*00*12456*20030326*1200****2


N1*P5**FI*999888777


N1*IN**FI*654456654


INS*Y*18*021*20*A***FT


REF*0F*123456789


REF*1L*123456001


DTP*356*D8*20021026


…..


…..


HD*021**DEN


DTP*348*D8*20021026


HD*021**VIS


DTP*348*D8*20021026


SE*22*123456


GE*1*12345


IEA*1*000031033


Merged Data

ST*834*123456


BGN*00*12456*20030326*1200****2


N1*P5**FI*999888777


N1*IN**FI*654456654


INS*Y*18*021*20*A***FT

REF*1L*123456001


DTP*356*D8*20021026


….

###### X12


This scenario will merge multiple X12 files:

- Same interchange ISA/EAs and same groups GS/GEs will be merged into one.
- Transaction level ST/SEs will not be merged.


###### HIPAA837I / HIPAA837P


These scenarios create one ST/SE segment in the 837 file from several 837 files with individual ST/SE segments. Each ST/SE segment has one provider in each 837 file. The providers from each ST/SE transaction are merged into one ST/SE transaction.In the following example, there are two similar files, where each ST/SE segment has one provider, and these two segments from two files are merged into one ST/SE transaction.

 Click to view sample data files


Input Data


ISA~00~Authorizat~00~Security I~01~Interchange Sen~01~Interchange Rec~121120~1538~*~00501~000000001~0~P~\


GS~HC~Ap~Ap~20121120~1538~1~X~005010X223A2


ST~837~0001~I


BHT~0019~00~R~20121120~1538~31


NM1~41~1~N~~~~~46~Id


PER~IC~~EM~C

HL~1~~20~1

N3~A


N4~Ci


REF~EI~R


HL~2~1~22~0


SBR~A


NM1~IL~1~N


NM1~PR~2~N~~~~~PI~Id


SE~15~0001


GE~1~1


IEA~1~000000001

The highlighted parts remain the same and everything starting from the Subscriber Loop (2000B) is different.






###### HIPAA837I_W_BHT / HIPAA837P_W_BHT


These scenarios work similarly to HIPAA837I / HIPAA837P scenarios but here the merging is based on the BHT segment value. This means that if an input EDI file has multiple ST/SE transactions each with unique BHT, the nested segments (for example, CLMs) are merged only within ST/SE transactions and are not merged into a single ST/SE segment (unlike plain HIPAA837I / HIPAA837P scenarios that would merge all CLMs from several ST/SE segments into a single ST/SE).

 Click to view sample data files


Input Data


A sample input 837 EDI file has 2 ST/SE transactions with different BHT segments:

GE~1~1


IEA~1~000000001


The highlighted loops from the second 834 file are added.






###### HIPAA835


HIPAA835 Transactions, also known as 835 EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) Transactions, communicate how a healthcare claim was paid, adjusted, or denied by the insurance company. Therefore, by representing one physical payment, HIPAA835 Transactions are not designed to be split or merged. As per the LDNS structure file for HIPAA835 Transactions, both the Claim and Provider levels have the flag **repackage**=`false`, which explicitly prevents XEngine from splitting the Transaction. Altering 835 Transactions , either by splitting or merging, can impact the integrity of the payment data, especially the total amount tied to a Claim Payment ID.


Additionally, because the BPR segment is intended to correlate exactly to one payment for a specific amount per Transaction, merging two BPR segments compromises this 1:1 mapping, which directly impacts the financial and audit integrity of the file, making it the primary reason why 835 merging is not supported out of the box.


The following considerations should also be taken into account:

- The 835 LDNS structure only extends to the Claim level, not to the Claim Payment level.
- Merging HIPAA835 Transactions would require recalculating the BPR segment and the 2000 LX loop line numbers – tasks that the HIPAA Merger is not designed to perform.
- From an audit and regulatory standpoint, altering the BPR segment structure is highly sensitive and risky.
- A custom splitting or merging scenario for HIPAA835 Transactions can become very complex, potentially requiring additional steps and significant custom implementation efforts beyond just defining a Merger Properties file:– use of a custom map– ingesting multiple sources– implementing a complex processing flow before and after the merger to maintain data fidelity

---

<a id="hipaa-merger-configuration-files"></a>

##### HIPAA Merger Configuration Files

> 📄 Source: [HIPAA_Merger_Configuration_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HIPAA_Merger_Configuration_Files.htm)

###### HIPAA Merger Configuration Files


With the configuration files, you can define the transaction segments to be compared and merged by HIPAA Merger. These files are located at ${XESRoot}/features/route-engine/components/aggregator-hipaa/config. The folder contains specific configuration files, each used for merging particular transaction types.


All parameters that contain "config" in their names, such as Transaction.config.0 are used as a schema for merging input files. The parameters which names do not contain "config" are used as a schema for parsing input files.


The following set of parameters defines the structure of input file to be parsed:


Example


Interchange.Level=0 // The external level is Interchange


Group.Level=1 // The next nested level is Group


Transaction.Level=2 // The next nested level is Transaction


Provider.Level=3 // The next nested level is Provider


###### config.[x].config[x] parameters


Parameters that contain "config.[x].config[x]" are used for merging configurations:

 Example 1


If any of the **config** value is -1, the level for which they are set will be considered different and will not be merged.


Example


Provider.config.0.config.0=-1


This configuration informs the system that there will be no merge at Provider level even if they are exactly the same.





 Example 2


Given the following merging configuration:


Example


Interchange.config.0=ISA


Interchange.config.0.config.0=4


Interchange.config.0.config.1=5


...


Having **Interchange.config.0=ISA**, we interpret the other parameters as:


**ISA.config.0=4**, which resolves to **ISA05: Interchange ID Qualifier**


and


**ISA.config.1=5**, which resolves to **ISA06: Interchange Sender ID**






The following table describes the contents of the sample merger_hipaa837i.properties contents:


| Level | Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|   Interchange | With these parameters, HIPAA Merger searches for the ISA segment and merges all identical input ISA segments into one single result ISA. | |
| Interchange.Level=0 | Defines the interchange level. | |
| Interchange.LevelName=Interchange | Defines the interchange level name. | |
| Interchange.HeaderSegment=ISA | Defines the name of a segment. | |
| Interchange.Nodes=-1 | Defines the maximum number of identical ISA segments to be merged. If exceeded, an exception is thrown. A negative value removes the limit. | |
| Interchange.config.0=ISA | Defines the ISA segment to be compared and merged if identical. | |
| Interchange.config.1.end=GS | Defines the end of ISA segment for merging criteria - all identical nodes preceding the Group (GS segment) are merged. | |
|   Group | With these parameters, HIPAA Merger searches for the GS segment and merges all identical input GS segments into the one single result GS. | |
| Group.Level=1 | Defines the group level. | |
| Group.LevelName=Group | Defines the group level name. | |
| Group.HeaderSegment=GS | Defines the name of a segment. | |
| Group.Nodes=-1 | Defines the maximum number of identical GS segments to be merged. A negative value removes the limit. | |
| Group.config.0=GS | Defines the GS segment to be compared and merged if identical. | |
| Group.config.1.end=ST | Defines the end of GS segment for merging criteria - all identical nodes preceding the Transaction (ST segment) are merged. | |
|   Transaction | Note that HIPAA Merger searches for the parameters marked green (REF, NM1, PER, NM1 segments) between the ST and first HL segment in a transaction. | |
| Transaction.Level=2 | Defines the transaction level. | |
| Transaction.LevelName=Transaction | Defines the transaction level name. | |
| Transaction.HeaderSegment=ST | Defines the name of a segment. | |
| Transaction.Nodes=-1 | Defines the maximum number of identical ST segments to be merged. A negative value removes the limit. | |
| Transaction.config.0=REF Transaction.config.1=NM1 Transaction.config.2=PER Transaction.config.3=NM1 | All REF/NM1/PER segments between ST and first HL segments are used as a merging criteria. To use only the first segment found, set the value to Transaction.config.0=REF^. | |
| Transaction.config.4.end=HL | Defines the end of ST segment for merging criteria - all identical nodes preceding the Billing Provider (HL segment) are merged. | |
| Billing Provider | Provider.Level=3 | Defines the Provider level. |
| Provider.LevelName=Billing Provider | Defines the Provider level name. | |
| Provider.HeaderSegment=HL | Defines the name of a segment. **Note** Entries containing **HeaderSegment** are used for the parsing configuration. | |
| Provider.HeaderSegment.config.0=2 | Identifies the segment as Billing Provider (it must be the same as the preceding Billing Provider index or equal to 20). This parameter specifies that the Provider is parsed and defined using the logic: Provider.HeaderSegment.config.0=HL.config.0=2 which resolves to HL03: Hierarchical Level Code. | |
| Provider.Nodes=-1 | Defines the maximum number of identical HL segments to be merged. A negative value removes the limit. | |



###### Provider parameters


The commented parameters can be used for comparing and merging specific segments. To allow HIPAA Merger to use the following segments for comparison, uncomment the parameters you need.


#Provider.config.0=PRV


#Provider.config.1=CUR


#Provider.config.2=NM1


#Provider.config.3=N3


#Provider.config.4=N4


#Provider.config.5=REF


#Provider.config.6=REF


#Provider.config.7=PER


#Provider.config.8=NM1


#Provider.config.9=N3


#Provider.config.10=N4


#Provider.config.11=REF


#Provider.config.12.end=HL


#Provider.config.0.end=HL


The parameter sets for different transaction types may vary due to difference in EDI structure. For example, merger_hipaa837i.properties does not contain the Prop.HLHierarchical parameter which is used to update HL IDs below the Transaction level. For 837 transactions, 837 HL hierarchy is used. Set Prop.HLHierarchical = Skip to skip the HL7 hierarchy updates.

---

<a id="use-hipaa-merger-command-line-tool"></a>

##### Use HIPAA Merger Command Line Tool

> 📄 Source: [Use_HIPAA_Merger_Command_Line_Tool.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Use_HIPAA_Merger_Command_Line_Tool.htm)

###### Use HIPAA Merger Command Line Tool


XEngine Server is distributed with the embedded HIPAA Merger test tool to allow you to perform test merging. The service is located at ${XESRoot}/test/hipaamerger and is designed only for testing. In this way, you can change configuration files and the change will not affect your XEServer run-time.


HIPAA Merger test tool has the following structure:


| Element | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| data | Directory | Contains sample data to be merged. |
| lib | Directory | Contains java executables. |
| results | Directory | Contains merged data. |
| scenarios | Directory | Contains configuration files for merging specific transactions. |
| HIPAAMergerCLI.bat | Windows Batch File | The .bat script to perform merging. |
| README | TXT file | Contains help on using the test tool. |



To run the test tool:

1. Start the command prompt.
2. Change your working directory to ${XESRoot}/test/hipaamerger.
3. Run the HIPAAMergerCLI.bat script with required arguments. For information about command arguments and sample commands, see README.txt.ExampleHIPAAMergerCLI.bat -s hipaa277ca .\data\input277ca


Your merged data is saved to the results directory.

---

<a id="work-with-hipaa-merger-keys"></a>

##### Work with HIPAA Merger Keys

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_HIPAA_Merger_Keys.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Work_with_HIPAA_Merger_Keys.htm)

###### Work with HIPAA Merger Keys


You can define the merging criteria using the Key parameter. Keys are used if merging of the business data (Claims, Subscribers) requires external information that is not present in data. For example, there is a group of claims that were extracted from a database and generated with a dummy subscriber ID. However, there is an external database, that provides the subscriber ID for a given Claim. Native EDI data does not have a proper Subscriber ID, but it is supplied to HIPAA Merger through a key. All Claims with the same Subscriber ID are merged together. Note that a key is a sequence of items, and in one and the same key, you can provide merging information for Interchange, Group, Provider, and so on, that are considered during merging.


The parameter is optional and can be passed with the message as the XEServer MergerKey message property. A Key is defined as one or multiple items separated by the forward slash, for example ISA1/GS1/ST1/Sub1/Dep1. When passed, a Key becomes one of the merging criteria, along with the currently used configuration. Items with the same key and different logical structure are not merged. This can handle the following situations:

- There are two subscribers named "Subscriber1". One has a valid response, and the other has an "AAA" error code due to the Dependent inquiry (AAA must be added to match the configuration).
- There are two subscribers named "Subscriber1" with different EB segments. The subscribers are merged and information about both subscribers is retained.
- There are two subscribers named "Subscriber1" with no inquiries or errors but with different Dependents. The subscribers are not merged and each one is saved with its own Dependent. If there are two keys where one is longer than the other, and the shorter key matches the longer one (key1 = \Subsriber1\Dependent1, key2 = \Subscriber1), the nodes are merged. The following is an example of such merging. Example Message1 key = \Subsriber1\Dependent1; Message2 key = \Subscriber1; Merged data: Subscriber1 Inquiry Dependent1 Inquiry.
- Merging through a key parameter also saves key items. The following example illustrates that nodes of all three keys are retained in merged data. Example Message 1: \ISA1\GS1\ST1\Subscriber 1\Dependent 1 Message 2: \ISA1\GS1\ST1\Subscriber 2 Message 3: \ISA1\GS1\ST2\Subscriber 1 Merged data: ISA GS ST1 Subscriber 1 Dependent 1 Subscriber 2 SE1 ST2 Subscriber 2 SE2 GE IEA

---

<a id="hipaa-merger-use-cases"></a>

##### HIPAA Merger Use Cases

> 📄 Source: [HIPAA_Merger_Use_Cases.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/HIPAA_Merger_Use_Cases.htm)

###### HIPAA Merger Use Cases


The following use cases are mostly driven from the 270-271 Request and Response example.


Merging the 271 Responses


The inbound 270 file:


ISA


GS


ST - 1


Subscriber 1


Inquiry 1


Dependent 1


Inquiry 2


Subscriber 2


Inquiry 3


Subscriber 1 (same as previous one)


Inquiry 4


SE


ST – 1


Subscriber 2


Inquiry 5


SE – 2


GE


IEA


The inbound file gets split into individual patients (Subscriber 1, Subscriber 1 – Dependent 1, Subscriber 2, Subscriber 1, Subscriber 2). Then, the back-end provides individual responses to these inquiries. The result sample is the following:


ST-1


Subscriber 1


SE-1


ST-1


Subscriber 1


Dependent 1


SE-1


ST-1


Subscriber 2


SE-1


ST-1


Subscriber 1


SE-1


ST-2


Subscriber 2


SE-2


The expected 271 transaction after the merge:


ISA


GS


ST - 1


Subscriber 1


Reponse 1


Dependent 1


Response 2


Subscriber 2


Response 3


Subscriber 1


Response 4


SE


ST – 1


Subscriber 2


Response 5


SE – 2


GE


IEA


An Error on Subscriber 1 Level


The following use case is an example of an issue with Subscriber 1 in the same input.


The result of merging the 271 transactions:


ISA


GS


ST - 1


Subscriber 1


AAA with an error


Subscriber 2


Response 3


SE


ST – 1


Subscriber 2


Response 5


SE – 2


GE


IEA


An Error for Subscriber 1 only in Dependent Response


In some cases, it is possible that only a dependent response generates an error for a subscriber. For example, the dependent’s inquiry date invalidates the provider’s date.


This is a sample of what you can expect to see in a merge of 271 transactions:


ISA


GS


ST - 1


Subscriber 1


Reponse 1


Subscriber 1 (corresponds to the Dependent’s response)


AAA with error


Subscriber 2


Response 3


Subscriber 1


Response 4


SE


ST – 1


Subscriber 2


Response 5


SE – 2


GE


IEA


A Combination of Errors for Subscriber 1


The preceding use case is extended with additional errors at the subscriber level from Response 4.


This is a sample of what you can expect to see in a merge of 271 transactions:


ISA


GS


ST - 1


Subscriber 1


Reponse 1


Subscriber 1 (corresponds to Dependent’s response)


AAA with error on Dependent


Subscriber 2


Response 3


Subscriber 1


AAA with error on Inquiry 4


SE


ST – 1


Subscriber 2


Response 5


SE – 2


GE


IEA


A Subscriber becoming a Dependent


In some cases, the back-end decides that a certain patient reported as a Subscriber is actually a Dependent and generates a 271 response accordingly.


The inbound 270 file:


Subscriber 1 (possibly a Kid 1)


Inquiry 1


Subscriber 2 (Kid 2)


Inquiry 2


The expected 271 Response:


Subscriber (Father)


Dependent 1 (Kid 1)


Response 1


Subscriber (Father)


Dependent 2 (Kid 2)


Response 2


A Dependent becoming a Subscriber


This is a case that is completely contrary, when a patient, reported as a Dependent in a 270 transaction, becomes a Subscriber in a 271 transaction.


The inbound 270 file:


Subscriber 1 (Father)


Dependent 1 (Father)


Inquiry 1


Dependent 2 (Mother)


Inquiry 2


The expected 271 Response:


Subscriber 1 (Father)


Response 1


Subscriber 2 (Mother)


Response 2


Merging a 277 Response


Unlike the 271, the 277 transactions with a Subscriber (Patient) and a Subscriber with a Dependent (Patient) are not merged into one Subscriber.


The inbound 277 file #1:


Info Source 1


Info Receiver 1


Provider 1


Subscriber 1


Sub 1 Response 1


The inbound 277 file #2:


Info Source 1


Info Receiver 1


Provider 1


Subscriber 1


Dependent 1


Dep 1 Response 2


The expected result:


Info Source 1


Info Receiver 1


Provider 1


Subscriber 1


Sub 1 Response 1


Subscriber 1


Dependent 1


Dep 1 Response 2

<a id="checkpoint"></a>

#### Checkpoint

> 📄 Source: [Checkpoint.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Checkpoint.htm)

##### Checkpoint


With the Checkpoint component, you can create recovery points in your routes and roll back the route execution to these points in case of a hardware crash or processing error. Using Checkpoint in combination with XEServer storage components (Data Storage, FS) allows you to build robust recovery mechanisms for your route. Click to see the sample profile that demonstrates reusable patterns and best practices on working with Checkpoint.


When an XEServer exchange passes through Checkpoint, the component stores the exchange properties and all messages in a database (the component actually uses the [Checkpoint service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Checkpoint_Service.htm) to connect to the database). If an error occurs further down the route after the Checkpoint, the route executions rolls back to Checkpoint and another processing retry is made. On each retry, the component sends the message through the checkpoint output and sets the property checkpoint_inbound_task_id to identify the retry iteration. If the number of unsuccessful retries reaches the limit, the message goes through the error output as failed.


*[Image: Checkpoint Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Service name** | Specify the [Checkpoint Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Checkpoint_Service.htm) that provides connectivity to a database. |
| **Retry limit** | Specify the maximum number of rollbacks after which the data goes to the error output as failed. |

---

<a id="router"></a>

#### Router

> 📄 Source: [Router.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Router.htm)

##### Router


Router component is used to redirect data flow based on the filter set within the component. You can add as many directions as required for your business needs.


*[Image: Router Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Report to all outputs where filter is passed | Messages go through all the filters and the result is passed to all the outputs with the matching filter. |
| Report only to first output where filter is passed | Data is passed to the first output with the matching filter without going through other filters. |



###### Outputs tab


*[Image: Router Outputs tab]*


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Component output names** | A list of result outputs for a route component. You can edit output names and reorder the list of outputs. For some components you can add and delete outputs. |
| **Message Exchange Properties** | Allows you to add, edit, or delete message exchange properties which are included in output results. For more information about setting filters, see [Components Message Exchange Properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Components_Output_Properties.htm). |



Filtering is available for this component. Use expression language to customize the output results to accommodate your business requirements. For more information, see [Expression language](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Expression_Language.htm).

---

<a id="sequencer"></a>

#### Sequencer

> 📄 Source: [Sequencer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Sequencer.htm)

##### Sequencer


The XEServer Sequencer route component picks up XEServer messages from a route, generates a Sequence ID based on the information retrieved from the Sequence Manager Service, and assigns this Sequence ID to the incoming message. The generated Sequence ID is unique identifier within a message group, and defines the order of the message, picked up by the Sequencer.



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Select the service to be used. Click View to go to the service settings page. If there are no services available on the page, click Create new to create a new service. |
| Group ID | The Group ID is used to split XEServer messages by abstract groups and perform message ordering within each of these groups. Specify the XEServer message property, from which the Group ID is received. Alternately, you may use a persistent Group ID, without having to read it from a message property. In this case, you need to set a message descriptor property with the current Group ID and generated Sequence ID to every message, outgoing from Sequencer. These properties can be resolved on next components by using the {MD_Group_ID} and {MD_Sequence_ID} macros correspondingly. By default, the macro {Prp[GroupID]} is used to resolve "GroupID" message property. |
| Sequence ID property name | The Sequence ID is used to arrange XEServer messages in the order they were received within a message group. Specify the XEServer message property, to which the Sequence ID is set. Alternately, you can set the Sequence ID to a message property on the Outputs tab of most of the components. By default, the "SequenceID" property name is used. |

---

<a id="resequencer"></a>

#### Resequencer

> 📄 Source: [Resequencer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Resequencer.htm)

##### Resequencer


The XEServer Resequencer route component is used to sort XEServer messages, according to the Sequence ID, and send them to the route for further processing.


*[Image: Resequencer component configuration]*


Note A route which contains Resequencer must have the [Maximum number of active tasks per instance](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Route_Properties.htm#Maximum) equal or greater than the product of Message threshold and Group threshold values.


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service name | Select the service to be used. Click View to go to the service settings page. If there are no services available on the page, click Create new to create a new service. |
| Group ID | The Group ID is used to split XEServer messages by abstract groups and perform message ordering within each of these groups. Specify the XEServer message property, from which the Group ID is received. Alternately, you may use a persistent Group ID, without having to read it from a message property. In this case, you need to set a message descriptor property with current Group ID and generated Sequence ID to every message, outgoing from Sequencer. These properties can be resolved on next components by {MD_Group_ID} and {MD_Sequence_ID} macros correspondingly. By default, the macro {Prp[GroupID]} is used to resolve the "GroupID" message property. |
| Sequence ID | The Sequence ID is used to arrange XEServer messages in order they were received within a message group. Specify the XEServer message property, from which the Sequence ID is received. Alternately, you can set the Sequence ID to a message property on the Outputs tab of most of the components. An integer number can be entered in this field, however this causes all processed messages, except the first one, to be directed to the out of order output as their Sequence Ids are the same for all messages. By default, the macro {Prp[SequenceID]} is used to resolve the "Sequence Id" message property. |
| Storage limitations | |
| Expiration timeout | The time period for a message to be stored in memory until the expected message is received. The "expected message" implies a message, whose Sequence ID suits the sequence, that is expected Sequence ID = Sequence ID + 1. For example: a message with Sequence ID = "3" is stored in the database for the time period, defined in this field, or until an expected message with Sequence ID = "2" is received. |
| Message threshold | The maximum number of messages in the scope of each message group that can be stored in a database during the expiration timeout. |
| Group threshold | The maximum number of groups that can be stored in a database during the expiration timeout. |



###### Outputs tab


| Output Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| in order | Messages that were arranged in the proper order are directed to this output. |
| out of order | Messages that could not be arranged in the proper order are directed to this output. For example, an expired timeout or message threshold values may prevent messages from being properly ordered. |
| original | Original messages are directed to this output. |
| error | Messages that could not be processed due to system, route, or component errors. |

---

<a id="sorter"></a>

#### Sorter

> 📄 Source: [Sorter.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Sorter.htm)

##### Sorter


With the Sorter component, you can arrange messages within a message exchange in the desired order. The sorting process is based on the value of XEServer 'sequence.id' message property, however you can define any message property name to be used as a key for sorting on the component Settings tab.



To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Sorter).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Sequence ID | The {M_Prp[]} macro with the name of the message property which value is used as a sorting key. By default, the {M_Prp[sequence.id]} value is used. |
| Sorter order | The sorting algorithm to be applied to incoming messages: Ascending: Messages are sorted in the ascending order from minimum to maximum sequence.id property value. Descending: Messages are sorted in the descending order from maximum to minimum sequence.id property value. |
| Allow duplicates | Select this option to allow Sorter to sort messages with duplicated Sequence ID. |
| Reject all messages if an error encountered | Select this option to redirect all messages with the same Sequence ID to the invalid output if at least one message contains erroneous data (wrong Sequence ID format or undefined Sequence Type, wrong number of duplicates for integer Sequence Type and so on). If not selected, then only messages, containing erroneous data are directed to the invalid channel. |
| Report in multimessage exchange | Select this option to report all sorted messages in one message exchange. If not selected, then each message is reported in a separate message exchange. |
| Message format | Select either the Multimessage or Zip format of the input message. Select Autodetect to allow Sorter identify input message exchange format automatically. Note Supported Zip archives can contain entries on the root level only. |
| Serialization threshold | Specify the maximum size of zipped data that can be stored in memory. If the data size exceeds this limit, it is serialized on the disc. By using this option, you can control memory usage during the processing of messages in zip format. The "B", "K", "M", "G" suffixes can be used to stand for bytes, kilobytes, megabytes, and gigabytes correspondingly. Note This option is available for the Zip message type only. |
| Sequence type | Select the data type of the Sequence ID property: Integer: Select if the Sequence ID property has an integer value. Additional options: Allow gaps - Select to allow gaps between sorted integer sequence IDs, for example 1, 3, 5, 9, .., . If not selected, the error is raised once there is a gap between sorted numeric sequence IDs. Generate missed items - Select to enable Sorter creating an empty message for each missed integer sequence ID (for each gap). If the Integer data type is selected, then messages with sequence.id = null property are directed to the Invalid output. String: Select if the Sequence ID property has a string value. Additional options: Ignore case - Select to ignore the string case. Custom: This option allows you to use additional data types, for example floating numbers, for sorting. Specify your custom java comparator class name to enable sorting messages by custom Sequence ID data types. |



###### Using Comparator Classes


The .jar file that contains your compiled sorter comparator class should be located in the ${XESRoot}/platform/lib.user/shared directory.


Your class should implement the interface under the "${XESRoot}/features/route-engine/components/sorter/src/com/edifecs/etools/xeserver/component/sorter/api/SorterComparator" directory.


For example, you can use a predefined sample comparator class that ignores the case of the Sequence ID message property.


Please refer to ${XESRoot}\features\route-engine\components\sorter\src\com\edifecs\etools\xeserver\component\sorter\comparators to see default comparator classes.


###### Outputs tab


| Output | Description |
| --- | --- |
| data | Messages that were sorted successfully are directed to this output. |
| invalid | Messages that could not be sorted according to the parameters, specified on the Settings tab, or due to invalid data type are directed to this output. |
| original | Original messages are directed to this output. |
| error | Messages that could not be processed due to system/route/component errors. |

---

<a id="splitter"></a>

#### Splitter

> 📄 Source: [Splitter.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Splitter.htm)

##### Splitter


This component allows you to split an input XEServer message body using a custom separator. The component creates a new XEServer exchange on the output for each split partition.


To see the list of macros supported for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Splitter).


*[Image: Splitter Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Parameter Name | Description | | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Record Separator** | Specify a separator to split input messages. To specify a sequence of bytes to be used as a separator, use ", " (a comma followed by the white space) delimiter, for example "0x0D, 0x0A". Only hexadecimal values are allowed for this parameter. | | |
| RetainRecordSeparator | Specify whether to retain the separator in the output messages. By default, the component does not include the separator bytes in the output. Example Mode | RetainRecordSeparator = false | RetainRecordSeparator = true |
| Input Message Body | test*foo*bar*123 | | |
| Record Separator | * (hex 0x2A) | | |
| Split Results | test, foo, bar, 123 | test, *foo, *bar, *123 | |



Note This parameter is available starting from XEServer 9.2.2 hotfix 1.






 **Suppress Empty Message**


 Specify whether you want to skip messages with empty body.

---

<a id="xml-splitter"></a>

#### XML Splitter

> 📄 Source: [XML_Splitter.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XML_Splitter.htm)

##### XML Splitter


With this component, you can split XML documents based on the [XPath](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath/). For each XML node that matches the XPath expression you provide, the component creates a new XML document (one XEServer message within an XEServer exchange) that contains the matched node.


###### XML Splitter Operating Modes


The component can work in two modes:

1. DOM-driven (default): In this mode, XML Splitter builds a document object model from the incoming XML data thereby loading the entire XML document to the RAM. The advantages of this mode are: - simple configuration - you only have to specify an XPath expression to split the incoming XML, - true XPath support - you can use the fully-fledged XPath language (including handy XPath features like predicates and wildcards) to locate an XML node. The main drawback is the inefficiency of the RAM usage (4x-10x RAM consumption from the original XML file size) that could lead to memory overload when you work with large XML files (> 1GB).
2. Scenario-based: In this mode, XML Splitter does not load the input XML data entirely to the RAM. Instead, the component scans the original XML from top to bottom searching for a node with a specific name. The mode's main advantage is the efficiency of memory usage. However, in this mode, you cannot use real XPath queries. For more information on how to enable and use this mode, see the Scenario-based mode.


Below you can view a sample XML document used throughout this section to demonstrate the component's usage. All examples refer to this XML document.

 Click to view sample XML document

```



		John
		Smith


		Caroline
		Edmunds


		Simon
		Lawrence



```






*[Image: XML Splitter Initial parameters tab]*


To see the list of macros for this component, see [XEServer supported macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#XML).


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Initial Parameters tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add header to split messages | Set this property to TRUE to include all the parent nodes (right up to the root node) of the matched node into the split XML documents. The following examples illustrate the difference between the two modes using the XPath expression: /company/employees/firstName[@test-attr='test']. Example 1 (Add header to split messages=true) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <company>     <employees>        <firstName test-attr="test">John</firstName>     </employees> </company> Example 2 (Add header to split messages=false) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <firstName test-attr="test">John</firstName> Using this option, you can preserve the tree hierarchy of the original XML data starting from the root node down to the node that matches your XPath expression. |
| **Create header messages** | Set this property to TRUE to create an additional XML document that contains all the parent nodes (right up to the root node) of the node that matches your XPath expression. This option acts the same way as **Add header to split messages** but stores the parent XML nodes as a separate XML document. |
| Namespace aware | Set this property to TRUE if you want to allow the component to distinguish XML nodes with the same name when they belong to different namespaces. |
| XPath expression | Enter an XPath expression that points to an XML node in the original document where you want the splitting to be performed. |



###### Namespace Context tab


On this tab, you can bind your own namespace prefixes to the namespace URIs in order to use namespaces in your XPath expressions. In Name, you enter your custom prefix that represents the namespace URI. In Value, you enter the actual namespace URI. For example, to use the namespace (xmlns="http://www.edifecs.com/xdata/100") in your XPath expressions, define the following name-value pair:


prefix1 = http://www.edifecs.com/xdata/100


Then, you can use this namespace in your XPath expression:


//prefix1:company/employees


###### Scenario-based Mode


To use this mode, you have to:

1. Create a new splitting scenario.
2. Put the splitting scenario file to ${XESRoot}\features\route-engine\components\xmlsplitter\resources\.
3. Restart EAM.
4. Enable the new splitting scenario on the XML Splitter component's settings.


###### Splitting Scenario Overview


A splitting scenario is an XML-based configuration file that contains rules on how to split input XML data. A splitting scenario file must address the following requirements:

- the file must be a valid XML file with a structure like in the example below.
- the file name must have the following format: {yourScenarioName}.scenario.template.xml
- the file must be placed at: ${XESRoot}\features\route-engine\components\xmlsplitter\resources\


Here is a sample splitting scenario with comments and highlighted parts that are important. Using this scenario, XML Splitter splits the input XML by the node <employee> whose parent is <company>.

```



``` Notice that <node name="y"> is nested within <node name="x">. This instructs XML Splitter to search for the node <employee> inside the node <company>. Similar to XPath, this nesting allows you to locate nodes deep down the XML tree. On the output, this scenario produces three XML documents with the following structure:

```


	{XXXX}
	{YYYY}

```


###### Enable Splitting Scenario


To make XML Splitter work in the character-based mode using your splitting scenario, you have to specify the property Splitting scenario on the component's settings. To do this:

1. On the XML Splitter component's Settings tab, open the Initial Parameters tab.
2. Select the property Splitting scenario, and then click Edit. Tip If the property Splitting scenario is not available, click New and add the property from the drop-down list. If the drop-down list does not contain your splitting scenario, restart EAM.
3. From the Value drop-down list, select your splitting scenario.
4. Save the configuration. Note When you change a splitting scenario file, you should use the Shutdown + Start XEServer commands for any changes to take effect.

<a id="timers"></a>

### Timers

> 📄 Source: [Book_Timers.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Book_Timers.htm)

#### Timers


This section has the following information:

---

<a id="timer-activation"></a>

#### Timer Activation

> 📄 Source: [Timer_Activator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Timer_Activator.htm)

##### Timer Activation


The Timer Activation component is used to start a route timer. A timer can be used to perform tasks at regular intervals from route startup, such as a batching function that is run every 12 hours. An event, such as an error or unavailable service, can start a timer too. Once a command starts a timer, the Timer Reporter component triggers the route at defined intervals. For example, if errors activate a timer, recurrent error alerts can be issued until the problem is resolved. Use the Timer Deactivation component to remove an active timer and its schedule.


*[Image: Timer Activation Scedule activation tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Schedule Activation tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Schedule name** | A name for the schedule. Macros can be used to provide schedule names. For example, Schedule_{R_Name}, where {R_Name} is resolved as a route name. A schedule with such name has the word "Schedule" as a prefix. |
| **Service name** | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| **Activate schedule on start** | Select this option to activate a timer based on a command or event. This method of activation enables input and output handles on the Activator icon in the Route Editor. The input handle receives an activation message, and the output handles can be used to push data to route components that perform tasks according to the timer schedule. |
| **Activate schedule based on a command** | Select this option when a route function needs to occur at regular intervals beginning at route startup. |
| **Pass exchange properties** | Select this option to include message exchange properties in messages routed from the current component to another routing component. |
| **Report error if message with same name already exists** | Select this option to report an error when a schedule having the same name already exists. When this option in not selected, the existing schedule is overwritten with the new schedule and processing proceeds. |



###### Schedule tab


|   |   |
| --- | --- |
| Simple schedule |   |
| **Delay (ms)** | The number of milliseconds to delay processing of incoming messages. |
| **Repeat count** | Specifies the number of times the schedule should be repeated. |
| **Repeat interval (ms)** | Specifies the time interval (in milliseconds) after which the schedule to be repeated. |
| Cron schedule |   |
| **Cron expression** | Click **Cron** to specify a cron expression. The eclipse control (...) displays the Time Expression Editor, which helps you to construct an expression. |
| **Time zone** | Specify the time zone in which the cron schedule functions. If not the local time zone, specify the time zone in relation to Greenwich Mean Time (GMT). |



###### Schedule Properties tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Name** | The name labels the component when it is displayed in the Route Editor and in the Outline pane. |
| **Value** | The value of the schedule property. |

---

<a id="timer-deactivation"></a>

#### Timer Deactivation

> 📄 Source: [Timer_Deactivator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Timer_Deactivator.htm)

##### Timer Deactivation


The Timer Deactivator component removes the specified schedule and its associated timer.


*[Image: Timer Deactivation Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Schedule name** | A name for the schedule. Macros can be used to provide schedule names. For example Schedule_{R_Name}, where {R_Name} is resolved as a route name. A schedule with such name has the word "Schedule" as a prefix. |
| **Service name** | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |

<a id="delay"></a>

#### Delay

> 📄 Source: [Delay.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Delay.htm)

##### Delay


The Delay component delays incoming messages for the specified time interval. Specify the interval either in milliseconds or with a Groovy-based expression. The expression supports macros.


*[Image: Delay Settings tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Delay (ms)** | The number of milliseconds to delay processing of incoming messages. |
| **Delay expression** | A Groovy expression that calculates the delay interval. To get a list of the available macros, press CTRL+space. |

---

<a id="job-scheduler"></a>

#### Job Scheduler

> 📄 Source: [Job_Scheduler.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Job_Scheduler.htm)

##### Job Scheduler


With this component, you can initiate a process in a route after receiving a trigger command from [Scheduler Server Service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Job_Scheduler_Server_Service.htm). When a command is received, the component generates an XEServer message and directs it to the route.


The component cannot directly receive commands from Scheduler Server Service, but it uses the Scheduler Client Service as a intermediate layer.


When the component is added to a route, you can view the following settings:



###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Service Name | Select the Job Scheduler Client service to be used by the current component. One Job Scheduler Client can be used by multiple Job Scheduler components. |
| Message body | Use the text area to provide a body for trigger message. For this field, [message descriptors](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Route_Macros.htm#Job) and route macros can be used. |



###### Outputs Tab


You can extract XEServer message exchange properties sent together with the trigger message from Job Scheduler Server service. For more information, see Scheduled Jobs section in XESManager help.

---

<a id="timer-reporting"></a>

#### Timer Reporting

> 📄 Source: [Timer_Reporting.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Timer_Reporting.htm)

##### Timer Reporting


This component reports a message based on the schedule. You can create your schedule or use a schedule that is already registered by providing its name.


*[Image: Timer Reporting Message reporter tab]*


###### Component Settings


Apart from [generic](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Component_Properties.htm) settings, you can configure the following component-specific settings.


###### Settings tab


###### Message Reporter tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Report message according to** | A name for the schedule. Macros can be used to provide schedule names. Own schedule Schedule with name |
| **Service name** | Select the XEServer [service](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/XEServer_Services_Overview.htm) associated with the current component. |
| **Message body** | Click Browse and provide message text or use macros to be resolved as message text. |
| **Load all schedule properties** | Selecting this option enables passing schedule properties from incoming messages to change the properties of the schedule that the timer component is currently using and pass other pertinent data to other components. For example, when a message is received you can save it to data storage with some specific ID and set the schedule to report an event in five seconds, passing this ID and as a property. When the event is reported, the Timer Reporter creates a message that contains the original ID as a property. On the next route step you can extract data from data storage with using this ID. |
| Discard events when route busy | Select this option to discard events when the processing queue is full (100 events). When a new timer event is received but cannot be processed because the processing queue is full (100 events), then the error is reported on the Timer Reporting component and the rout stops. |
| Report warning for discarded events | Used only in conjunction with the Discard events when route busy option. Select this option when you want server to report TRIGGER_EVENT_DISCARDED with warning severity. If the Report warning for discard events option is not selected, then a debug message is reported. |



###### Schedule tab


|   |   |
| --- | --- |
| Simple schedule |   |
| **Delay (ms)** | The number of milliseconds to delay processing of incoming messages. |
| **Repeat count** | Specifies the number of times the schedule should be repeated. |
| **Repeat interval (ms)** | Specifies the time interval (in milliseconds) after which the schedule to be repeated. |
| Cron schedule |   |
| **Delay (ms)** | The number of milliseconds to delay processing of incoming messages. |
| **Cron expression** | Click **Cron** to specify a time expression. For more information, see [Time Expression Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/eam/9.4/Content/Time_Expression_Editor.htm). |
| **Time zone** | Specify the time zone in which the cron schedule functions. If not the local time zone, specify the time zone in relation to Greenwich Mean Time (GMT). |



###### Schedule Properties tab


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Name** | The name labels the component when it is displayed in the Route Editor and in the Outline pane. |
| **Value** | The value of the schedule property. |
